title: Real-Time Atmospheric Turbulence Mitigation Using CPU-Based Multiframe Fusion on Embedded Electro-Optical Systems
authors: Zuhal Hançer, Ozan Şenyayla, Velİ Bakircioğlu
abstract: Atmospheric turbulence degrades long-range electro-optical (EO) imagery with geometric warping, blur, and temporal jitter. We present a CPU-only, registration-free, tile-wise multi-frame fusion operator specifically tailored for embedded mission systems operating under strict latency constraints. Instead of relying on computationally expensive dense motion compensation (e.g., optical flow or phase correlation), temporal fusion is governed by two lightweight heuristic gates: (i) a local monotonicity test evaluated on the per-tile normalized cross-correlation (NCC) trajectory to suppress geometric flicker, and (ii) a global active-tile ratio that triggers immediate buffer resets to robustly handle sudden platform or scene motion. Stable tiles are seamlessly combined via NCC-weighted convex interpolation, while persistently moving tiles intentionally bypass fusion to prevent ghosting artifacts. This architecture ensures linear computational complexity with respect to pixel count. To enable rigorous quantitative assessment, we adopt a paired evaluation protocol, utilizing controlled parametric degradation on pristine field sequences to synthesize realistic turbulence severities. Evaluation across visible (TV) and mid-wave infrared (IR) modalities demonstrates consistent improvements in temporal geometric coherence (TSI) and perceptual quality (BRISQUE, NIQE, PIQE), alongside structural recovery evidenced by SSIM gains; in low-contrast IR sequences, PSNR can decrease modestly while temporal stability and perceived sharpness improve. A single-threaded C++ implementation executing on a resource-constrained embedded CPU (NVIDIA Jetson Orin NX, with hardware accelerators disabled) processes operational field imagery in 26.25 ms per frame (38.1 FPS). Comprehensive runtime comparisons against classical CPU-feasible baselines confirm highly favorable quality-latency trade-offs. These results demonstrate that computationally prohibitive explicit registration is not a strict necessity for operational turbulence mitigation; the proposed similarity-gated fusion framework provides a transparent, deterministic, real-time baseline suitable for immediate deployment on deeply constrained EO payloads.

title: DynaFusion-VIO: A Hybrid Visual Inertial Approach for Dynamic World Navigation
authors: R. Mojtahedi, M. R. Mosavi
abstract: This paper presents an advanced Visual–Inertial Odometry SLAM (VIO-SLAM) system designed to deliver robust and accurate performance in dynamic environments. The proposed front-end employs a deep learning–based visual odometry model capable of identifying and handling dynamic objects, thereby preventing their adverse influence on the motion estimation process. To further enhance robustness, the system integrates this visual front-end with a reliable inertial fusion module inspired by PVGO, enabling consistent trajectory estimation even in challenging conditions. The core contribution of this research lies in the intelligent integration of a deep learning–driven dynamic-aware front-end with an optimization-based inertial back-end. This combination results in a stable and precise estimation framework suitable for complex and crowded environments. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed system significantly outperforms existing approaches in terms of Average Trajectory Error (ATE) and overall system stability, particularly in scenarios where traditional methods struggle due to dynamic elements. The simulation codes and implementation details will be made publicly available at the GitHub repository below upon the publication of the paper: https://github.com/rezamj201/DynaFusion-VIO.

title: Wireless-Powered Communications for Next-Generation Networks: A Comprehensive Throughput Analysis under Nonlinear Energy Harvesting
authors: Yazan H. Al-Badarneh, Osamah S. Badarneh, Mustafa K. Alshawaqfeh, Tamer M. Khattab, Mazen O. Hasna, Khalid A. Qaraqe
abstract: In this paper, we investigate the performance of wireless-powered communication (WPC) systems operating under the harvest-then-transmit protocol with a three-piecewise nonlinear energy harvesting (EH) model, which explicitly captures the sensitivity and saturation behavior of practical EH circuits. In parallel, we adopt a versatile fading characterization by assuming Nakagami-m channels in the EH phase and generalized Gamma channels in the information transfer phase, the latter being of practical interest as it unifies several standard fading distributions and accurately approximates fading environments in next-generation networks. Based on this setting, we develop a novel analytical framework that yields, for the first time, exact analytical expressions for the average throughput in the delay-limited, delay-tolerant, and Quality of Service (QoS) delay-constrained transmission modes. We then apply the developed framework to study the throughput of WPC-enabled reconfigurable intelligent surface-assisted systems as a direct application example. We conduct extensive Monte-Carlo simulations under various system configurations to confirm the accuracy of the analytical results. Our results show that nonlinear EH effects yield a more realistic benchmark for the average throughput compared to the conventional linear EH model, particularly under stringent sensitivity and saturation constraints.

title: Enhancing Stability of Probabilistic Model-Based Reinforcement Learning by Adaptive Noise Filtering
authors: Wenjun Huang, Xinrui Yue, Yidong Chen, Tianfu Sun, Yunduan Cui
publish: IEEE Transactions on Neural Networks and Learning Systems
abstract: This article proposes stabilized model-based policy optimization (SMBPO) to address the stability and efficiency issues in current probabilistic model-based reinforcement learning (MBRL) approaches. It adaptively filters the noises caused by imperfect models in both model and policy updates: 1) dimensions with abnormal distributions in the prediction are refined to stabilize the training of probabilistic models and 2) predicted states and estimated value functions are clipped to mitigate the negative impact of model bias on the learned policy. Leveraging the stability above, SMBPO employs batch normalization (BN) to significantly boost its learning efficiency. Evaluated on five MuJoCo control benchmarks and one dexterous hand scenario, SMBPO reduces training time by 90% while achieving 50% more cumulative rewards than state-of-the-art model-free and MBRL baselines, thereby extending the feasibility of MBRL in practice. The open source code of SMBPO is available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/mrjun123/SMBPO</uri>

title: Difficulty-Controllable Multiple-Choice Question Generation Using Large Language Models and Direct Preference Optimization
authors: Yuto Tomikawa, Masaki Uto
abstract: Difficulty-controllable question generation for reading comprehension has gained significant attention in the field of education as a fundamental tool for adaptive learning support. Although several neural question generation methods have recently succeeded in controlling difficulty, conventional approaches still face two major limitations. First, they cannot directly generate multiple-choice questions, which are the most widely used question type in educational contexts. Second, they are not explicitly trained to optimize the accuracy of difficulty control, leaving room for further improvement in difficulty controllability. To address these limitations, this study proposes a novel difficulty-controllable multiple-choice question generation method for reading comprehension which leverages a large language model trained using a direct preference optimization technique to improve the accuracy of difficulty control. Experiments on an actual multiple-choice reading comprehension question dataset showed that the proposed method effectively improves difficulty controllability while maintaining fluency, content relevance, and answerability. Additionally, comparison of the proposed method with a few-shot learning approach indicates that few-shot learning is insufficient for achieving difficulty control, highlighting the advantages of the proposed method.

title: Improving Trajectory Continuity in Drone-based Crowd Monitoring Using a Set of Minimal-cost Techniques and Deep Discriminative Correlation Filters
authors: Bartosz Ptak, Marek Kraft
abstract: Drone-based crowd monitoring has become an important component of multimedia video analytics for applications in surveillance, public safety, and event management. However, maintaining long-term tracking continuity and temporal consistency in aerial video streams remains a significant challenge. Traditional detection–assignment tracking methods suffer from false positives, false negatives, and frequent identity switches, which degrade counting accuracy and limit higher-level spatio-temporal analysis. This paper introduces a point-oriented online tracking algorithm that improves trajectory continuity and counting reliability in drone-based crowd monitoring from video data. The proposed method builds on the Simple Online and Real-Time Tracking (SORT) framework, replacing the original bounding-box-based association with a point-distance metric tailored for dense, small-object video tracking. The algorithm is further enhanced with three computationally efficient techniques: camera motion compensation, altitude-aware adaptive assignment, and classification-based trajectory validation. In addition, Deep Discriminative Correlation Filters (DDCF) are integrated to improve temporal robustness in the presence of missed detections by reusing spatial feature maps computed by the localisation network, enabling efficient feature sharing without additional inference cost. The proposed approach is evaluated on the DroneCrowd benchmark and the newly introduced UP-COUNT-TRACK dataset, demonstrating substantial improvements in tracking performance, with trajectory counting errors reduced to 23% and 15%, respectively. The results further show a significant reduction in identity switches while maintaining high tracking accuracy, outperforming strong online baselines and even a globally optimised offline tracking method.

title: Reconfigurable Integrated Sensing and Communications (RISAC): Sparse MIMO and Hybrid Beamforming in Far and Near Fields
authors: Xiangrong Wang, Fulvio Gini, Kaiquan Cai
abstract: This article provides a comprehensive investigation into reconfigurable integrated sensing and communications (RISAC), an emerging paradigm designed to maximize the performance–cost tradeoff by exploiting the inherent spatial sparsity of multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) arrays. Deviating from conventional static ISAC, RISAC adopts a cognitive “perception–action” cycle, enabling the adaptive reconfiguration of array geometries and beamforming weights in response to dynamic environmental feedback. Central to RISAC is the exploitation of two intertwined layers of degrees of freedom (DoFs): element-space sparsity via sparse MIMO array design and beam-space sparsity via hybrid beamforming (HBF). We further demonstrate that by synergistically co-designing these coupled DoFs, sparse MIMO HBF can rival the performance of fully digital systems when accounting for practical mutual coupling, at a significantly reduced hardware cost. This work analyzed both far-and near-field propagation regimes across various ISAC frameworks, including radar-centric, communication-centric, and joint co-design. In addition to downlink co-design, we also examine uplink coexistence via shared waveform design. Finally, this article outlines promising research directions, positioning RISAC as a critical evolution toward adaptive, hardware-efficient, and dual-functional systems.

title: SA-YOLO: Stage-Aware Attention for Real-Time Fire and Smoke Detection on Edge Devices
authors: Jinho Kim, Gyeyoung Kim
abstract: Fire incidents in modern industrial facilities and dense urban environments can spread rapidly, making detection within the initial golden time window critical for minimizing casualties and property damage. Conventional sensor-based fire detection systems are inherently limited by physical constraints, often resulting in delayed responses and exhibiting high miss rates in large-scale or high-ceiling environments. While vision-based detectors offer a promising alternative, lightweight models often lose representation power for small flames or low-contrast smoke under complex backgrounds. Although recent attention-centric detectors such as YOLOv12-n improve global feature modelling, their uniform attention design remains suboptimal for fire–smoke detection, where different visual cues dominate at different feature scales. This mismatch is particularly critical in fire–smoke detection, where flames and smoke exhibit fundamentally different scale-dependent visual characteristics that cannot be effectively handled by uniform attention refinement. To address this limitation, we propose Stage-Aware YOLO (SA-YOLO), a lightweight real-time detector that strategically allocates heterogeneous attention modules to backbone stages according to their representational roles. Specifically, Efficient Channel Attention (ECA) is deployed at the lower stage (P3) to preserve fine, high-frequency details of small flames; Parallel Attention Module (PAM) is applied at the middle stage (P4) to enhance contextual cues around fire regions; and Residual Efficient Channel Attention (ResECA) is integrated at the higher stage (P5) to stably preserve weak and diffuse smoke signals that tend to vanish in deeper layers. Extensive experiments on the AI-Hub Fire Prediction Video dataset demonstrate that SA-YOLO consistently outperforms the YOLOv12-n baseline across single-class (fire-only and smoke-only) and multi-class (fire+smoke) settings. In the multi-class setting (100 epochs), SA-YOLO improves mAP@50 by 1.95 percentage points and Recall by 4.0 percentage points while preserving real-time inference speed. Despite the introduction of stage-specific attention modules, SA-YOLO maintains a throughput of 61.08 FPS with minimal computational overhead, confirming its suitability for edge-oriented fire monitoring systems operating under challenging visibility conditions.

title: Control Plane Load-Balancing Optimization
authors: Zina Houhamdi, Mohamed Raid Athamena, Belkacem Athamena
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Communication, Networking and Services (ICCNS)
abstract: This paper presents a novel two-tier load-balancing framework tailored for control plane management. The system relies on a global controller that intelligently allocates workloads among local controllers by tracking critical performance indicators, including CPU utilization, system load, and response latency. When a switch encounters an unfamiliar data flow during operation, it forwards the request to the global controller. The global controller evaluates real-time network conditions and delegates the flow to the most suitable local controller. The switch subsequently receives optimized routing instructions, ensuring reliable and efficient data delivery. The framework includes a traffic classifier combined with the Analytical Hierarchical Process (AHP) to improve performance further. This integrated method allows for real-time network flow prioritization while maintaining scalability and fair load distribution throughout the system.

title: Implementation of Funnel Control for Water Level Tank System
authors: Zaina Ait-Chekdhidh, Aghiles Ardjal, Maamar Bettayeb
publish: 2025 Advances in Science and Engineering Technology International Conferences (ASET)
abstract: This paper offers some important advancements in its investigation of the use of funnel control methods in a water reservoir. First off, by employing funnel control approaches to optimize the performance of the water level monitoring system, it ensures the system's accurate dynamic behavior and strengthens its capacity to meet control requirements. To evaluate how well three distinct funnel controllers manage a water level tank system, they are put into practice. The implemented controllers consist of an asymmetric funnel controller and a regular funnel controller, and PI-funnel controller. These results enhance the efficacy and flexibility of control systems in water reservoir operation.

title: GAT-MADDPG Algorithm-Based Volt-VAR Control for Active Distribution Networks
authors: Jingxuan Ding, Xiaobo Dou, Congyue Zhang, Rui Liu, Xinghua Huang
publish: 2025 4th Asia Power and Electrical Technology Conference (APET)
abstract: The widespread development of distributed photovoltaic units creates voltage regulation challenges in distribution networks. Traditional MADDPG algorithms use uniform policies in multilayer perceptrons, ignoring electrical topology and restricting coordination along physical connections. To address this, we propose a Graph Attention Network (GAT)-based MADDPG algorithm, referred to as GAT-MADDPG. This method integrates GAT into actor strategies, enabling agents to learn attention weights based on network connectivity and prioritize coordination with electrically connected agents. Network topology and PV location are automatically learned through an automated pipeline using scenario metadata, without explicit inputs. Tested on the IEEE 33-bus system, the method reduces voltage violations by 34 %, active power loss by 18 %, and improves control response speed by 25 % compared to MADDPG. Notably, the learned attention coefficients correlate (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$r=0.280$</tex>) with voltage deviation, showing that the model naturally focuses on electrically influential neighbors. This behavior emerges from training on raw telemetry data without explicit supervision.

title: ACARIS: Attempting to Improve Conversational AI and Human Social Skills using User Embeddings
authors: Tejas Pravinbhai Patel, Sandeep Shivam, Amit Kumar Padhy, Bharadwaj Vulugunda, Chaitanya Kulkarni, Chandrashekhar Medicherla
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: ACARIS (Advanced Communication Augmentor and Relational Insights System) proposes a novel method to analyze emotional state, intent, and interest in text communication, aiming to improve human social skills and conversational AI performance. By leveraging user embeddings and DistilBERT architecture modifications, ACARIS seeks to predict human behavior with high confidence, offering personalized insights for better interpersonal relationships and AI interactions. Despite initial challenges, ongoing research aims to refine ACARIS for broader applications in understanding human dynamics. This work provides valuable insights into when static user embeddings do and do not improve sentiment classification performance.

title: Modeling and Simulation of LTE Modulation Schemes: A Practical Approach Using MATLAB and Simulink
authors: John Harb, Abdallah Kassem, Chady El Moucary
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: In today's world, wireless communication is becoming more and more necessary, and LTE (Long-Term Evolution) technology is key to this development. Compared to earlier technologies, LTE offers faster data rates, higher economy, and improved dependability, which has completely changed communication. An overview of the LTE standard is given in this document, with special attention to important components including modulation, demodulation methods, error correction and detection methods. The research performs simulations of QPSK, 16QAM, and 64QAM, which represent both efficient and high-speed modulation schemes, to explain the modulation methods used in LTE. It then analyses their performance in terms of Bit Error Rate (BER) under varied Eb/N0 ratios. In addition, we simulated OFDM utilizing 16QAM and QPSK and compared the results of their BER vs Eb/No. We also used our previously tested schemes for text transmission utilizing QPSK, 16QAM, and 64QAM, and we compared the outcomes with various SNRs. The research uses these simulations to show how LTE can improve network performance overall, enable faster data rates, and improve communication quality.

title: Space-Native Hardware: A Vision for a New Architecture for On-Satellite LLM Inference
authors: Daliang Xu, Yixing Luo, Songwei Pei, Qing Li, Mengwei Xu, Shangguang Wang
abstract: Low Earth orbit (LEO) satellites are increasingly equipped with on-board computing, where large language model (LLM) inference has emerged as a representative workload for tasks. Yet, LLM inference is highly resource-intensive and faces unique LEO challenges, including irradiation effects, energy and battery constraints, and thermal throttling. Existing approaches fall short: edge accelerators lack space awareness, while space-grade hardware remains costly, limited in performance, and inefficient for LLM workloads. In this work, we provide a detailed analysis of these limitations and envision space-native hardware, an architecture-level design that combines the radiation resistance and reliability of space-grade systems with the cost efficiency and LLM capability of COTS hardware. Our vision further incorporates energy- and battery-aware intermittent execution, adaptive compute units responsive to power and thermal variations, and a space-native ISA with customized instructions for fault tolerance, checkpointing and recovery, and execution-mode control.

title: A Data-Driven Multi-Output Aircraft Flight Simulation Model Construction Method
authors: Jiayi Wang, Shuhan Li, Zhexu Liu
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Science, and Information Processing (AICSIP)
abstract: To further improve the accuracy of civil aviation system simulation, this paper proposes a data-driven multioutput aircraft flight simulation model construction method. First, an interpretable supervised learning algorithm is used to perform feature selection on the raw data, followed by Random Forest for quantifying the importance of each feature. Then, to address the limitations of the traditional Bidirectional Gated Recurrent Unit (BiGRU) model, a Self-Attention mechanism is introduced to enhance the model's featurecapturing ability. Based on the selected features, an aircraft flight simulation model is constructed. Finally, through experiments using real flight data collected from the Quick Access Recorder (QAR), the proposed method demonstrates significant advantages in aircraft flight simulation performance.

title: A Scale-Free Interbank Connection Network for Banking Systemic Risk Modeling
authors: Yeftanus Antonio, Moch. Fandi Ansori, Shan Shavera, Natasha Avrila, Ayomi Sasmito, Hoirun Nisa
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: In banking systems, systemic risk is a serious issue as interbank relationships may allow the collapse of one institution to spread. This study uses a probabilistic scale-free network to simulate interbank ties to reflect the intricacy and unpredictability of financial connections. To examine the spread of risk during several phases of bank collapse, a network-based systemic risk framework is created. The findings demonstrate that interbank links serve two purposes: they sustain liquidity and disperse shocks under moderate stress, which improves stability, but they also promote contagion and magnify failures under extreme circumstances. These results show how crucial network structure is in determining systemic risk and give regulators guidance for creating robust interbank networks.

title: Design and Fault Diagnosis Strategy for Series-Type Hybrid DC Circuit Breakers
authors: Caifa Fan, Longfei Tang
publish: 2025 4th Asia Power and Electrical Technology Conference (APET)
abstract: DC circuit breakers are crucial for protecting the normal operation of DC distribution networks. This paper proposes an improved series-type hybrid DC circuit breaker and its breaking strategy. While suppressing short-circuit currents, the method enables simple and rapid fault-type diagnosis, and controls the breaking operation of the mechanical circuit breaker based on diagnostic results, thereby enhancing the reliability of the hybrid DC circuit breaker. A coupled inductor designed through the finite element iterative optimization method provides a long-duration, low-current-ripple opening window for the mechanical circuit breaker, extending its contact lifespan. Simulation and experimental results demonstrate that the proposed topology and control strategy effectively achieve rapid short-circuit current suppression, accurate fault identification, and arc-less breaking of the mechanical circuit breaker in a 60V/6A DC system. This offers a feasible solution for the safety protection of DC distribution systems.

title: A Low Noise CMOS Image Sensor with Column-Parallel Readout
authors: Amandeep Kaur, Mukul Sarkar
publish: 2026 39th International Conference on VLSI Design &amp; 25th International Conference on Embedded Systems (VLSID)
abstract: This work proposes a vertical fixed pattern noise (VFPN) reduction technique in CMOS image sensors. The technique incorporates the use of double sampling circuit wherein the offset voltage of the amplifier is stored during the sampling phase and canceled in the amplification phase without any increment in chip area and clock cycles. The proposed technique is implemented as a column-parallel readout with the pitch of 9 µm and verified over silicon for a 128 × 96 pixel array. The measurement results show the reduction in vertical FPN to 0.069%. The prototype image sensor is designed and fabricated in AMS 350 nm CMOS OPTO process using 3.3 V power supply. The designed image sensor occupies an area of 1.69 mm × 5.75 mm and consumes 55 mW of power.

title: A Novel VLSI Architecture and FPGA Implementation of Hardware-Efficient Mfocuss-Based Wideband Spectrum Sensor for Dynamic Spectrum Access
authors: Aayush Shrivas, Rahul Shrestha
publish: 2026 39th International Conference on VLSI Design &amp; 25th International Conference on Embedded Systems (VLSID)
abstract: This work presents the hardware-friendly implementation of the Modified FOCUSS (MFOCUSS) algorithm for multicoset-based wideband spectrum sensing. The design enables low complexity and reduced latency by replacing computationally expensive matrix operations with hardware-optimized equivalents, reusing functional modules such as matrix multipliers across multiple stages, and minimizing storage through the diagonal structure of weight matrices. A hardware-efficient halting criterion based on real-valued weight updates is introduced, eliminating costly complex norm and division operations while preserving algorithmic correctness. Performance analysis shows that the proposed approach achieves a detection probability of 0.8 at an −5 dB SNR with 70% prior information using 10 cosets. The complete wideband spectrum sensor (WSSR) is implemented on a Stratix IV FPGA, achieving a sensing time of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$132 \mu \mathrm{s}$</tex>. Compared with prior Stratix IV implementations, the proposed architecture reduces dedicated logic usage by 62.4%, combinational ALUTs by 4.5%, block memory by 99.7%, latency by 64%, and sensing time by 24.6%, making it suitable for real-time spectrum sensing. To the best of our knowledge, this is the first hardware implementation of the MFOCUSS algorithm for wideband spectrum sensing.

title: An Intelligent Fuzzy Logic Controller for Optimal Thermal Management of Electric Vehicle Batteries
authors: Emna Horchani, Salem Saidi, Wrida Khriji, Rabeh Abbassi
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Sustainable Energy and Net-Zero Emissions (SENZE)
abstract: The temperature regulation of electric vehicle (EV) batteries has become a critical concern in modern electric mobility, as it directly influences battery performance, safety, and long-term sustainability. With the global push toward decarbonization and the mass adoption of EVs, maintaining batteries within their optimal thermal range is no longer optional but essential. In this context, this paper presents a liquid-based battery cooling system for EV, designed to maintain the optimal temperature of the Lithium-ion battery pack. The system uses a liquid-cooling plate beneath the battery, activated by a microcontroller when temperature thresholds are exceeded. A Fuzzy Logic Controller (FLC) and a Proportional-Integral-Derivative controller (PID) are suggested to adjust the coolant flow rate based on the temperature error and its derivative, ensuring responsive and efficient cooling.

title: Implementation Guide for Secure RESTful Asset Management APIs under IEC 62443-4-2: A Redfish Case Study
authors: Douglas S. Almeida, Péricles Rezende Barros, George Acioli Júnior, Thiago A. M. Euzébio
abstract: The use of RESTful APIs for asset management has become a standard practice in IT environments due to its interoperability and flexibility. Recently, this paradigm has extended to Operational Technology (OT) networks, where protocols like Redfish are increasingly adopted to manage industrial automation devices. However, this convergence necessitates rigorous cybersecurity compliance, with the IEC 62443 series serving as the dominant framework for securing Industrial Automation and Control Systems (IACS). This paper bridges the gap between the IEC 62443-4-2 standard and practical software engineering by presenting an implementation guide for securing Redfish APIs at Security Level 2 (SL2). We detail the integration of critical controls, including Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) and DoS protection, into a Python-based server. Experimental validation conducted on industrial distributed control nodes demonstrates the system’s resilience against common threats while maintaining telemetry availability, providing a verified reference for secure industrial component development.

title: High-Gain Printed Helical Antenna Driven by Resonant Planar Endfire Circularly Polarized Launcher
authors: Shan-Shan Gu, Wen-Jun Lu, Lei Zhu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Antennas and Propagation
abstract: A hybrid structure high-gain planar endfire circularly polarized antenna (PECPA) is proposed. A resonant PECPA using a 2-D electric sectorial dipole and a rectangular magnetic dipole is employed to drive a travelling-wave helical radiator to yield high-gain characteristic while maintaining relatively compact size. Owing to the hybrid resonant and travelling-wave structure, both half-power beamwidth (HPBW) and 3-dB axial ratio beamwidth (ARBW) are simultaneously adjusted to be coincided. Validations shows that the endfire gain reaches over 9.4 dBic, with respectively coincided HPBW and ARBW of 42° (elevation) and 38° (azimuth) in 2.35-2.45 GHz band. The hybrid structure antenna maintains the low-profile merit of PECPAs at the basic form, with a resultant height of less than 0.06-wavelength. It also possesses a width of about half-wavelength, which makes it potential for array designs.

title: Exploitation of EV Drive Powertrain Power Converter for Real-Time Battery Impedance Identification Using Electrochemical Impedance Spectroscopy
authors: Nuradin Muktar, Shady Gadoue, Kamyar Mehran
abstract: Battery impedance identification is an important non-invasive technique for assessing the state of charge (SoC) and health of lithium-ion batteries in electric vehicle applications. Conventional electrochemical impedance spectroscopy (EIS) requires offline laboratory equipment and long acquisition times, limiting its suitability for embedded monitoring. This paper presents an inverter-based online EIS implementation within a standard field-oriented controlled motor drive and positions the contribution as a practically validated advancement beyond prior inverter-based EIS studies. A small sinusoidal perturbation is injected through the id axis, and battery impedance is extracted from terminal voltage and current using fast Fourier transform analysis over 32 Hz–1 kHz. The study establishes an operating-condition guideline showing that reliable DC-side impedance observability depends on a biased id operating point, and introduces a frequency-scaled excitation strategy that maintains an approximately constant battery-terminal voltage ripple despite DC-link and drivetrain dynamics. The method is experimentally benchmarked against laboratory potentiostat measurements across multiple SoC levels and validated on both permanent magnet synchronous motor and induction motor drives under real-time operation. Quantitative analysis shows a maximum root mean square error of approximately 10% relative to offline measurements, while multi-frequency torque ripple characterization confirms limited impact on drivetrain performance. The results demonstrate that accurate online impedance identification can be achieved in active EV powertrains without additional hardware.

title: Three-Phase Current Reconstruction Method for Series-end Winding Motor Drive Using a Single DC-Link Current Sensor
authors: Junnan You, Zicheng Liu, Yanchao Xiong, Guangyu Wang, Bin Li, Qiong Liu, Dong Jiang
abstract: The series-end winding motor drive (SWMD) represents a novel topology that offers a broad speed control range. It has gained significant attention in motor drive systems owing to its potential to reduce costs. The current reconstruction method also presents advantages in terms of cost and size reduction, and it has been widely used in motor control. Nevertheless, a current reconstruction method tailored for SWMD is currently unavailable. This paper aims to address this gap and further reduce system cost and size. Firstly, the relationship between the DC-link current and phase currents in SWMD is analyzed, and a current reconstruction method using a single DC-link current sensor suitable for SWMD is proposed. Subsequently, the challenges in current reconstruction for SWMD are discussed, particularly the issues of current reconstruction dead zones and current reconstruction errors. To address these issues, an optimization method is proposed: the minimum ripple current phase-shifting PWM (PS-PWM) method with the average current prediction compensation. Lastly, experimental validation on a permanent-magnet synchronous motor (PMSM) demonstrates a notable reduction in reconstruction dead zones and effective reconstruction errors compensation, achieving merely 5.8% current error under rated conditions. The proposed approach enables high-accuracy current reconstruction while maintaining SWMD's cost and size advantages.

title: Advancing Privacy and Accuracy With Federated Learning and Homomorphic Encryption_supp1-3670702.pdf
authors: Tuy Nguyen
abstract: This paper presents a novel theoretical framework integrating federated learning (FL) with homomorphic encryption (HE) through the Cheon-Kim-Kim-Song (CKKS) algorithm to address fundamental privacy-accuracy trade-offs in distributed machine learning. Our primary contribution is a Taylor-based diagonal Hessian approximation method that enables efficient gradient computation under encryption constraints while maintaining provable convergence guarantees. The framework incorporates Byzantine-resilient client selection mechanisms and establishes formal security bounds against adversarial attacks. Experimental validation across Fashion-MNIST, medical X-ray, and Non-IID CIFAR-10 datasets demonstrates competitive accuracies (92.5–97.3%) with superior attack resilience, limiting accuracy degradation to 2–5% under intensive poisoning scenarios compared to 15–25% in conventional approaches. The theoretical foundations and empirical results establish the practical viability of privacy-preserving FL for real-world deployment in sensitive domains.

title: Ampli-Flection for 6G: Active-RIS-Aided Aerial Backhaul with Full 3D Coverage
authors: Hong-Bae Jeon, Chan-Byoung Chae
abstract: In this paper, we propose a novel aerial backhaul architecture that employs an aerial active reconfigurable intelligent surface (RIS) to achieve energy-efficient, full 3D coverage including UAV-BSs and ground users in 6G wireless networks. Unlike prior aerial-RIS approaches limited to 2D coverage with only servicing ground users or passive operation, the proposed design integrates an active-RIS onto a high-altitude aerial platform, enabling reliable line-of-sight links and overcoming multiplicative fading through amplification. In a scenario with UAV-BSs deployed to handle sudden traffic surges in urban areas, the aerial-active-RIS both reflects and amplifies backhaul signals to overcome blockage. We jointly optimize the aerial platform placement, array partitioning, and RIS phase configuration to maximize UAV-BS energy-efficiency. Simulation results confirm that the proposed method significantly outperforms benchmarks, demonstrating its strong potential to deliver resilient backhaul connectivity with comprehensive 3D coverage in 6G networks.

title: YOLO-CX: Towards Better Small Object Detection in Aerial Scenes via Context-Aware Learning and Derivative-Conditioned Attention
authors: Youssef A. Khadragy, Maggie Mashaly
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: Small object detection remains a critical challenge in computer vision, particularly in high-altitude aerial images captured by drones, where extreme camera-to-target distances, motion blur and cluttered backgrounds exacerbate objects’ resolution loss and scale variability. While state-of-the-art detectors achieve high accuracy in general detection tasks, their performance degrades significantly in drone-view scenarios with dense small targets. To address this, YOLO-CX is introduced, which is a context expert framework tailored for small object detection. The proposed approach integrates four key innovations: a Dynamic Context Routing Network (DCRNet) that adaptively interpolates continuous receptive fields at each spatial location through soft routing between low and high context experts; a Cosine-Annealed Noise Regularization (CAN-Reg) strategy, embedded within the routing mechanism of DCRNet, that injects scheduled Gaussian noise during training to improve robustness and mitigate early overfitting; an Edge-Aware Downsampling (EAD) block leveraging a novel Derivative-Conditioned Attention Mechanism (DCAM) to amplify salient structures prior to resolution reduction via a pixel unshuffle operation; and a Bi-level Reweighting via Dynamic Gated Excitation (BRiDGE) block that fuses feature maps from different abstraction levels through adaptive concatenation. Experiments on VisDrone2019 show that the proposed method improves mAP<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">50</inf> by 13.9% and mAP<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">50:95</inf> by 6.7% over the baseline YOLOv11s model, bridging the gap between general-purpose detectors and the demands of drone-based small object detection.

title: Funnel Control for the Speed of Wind Energy Systems for Maximum Power Point Tracking
authors: Zaina Ait-Chekdhidh, Aghiles Ardjal, Maamar Bettayeb
publish: 2025 Advances in Science and Engineering Technology International Conferences (ASET)
abstract: This study examines two different types of Funnel control (FC): classical Funnel control and Funnel control with scaling gain, for regulating the speed of wind energy systems (SWES). We also analyze the influence of three distinct Funnel boundaries (FB) “linear, exponential, and Gaussian” on the system's performance, thereby highlighting the significant importance of the choice of (FB). The goal of (FC) is to ensure precise tracking with specified transient dynamics and to achieve the maximum power point tracking (MPPT). We present a comparative study between scaling-gain Funnel Control and traditional Funnel control. The controllers are evaluated through modeling in the Simulink/Matlab environment. Simulation results demonstrate satisfactory performance, particularly with Gaussian Funnel boundaries. The comparison between the two controllers emphasizes the advantages of gain-scaled Funnel control over its classical counterpart.

title: A 8-16 Gb/s/pin Full-Duplex Voltage-Mode Transmitter with Pulse Width Modulation Based Equalization and Analog Echo Canceller
authors: Saurabh Saxena, Vinod Ganesan, Shubham Choudhary
publish: 2026 39th International Conference on VLSI Design &amp; 25th International Conference on Embedded Systems (VLSID)
abstract: We present an 8-16 Gb/s/pin full-duplex voltagemode transmitter with a multiplexed PWM-based equalizer and an analog echo canceller. A 2:1 MUX is inserted in a differential N-over-N voltage-mode output driver. PWM-based equalization compensates for channel loss with equalized eyeopening more than the residual echo at the output of the analog echo canceller. The latter taps the output driver at two different nodes and conditions the signals based on channel input impedance before combining them at the input of a transimpedance amplifier. The transmitter is designed in TSMC 65 nm CMOS process and simulated with post-layout RC parasitics. The TX achieves an energy efficiency of 6.05 pJ/bit while transmitting 0.5 V peak-to-peak output swing, compensating 7.8 dB channel loss, and achieving 48 mV<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">pk-pk</inf> residual output swing for near-end echo cancellation in simplex mode of operation. In full-duplex mode, the vertical and horizontal eye openings are 36 mV and 50 ps at 16 Gb/s/pin, increasing to 56 mV and 60 ps at 8 Gb/s/pin for the same channel loss at the Nyquist frequency.

title: Fault-Type Identification and Switching Strategy for MMC-Based DC De-Icing Lines Using Active Signal Injection
authors: Yao Zhong, Chuang Fu, Xiaodong Ren, Zhuxin Shi, Qi Yang, Lusong Zhang, Huarong Zeng
publish: 2025 4th Asia Power and Electrical Technology Conference (APET)
abstract: This paper proposes a fault detection and location scheme for a modular multilevel converter (MMC) based DC deicing system. The scheme actively injects low-frequency signals into the de-icing circuit and uses the voltage and current response to identify the fault type. In particular, a novel switching operation strategy is introduced to localize cross-line faults (inter-circuit short circuits) without the need for additional sensors. The method reliably distinguishes between pole-to-ground and interpole faults and accurately estimates fault distance along a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{1 0 0 ~ k m}$</tex> transmission line. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed strategy achieves high fault location accuracy over a range of fault resistances.

title: Integral Design of Active Mobile Health-Assist Walker Robot
authors: Peng Ni, Xiaodong Zhang, Tong Mu, Yulu Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Micro/Nano Sensors for AI, Healthcare, and Robotics (NSENS)
abstract: Accurate simulation of gait pattern is an critical evaluation criterion of a Health-Assist Walker Robot. We present the innovative active mobile health-assist walker robot, whose mechanical structure is comprising an exoskeleton and a mobile weight reduction platform, The exoskeleton simulates lower limb gait trajectories, while the mobile platform provides natural walking feedback relative to ground movement. Simulation tests confirm that the structural integrity of the robot components meets design specifications, and modal analysis verifies that the robot does not resonate under various use states. The standard gait data is fitted as a function by Fourier series combined with least square method, and a gait period is discretized into 59 instantaneous positions as P-T control signals. The PID linear interpolation position control method is used to control the robot. The hardware system is built by using PMAC position control card and mobile platform independent controller, and the robot upper computer control software is written by visual studio. Experimental results demonstrate excellent performance in both gait trajectory tracking and pace synchronization.

title: Evaluating a Virtual Assistant's Effectiveness in Enhancing in-Vehicle Safety: A Comparative Study
authors: Luqman Ali, Hamad AlJassmi, Ahmad Ghaleb Issa, Fahed Saghir, Omar Aldhaheri, Mohamad Razouk, Zayed Alhammadi, Fady Alnajjar
publish: 2025 Advances in Science and Engineering Technology International Conferences (ASET)
abstract: Driver distraction is a leading cause of road accidents and a critical human-machine interaction (HMI) challenge to ensure safety. This study investigates the potential of AI-powered Interactive Virtual Assistants (IVAs) to enhance driver-system interaction and responsiveness through multimodal interface design, compared to traditional safety communication methods. Under simulated driving conditions, the research evaluates interface effectiveness metrics including driver reaction times, compliance with safety instructions, and user satisfaction across three HMI configurations: a static visual interface, an audio system with Natural Voice Alerts, and an avatar-based assistant providing real-time guidance. Our comparative HMI evaluation reveals that the Natural Voice Alert system significantly enhances humanmachine communication, achieving the highest compliance rate (85.4 %) compared to avatar-based (63.7 %) and visual interfaces <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(61.9 \%)$</tex>. Statistical analysis <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\mathbf{n}=30)$</tex> demonstrates a significant negative correlation between warning frequency and compliance (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$r=-0.536, p<0.05$</tex>), supporting the cognitive load optimization hypothesis. Through systematic analysis of interaction modalities, Natural Voice Alerts improve compliance and reaction times while optimizing the distribution of cognitive workload between primary driving tasks and secondary system interactions, offering a promising direction to advance intelligent HMI design in safetycritical systems. This research contributes to a broader understanding of adaptive interface design principles for Industry 4.0 applications where seamless human-machine collaboration is essential for operational safety and efficiency.

title: Ensuring Data Integrity and Secured Storage in Cloud with mDFA-Based Encrypted Data Search and Keystamp Generation
authors: Asha Rani Borah, Ginni Nijhawan, K.Pushpa Rani, Dinesh Kumar Yadav, Rusul Najm, Mohammed Sahal Mohamed Salim Shaikh, Mahesh Raj Nagaraja
publish: 2025 Advances in Science and Engineering Technology International Conferences (ASET)
abstract: The scalability and flexibility of cloud computing data management are compromised because untrusted cloud storage servers raise privacy issues. The research establishes a secure searchable file encryption system which combines Minimized Deterministic Finite Automaton (mDFA) with KeyStamp Generation (KSGM) to promote data security. The system maintains privacy through salt-based hashing with a cryptographic access control system design. Data demonstrates that mDFA encryption duration outperforms current models because it takes 47 milliseconds to process a query which is 8% faster than the existing approach. The time needed to generate Stamp decreased by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$4-5 \%$</tex> according to test cases which demonstrated 62 ms. The developed keyword search system achieved <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$10-15 \%$</tex> better efficiency by processing more than 3600 search keywords within specific time frames. The reduction in data communication expenses reached 6070% because the encrypted text became shorter after decreasing from 62,360 bytes to 49,140 bytes. The proposed model proves resilient against Keyword Guessing Attacks (KGA) due to its improved efficiency in secure cloud storage operations. The implemented system demonstrates both performance scalability and operational effectiveness which enables its use in big cloud storage systems.

title: Identification of Serious Discharge State of Transformer Surface Discharge Defects Based on Chaos Theory
authors: Yi Liu, Xu Yang, Li Xiao, Hui Wang
publish: 2025 4th Asia Power and Electrical Technology Conference (APET)
abstract: EHV and UHV transformers have compact structure and low insulation margin. The field strength concentration area is likely to cause “rapidly developing” discharge faults under the high voltage. In this paper, the highfrequency PD impulse current signals under different discharge development stages were obtained by building a “rapidly developing” surface discharge experimental platform for transformer oil-paper insulation based on the “pin-plate” defect. Applying chaos theory, the maximum Lyapunov exponent and correlation exponent of high-frequency pulse sequences in partial discharges at different developmental stages were calculated. A comparative analysis of chaotic characteristic parameters between “rapidly developing” and “ordinary” surface discharges was conducted to determine whether a surface discharge defect exhibits “rapidly developing” behaviors. This enables assessment of whether a transformer's surface discharge defect is in severe discharge state.

title: Research on Optimizing SLAM Technology Based on Brainstorming Algorithm
authors: Zebiao Lin, Yuanzi He
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Science, and Information Processing (AICSIP)
abstract: To address the issues of particle weight degradation and diversity loss in particle filter-based Simultaneous Localization and Mapping (SLAM), a SLAM optimization technique based on the Brain Storm Optimization (BSO) algorithm is proposed in this study. Specifically, particle weights are treated as individual fitness values in the BSO algorithm, and particle filter resampling is optimized through clustering and mutation operations before being applied to the FastSLAM 2.0 framework. Comparative experiments were conducted in MATLAB with classical SLAM algorithms and genetic algorithm (GA)-optimized SLAM algorithms. The results indicate that the mean positioning error of the BSOoptimized FastSLAM 2.0 is 24.2% lower than that of the GAoptimized FastSLAM 2.0, with superior stability. This research enhances SLAM accuracy and provides an effective solution for robot localization and mapping in complex environments.

title: Intelligent Evolution of AWS Glue for Scalable Serverless ETL
authors: Prema Kumar Veerapaneni, Vinoth Punniyamoorthy, Akash Kumar Agarwal, Nitin Saksena, Mayilsamy Palanigounder, Durgaraman Maruthavanan, Ravi Kiran Kodali, Seema G. Aarella
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: As data volumes and processing demands surge across industries, organizations increasingly rely on serverless Extract, Transform, and Load (ETL) platforms to enable scalable, cost-efficient, and intelligent data integration. AWS Glue, Amazon’s serverless ETL service, has become a cornerstone for modern data engineering; however, evolving workloads now demand capabilities that extend beyond its current batch-centric design. This paper presents an analytical and forward-looking exploration of the intelligent evolution of AWS Glue toward supporting real-time data processing, multi-cloud and hybrid interoperability, machine learning-driven automation, and enhanced data governance. We examine architectural innovations such as predictive scaling, AutoML-based optimization, and cross-cloud metadata synchronization using open table formats like Apache Iceberg and Delta Lake. The study further analyzes the implications of data egress costs, security key federation, and compliance automation under global data privacy regulations. By integrating adaptive streaming, ML-powered orchestration, and fine-grained governance, the next generation of AWS Glue can evolve into a self-optimizing, multi-cloud-aware ETL ecosystem that delivers sub-second latency, intelligent resource allocation, and end-to-end compliance for data-driven enterprises.

title: AI-Agent-Based Autonomous Defense Against DoS/DDoS in AI Data Centers
authors: Rama Rao Katta, Venkateshwaran Dorai
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: Artificial intelligence (AI) data centers host large-scale inference and training workloads that generate high volumes of legitimate network and compute traffic[1],[2]. This operational intensity makes it challenging to distinguish between normal usage spikes and malicious Denial-of-Service (DoS) or Distributed DoS (DDoS) attacks[3],[4]. Traditional defense mechanisms such as static firewalls, signature-based detection, and fixed rate limiting are insufficient against adaptive or AI-generated attacks[5],[6].This article proposes an autonomous defense framework based on AI agents that continuously analyze real-time telemetry from ingress gateways, service meshes, and workload schedulers. Each AI agent employs anomaly detection, pattern classification, and reinforcement learning[7] to identify abusive behaviors while preserving high-throughput AI services. Upon detecting an attack, agents collaboratively enforce mitigation strategies such as dynamic throttling, micro-segmentation, and workload isolation, minimizing collateral impact on active compute tasks[8]. The proposed approach establishes a foundation for self-defending, autonomous[9] AI infrastructures that can withstand increasingly sophisticated DoS threats.This article is structured as follows. Introduction outlines the rapid growth of AI data centers and the escalating threat of sophisticated DoS and DDoS attacks, while emphasizing the limitations of conventional firewalls and intrusion detection systems. This motivates the need for autonomous, AI-driven security mechanisms capable of real-time decision-making. Related work section reviews both traditional defense mechanisms and recent machine learning-based detection strategies. Proposed framework details the multi-layered hybrid architecture of the AI agents, their data flow model, and potential deployment configurations in cloud and edge environments. The detection and mitigation pipeline is elaborated through the classification models employed and an adaptive response strategy optimized for varying threat intensities. Paper concludes by highlighting the advantages of the autonomous AI-driven approach, acknowledging current limitations, and proposing directions for future enhancement.

title: Temporal Data Encoding and On-Chip Training of an SNN for ECG Classification
authors: Saras Mani Mishra, Hanumant Singh Shekhawat, Gaurav Trivedi
publish: 2026 39th International Conference on VLSI Design &amp; 25th International Conference on Embedded Systems (VLSID)
abstract: This paper presents an on-chip training framework for a spiking neural network (SNN) targeting cardiac arrhythmia detection. Cardiac arrhythmia diagnosis is a major healthcare challenge and a significant application of artificial intelligence (AI) systems. SNNs, with their event-driven operation and high energy efficiency, are particularly advantageous for implementation in low-power biomedical systems. A modified step-forward temporal data encoding scheme transforms electrocardiogram (ECG) signals into spike-based representations. The proposed architecture incorporates spatial and temporal synaptic weight adaptation, where temporal dependencies are modeled using a triplet spike-timing-dependent plasticity (TSTDP) rule and spatial dependencies are addressed through a backpropagation (BP)-based update mechanism. Evaluated on the MIT-BIH arrhythmia database, the design achieves 89% accuracy. Implemented in TSMC 40 nm bulk CMOS technology, it occupies 0.1803 mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> and consumes <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.41 \mu \mathrm{W}$</tex> during inference and 1.5 mW during training at 500 kHz. The compact, ultra-low-power design enables real-time learning and classification for wearable healthcare monitoring.

title: A Hybrid VMD-GRU Model for Enhanced Photovoltaic Power Forecasting
authors: Ahmed Elottri, Ali Teta, Nadji Hadroug, Kouzou Abdellah, Khelifi Elmabrouk, Matilde Pietrafesa, Mario Versaci
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Sustainable Energy and Net-Zero Emissions (SENZE)
abstract: Accurate photovoltaic (PV) power forecasting is crucial to grid stability, energy market operations, and storage optimization. However, the inherently non-linear and variable nature of PV power generation poses significant challenges to traditional forecasting methods. In this paper, we propose a hybrid model that combines variational mode decomposition (VMD) and gated recurring unit (GRU) networks to address these challenges. VMD decomposes the PV power data into intrinsic mode functions (IMFs) to isolate key frequency components, while GRU networks are utilized to model temporal dependencies for each IMF. The proposed VMD-GRU model was evaluated in the short, medium and long-term forecast horizons using real-world photovoltaic power data. Experimental results demonstrated that the hybrid model significantly outperformed the standalone GRU and Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) models, achieving reductions in Root Mean Square Error (RMSE) and Mean Absolute Error (MAE) and a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R^{2}$</tex> value exceeding 99 % across all horizons. These results highlight the effectiveness of combining signal decomposition with advanced deep learning techniques to improve forecast accuracy. This study contributes to the development of robust and precise PV power forecasting models, which are essential for effective grid management and the integration of renewable energy into modern power systems.

title: An Explainable Hybrid Ensemble Learning Approach for Predicting Cardiovascular Risk Prediction
authors: Rowthu Neelima, Santosh Kumar Henge
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Advanced Computing and Emerging Technologies (ACET)
abstract: In terms of both mortality and morbidity, cardiovascular diseases (CVDs) rank high worldwide. In order to effectively prevent and treat diseases, early diagnosis and precise prediction are essential. However, there are some limitations to the actual execution of classical machine-learning (ML) models in healthcare when it comes to CVD prediction. These include data imbalance, low generalization across datasets, and an absence of interpretability. To overcome these shortcomings, this research presents a HXAI-ML model, which stands for hybrid-XAI-based machine-learning. With XAI tools such as SHAP, LIME, and PIA, the suggested framework integrates state-of-the-art data balancing methods. Healthcare providers are better able to understand decision-making processes thanks to this integration, which improves forecast accuracy and guarantees model transparency. The best results were obtained by merging TL and RO with the Extra-Trees-Classifier(ETC). On the Framingham dataset, the combined accuracy and precision were 99.24 %, and on the Cleveland dataset, they were 98.78 % and 98.82 %, correspondingly. In terms of accuracy and interpretability, these outcomes display that the model can beat the competition.

title: Experimental Study of Single-Array Active Time-Reversal Mirror Focusing Technique
authors: Yanmin Ren, Lihua Guo, Changying Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Science, and Information Processing (AICSIP)
abstract: This article investigates the time reversal focusing characteristics of a single array element from both theoretical and experimental. The experiment used a pointed broadband transceiver combined transducer to transmit a single frequency narrow pulse signal for measurement. The results obtained were compared with the theoretical calculation results in the time and frequency domains, respectively, proving good agreement. The results obtained were compared with the theoretical calculation results in the time and frequency domains, respectively, proving good agreement. After experimental verification, the single channel time reversal active acoustic detection technology has been applied to the detection process of buried targets, with good focusing effect, simple system structure, and strong engineering feasibility.

title: Emotion-Aware Learning Analytics: Transformer Based Sentiment Analysis on Online Discussions Forums
authors: Laila A. Abd-Ellatif, Al-Ayham M. Shamaki
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: Online learning has exploded in recent years, with discussion forums becoming crucial spaces for student interaction. These digital environments make it difficult for instructors to understand student emotions. This paper proposes a framework for analyzing sentiments in these forums to enhance learning experiences. The approach combines transformer-based models with statistical analysis to correlate emotions with engagement. Steps include data collection from various platforms, preprocessing, and multi-level analysis examining both sentiment polarity and specific emotions. The framework also investigates relationships between emotional states and engagement metrics like posting frequency and discussion depth. Drawing from classroom experience, this research provides practical insights for creating supportive online communities. The framework contributes to educational data mining by offering solutions that could improve teaching strategies and platform design. The implementation of this framework through the Student Feedback Analyzer system demonstrates its practical application in real educational settings.

title: Scaling Limits and Reliability Challenges of Nanoscale GaN HEMTs: A Path Toward Advanced Node Benchmarking for DC and RF Applications
authors: Shivansh Awasthi, Vikas Kumar, Ankur Gupta
publish: 2026 39th International Conference on VLSI Design &amp; 25th International Conference on Embedded Systems (VLSID)
abstract: In this work, we investigate the key challenges that arise in nanoscale GaN HEMTs with gate lengths down to 20 nm and source/drain access spacings as low as 10 nm. As GaN HEMTs scale toward advanced technology nodes for high-speed and high-frequency applications, understanding their reliability and physical limits becomes critical. Moving beyond conventional RF performance benchmarks, this work focusses on reliability limiting factors such as short-channel effects, trapinduced degradation, and RF performance degradation. Using numerical simulations, we assess device performance and transconductance degradation, under scaled geometries for DC and RF operation. The findings highlight key reliability considerations and scaling challenges that designers should account for when evaluating GaN HEMT miniaturization, offering guidance for balancing performance and long-term stability in future DC and RF applications.

title: Sustainable Protection of IoT and Energy Devices in Harsh Environments: Toward Net-Zero Systems
authors: Kamal Hussein, Alicia Triviño Cabrera, Pablo Otero
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Sustainable Energy and Net-Zero Emissions (SENZE)
abstract: IOT and energy devices are often situated in extreme environmental conditions, subject to variation in temperature, humidity, pollution particles and mechanical stress leading to the short life-time of components and increased maintenance cost limiting the sustainability/net zero goal. In this paper, a green-conscious protection system that is modular and composed of eco-friendly materials including robots open and close protective systems is considered. The case studies based on renewable microgrids and UAV monitoring demonstrate the efficacy of such methodology. The surviving probability at 12 month has been found alive yet at 0.78 for multi-filament canisters against to 0.52 for standard housing systems causing the higher reliability of the system. Enhanced performance report on the order of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3 5}-\mathbf{4 5} \boldsymbol{\%}$</tex> lifetime improvement and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\boldsymbol{\sim} \mathbf{2 2} \boldsymbol{\%}$</tex> emission reduction, in addition to more than 45 % protective duty cycle in UAV. The ability to withstand harsh environments and environmental performance is further enhanced with Harshness Index (HI) and Sustainability Index (SI). The model demonstrates sustainable security as an enabler goes beyond improved capability but combines with resilient smart city, renewable energy autonomy and Industrial IoT net-zero infrastructure.

title: Investigating Hydrodynamic Influences on Coastal Litter Distribution Using UAS Surveys
authors: Imen Chebbi, Mariachiara Donato, Stefania Lanza, Giovanni Randazzo, Carla Faraci, Claudio Iuppa
publish: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Metrology for Green Technologies, Renewable Energy and Ecological Sustainability (MetroGREENST)
abstract: Recent studies have demonstrated the feasibility of using Unmanned Aerial Systems (UAS) to monitor marine pollution. Litter on beaches, dunes, and riverbanks-as well as floating debris in coastal and river waters-can be effectively detected and mapped using aerial drone imagery. In this study, a manual screening of orthophotos enabled a detailed classification of marine litter categories in the North coast of Sicily (Italy), over three different periods. The results show that the second orthophoto revealed the presence of a mean litter density of 3.0 items per 100 m<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>, followed by a decrease in density, suggesting that precipitation events and wave dynamics, as well as human activities, play a significant role in the clean-up of the beach. These findings underscore the importance of developing standardized protocols for drone-based litter surveys to harmonize assessments of beaching and retention data.

title: An Effective Software Risk Prediction Management Analysis of Data Using Machine Learning and Data
authors: Jinxin Xu, Yue Wang, Ruisi Li, Ziyue Wang, Qian Zhao
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Science, and Information Processing (AICSIP)
abstract: For one to guarantee higher-quality software development processes, risk management is essential. Furthermore, risks are those that could negatively impact an organization's operations or a project's progress. The appropriate prioritisation of software project risks is a crucial factor in ascertaining the software project's performance features and eventual success. They can be used harmoniously with the same training samples and have good complement and compatibility. We carried out in-depth tests on four benchmark datasets to confirm the efficacy of our CIA approach in closedworld and open-world scenarios, with and without defence. We also present a sequential augmentation parameter optimisation technique that captures the interdependencies of the latest deep learning state-of-the-art WF attack models. To achieve precise software risk assessment, the enhanced crow search algorithm (ECSA) is used to modify the ANFIS settings. Solutions that very slightly alter the local optimum and stay inside it are extracted using the ECSA. ANFIS variable when utilising the ANFIS technique. An experimental validation with NASA 93 dataset and 93 software project values was performed. This method's output presents a clear image of the software risk elements that are essential to achieving project performance. The results of our experiments show that, when compared to other current methods, our integrative fuzzy techniques may perform more accurately and effectively in the evaluation of software project risks.

title: Radiation Effects Specific to State-of-the-Art Global Shutter CMOS Image Sensors
authors: A. Jouni, A. Vriet, L.-S. How, A.-S. Merlenghi, F. Berthet, S. Kowaltschek, M. Beaumel
abstract: Radiation-induced degradation arising from the operation principle of Global Shutter (GS) CMOS Image Sensors (CIS) are studied. The study is focused on voltage domain and charge domain pixels irradiated with γ-rays up to 50.2 krad and 62-MeV protons with Displacement Damage Doses (DDD) reaching 1492 TeV/g. Increased charge generation rate in voltage and charge domain pixels memory nodes (MN), and thus increased Parasitic Storage Node Leakage (PSNL), are highlighted. For the charge domain XGS16M detector, the PSNL degradation is explained by the increasing number of charges in the storage node before the row readout, due to radiation-induced interface traps and defect clusters within the silicon volume. HDPYX330-G charge domain pixels, embedding Capacitive Deep Trench Isolation (CDTI) technology and vertical storage of charges during the sensor readout, are less sensitive to Total Ionizing Dose (TID) effects but more vulnerable to DDD due to a deeper silicon volume in the MN area. For the CMV2000 voltage domain detector, asymmetries in the MN architecture using two collecting capacitors lead to a mean PSNL increase and PSNL non-uniformity (PSNLNU), meaning the increase of the row standard deviation with the readout. On the other hand, Parasitic Light Sensitivity (PLS) is not affected by γ-ray or proton irradiation for all detector types. The contribution of TID and DDD defects on the pixel MN are distinguished by studying the induced PSNL distributions. Voltage domain-based pixels seem to exhibit better performances with respect to ionizing radiation compared to charge domain detectors. Furthermore, PSNL distributions indicate that both TID and DDD contribute to the PSNL degradation of the two charge domain detectors, but also highlight the lower TID sensitivity of the HDPYX330-G sensor. Finally, activation energy measurements point out the diversity of defects contributing to the PSNL depending on the charge domain detector under study, and thus depending on the MN design. Independently on the type of signal storage before row-by-row readout, PSNL characterizations appear to be mandatory when studying GS CIS under irradiation.
