title: A Review and Performance Analysis of Metaheuristic Approaches for Optimizing Maximum Power Point Tracking in PV Systems Under Partial Shading Conditions
authors: Vidhya Viswambaran, Marcus Dela Cruz, Anu Malpan Lonappan, Swaroop Pillai
publish: 2025 Advances in Science and Engineering Technology International Conferences (ASET)
abstract: This research aims at optimizing the performance of a PV system under partial shading conditions (PSC) using Metaheuristic approaches. The main objectives include theoretical system design for measuring the ideal maximum power point (MPP), and Metaheuristic based MPPT algorithms are developed and global MPP tracking efficiency is evaluated. The system is modelled using MATLAB Simulink where 5 separate cases of irradiance level are applied to the PV system design, to investigate the tracking efficiency developed MPPT techniques. The performances of Conventional and Metaheuristic based algorithms for MPPT are then compared. The results show that Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO) can efficiently track the global MPP in PV (PSC) with an average of 99.94 percent tracking efficiency. Whereas conventional methods such as Incremental Conductance (InCond) is only able to track 1 out of the 5 cases, producing an average tracking efficiency of 86.75 percent.

title: An Improved YOLO Object Detection Method for Adhesive Quality of Cigarettes Carton
authors: Tianwen Guo, Yinhu Bai, Baobin Luo, Yaping Ding, Rui Wang, Fei Ru, Min Wei, Jun Liu, Jianguang Yi, Jing Ma, Wencan Li
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Science, and Information Processing (AICSIP)
abstract: Abnormalities in the adhesive tape used to seal cartons can cause products to crush, collide and fall during transportation and storage. In this paper, an improved YOLOv8 object detection method involving a Space-to-depth convolution (SPD-Conv) module and a SlimNeck structure is proposed for detecting the abnormalities of adhesive tapes. The module is developed for the downsampling process to increase the model's detection capability for small-scale objec. The structure is adopted to reduce the number of model parameters for a lightweight method with high detection accuracy. This method has been validated at the industrial level in Baoji Cigarette Factory, China Tobacco Shaanxi Industrial Co., Ltd. The average detection accuracy of the proposed method reached 97.8%. The false detection rate achieved 1.19% with a detection speed of 67 FPS. Finally, the results illustrate the high accuracy and reliability of the proposed improved YOLOv8 method.

title: A Study on Coupling and Coordination of Regional Digital Economy and High-Quality Economic Development Based on Integrated Learning: Joint Application of XGBoost and Random Forest
authors: Ning Ma, Wenyuan Xie, Xinxin Yang, Cheng Jia
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Science, and Information Processing (AICSIP)
abstract: The coupling and coordination of the digital economy with high-quality economic development is crucial for regional sustainable development. However, traditional methods are limited in uncovering their complex nonlinear relationships and dynamic characteristics. This study innovatively integrates the XGBoost and Random Forest algorithms to create a dual-model analysis framework. It uses XGBoost to predict the degree of coupling and coordination, and Random Forest to identify key influencing factors. An empirical analysis was conducted using multi-dimensional data from Tai'an City (2000-2022). Results show that XGBoost's predictive performance significantly surpasses traditional econometric models (with a 75 % reduction in mean squared error and a 50 % reduction in mean absolute error). The high association indicators identified by Random Forest are highly consistent with traditional methods <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(100 \%$</tex> overlap in the top five indicators), confirming the model's validity. The study found that the coupling and coordination between Tai'an's digital economy and high-quality economic development has advanced from basic to advanced coordination, with core driving factors focusing on digital infrastructure and innovation investment. This research offers high-precision analytical tools and a scientific basis for regional economic policy formulation. It also broadens the application of integrated learning in complex socio-economic systems research.

title: An Optimized Strategy for Soft-Switching and Current Stress in a Three-Level Full-Bridge DAB
authors: Zhicheng Li, Liansheng Huang, Xiaojiao Chen, Shiying He, Xiuqing Zhang, Sheng Dou
publish: 2025 4th Asia Power and Electrical Technology Conference (APET)
abstract: This paper focuses on a three-level dual-activebridge (3L-DAB) DC-DC converter under dual-phase-shift (DPS) control. The operational principles of the practical circuit are explained, and the converter's degrees of freedom under different working conditions are analyzed. Issues potentially arising in these conditions are discussed, leading to the selection of suitable operating modes. Across the full power range (from 0 to maximum power), the Karush-Kuhn-Tucker (KKT) algorithm and boundary analysis are employed to examine both interior and boundary cases based on the tightness of inequality constraints. A modulation strategy is proposed to achieve soft switching for all primary or secondary switches, with concurrent optimization of current stress.

title: Quantitative Analysis Method for Resonant Stability of Fractional-Frequency Offshore Wind Power System Based on Frequency-Domain Impedance Criterion
authors: Haitao Zhang, Ruxi Zhang, Chenting Zhao, Jizhi Han, Taiyu Wang, Qianwen Zhang, Yamin Sun, Xiao Wang
publish: 2025 4th Asia Power and Electrical Technology Conference (APET)
abstract: Fractional-frequency transmission technology is a crucial solution for long-distance offshore wind power transmission. As a typical interconnected system with AC networks of different frequencies, the power electronics characteristics of the fractional-frequency offshore wind power system are prominent, and it is susceptible to broadband oscillation caused by interactions among the power-frequency system, fractional-frequency system, and their interconnections. This poses severe challenges to system stability analysis. Existing research lacks an in-depth analysis of this issue. To tackle this problem, this paper first proposes a quantitative method for resonance stability analysis of fractional-frequency offshore wind power system based on frequency-domain impedance criterion, integrating impedance analysis with matrix Schur transformation. Next, based on this stability analysis method, a quantitative stability margin index is proposed, and we quantitatively evaluate the impact of different control parameters on the system stability level. Finally, the effectiveness of the proposed method is validated via Matlab/Simulink simulations.

title: A Robust Deep Learning Approach to Tomato Plant Disease Classification Using Resnet-50
authors: Bhagya Laxmi. B, Rupesh Kumar Mishra
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Advanced Computing and Emerging Technologies (ACET)
abstract: Technological advances have contributed significantly to the control of plant diseases. A way to solve the problem in identifying plant diseases is to use deep learning for digital image processing. Global nutrition security and sustainable agriculture depend on the quick and accurate finding plant diseases. The use of CNN architectures for the Resnet 50 is investigated in this work to deep learning, particularly classify leaf diseases in tomato plants. Data record of 150 images divided into 10 categories: healthy, Bacterial spot, Early blight, Late blight, Leaf Mold, Septoria leaf spot, Target Spot, Tomato mosaic virus, Yellow Leaf Curl Virus, and Two-spotted spider mite was used, with data split into training (80%), validation (10%), and test (10%). Batch size 32 was evaluated to optimize training, with validation accuracy of 97.62%, training accuracy of 96.02%, and batch size of 32. In the classification of tomato leaf diseases, ResNet-50 derived something important from the basic CNN model. ResNet50 shown gains in accuracy, recall, and F 1 values, increasing accuracy of plant disease detection and classification. Results confirm effectiveness of ResNet-50 in improving diagnostic accuracy and highlighting its potential in real-world agricultural applications. Future work could examine ensemble models, expanded data and broader disease rates to further improve diagnostic systems and support sustainable agriculture efforts.

title: Development and Optimization of a Miniaturizable System for ATP Detection in Water Samples
authors: Giuseppe E. Capuano, Giuseppe A. Screpis, Domenico Corso, Fabio Matera, Roberta Farina, Serena C. R. Reina, Maria Anna Coniglio, Sebania Libertino
publish: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Metrology for Green Technologies, Renewable Energy and Ecological Sustainability (MetroGREENST)
abstract: This work presents the design and optimization of a miniaturizable, high-sensitivity system for real-time detection of adenosine triphosphate (ATP)-the primary energy carrier in biological systems-in water samples. Detection was based on the chemiluminescent ATP-luciferin driven reaction, monitored using a silicon photomultiplier (SiPM). Three different set-up configurations were investigated, focusing on the effects of reaction chamber size and total solution volume. The system achieved a minimum detectable ATP concentration of 0.05 nM, with a limit of detection (LoD) of 0.03 nM and a sensitivity of 11.9 A/M.

title: Enhancing Performance of Wide Area Network (WAN) based on Hybrid FSO Link by using Software Defined Networking (SDN) Approach
authors: Sachin Kumar, Ashutosh Kumar Singh
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Advanced Computing and Emerging Technologies (ACET)
abstract: In the present era, we have adopted 5G and the upcoming 6G technology to deliver exceptionally high bandwidth and broadband capabilities for operating various bandwidth-intensive applications like the Internet of Things (IoT) and those centred around Artificial Intelligence (AI), Machine Learning (ML), and Robotics. Global Fifth Generation <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(5 \mathrm{G})$</tex> connectivity networks are not fully ready to handle such a vast number of IoT devices. To mitigate the existing WAN network challenges such as bandwidth restrictions, latency and throughput efficiency, SDN based technology are being deployed. In this study, an SDN-driven load balancing method has been created to improve the efficiency of FSO-based WAN networks through the application of the SDN load balancing strategy for providing an efficient backbone network which improve the TCP throughput by 80% similarly for UDP traffic throughput enhanced by 90%. The backbone WAN network will be deployed for 5G and 6G technology.

title: Enhancing Mental Stress Classification Through EEG Channel Selection*
authors: Yara Badr, Ghufran Alsalloum, Usman Tariq
publish: 2025 Advances in Science and Engineering Technology International Conferences (ASET)
abstract: This study proposes a novel EEG channel selection framework for mental stress classification that integrates Gaussian Mixture Models (GMM) with Wasserstein distance analysis. Unlike conventional methods that rely on all channels, our approach first models Power Spectral Density (PSD) distributions with GMM to compute each channel's log-likelihood, then applies the Wasserstein distance between stress and control conditions to quantify inter-condition dissimilarity. Thresholding and clustering strategies are used on the Wasserstein distance to identify the most significant channels. Across alpha, beta, delta, and theta bands, the selected channels were benchmarked against using all 64 channels. Median Wasserstein thresholding achieved 79.3% accuracy in the Alpha band, with a 70 % reduction in computation time. In the Beta band, it preserved comparable accuracy while reducing time by 90 %. Notably, in the Delta band, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$k$</tex>-means clustering improved accuracy from 84.6% to 87.3 % while cutting computation time by 85 %. Further investigation into the relationship between selected channels and brain locations is recommended.

title: Distributed Optimization Scheduling Strategy for Wind-Solar-Building Multi-Agent Energy System Considering Electricity Price Uncertainty
authors: Zhiwen Zhao, Jingzheng Li, Yilu Wang, Bolin Zhao, Yusheng Fang, Tao Jin
publish: 2025 4th Asia Power and Electrical Technology Conference (APET)
abstract: With the global energy transition accelerating, renewable energy technologies such as wind and solar have made significant progress. This paper proposes a distributed optimization scheduling strategy for a wind-solar-building multiagent energy system under electricity price uncertainty, aiming to address multi-agent benefit allocation and cooperative scheduling. First, a system framework including wind farms, PV plants, and buildings is constructed, and robust optimization is used to characterize price uncertainty. Then, based on Nash bargaining theory, a cooperative operation model is developed and decomposed into coalition benefit maximization and profit allocation subproblems. For profit allocation, an asymmetric Nash bargaining approach ensures fair distribution among agents. Finally, the alternating direction method of multipliers (ADMM) is applied to solve the subproblems while preserving data privacy. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed strategy enhances renewable energy utilization, effectively coordinates energy supply-demand, and maximizes agents' profits under price fluctuations.

title: A Method for Extracting New Health Feature of Lithium-Ion Batteries in Constant Voltage Charging Curve for SOH Estimation
authors: Junqi Bai, Ke Xu, Laiqiang Kong, Sidun Fang
publish: 2025 4th Asia Power and Electrical Technology Conference (APET)
abstract: The State of Health (SOH) of lithium-ion batteries is crucial for battery management and the avoidance of safety accidents. Currently, the extraction of health features (HF) is mainly focused on the constant current charge and discharge curves. Due to the fact that batteries rarely fully discharge during actual use, the data extraction during the constant current stage is incomplete, which limits their application. On the contrary, the constant voltage charging curve data is easily preserved in its entirety and is not easily affected. Therefore, it is of great practical value to extract health features only from the constant voltage charging curve. This paper first extracts the data of constant voltage stage, and then takes the charge change in a specific period of time in the constant voltage charging stage as the health feature as <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\Delta Q$</tex>. Among them, the particle swarm optimization (PSO) algorithm is used to determine a specific time period, and the Pearson correlation coefficient is used as the fitness value. Finally, the SOH is predicted by using LSTM network. In addition to comparing with GPR and BP algorithm, the proposed method is also verified by data sets collected from different batteries under different conditions. The results show that the method has high accuracy and generalization, and the new HF proposed have high adaptability.

title: Characterization of Bentazone in Potable Water by Combined UV-Visible Spectroscopy
authors: Jiarui Jin, Xiaorong Sun, Gang Yu, Heng Wang, Shanzhe Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Science, and Information Processing (AICSIP)
abstract: Water safety plays an important role in our daily life. In this work, UV-visible spectroscopy was used to detect pesticide residue in potable water. The UV-visible spectroscopy of different concentrations of bentazone was collected to establish a qualitative discriminative model, which combined with the Probabilistic neural network (PNN) and Support vector machine (SVM) algorithms. This model was employed to characterize the bentazone content in potable water, with a recognition accuracy of up to 94.44%. Competitive adaptive reweighted sampling (CARS) was selected as the best feature extraction algorithm using two preprocessing methods and four feature extraction algorithms. Furthermore, the quantitative prediction model of bentazone content was set by Echo state network (ESN) and the classical linear algorithm Partial least squares regression (PLSR). Experimental results suggested that the ESN prediction model performed better than PLSR prediction model. The <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R_{p}^{2}$</tex> of ESN prediction model reached 0.9937, and the RMSEP was 0.0987 for the validation set. In addition, the lowest detection limit of ESN prediction model achieved 0.03 mg/L for bentazone content in potable water. With its advantage of rapidness, accurate and nondestructive, UV-visible spectroscopy may serve as a powerful tool to ddetect the pesticide residues of potable water.

title: Electricity Demand Forecasting: A Review of Statistical, Machine Learning, Deep Learning and Hybrid Approaches
authors: Shivam Sharma, Anamika Goel, Vedang Tiwari, Shiv Pratap Singh Waghel
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Advanced Computing and Emerging Technologies (ACET)
abstract: Electricity demand forecasting is highly important for the planning, operation, and stability of electricity grid and power systems. The growing complexity of load patterns—driven by renewable integration, urbanization, and climate variability—has led shift from the conventional methodologies for the demand prediction such as the statistical and probabilistic approaches towards the more advanced machine learning (ML) and deep learning (DL) approaches. This review purpose focuses on the insights from recent literature covering statistical, ML/DL, and hybrid forecasting models, with particular attention to accuracy, adaptability, practical applications and their limitations. The comparative findings highlight that while statistical methods remain effective for linear and short-term trends, ML and hybrid models provide superior accuracy in large & complex, nonlinear, high-dimensional, and seasonal data and context. Challenges such as data quality, interpretability, explainability, reasonability and computational costs are discussed, alongside future opportunities for integrating AI with smart grids and renewable-centric systems.

title: RoboCache: A Distributed Key–Value Store for Petabyte–Scale Multimodal Robot Learning Datasets
authors: Tejas Pravinbhai Patel, Sandeep Shivam, Amit Kumar Padhy, Bharadwaj Vulugunda, Chaitanya Kulkarni, Chandrashekhar Medicherla
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: RoboCache is a high-performance, fault-tolerant distributed key-value store explicitly designed to store, version, and serve petabyte-scale multimodal robot learning datasets, including images, point clouds, proprioceptive signals, actions, and language annotations. Existing cloud object stores and research datasets such as RoboNet and RT-X exhibit high latency, weak random-access performance, and poor versioning semantics, which bottleneck the training of robot foundation models. RoboCache introduces (1) multimodal-aware partitioning that co-locates temporally and semantically related robot traces, (2) a hierarchical tiered-engine enabling zero-copy data movement across NVMe, GPU memory, and remote nodes, (3) built-in dataset versioning and time-travel queries using snapshot-based copy-on-write, and (4) a robot-native query API supporting filtering by embodiment, timestamp, task domain, or natural-language annotations. Deployments on a 128-node cluster sustain 1.8 million random reads/sec and reduce data-loading time by 42× compared to S3+SQLite baselines and 12× compared to state-of-the-art multimodal dataset systems. RoboCache is open-source and currently powers multiple large-scale robot foundation model training pipelines.

title: Agentic AI Solutions: The Future for Intelligent and Secure Healthcare
authors: Fatima Qiyam, Assal Alqudah, Shadi AlZu'bi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Communication, Networking and Services (ICCNS)
abstract: Agentic Artificial Intelligence (Agentic-AI) represents a new frontier in the design of intelligent systems capable of autonomous, adaptive, and secure decision-making in dynamic healthcare environments. With the healthcare industry facing unprecedented challenges-from managing chronic diseases to ensuring rapid emergency response-there is a pressing need for scalable, accurate, and real-time solutions. This paper introduces a novel Agentic AI framework that integrates real-time anomaly detection, predictive diagnostics, and secure clinical decisionmaking to improve patient outcomes. We validated the system across three high-impact clinical scenarios. For early disease detection, the model achieved a 92.3% prediction accuracy using historical patient records. In real-time patient monitoring, the agentic module reached a 98.7% anomaly detection rate with a 2.5-second alert response time, proving critical for emergency interventions. Finally, the diagnostic support system demonstrated a 96.5% confidence score in detecting pulmonary anomalies, reducing misdiagnoses by 22% compared to human and existing AI baselines. The dataset was curated from reputable sources including MIMIC-III for electronic health records, PAMAP2 for wearable sensor data, and TCIA for medical imaging. This work bridges the gap between theoretical AI innovation and real-world deployment by introducing a clinically relevant, autonomous AI solution. The proposed system sets a new benchmark for intelligent and secure healthcare by providing timely, accurate, and explainable outcomes, paving the way for next-generation clinical decision support systems.

title: Capsule Network-Based Brain Cancer Detection and Survival Prediction Using MRI Scans
authors: Zuha Khan, H Shaheen
publish: 2025 Advances in Science and Engineering Technology International Conferences (ASET)
abstract: This paper presents a deep learning-based diagnostic system for early brain cancer detection using Capsule Networks (CapsNet). MRI brain scans are classified into four categories: glioma, meningioma, pituitary, and healthy brain MRI images. Developed using TensorFlow and deployed via Streamlit, the model offers real-time predictions, survival probability estimates, and basic medical recommendations. CapsNet was selected due to its ability to preserve spatial hierarchies, outperforming traditional CNNs in recognizing cancer structures under rotation and scale changes. A synthetic clinical dataset was created and linked to MRI data for survival prediction. The model achieved 99% training accuracy and 93.84% validation accuracy, with strong precision and recall metrics. Compared to CNNs, CapsNet demonstrated superior generalization and interpretability in small-sample settings. The platform aims to support clinicians with automated, explainable, and efficient diagnostic assistance.

title: Blockchain based Privacy Solutions Brokered with AI for Data Compliance
authors: Nachiappan Chockalingam, Rajesh Purushothaman, Jeevan Shanbhag, Arun Kumar Elengovan, Nitin Saksena, Akash Kumar Agarwal, Darshan Mohan Bidkar, Deepak Jayabalan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: This paper introduces an AI based Privacy Broker (AIBPB), a unified framework that combines blockchain, zero knowledge proofs, attribute based encryption, and differential privacy to enable verifiable and compliant data sharing in regulated environments. The system automatically interprets high level policies such as GDPR, HIPAA, and CCPA, and synthesizes optimized proof strategies through a multi objective cost privacy model. The architecture blends off chain cryptographic computation with on chain verification to balance privacy and transparency. Simulation based experiments demonstrate a 3.2 times improvement in proof generation latency, a 7.8 times reduction in information disclosure, and regulatory satisfaction rates exceeding 95% compared to baseline approaches. The results show that AI driven proof orchestration can significantly enhance scalability, compliance automation, and privacy protection in blockchain based systems.

title: Design of a Proton Exchange Membrane (PEM) Fuel Cell with Serpentine Flow Field to Evaluate the System Performance
authors: Ahana Fatima Alex, R Vinoth, Ravishankar Dudhe, Hassan Abdulmouti
publish: 2025 Advances in Science and Engineering Technology International Conferences (ASET)
abstract: In recent years, the need for clean energy has not only grown exponentially but has also become a necessity. Clean energy has made a great shift to renewable energies and low-emission energy systems from the conventional dependence on fossil fuels, that has led to increasing levels of pollution and in short long-term damage to the ecosystem. Current trends in clean energy are the use of fuel cells; one such is the Proton Exchange Membrane Fuel Cell (PEMFC) which is one of the most effective fuel cells that converts chemical energy into electricity, heat and water with zero harmful emissions. Hence PEMFC is of great research interest. One major area of interest in the functioning of PEMFC is the design of its flow field, which controls the motion of gases through the cells. Improving the alignments of the channel expedites electrochemical reactions within the catalytic layer, helping to prevent flooding on the cathode side. In this work, a high temperature proton exchange membrane fuel cell is designed with a serpentine flow field. The computational fluid dynamic (CFD) method is put in to optimize the construction of the serpentine geometry of the flow channels. The results showed that the curved/ “crimped” flow channel is overall preferable than the straight flow field in stimulating transport of reactant gases, elimination of liquid-water gathered in the micro-porous layers and avoiding concentration due to heat stresses in the exchange membrane. The gas stream at cell voltages of 0.7 V and 0.5 V were determined and were found to show a decreased oxygen level towards the outlet in both cases. The current density was found to be higher at 0.5 V which is <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$26.4 \%\left(1.2 \mathrm{A} / \text{cm}^{2}\right)$</tex> higher than that of conventional flow channel. Hence the PEM fuel cell with serpentine flow field can be suggested over traditional models for better performance which is economically reliable too. Therefore, future investigations at higher temperatures for sustainable applications should be done exclusively with the inclusion of experimental trials.

title: Optimized Operation of Integrated Energy Systems Based on TCN-LSTM Prediction and Multi-Agent Cooperative Games
authors: Yilu Wang, Xianyang Cui, Zhiwen Zhao, Bolin Zhao, Yushen Fang, Tao Jin
publish: 2025 4th Asia Power and Electrical Technology Conference (APET)
abstract: To enhance load forecasting accuracy, operational efficiency, and collaborative fairness among multiple agents in Integrated Energy Systems (IES) under the “dual carbon” goals, this paper proposes an optimized operational framework based on Temporal Convolutional Network-Long Short-Term Memory (TCN-LSTM) forecasting and multi-agent cooperative game theory. First, a hybrid TCN-LSTM model is developed to conduct day-ahead short-term forecasting of multi-energy loads (electricity, heat, and gas) and renewable energy output. Second, a cooperative game-based optimization model is established to realize multi-energy coupling scheduling with the objective of minimizing the total system cost. Furthermore, the Shapley value method is employed to allocate benefits according to marginal contributions, thereby ensuring fairness and sustainable cooperation among all participating agents. Case study results demonstrate that the proposed approach significantly improves forecasting accuracy and reduces operational costs, while also achieving equitable benefit allocation, thus providing strong support for the intelligent operation of IES.

title: Multiclass Intrusion Detection System for IoT Networks Using Gradient Boosted Trees
authors: Wedyan Alsakran, Lamya Albraheem
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: Network anomalies, defined as deviations from normal traffic behavior, represent a significant challenge to the security of Internet of Things (IoT) networks. While existing studies have reported high detection performance for binary anomaly classification, extending these approaches to multiclass attack classification remains challenging due to increased class complexity and data imbalance. To address this limitation, this work uses the Gradient Boosted Trees (GBT) classifier with the UNSW-NB15 dataset. GBT is selected for its effectiveness in handling imbalanced data distributions, which are prevalent in network traffic datasets and often degrade multiclass classification performance. Experimental results indicate that the proposed approach outperforms existing multiclass classification methods. The model achieves a 91% accuracy, 91% recall, 0.90 F1 score and average precision 93% across all attack classes. These findings demonstrate that the proposed framework effectively mitigates the performance degradation commonly observed in multiclass anomaly detection tasks. The results suggest that the proposed method provides a strong benchmark for multiclass anomaly detection in IoT networks.

title: Overcoming the Cost Barrier in Cloud Machine Learning
authors: Anshika Sharma, Swasti Rani Maity, Purnima Gupta, Anshu Kumar Dwivedi
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Advanced Computing and Emerging Technologies (ACET)
abstract: With larger and more complex machine learning deployments, cloud platforms are becoming the ecosystem of choice in artificial intelligence innovation because of their scalability, flexibility, and managed services. However, this comes at a high financial cost especially in the training of large models and real time inference. This study investigates the ways of developing and deploying cost effective machine learning procedures within the cloud by optimizing the ratio of the computation and economic viability. The multi-level optimization strategy that we propose combines modellevel tools, such as transfer learning, quantization, pruning, and knowledge distillation, with system-level methods such as as autoscaling, resource tiering, spot instances, and serverless inference. A comparison of AWS, Google Cloud and Azure reveals that they differ in terms of pricing, scaling and their architectures of service. The project also implies the intelligent orchestration module which adjusts the deployment settings in real-time based on the real-time workloads and consumption. We have tested our methodology in a large variety of NLP and computer vision tasks and found it to be significantly less costly than available cloud services in terms of performance and latency. The article provides professionals and scholars with viable insights into how to build successful and scalable AI systems through cloud economics and machine learning architecture.

title: Critical Analysis of Custom AI agents Vs. Generic agents for Integration with Existing Systems and Improvement of Data Privacy and Security to deliver efficiency in terms of Cost and Complexity of Use Cases
authors: Krishna D Jadhav, Rajesh Nelwadkar
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: Whenever a digital disruption takes place within an organisation, there are several changes that are adopted which tend to satisfy the structural and functional units associated with the transformation. Similarly, while implementing AI agents in business processes, it is necessary to specify the type of change that the organisation intends to introduce through the help of this technological advancement. Due to the fact that AI agents have a wide range of applications to enhance business operations, this study explores its use cases in terms of privacy and security. This is because, in a cloud based environment, several information exchange operations take place which are vulnerable to cyber-attacks. Prevention of such attacks requires advanced mechanisms with the help of which identification of the vulnerability would be done along with a solution to its impact on confidentiality and privacy. In such a context, Generic and Custom AI agents are observed to play a major role in enhancing the security and privacy of data with respect to a wide range of business cases.

title: Comparative Analysis and Failure Diagnosis of 220 KV Cable Termination Stress Cones
authors: Chenhui Zhang, Jianan Yan, Panqing Chen, Danyang Zhang, Han Wu, Yuqiang Hou, Liming Yang, Fanyi Cai
publish: 2025 4th Asia Power and Electrical Technology Conference (APET)
abstract: A comparative investigation was conducted on two 220 kV cable terminations with different stress cone materials one using ethylene-propylene-diene monomer (EPDM) rubber and the other using silicone rubber. The study evaluated their electrical performance under power-frequency AC voltage in both air and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{SF}_{6}$</tex> gas environments. Key performance indicators included corona inception voltage, brush discharge (partial discharge) inception voltage, and flashover voltage. The EPDMbased stress cone and the silicone-based stress cone were tested to identify any design deficiencies and to verify insulation performance. Results show that in ambient air, the silicone rubber stress cone achieved a higher flashover voltage with no visible corona or brush discharge, outperforming the EPDM cone. In pressurized <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{SF}_{6}$</tex>, both designs withstood voltages up to nearly four times the phase-to-ground operating voltage without significant discharges. A failure analysis on a field-failed EPDM termination is also presented, revealing that installation-induced air gaps and insulation degradation were primary causes of breakdown. The findings confirm that while both stress cone designs meet industry standards, the silicone rubber design exhibits a more uniform field distribution and better discharge performance in air. Recommendations are given for improved installation practices, routine maintenance, and design verification to ensure reliable operation of high-voltage cable terminations.

title: Heart Disease Detection and Classification Using a Tuned Soft-Voting Ensemble Approach
authors: Priyanshi Agrawal, Akshara Sharma, Priyanshi Rajawat, Yasha Istwal
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Advanced Computing and Emerging Technologies (ACET)
abstract: Across the globe, cardiovascular conditions persist as a dominant factor responsible for a high number of deaths, emphasizing the importance of early and accurate diagnosis. This study proposes a ML architecture for CVD detection using a tuned soft-voting ensemble that integrates three highperforming models-XGBoost, Random Forest, and Extra Trees. The dataset, derived from the UCI Heart Disease repo, was preprocessed through feature encoding, normalization, and noise removal to ensure input quality. After hyperparameter tuning via GridSearchCV, the proposed ensemble achieved 95 % accuracy, 94.5 % precision, 95.2 % recall, and an ROCAUC of 0.98, outperforming individual base models. Feature importance analysis identified clinically relevant factors including visible ST-segment decline, peak heart rate performance, and lipid levels act as key signals. These findings underline the ensemble's reliability and interpretability, suggesting its potentially adapting the system so it can function within routine medical decision-aid technologies for early CVD detection.

title: Research on Operating Characteristics of a Novel High-Power Rectifier with Source Filtering and Dynamic Reactive Power Compensation Capabilities
authors: Yao Zhong, Chuang Fu, Changhao Yu, Kui Xu, Xianyin Mao, Kang Li, Minghao Wu, Huarong Zeng
publish: 2025 4th Asia Power and Electrical Technology Conference (APET)
abstract: The available high-power rectifier suffer from large energy consumption, high harmonic, low efficiency and power factor. Sometimes it is necessary to absorb a large amount of reactive power from the power grid. This paper proposes a novel high-power rectifier with both active filtering and dynamic reactive power compensation functions. Firstly, the topology and the closed/open states of its switches under different working modes are introduced. Then, a detailed analysis and design of its control strategies are carried out. Finally, the simulation analysis shows that the rectifier can compensate for the required reactive powers of the ac three-phase system dynamically. At the same time it can reduce the harmonic components which are injected into the distribution grid through the rectifier while achieving adjustable DC output voltage.

title: An Optimized and Explainable Hybrid Model for Soil Degradation Detection
authors: Yash Pratap Singh, Mansi Sharma, Anshika Chaudhary, Abha Kaushik
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Advanced Computing and Emerging Technologies (ACET)
abstract: Soil degradation nowadays is one of the most serious problems of this era, not only for the soil itself but also for humans, crops, animals, and the whole environmental balance around. Farmers are the most affected ones because, as said earlier, many of them don't have proper knowledge about soil health. Repeating the same crops again and again makes the land weak, and it loses fertility over time. This practice creates a big risk for global food security and also harms nature in the long term. Different programs and schemes were started to reduce soil degradation, but many are too costly or not really practical for large farming. Again, it must be noted that soil provides almost 98.8% of all human calories, but its other functions, like keeping ecological balance or stopping land damage, do not get enough importance. Most predictive models of soil health are based on a single model type, and this approach usually suffers from overfitting and poor generalization. Also, transparency is missing, so it is not clear which parameter is really causing degradation. Some multi-model approaches are tried, but the accuracy is not so high. Because of this, a hybrid framework was proposed which combines Random Forest with XGBoost, tuned for both efficiency and accuracy. Predictions depend on NPK levels, temperature, moisture, and humidity. NPK shows fertility; temperature and moisture influence microbes, chemical processes, and crop growth, while humidity controls evaporation and water balance. The hybrid model gave strong results. For regression Test R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> was 0.9964, and for classification, Test Accuracy was 96.88% with an F1 score of 0.9686, higher than Random Forest. To make results more understandable, SHapley Additive Explanations (SHAP) was used, showing the contribution of each input. So overall, this method joins technical depth with practical use, offering a reliable tool for land management and a way to reduce soil degradation while supporting ecological stability.

title: Gen AI Based Placement Prepration Platform-Prepto
authors: Riya Bansal, Punit Mittal, Sneha Gupta, Riya Bhati, Srishti Goel, Swati Sharma
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Advanced Computing and Emerging Technologies (ACET)
abstract: The suggested platform is based on a number of important parts that help people get ready for jobs in a complete way. The Job Preparation Platform is at the center of it all. It combines several elements that help users get ready for work. With the Resume Builder, users may make organized and professional resumes that are specific to different jobs and fields. The ATS Score module works with this by employing an Applicant Tracking System algorithm to compare the resume to a specific job description. This gives users an idea of how well their CV meet the expectations of recruiters. The Interview Simulation tool gives users a fake interview that is just like a real one, including technical, coding, and aptitude questions that test their skills under time pressure. The Mock Tests module is another important part. It lets users practice English, reasoning, aptitude, and coding by topic, which helps them find and improve their weak points. A MongoDB backend database manages all user interactions and data. This makes sure that the database is always connected and that the data is safe.

title: MatchSVMM: Bidirectional Matching Algorithm Based on Statistical Voting and Matrix Modeling
authors: Yuanlin Yang, Dingxing Xie, Dequan Xu, Bin Shuai, Mu Zhang, Lei Peng
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Science, and Information Processing (AICSIP)
abstract: The two-way matching problem frequently occurs in fields such as educational resource allocation, enterprise recruitment, and public service scheduling. Its core lies in achieving a reasonable pairing between the elements of two sets. Although traditional algorithms such as the Hungarian algorithm can solve the weighted matching problem, they rely on a preset fixed weight matrix and are difficult to cope with the dynamically changing bidirectional selection scenarios, resulting in a lack of flexibility and adaptability in the matching results. We address dynamic two-way matching in education and recruitment with MatchSVMM, a transparent algorithm that fuses voting-based scoring with classical assignment. Agents express mutual preferences; the method aggregates these selections into an integer score matrix whose entries directly count co-endorsements. The Hungarian algorithm then solves this matrix for maximum total weight. This design yields three advantages: (1) interpretability-scores are literal preference counts, enabling FAC <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$>93 \%$</tex>; (2) adaptability-score matrices are rebuilt in <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$O(\text{mn})$</tex> time per update; and (3) efficiency-space and runtime remain practical for medium-scale instances. Experiments on synthetic and real datasets show MatchSVMM matches or surpasses Hungarian, RL, and GNN baselines in quality while offering 5-30 times faster and more faithful explanations.

title: JMFF: Joint Multi-Frames Fusion Based Broadband Carrier Signal Detection
authors: Hao Huang, Xinyuan Zhang, Jiao Wang, Yiting Gao, Ke Wang, Jianqing Li
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Science, and Information Processing (AICSIP)
abstract: Traditional spectrum detection methodologies (e.g., Spectrum Center Networks (SCN) and 1D-Fully Connected Networks (1D-FCN)) exhibit performance degradation in short-range communication scenarios employing dense multi-carrier modulations, particularly within ultrashort wavebands, attributable to their exclusive reliance on single-frame spectral amplitude characteristics. While deep learning-based time-frequency analysis utilizing image representations can partially alleviate this limitation, such approaches introduce three critical challenges: elevated computational complexity, frequency-domain segmentation artifacts, and edge information degradation from spectral truncation. To overcome these constraints, this paper presents a joint multi-frame fusion detection framework that directly integrates sequential power spectral sequences while preserving full frequency resolution. The algorithm operates through three sequential phases: (1) Multi-frame spatiotemporal fusion using 0.5 ms-resolution spectral sequences to capture inter-frame correlations across multicarrier elements; (2) IoU-based non-maximum suppression (NMS) to eliminate redundant spectral components while preserving frequency-domain coherence; (3) Sliding window temporal mapping for concurrent estimation of carrier frequencies and temporal durations. Experimental results confirm enhanced detection capabilities compared to conventional methods. The experimental results demonstrate that the proposed joint multi-frame fusion method achieves superior detection accuracy compared to conventional singleframe analysis approaches. By integrating temporal correlation features across consecutive frames through the FFSCN network architecture, our methodology effectively addresses the challenges posed by variable-duration signals in complex electromagnetic environments.

title: Low-Power UART Architecture with Integrated SECDED for Reliable Asynchronous Communication
authors: Vatsal Rathod, Jay Patel, Gunjan Thakur, Abhishek Ray
publish: 2026 39th International Conference on VLSI Design &amp; 25th International Conference on Embedded Systems (VLSID)
abstract: This paper presents a compact, low-power UART architecture integrated with Single Error Correction and Double Error Detection (SECDED) for reliable serial communication in noisy and resource-constrained environments. The design incorporates an extended Hamming encoder—decoder within an FSM-controlled pipelined framework, enabling real-time error correction without violating standard UART timing conventions. Operating at only six times the baud rate, the proposed approach reduces hardware complexity and power consumption compared to traditional oversampling schemes. Implemented in Verilog and synthesized on 150 nm ASIC and FPGA platforms, the architecture achieves error-free data transfer while consuming just 0.099 W, with dynamic power limited to 2% through optimized clock gating. FPGA resource utilization remains below 1%. Extensive verification—including unit tests, errorinjection, and timing analysis—confirms protocol compliance and robust SECDED operation. With minimal area overhead and enhanced reliability, the proposed UART is well suited for IoT, smart home, and other safety-critical applications requiring energy-efficient and fault-tolerant serial links.

title: Fast Day-Ahead Co-Allocation of Pumped-Storage Under Reserve Activation Risk
authors: Hanqi Tao
publish: 2025 4th Asia Power and Electrical Technology Conference (APET)
abstract: Pumped-storage hydropower (PSH) is regaining prominence as renewables rise and ancillary markets increasingly reward capacity and fast response. Utilities must split limited PSH capability between energy arbitrage and spinning/nonspinning reserves on a daily basis. However, heuristic splits often ignore activation risk and inventory targets, while bilevel market-clearing models are too heavy for routine use. We present a lightweight single-layer (M)ILP that co-allocates PSH capacity across energy and reserves. Prices and activation rates are exogenous; expected activation is captured by a closedform linear penalty, with simple power/energy limits and an optional one-binary per period for charge-discharge exclusivity. A representative <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 4 - 4 8}$</tex> hours case with realistic price traces and multiple reserve/activation scenarios is evaluated. Results show co-allocation consistently outperforms pure arbitrage in expected gross margin while meeting terminal-energy requirements. Sensitivity reveals higher reserve prices or lower refill prices shift capacity toward reserves; higher activation rates make nonspinning less attractive. The model is transparent, fast to solve, and practical for utility decision support.

title: Analysis of Dynamic Response Characteristics of EHV Transmission Tower Under Cumulative Effect of Mine Earthquake in Mining Area
authors: Hongbin Zai, Zhihua Jin, Limin Zhang, Dandan Wang
publish: 2025 4th Asia Power and Electrical Technology Conference (APET)
abstract: This study investigates the dynamic response and failure characteristics of 500 kV double-circuit mountain transmission tower systems subjected to multiple mining-induced seismic events. A three-dimensional model was developed using finite element analysis, where tower components were modeled as beam elements and conductors/ground wires simplified as overhead lines subjected only to tensile forces. A earthquake sequence was simulated to represent frequent high-frequency seismic events. Computational analysis revealed three distinct displacement evolution stages: initial linear relationship between displacement and earthquake frequency during loading phases; accelerated displacement growth with increasing seismic events causing subtle structural damage; and nonlinear exponential growth in later loading phases. Comparative analysis of conductor/ground wire crossarms and tower head displacements indicated concentrated damage at the tower base. The study demonstrates that prolonged mining-induced seismic activity significantly reduces transmission tower stability, with component damage resulting from cumulative plastic deformation.

title: Simulation, Analytical and Experimental Validation of Triboelectric Generator Response and its Application as Self-Powered Switch
authors: Bushara Fatma, Shimna Shafeek, Kamran Khan
publish: 2025 Advances in Science and Engineering Technology International Conferences (ASET)
abstract: Around the globe, there is an enormous requirement for sensors in the efficient functioning of the Internet of Things. With the development of smart systems in various fields, the use of sensors in a sustainable path is a necessity in Internet of Things (IoT). Around 43 billion devices are predicted to be connected to the IoT by 2025. Sensors enable these devices to function in IoT to collect data for making smarter decisions. Traditionally sensors are powered by conventional batteries. Usage of batteries in IoT and Industry applications invites tremendous usage of batteries as a power source for sensors to work in devices. Looking into the disadvantages of conventional batteries where replacement and disposal are a major concern, the idea of developing sensors as active or self-powered sensors came into existence. The idea of harvesting abundant ambient and waste mechanical energy by various technologies developed the idea of powering the sensors to work as sustainable active sensors. The emergence of Triboelectric nanogenerators (TENG) in harvesting ambient energies like motion, vibration etc. find advantages over Piezoelectric, Thermoelectric and Electromagnetic harvesting techniques. Triboelectricity works on the principle of electrostatic induction and triboelectric charge generation between two different electron affinity materials when subjected to contact-release periodic motion. An electric current will flow when it is connected as a closed circuit. Mechanical energy to electric energy conversion ability of TENG helped it to be used as a sensor in various low power applications. The current study shows electrostatic and circuit simulative study and evaluation in triboelectric generator (TEG), experimental analysis and application of a cost-effective polymer TEG device to work as an active sensor utilizing mechanical forces from various sources.

title: Agentic LLM-Assisted Rotation Policies for Multi-Cloud Credential Management: A Systems Case Study With. NET 8, Zero-Knowledge Vault, and Terraform
authors: Nihal Singh
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Advanced Computing and Emerging Technologies (ACET)
abstract: We study how NLP/LLM components can assist (not replace) credential-rotation policy in multi-cloud systems under strict guardrails. Our system integrates (i) a. NET 8 lifecycle manager, (ii) a zero-knowledge managed vault as the single source of truth for secrets, and (iii) Terraform for auditable projection into AWS/Azure. We formalize zero-downtime add-then-remove rotation, present an agentic policy-assist layer (secret discovery, policy synthesis, risk triage) constrained by OWASP/NIST safeguards, and report evidence: provisioning time drops from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim 12$</tex> to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim 2.5$</tex> minutes and fleet rotation (6 VMs) from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim 30$</tex> to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim 4$</tex> minutes while keeping secrets out of repos/state. Figures are illustrative, original schematics drawn by the author; scripts are provided for reproduction. This paper evaluates the approach through empirical experiments on AWS/Azure environments.

title: From GANs to Transformers: A Comprehensive Survey of Generative AI Architectures, Benchmarks and Applications
authors: Akash Chauhan, Dev Baloni, Pratik Kumar, Chandradeep Bhatt
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Advanced Computing and Emerging Technologies (ACET)
abstract: Generative Artificial Intelligence (GenAI) has advanced rapidly with the emergence of high-fidelity image, audio and text generators. Modern model families — Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs), Variational Autoencoders (VAEs), large Transformer-based models, and Diffusion Models — demonstrate complementary strengths and have enabled new multimodal systems. This survey consolidates architectural characteristics, benchmarked performance, dataset usage, and ethical considerations from recent literature (2021-2025). We present four comparative tables (architecture, datasets, quantitative benchmarks, ethical mitigation) and critically analyze trade-offs across fidelity, compute, and safety. The manuscript emphasizes reproducible comparison practices and outlines research directions for efficient, robust and aligned generative systems.

title: A BiLSTM with Attention Approach Using 2D Skeletons for Autism Activity Recognition
authors: Mohammad Kassir, Sandy Mahfouz, Farah Mourad-Chehade, Khouloud Samrouth
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: Autism is a complex neurodevelopmental disorder that significantly limits the social and cognitive skills of the child, and its symptoms are diverse and difficult to predict. They differ significantly across age ranges and also between males and females, with females tending to hide the autism symptoms, making it more difficult to detect. For this reason, early detection of the autism disorder is challenging through solely therapist or parent intervention, which suggests using automatic detection of the disorder. In this work, we propose a lightweight approach for activity recognition for autism using normalized 2D skeleton data. In contrast to existing methods, our approach reduces model complexity by using only one type of data while preserving high accuracy. It is also aware of data leakage issues and evaluates the model on the MMASD dataset, achieving an accuracy of 94.84%. This performance approaches that of multimodal models, but with fewer computational resources, making it more applicable to real-world scenarios.

title: Expense Radar: An AI-Driven Budget Management System
authors: Khalid Abdulla Al-Emadi, Ali Baydoun
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: This paper showcases Expense Radar, an AI-powered web-application built to enhance personal budget management. Traditional budgeting tools suffer from rigid rule-based systems, a steep learning curve, and rigid financial planning. Expense Radar helps to mitigate the gaps on these limitations by utilizing modern cutting-edge technologies such as Artificial Intelligence (AI), specifically through the integration of generative AI and Machine Learning (ML). It includes an AI-driven PDF parser that extracts the user's transactions and predicts their category, budget forecasting using the user's transaction history, and a virtual assistant chatbot powered by a large language model (LLM) with access to the user's transactions to deliver personalized natural language responses. Furthermore, the system provides secure user authentication to ensure that data is stored and accessed securely. It also provides zero-based budgeting tools that allow users to allocate their spending accordingly. The final product transforms traditional budgeting into a dynamic, easy-to-use financial assistant. Through intelligent data analysis and natural language interaction, Expense Radar empowers users to make informed decisions, gain financial insights, and adopt better budgeting habits tailored to their unique behaviors and financial goals.

title: AI-Based Disposable Email Detection System
authors: Gasser Sayed Abdelmohsen, E. Emary, Mahmoud A Attalla
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: Disposable email addresses (DEAs) pose significant security risks to the banking sector, enabling fraudulent activities through fake account creation and phishing campaigns. Traditional blacklist-based approaches struggle to combat evolving disposable email services. This paper presents a novel hybrid method combining domain validation with natural language processing (NLP) and machine learning techniques. Our approach employs multiple classifiers including Gaussian Naive Bayes, Multinomial Naive Bayes, Support Vector Classifier, Linear Discriminant Analysis, XGBoost, K-Neighbors, Random Forest, and Logistic Regression. Trained on a comprehensive dataset of over 20,000 disposable email metadata samples, our system demonstrates superior detection capabilities compared to conventional blacklist methods, providing financial institutions with an effective tool for combating disposable email-based fraud.

title: Enhancing Hematological Diagnostics: A Machine Learning Framework Featuring Michael’s Classifier for Beta Thalassemia
authors: Muhammad Zaman, Muhammad Danial, Sidra Mumtaz, Nikola Ivković, Vedran Uros, Adnan Akhunzada
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: Beta thalassemia is a genetic disorder that causes significant health and economic challenges, particularly in regions like the Mediterranean, the Middle East, and South Asia, due to the lifelong medical care required. Although advances in machine learning and deep learning have improved disease diagnosis, current models often struggle with accurately diagnosing beta thalassemia due to the disease’s complexity and the variability in the quality of medical data. Despite numerous studies applying artificial intelligence to genetic diseases, the use of critical data, such as gene sequences, medical records, and diagnoses, remains limited, which impedes the accuracy and generalizability of these models across different populations. This study aims to develop a deep learning model that integrates both genetic data and clinical images to provide a more precise and meaningful diagnosis of beta thalassemia, addressing the limitations of existing diagnostic methods. We employed a deep learning architecture that combines convolutional neural networks (CNN) for image analysis with long short-term memory (LSTM) networks for data processing. While more rigorous training (with a training accuracy of 0.75) raised concerns about overfitting due to the disparity in performance between the training (0.75), validation (1.00), and testing (1.00) stages, the proposed model holds promise for significantly enhancing diagnostic accuracy and efficiency. This is particularly beneficial in healthcare settings with limited resources, enabling more timely treatment and better overall patient outcomes.

title: IncepLite: An FPGA Based InceptionNet Accelerator for ECG Classification at Edge Devices
authors: Anju Yajjala, Muralidhar Pullakandam, Gopala Krishna Thota
publish: 2026 39th International Conference on VLSI Design &amp; 25th International Conference on Embedded Systems (VLSID)
abstract: The increasing demand for real-time, energy-efficient health monitoring has accelerated the development of deep learning models that can function on edge platforms. This paper presents IncepLite, a hardware-efficient convolutional neural network designed for classifying ECG signals on field-programmable gate arrays (FPGAs). The core innovation is a lightweight inception block made for one-dimensional temporal signals. This enables multi-scale feature extraction with minimal hardware cost. The model was trained and tested using a publicly available ECG dataset, achieving high classification accuracy. The design was developed using high-level synthesis and deployed as a reusable intellectual property (IP) core on a Xilinx ZCU104 FPGA. It achieved 45 % LUT and 35 % DSP usage at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{2 5 0 ~ M H z}$</tex>. A comparative analysis shows up to a 50 % reduction in effective LUT usage compared to previous FPGA-based ECG classifiers. A detailed performance analysis, including resource use and latency, shows that IncepLite is suitable for real-time biomedical signal processing on low-power embedded systems.

title: Panel Discussion: NFPA 70E Compliance and Human Factors
authors: David Durocher, Greg Drewiske, Emily Held
publish: 2025 IEEE IAS Pulp and Paper Industry Conference (PPIC)
abstract: Compliance with NFPA 70E can be difficult for many reasons, ranging from financial, logistical, leadership, or personnel. Changes are often met with hesitation. These complexities can be difficult to manage even in a mill with a strong safety culture. This panel discussion will highlight best practices to engage management and the workforce to work towards compliance with NFPA 70E, proper electrical PPE usage, and a better electrical safety program for your company.

title: Ku-band Low Noise Amplifier Configuration and Optimization through BiLSTM-based Deep Neural Networks
authors: Lida Kouhalvandi, Ladislau Matekovits
abstract: This work proposes an automated configuration and sizing framework for a low-noise amplifier (LNA) operating in the Ku-band. The methodology is based on the implementation of deep neural networks (DNNs), specifically a bidirectional long short-term memory (BiLSTM) classifier and a BiLSTM regression model, which are employed sequentially to first generate an appropriate LNA topology and subsequently predict the optimal design parameters of the selected configuration. To further refine the design toward multiple conflicting objectives, a multi-objective multiverse optimization (MOMVO) algorithm is integrated into the workflow to balance key performance metrics such as noise figure, and gain. The overall process is fully automated through the coordinated use of an electronic design automation (EDA) tool and a numerical analysis environment, wherein the circuit topology is synthesized and simulated in the EDA platform, while parameter optimization and decision-making are carried out in the numerical analyzer. This closed-loop framework enables rapid convergence to high-performance solutions with minimal human intervention. The practical effectiveness of the proposed methodology is validated by designing and optimizing an LNA suitable for a Ku-band small satellite receiver, achieving a noise figure below 1.5 dB while satisfying gain and stability constraints, thereby demonstrating the viability of deep learning–assisted automated radio frequency circuit design.

title: Prescribed-Time Fuzzy Adaptive Consensus Control for Photovoltaic Systems With Dead-Zone Input and Actuator Faults
authors: Zilong Tan, Gaochang Wu, Yang Liu
abstract: This article presents a novel prescribed-time fuzzy adaptive consensus control scheme for nonlinear photovoltaic (PV) energy systems with dead-zone and actuator faults. The PV panels are controlled to track the maximum power point so that the considered systems can efficiently operate under different complex conditions. To quickly regulate the voltage to a desired reference, a novel prescribed-time performance function is presented to guarantee systems states convergence within a specified time. Besides, dead zones and faults are serious nonlinearity constraints in practical grid-connected PV systems. Thus, a finite-time tracking controller and the adaptive laws are designed to compensate for the effect of unknown nonlinear constraints. By applying fuzzy logic systems, the difficulty of dealing with unknown nonlinear dynamics is overcome. The stability of the closed-loop system is rigorously proven using Lyapunov stability theory, demonstrating the uniformly ultimately boundedness of all signals. Finally, extensive simulation experiments validate the effectiveness and superiority of the proposed approach.

title: Research on AI-driven Knowledge Transfer and Multi-objective Collaborative Optimization in Low-altitude Equipment Design
authors: Heqi Zhao
abstract: To address the issues of task similarity differences, uneven resource allocation, and insufficient search efficiency faced by multi-objective collaborative optimization in low-altitude equipment design, this paper proposes an AI-driven knowledge transfer and multi-objective collaborative optimization framework (AMTO). Automatically extract the deep features of tasks through deep metric learning, quantify the transferability between tasks, and provide a basis for knowledge transfer. Design an adaptive knowledge transfer mechanism, dynamically adjust the transfer probability and content in combination with real-time optimization status, and introduce a deviation correction module between tasks to significantly reduce negative transfer interference. Develop a dynamic resource allocation strategy based on reinforcement learning, intelligently allocate computing resources according to task difficulty and optimization potential, and improve the overall efficiency of high-dimensional multi-objective scenarios. At the algorithmic level, a dynamic group search strategy is adopted, dividing the population into three subgroups: leaders, followers, and wanderers. Combined with the adaptive step size control of multi-objective success rate, the global exploration and local convergence capabilities are enhanced. Experiments show that AMTO outperforms mainstream multi-task optimization algorithms in terms of convergence speed, solution quality and stability when covering 9 types of basic tasks and 10 types of complex tasks with different degrees of intersection and similarity, verifying the effectiveness of AI-driven mechanisms in multi-objective collaborative optimization of low-altitude equipment.

title: Lightweight Dual-Agent Architecture for 2D Shadow Puppetry: Real-Time Edge Deployment and Evaluation
authors: Qi Guo, Yixuan Li, Yaoxi Kuang, Chuanbo Qin, Tao Chen, Junying Zeng
abstract: Shadow puppetry, as one of China’s ancient folk arts, carries unique cultural symbols and historical significance. This paper presents a 2D shadow puppet digital human interaction system based on a lightweight dual-agent architecture capable of creating personalized digital shadow-puppet images. The system uses MediaPipe to sample human body video keypoints for building a motion library and utilizes Pygame to render shadow puppet images from a custom material library. The proposed dual-agent architecture consists of a high-level agent for semantic understanding and a low-level agent for behavior-driven tasks. The system is implemented with Gemma3-4b as the base model and is benchmarked against the Phi-3 series models and GPT-4o. Furthermore, this paper systematically compares serial and parallel dual-agent collaboration modes, and introduces the mainstream multi-agent architecture SMoA as a comparative baseline. Experimental results show that on the Auto-SLURP dataset, the proposed serial dual-agent architecture reduces the intent error rate by 12%, and achieves 79% accuracy on the custom-built ShadowPlay Instruction Dataset, outperforming the compared architectures.The system achieves real-time rendering at 44.8 frames per second on the Jetson AGX Orin, with a frame drop rate below 0.89% and an average response time of 4.8 seconds, meeting deployment requirements for edge devices. This research provides a practical system-level approach for fully digital 2D shadow puppetry interaction under resource-constrained edge deployment, leveraging LLM-driven dual-agent coordination and a lightweight rendering pipeline. Code availability is provided in the paper.

title: Ku-Band High-Selectivity Waveguide RF Diplexer With Spectrally Adjacent Channels in an All-Inductive-Coupling-Iris Configuration
authors: Mohamed Malki, Jingbo Wang, Roberto Gómez-García
publish: IEEE Microwave and Wireless Technology Letters
abstract: A class of Ku-band quasi-elliptic-type RF diplexer implemented in rectangular waveguide technology is reported. As building transmission zero (TZ)-creation block, its frequency-contiguous channel filters use a cross-coupled cell comprising two standard cavities operating in their TE<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">101</sub> mode and one oversized cavity functioning in its TE<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">102</sub>/TE<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">201</sub> modes. This triple-cavity cell increases the order of the channel filter by four and creates two out-of-band TZs at the lower side of its passband and one stopband TZ at the upper side without requiring capacitive-coupling irises. The theoretical foundations in terms of coupling-routing-diagram (CRD) modeling of the proposed sharp-rejection RF diplexer are provided, including the integration of the phase-shifting elements of the T-junction at the input of both channel filters. Furthermore, for practical-validation purposes, a 14.82/16-GHz asymmetrical-order diplexer prototype—lower and upper channel filters of orders eight and six, respectively—is designed, manufactured, and experimentally characterized.
