title: Machine-Learning-Enhanced Iontronic Sensor Array for 3-D Tactile Perception_supp1-3671911.docx
authors: Taihong Wang
abstract: Tactile interfaces are crucial for enhancing human–machine interactions. However, achieving a precise perception of 3-D force under flexible contact conditions remains challenging due to strong signal coupling, computationally intensive data processing, and limited model interpretability. To overcome these limitations, we designed a flexible iontronic sensor array composed of four-capacitor integrated units to sense human hand grasping interactions. Furthermore, we developed a machine learning framework for 3-D force detection that leverages feature engineering. By integrating physics-inspired features into a lightweight random forest (RF) model through a selective process, our approach facilitates efficient and interpretable force decoupling. Experimental results demonstrate competitive accuracy in flexible-to-flexible interaction scenarios, with a mean squared error (mse) below 0.1 and an R2 value of 0.94. We integrated this approach with our sensor array to create a flexible multidimensional force-tactile sensing system capable of supporting real-time applications, such as virtual 3-D interactive operation. This work not only demonstrates the potential for precise force control in virtual reality (VR) and interactive devices but also provides new insights for developing AI-enhanced sensor technologies.

title: U-SLAM: Ultrasonic Simultaneous Localization and Mapping for Underwater Pipeline Defect Inspection
authors: Jun Zhang, Haozhe Qian, Yihao Wang, Chengyu Liang, Aiguo Song
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: This paper presents an Ultrasonic Simultaneous Localization and Mapping (U-SLAM) system for underwater pipeline inspection. Addressing the limitations of existing SLAM technologies in water environments, including visual degradation, laser absorption, and bulky sonar equipment, we developed the compact U-SLAM system based on a designed ultrasonic radar formed by high-frequency ultrasonic probe arrays. The system features a miniaturized design (diameter = 13 cm, weight = 465 g) that achieves 1.8° high-precision 360° scanning via a symmetric four-probe configuration, with a dedicated SLAM algorithm. The algorithm combines multi-sensor fusion-based coarse localization, linear-stage-controlled fine-motion positioning, and point-cloud loop-closure constraints, enabling millimeter-level positioning accuracy and highly real-time point cloud modeling. We derived and simulated the model of the ultrasonic transducer in static and flowing water, designed the ultrasonic radar and SLAM algorithm, and analyzed the SLAM error. Finally, we developed a radar prototype and performed experiments. Results in simulated pipeline environments (50cm diameter) showed the system’s capability to reconstruct non-small-area structures (e.g., flanges, sediment deposits), achieving a mean ultrasonic ranging error of 1.14mm. Further round-trip scanning in a realistic pipeline with an integrated robot confirmed that the U-SLAM system reliably performs localization and 3D mapping, producing structurally consistent point-cloud models. While challenges remain in modeling small-area fine features like cracks, U-SLAM’s real-time performance, miniaturization, and environmental adaptability provide an innovative technical solution for underwater inspection robots.

title: Temperature-Dependent Conduction and Dielectric Engineering of Dual-Barrier Pt/PtO x /Nb 2 O 5 /SiO 2 / β -Ga 2 O 3 MOS Capacitors
authors: N. Manikanthababu, K. Prajna, J. V. Vas, H. P. T. Nguyen
abstract: Pt/PtO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">x</sub>/Nb<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>O<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">5</sub> (63.5 nm)/SiO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> (6.6 nm)/<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\beta $</tex-math> </inline-formula>-Ga<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>O<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub> metal–oxide–semiconductor (MOS) capacitors exhibit stable electrical operation across <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$25~^{\circ }$</tex-math> </inline-formula>C–<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$300~^{\circ }$</tex-math> </inline-formula>C to establish the dominant transport mechanism and quantify thermal stability. <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$I$</tex-math> </inline-formula>–<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$V$</tex-math> </inline-formula> measurements confirmed Poole–Frenkel (PF) emission as the prevailing mechanism, with the extracted trap barrier height ranging from 0.59 to 0.40 eV as the temperature increased, while Arrhenius analysis yielded activation energies that decreased from 0.5 to 0.42 eV as the temperature raised. The devices retain ~97% of the original accumulation capacitance obtained from <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$C$</tex-math> </inline-formula>–<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$V$</tex-math> </inline-formula> measurements and maintain reasonable border (D<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">bt</sub>), oxide (D<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">ot</sub>), and interface trap (D<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${}_{\text {it}}\text {)}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> densities. D<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">bt</sub> is found to be <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\sim\!\!5\times 10^{{11}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> cm<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${}^{-{2}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> at <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$25~^{\circ }$</tex-math> </inline-formula>C and it raised to <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$8.1\times 10^{{11}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> cm<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${}^{-{2}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> at <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$300~^{\circ }$</tex-math> </inline-formula>C. D<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">it</sub> is found to be <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\sim\!\!5\times 10^{{11}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> cm<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${}^{-{2}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>eV<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${}^{-{1}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> at <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$25~^{\circ }$</tex-math> </inline-formula>C and it raised to <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$1.2\times 10^{{12}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> cm<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${}^{-{2}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>eV<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${}^{-{1}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> at <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$300~^{\circ }$</tex-math> </inline-formula>C. Similarly, D<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">ot</sub> is found to be <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\sim\!\!2.8\times 10^{{12}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> cm<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${}^{-{2}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> at <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$25~^{\circ }$</tex-math> </inline-formula>C and it raised to <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\sim\!\!6.2\times 10^{{12}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> cm<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${}^{-{2}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> at <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$300~^{\circ }$</tex-math> </inline-formula>C. X-ray photoelectron spectroscopy (XPS) confirmed the Nb<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${}^{{5}+}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> oxidation state and a sharp O 1s spectra, and found oxygen-vacancy density reduction after annealed at <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$400~^{\circ }$</tex-math> </inline-formula>C in O<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>. Further, high-resolution transmission electron microscopy (TEM) revealed an abrupt and conformal Nb<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>O<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">5</sub>/SiO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>/<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\beta $</tex-math> </inline-formula>-Ga<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>O<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub> stack without any crystallization of Nb<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>O<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">5</sub> up to <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$400~^{\circ }$</tex-math> </inline-formula>C.

title: Federated Learning-Driven Consumer-Centric Digital Twins for Secure and Intelligent Healthcare 5.0 Systems
authors: Sanchit Vashisht, Shalli Rani
abstract: The Internet of Medical Things (IoMT) and Consumer-Centric Digital Twins (CCDTs) are transforming modern healthcare by enabling personalized, intelligent, and secure medical services. Digital Twins act as real-time virtual replicas of medical devices and patients, supporting continuous monitoring, predictive analysis, and informed clinical decision-making. But using CCDTs makes it harder to protect privacy, make sure that different IoMT networks can work together, and securely share data between them. This research offers a Federated Learning (FL)-driven CCDT architecture that combines Personalised Federated Averaging (Per-FedAvg) and Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) to make anomaly detection stronger and protect privacy. The approach uses transformer-based contextual learning and Bayesian optimisation to make feature extraction and model generalisation better. The IoT-23 and ToN-IoT datasets are used for experimental evaluation to show how successful the suggested framework is. 99.3% accuracy, 99.5% precision, 99.1% recall, and a 99.0% F1-score are attained by the model. These outcomes demonstrate reduced communication overhead and increased energy efficiency, indicating that they are appropriate for IoMT environments with limited resources. The results show that the suggested architecture is a scalable and safe solution for Healthcare 5.0. It makes predictive diagnostics, adaptive automation, and real-time consumer-driven healthcare information possible.

title: Improving the Spatiotemporal Resolution of Satellite Remote Sensing Precipitation in Complex Terrain—Based on the Random Forest Method
authors: Xinyu Luo, Jinzhi Liao, Hao Wang, Tao Zhang, Qiangyu Zeng, Tiantian Yu, Zhi Li
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Obtaining accurate precipitation distribution information in regions with complex terrain poses a challenge due to the limitations of sparse ground-based observations. Consequently, satellite-based precipitation estimates, offering all-weather, large-scale, and continuous spatiotemporal coverage, provide a valuable alternative. However, the utility of existing satellite precipitation products for meteorological applications is limited by their coarse spatial resolution and inherent systematic biases. To address these limitations, we propose a random forest-co-kriging (RF-CK) based downscaling and calibration framework to enhance the spatiotemporal resolution of GPM satellite precipitation data for improved applicability in operational meteorology. This study focuses on Yunnan Province, a region characterized by complex terrain. A statistical downscaling model based on the random forest (RF) algorithm was developed using 2021-2023 Global Precipitation Measurement (GPM-IMERG) data, incorporating high-resolution environmental covariates (including topography, EVI, land surface temperature, evapotranspiration, longitude, and latitude) and temporal factors. he downscaled estimates were then calibrated using gauge data via the co-kriging method to enhance the resolution of GPM precipitation data from 11 km to 1 km, ultimately generating a high-resolution, high-accuracy satellite precipitation dataset. Results indicate that: (1) the RF model effectively captures the non-linear relationships between environmental covariates, temporal factors, and precipitation; (2) the RF-CK framework reduces the mean absolute error (MAE) and root mean square error (RMSE) relative to the original GPM data while improving the correlation coefficient (CC), yielding enhanced resolution and accuracy; and (3) the inclusion of temporal factors and evapotranspiration (ET) improves the model fit, demonstrating their significant influence on precipitation. The proposed RF-CK method effectively enhances the spatiotemporal resolution of GPM satellite precipitation data, offering more accurate precipitation information for meteorological applications and disaster risk reduction.

title: A Collaborative Task Scheduling Method for Heterogeneous LEO Satellite Networks Based on Mixed-strategy Game
authors: Qingyuan Niu, Haojun Teng, Shilong Jin, Yingjie Wang, Wenhan Hou, Cong Liu
abstract: With the rapid deployment of Low Earth Orbit (LEO) satellite constellations and the increasing demand for satellite–ground collaborative computing, efficient resource management and low-latency processing have become pressing challenges. This paper proposes a novel collaborative planning algorithm, the Evolutionary Reward Function (ERF), which integrates mixed-strategy game theory with Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL). ERF models the interactive decision-making among heterogeneous satellite nodes through mixed-strategy games and incorporates a dynamic policy adjustment mechanism to enhance task scheduling efficiency, thereby improving mission completion rates in dynamic LEO environments. Extensive simulation results demonstrate that ERF outperforms conventional DRL methods in terms of convergence speed and overall performance.

title: Real-Time Rolling Optimization Scheduling Technology for Cascade Hydropower Stations
authors: Changping Li, Feixiong Chen, Junjie Lin
publish: 2025 4th Asia Power and Electrical Technology Conference (APET)
abstract: This study proposes a real-time rolling optimization method to reduce scheduling deviations in cascade hydropower systems caused by inflow uncertainty and load changes. A mixedinteger optimization model has been developed to minimize overall discharge, whilst considering water balance, power balance, turbine performance, and reservoir level restrictions. By continuously assimilating real-time hydrological and load data, the model adaptively updates to coordinate power generation across cascade stations. Case studies conducted on a cascade hydropower system in southern China demonstrate that the proposed method effectively manages dynamic uncertainties, sustains system power equilibrium, and reduces water consumption, all while enhancing operational efficiency and flexibility. The findings suggest that this approach offers a practical and scalable framework for the multi-time-scale optimization of cascade hydropower scheduling.

title: A Capacitorless On-Chip LDO for Jitter Reduction in 7.2 Gbps HBM3E PHY Clock Network with Aging and Power Delivery Analysis
authors: Javed S Gaggatur
publish: 2026 39th International Conference on VLSI Design &amp; 25th International Conference on Embedded Systems (VLSID)
abstract: High-speed memory PHYs such as HBM3E demand extremely low-jitter clock distribution for error-free data recovery across wide parallel interfaces. Power supply noise is a major contributor to clock jitter in such systems, especially under dynamic load conditions. This work presents an on-chip capacitor-less low dropout (LDO) regulator designed in Intel 3 FinFET technology to power clock distribution networks in a 7.2 Gbps HBM3E PHY. The proposed LDO operates across a wide output load range of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0-175 ~\text{mA}$</tex> with a dropout voltage of 125 mV, achieving <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{1 2 5} \mu \mathrm{V} / \text{mA}$</tex> load regulation and; 3.7 mV transient excursions for a 35 mA current step. A MATLAB-based system-level analysis using a 3-tone power supply noise profile (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$100 \text{kHz}, 1 \text{MHz}, 25 \text{MHz}$</tex>) demonstrates a 16 ps (0.11 UI) reduction in RMS jitter in the clock eye diagram when the LDO is inserted in the clock power path. The design is further evaluated under accelerated aging conditions to assess long-term PSRR degradation and reliability impact. This work highlights the effectiveness of a compact, capacitor-less LDO in enhancing clock integrity in advanced memory interfaces, while meeting area, noise, and aging constraints of modern SoC integration.

title: Prediction of Circuit Breaker Closing Time for Small-Sample Conditions with a Multi-Output XGBoost Model
authors: Jia Zhou, Qiang Zeng, Weiping Zhang, Dongqi Liu
publish: 2025 4th Asia Power and Electrical Technology Conference (APET)
abstract: When a transformer is energized under no-load conditions, the unpredictable residual flux in the core can lead to high-magnitude inrush currents. These currents not only risk maloperation of protective relays but also jeopardize equipment safety. Phase-controlled closing offers an effective solution, yet its success hinges on precise control of the breaker's closing instant. To tackle the issue of widely dispersed and multi-factor-dependent closing times in SF6 circuit breakers, this paper proposes an XGBoost-based prediction model. Trained on limited three-phase closing delay data collected from field tests, the model achieves a prediction deviation of less than 0.5 ms — meeting the stringent accuracy requirements for phase-controlled closing and demonstrating its practical value even with small-sample data.

title: Integrating Tidal Harmonic Analysis with Temporal Fusion Transformer for Sea Level Prediction: A Case Study of Semarang Coastal Area
authors: Faiz Maula Ahmad Edwin Putra, Didit Adytia
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: Tidal flooding is a persistent and severe hazard in the coastal areas of Semarang, driven by the interaction between astronomical tides, long-term sea level rise, and land subsidence. Accurate forecasting of the total sea level is crucial for mitigating these recurring tidal flooding hazards. This study aims to develop an accurate sea level prediction model using the Temporal Fusion Transformer (TFT), a deep learning architecture capable of capturing long-term temporal dependencies and integrating heterogeneous variables. The study evaluates the influence of tidal and non-tidal components, where correlation analysis shows that the Tidal Harmonic Analysis (THA) component has a dominant effect (CC = 0.91), while meteorological variables from ERA5 (precipitation, wind speed) exhibit weak correlations and are therefore excluded from the final modeling. Experiments are conducted by integrating the THA component as known future inputs to enhance prediction accuracy. Sensitivity analysis indicates that using a 168-hour lookback window provides the best stability for long-term predictions (14 days). In the 14day testing scenario, the proposed TFT model achieves superior performance with a Mean Absolute Error (MAE) of 0.0450, Root Mean Square Error (RMSE) of 0.0557, and a Correlation Coefficient (CC) of 0.9785. Comparative results show that TFT outperforms standard BiLSTM and Transformer models, making it an effective method for mitigating tidal flooding hazards in Semarang.

title: Univariate Time Series Forecasting of Electrical Energy Consumption: A Comparative Performance Analysis of ARIMA, CNN, and LSTM Architecture
authors: Arnel C. Fajardo, Jerry I. Teleron, Shem L. Gonzales, Al D. Horteza
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: This study presents a comparison of three forecasting models namely ARIMA, CNN, and LSTM to determine which approach best predicts energy load patterns. Using a prepared dataset that underwent cleaning, scaling, and formatting into 24-hour sequences, each model was trained and evaluated using standard performance metrics such as MAE, MAPE, RMSE, and R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>. The ARIMA model delivered the weakest results, with an R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> of –0.11, an MAE of 3993.59, a MAPE of 15.08%, and an RMSE of 4764.70. These values clearly show that ARIMA struggled to identify the non-linear and highly variable nature of the energy load data, performing worse than expected for a reliable forecasting tool. In contrast, the CNN model demonstrated much stronger predictive capability. With an R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> of 0.97, an MAE of 515.73, a MAPE of 1.83%, and an RMSE of 728.37, the CNN was able to capture relevant short-range patterns within the time series, leading to significantly improved accuracy. However, the LSTM model provided the best performance overall. It achieved an R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> of 0.98, an MAE of 384.99, a MAPE of 1.33%, and an RMSE of 578.55, showing its strength in understanding long-term temporal relationships in the data. These results highlight how deep learning models, especially LSTM, are far better suited than traditional models like ARIMA for forecasting complex energy consumption trends. The findings suggest that advanced architectures capable of learning both local and long-range dependencies provide more accurate and reliable predictions for energy load forecasting tasks.

title: Hybrid Machine Learning Models for Detecting Identity Deception on Social Media Platforms
authors: Alaa Hussain Alenezi, Abdel-Rahman Hedar, Mamdouh F. Mohamed, Majid A. Askar
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: Social media platforms are increasingly vulnerable to identity deception driven by fake content generated by artificial intelligence. Existing detection systems often fail to generalize across multimodal and large-scale data, especially when applied to diverse real-world social media environments, particularly when faced with complex cross-platform interactions. This paper introduces a hybrid deep learning framework that integrates Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) for visual forensics, Deep Neural Networks (DNNs) for textual analysis, and metadata-based behavioral features to identify fraudulent accounts and manipulated media. The proposed hybrid model extends previous single-modality approaches by combining heterogeneous data sources into a unified multimodal representation. The proposed model fuses content, context, and network information levels through ensemble learning, thus improving robustness, interpretability, and scalability across diverse social media ecosystems. Extensive experiments were conducted on benchmark datasets including FaceForensics++, DFDC, and Celeb-DF, achieving detection accuracies exceeding 95%, which surpass conventional baselines and confirm the model’s strong cross-domain generalization capabilities. Interpretability is provided through visualization tools, enabling transparency in model decision-making and facilitating analysts’ understanding of feature relevance. The framework is also optimized for real-time deployment, ensuring scalable and low-latency integration into social media moderation pipelines and cybersecurity infrastructures.

title: Reverse Engineering and Additive Manufacturing Metrological Evaluation for Sustainable Digitalization in Shipyard Environments
authors: Guido Di Bella, Simone Panfiglio, Elnaeem Abdalla Babiker Abdalla, Fabrizio Freni, Antonio Cannuli, Roberto Montanini
publish: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Metrology for Green Technologies, Renewable Energy and Ecological Sustainability (MetroGREENST)
abstract: This work presents an experimental validation of a reverse engineering workflow applied to shipyard mechanical components, combining 3D optical scanning and additive manufacturing. The study aimed to assess the capability of the scanning system to accurately reproduce the dimensions of a mechanical manufact, evaluating the effect of the surface conditions. A structured test campaign was developed, starting with a familiarization phase using reference metrological blocks and progressing to the design and production of a multifunctional component, fabricated in both PLA (via Fused Filament Fabrication) and metal (via traditional machining). Scans were performed using portable structured-light and laser-grid assisted 3D scanners, with data processed through proprietary and open-source software. The reconstructed geometries were evaluated through mesh analysis, cross-software comparison, and dimensional verification. Results highlight the limitations of automated scanning settings in real-world conditions and the necessity of customized acquisition protocols. The study demonstrates how reverse engineering, supported by additive validation, can serve as a sustainable and flexible metrological tool for shipyard applications, enabling the digitalization of complex components under realistic constraints.

title: Pneumonia Detection Using Deep Learning
authors: Priyanka Tyagi, Ishita Kumari, Kanishka Sharma
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Advanced Computing and Emerging Technologies (ACET)
abstract: Pneumonia is life threatening disease that can affect any age group and is often difficult to diagnose from chest X-rays, as it may resemble other lung issues. While computer-based tools exist, they still lack accuracy and consistency. To address this, researchers are applying learning and federated learning, which enables training on medical data without compromising privacy. In this study, models like MLP, InceptionV3, and EfficientNetB3 were tested under federated settings. EfficientNetB3 with FedProx achieved the best performance, reaching 99.32% accuracy on similar data and 96.98% on diverse data. This highlights the potential of AI to build accurate, private and scalable pneumonia systems, with room for further improvement using advanced techniques.

title: Application of Graphene Smart Sensors in Detecting High-Frequency Partial Discharge in Polyimide Insulating Films
authors: Yuxing Lei, Bo Li, Mengyao Zhu, Jiao Sun, Zhongli Zhang
publish: 2025 4th Asia Power and Electrical Technology Conference (APET)
abstract: With the continuous advancement of ultra-high voltage transmission and the intelligentization of power equipment, the reliability of polyimide insulating films—as a key insulating material—under complex operating conditions such as high temperature and high frequency has become increasingly critical. High-frequency partial discharge is a key indicator reflecting early-stage insulation degradation. However, traditional detection methods such as the pulse current method and ultra-high frequency method suffer from issues such as low sensitivity, poor adaptability to flexible surfaces, and weak anti-interference capability. This paper presents experimental research on the partial discharge characteristics of polyimide insulating films under high-frequency voltage based on graphene smart sensors. By constructing a high-frequency partial discharge experimental platform using a sphere-plane electrode configuration and integrating graphene Hall effect sensors, partial discharge signals were systematically collected at multiple frequencies including 10 kHz, 15 kHz, and 20 kHz. Feature analysis was performed using the partial discharge density spectrum <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\varphi-q-n)$</tex>. The results indicate that as the frequency increases, the phase distribution of partial discharges gradually shifts forward, the number of low-amplitude discharges increases significantly, and the amplitude of the main discharge region decreases, exhibiting a distinct “frequency inflection point” phenomenon. Furthermore, from the perspective of dielectric loss and space charge dissipation mechanisms, the study reveals a competitive mechanism in which increased frequency leads to greater relaxation polarization loss, intensified temperature rise, and charge accumulation that suppresses discharge. This research validates the high sensitivity and strong anti-interference capability of graphene sensors in high-frequency partial discharge detection, providing a new material solution for online monitoring of flexible insulating materials. It offers valuable insights for enhancing the accuracy and reliability of condition monitoring in power equipment.

title: Privacy on Autopilot: Exploring User Attitudes Toward Data Use in Autonomous Vehicles
authors: Noora Zeyad AlRais, Saif Matar AlHajeri, Hilal Almansoori, Abdulrahman AlAwadhi, Alavikunhu Panthakkan, Saad Ali Amin
publish: 2025 Advances in Science and Engineering Technology International Conferences (ASET)
abstract: The rapid evolution of autonomous vehicle (AV) technology driven by artificial intelligence (AI) and big data analytics is transforming the landscape of modern transportation. Yet, this technological progress brings pressing concerns about data privacy and user trust, particularly due to the constant collection, sharing, and analysis of real-time data. This study explores user perceptions of data privacy in autonomous vehicles, with a focus on the context of Dubai. Utilizing machine learning (ML) techniques and k -fold crossvalidation, we analyzed user data across various demographic factors, including age, gender, and education, to accurately predict privacy concerns. Among several classifiers K-Nearest Neighbors, Decision Tree, Support Vector Machine (SVM), Random Forest, Neural Networks, and AdaBoost Gradient Boosting demonstrated the highest predictive accuracy. The results underscore the importance of robust data governance frameworks, public awareness initiatives, and cross-sector collaboration among policymakers, AI researchers, and the automotive industry. Future work should consider longitudinal studies, the application of advanced deep learning models, and the development of comprehensive regulatory strategies to foster a privacy-conscious, AI-enabled mobility ecosystem.

title: Enhancing Education through Ethical Integration of Generative AI
authors: Drishti Joshi, Deepro Chatterjee, Vikas Upadhyaya
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: In today’s era of the digital world the learning environment is changing in a fast track with a constant development of the new applications of Generative AI (Gen AI). Tools like Chat-GPT, Bard, Deep Seek, DALL.E and many more are facilitating the new engagement which accounts for the students for personal customization, assistance, promotes critical thinking, allows access to a new level of assistance which introduces and addresses the individual interest and needs. However, along with these comes the ethical implications of applying Gen AI tools such as data privacy, dependency on AI, and wrong information can cause complications which needs to be considered and reviewed carefully. This research presents the critical literature review of the research work done by other researchers in the domain along with recommendations being proposed for utilization of this technology for attaining maximum advantage. In this research, we proposed the framework to guide the educator in the usage of Gen AI tools which could help them in an adaptive and more interesting way of teaching and learning. There are four stages in our framework: the content generation, human moderation, student interaction and feedback mechanism. As a result, we have designed an AI tutor using the combination of Sparse and Dense Retrieval which would help the teachers in making the customized education for the students ensuring that most of the ethical issues are considered and worked on along with the personalization and comfort learning for the students and teaching of the faculty members.

title: Reliability Analysis of Deep Learning Models for Cold-Start Bug Recommendation Systems
authors: Vinod Kumar, Anurag Singh Baghel
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Advanced Computing and Emerging Technologies (ACET)
abstract: The reliability of deep learning-based models are critical for effective bug recommendation systems, especially in addressing the cold start problem. In this work, application BERTopic, is presented a bug recommendation system. This problem arises when a bug tracking system or a similar platform lacks sufficient data about a particular bug, such as past occurrences, related components, or assigned developers. The cold start problem can hinder the effectiveness and efficiency of bug triaging and resolution processes. To address this problem, A bug triaging system developed titled BERTopic, this has been utilized to assign bugs into different topics based on their descriptions. This topic assignment allows for matching similar components from bug reports. BERTopic offers advantages in handling large document datasets efficiently by reducing dimensionality. It introduces a class-based variation of TF-IDF, which differs from traditional centroid-based methods used in topic modelling. Reliability analysis confirms that the system enhances recommendation accuracy and robustness under sparse data conditions, leading to faster bug triaging and improved developer productivity. The average NPMI calculated through the UDAP method was 0.726, surpassing traditional topic modelling methods like LDA and NMF.

title: Adaptive Fraud Detection in Financial Ecosystems Using Hybrid Machine Learning and Deep Learning Models
authors: Amar Deep Gupta, Anshee Seth, Bhanu Prakash Lohani, Pradeep Kumar Kushwaha, Tejaswi Khanna, Deepshikha Bhargava
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Advanced Computing and Emerging Technologies (ACET)
abstract: Fraudulent activities in digital transactions pose significant threats to financial institutions, e-commerce platforms, and consumers worldwide. Conventional rulebased detection systems often fail to adapt to rapidly evolving fraud patterns, resulting in higher false positives and undetected anomalies. To address these limitations, this paper proposes a Machine Learning (ML)powered fraud detection system that leverages supervised learning, anomaly detection, and neural networks to enhance accuracy and scalability. The system integrates historical transaction data, customer behavior profiles, and real-time monitoring to identify suspicious patterns. Feature engineering techniques are applied to extract meaningful attributes such as transaction frequency, amount variability, and geolocation inconsistencies. The model is trained and validated using a hybrid approach combining decision trees, logistic regression, and deep learning to achieve improved detection performance. Experimental evaluation demonstrates reduced false alarm rates and faster response times compared to traditional methods. Furthermore, the system is designed for adaptability, allowing continuous learning from new data to counter emerging fraud tactics. The proposed framework highlights the potential of ML-based models in providing secure, reliable, and scalable fraud detection mechanisms for modern financial ecosystems.

title: Intelligent Textile for Dynamic Thermal Adaptation and Healthcare Monitoring
authors: Lung Chow, Xinge Yu
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Micro/Nano Sensors for AI, Healthcare, and Robotics (NSENS)
abstract: Traditional textiles often struggle to adapt effectively to thermal fluctuations, lacking the responsiveness needed for optimal regulation and functionality. They frequently fail to address the primary thermal cue, perspiration, leading to reduced air permeability post-moisture absorption, hindering efficient body cooling. This discomfort is particularly pronounced when transitioning between varying environmental temperatures. In this study, we present an intelligent textile designed for thermal regulation and health monitoring. This advanced textile swiftly responds to perspiration, efficiently absorbs moisture, and dynamically adjusts its structure to enhance heat dissipation. Moreover, its integrated colorimetric sensors enable the rapid detection of critical health indicators such as body temperature, UV radiation levels, and sweat <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{p H}$</tex> values. The development of this textile is poised to advance both industrial and academic domains of smart textiles, heralding new possibilities in the evolution of sophisticated multifunctional wearable electronics for the next generation.

title: Research on Technologies of Offshore Wind Power Off-Grid Black Start Supported by Grid-Forming Energy Storage
authors: Rui Xie, ZiYang Li, ZhaoPei Liang, Bin Lin, Wei Chen, ZhenYu Yang
publish: 2025 4th Asia Power and Electrical Technology Conference (APET)
abstract: Against the backdrop of global energy transition and large-scale development of offshore wind power, the traditional black start mode relying on thermal and hydropower has been increasingly incompatible with the emergency recovery needs of power grids with high renewable energy penetration. Black start in offshore wind off-grid scenarios has become a technical bottleneck due to weak voltage support, difficulties in coordination during the start-up process, and significant impact from grid connection at different stages. This paper focuses on the application of grid-forming energy storage (GFESS) in offshore wind off-grid black start. Through theoretical analysis, feasibility verification, and simulation validation, it reveals the technical advantages of GFESS, including voltage-source control and millisecond-level response. A black start sequence of “initial voltage establishment by energy storage - connection of submarine cables and main transformers - voltage boost to rated value - connection of reactive power compensation equipment” is proposed to optimize the control structure of the traditional offshore wind power black start process. Simulation results demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed scheme in terms of voltage and frequency stability as well as start-up efficiency. The research findings indicate that GFESS can break through the technical bottlenecks of traditional offshore wind off-grid black start, providing a feasible path for emergency support of renewable-rich power grids with significant engineering application value and social benefits.

title: Systematic Literature Review of LLM-Based Student Q & A System with Metadata Based Personalization for Academic Documents
authors: YMBD Aberathna, PPG Dinesh Asanka, Thilini V. Mahanama
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Innovations in Engineering, Science and Technology for Sustainable Development (ICEST)
abstract: Augmented Generation (RAG), semantic ontologies, and metadata-driven personalization can be integrated into Large Language Models (LLMs) to improve academic Q&A systems. The limitations of standalone LLMs, such as factual errors, out-of-date knowledge, and a lack of contextual adaptation, are highlighted, along with how RAG pipelines address these problems by establishing responses in institutional documents. It summarizes findings from peer-reviewed studies. The review highlights how metadata filters modify responses to studentspecific parameters like department, batch year, and curriculum version, while ontologies model academic structures (e.g., Curriculum → Course → Syllabus) and enforce policy constraints. Although there are still issues with real-time ontology updates, metadata consistency, and domain-specific evaluation, there is evidence that hybrid designs that combine vector retrieval and ontology rules improve both relevance and pedagogical accuracy. The paper concludes with a framework for developing reliable, context-aware academic assistants that align responses with current curricula, institutional policies, and individual learner needs.

title: Influence of the Welding Process on Iron Loss Characteristics in Interior Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motor
authors: Hiroto Yamaguchi, Yanhui Gao, Hamzbahmani Hamed
publish: 2025 4th Asia Power and Electrical Technology Conference (APET)
abstract: In this study, the effect of welding during the assembly process of an interior permanent magnet synchronous motor (IPMSM) on iron loss was comparatively evaluated. For the analysis, a benchmark IPMSM model provided by JSOL Corporation was used, and three-dimensional nonlinear finite element analysis was performed using the electromagnetic field simulation software JMAG. Specifically, detailed investigations were conducted on the behavior of local loss increases in the stator with welding, under ideal sinusoidal drive waveforms. The analysis revealed that eddy current losses increased significantly due to electrical conduction between laminated steel sheets in the welded areas. Quantitatively, torque efficiency decreased by 0.44%, and eddy current losses increased by 3.39%. Furthermore, it was identified that the primary causes of this loss increase were insulation degradation caused by welding and the resulting interlaminar eddy currents. This study provides valuable insights for loss evaluation and thermal design in motor development.

title: PBL-CDIO: Design and Implementation of a Zerocarbon Robot Car for Stem Education
authors: Anuparp Boonsongsrikul, Norrarat Wattanamongkhol
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Innovations in Engineering, Science and Technology for Sustainable Development (ICEST)
abstract: Modern STEM education faces the dual challenge of preparing students with advanced practical skills for a rapidly evolving technological landscape while simultaneously addressing persistent educational inequalities. This paper proposes a learning management model that combines ProblemBased Learning (PBL) with the CDIO (Conceive, Design, Implement, Operate) framework to design and build carbonfree robot cars. The study explores the impact of this integrated model on student participation, computational thinking, teamwork, creativity, and STEM proficiency. A sample of 131 high school students from five Thai schools participated. The model includes four stages: 1) Problem awareness and goal setting, 2) Design and planning, 3) Building and programming, and 4) Implementation. The learning outcomes were assessed using a 4-choice test aligned with Bloom's Taxonomy. Results showed significant improvement in student skills, with posttraining scores rising from 2.5-3.5 to 4.0-4.5 (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{p}<0.05$</tex>). Teams that completed tasks, such as controlling robot movement, received high satisfaction ratings (average <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=4.3$</tex>). The model significantly enhanced students' robot control and engineering thinking, demonstrating its effectiveness in promoting digitalage education.

title: An Event-Based Approach for Non-Intrusive Load Monitoring in Industrial Power Systems
authors: Simone Mari, Andrea Credo, Andrea Fioravanti, Edoardo Fiorucci, Álvaro Hernández Alonso, Daniel Pizarro Pérez
publish: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Metrology for Green Technologies, Renewable Energy and Ecological Sustainability (MetroGREENST)
abstract: Non-Intrusive Load Monitoring (NILM) techniques aim to identify the operating states of individual electrical loads by analyzing aggregate measurements of voltage and current. While NILM has shown promising results in residential contexts, its application in industrial settings presents unique challenges due to the complexity of load profiles, higher power levels, and increased noise. This paper introduces an event-based NILM system designed specifically for industrial environments. The system operates on the edge and combines high-resolution signal acquisition, event detection through RMS analysis, and load classification using features extracted around switching transients. A lightweight training strategy is adopted, based on single-load activation scenarios, to ensure practical deployment without requiring extensive labeled data. The proposed method is validated using real measurements collected from diverse industrial machines under realistic operating conditions.

title: Unveiling the Potential of Open Innovation for Sustainable Supply Chain Development: A Systematic Review
authors: Samrena Jabeen, Habil Slade, Muhammad Imran Qureshi, Nohman Khan, Soinestey Das
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: This research investigates the integration of open innovation within sustainable supply chains, identifying the primary opportunities and obstacles that define this emerging field. Following the PRISMA guidelines, a systematic review was conducted on 123 selected studies to synthesize current literature and categorize existing barriers to adoption. Utilizing R-based bibliometric clustering, the analysis uncovered four dominant research themes: business model innovation, supplier involvement within the value chain, the implementation of cyber-physical systems and blockchain, and the role of e-commerce in technology transfer. The findings suggest that achieving a sustainable competitive advantage requires rigorous strategic alignment across all organizational levels. Furthermore, the study emphasizes that successful implementation relies heavily on robust governance, mutual trust, and the protection of intellectual property. To advance the field, the authors recommend that future research explore the synergy between Industry 4.0 technologies and open innovation, the impact of commitment-based HR practices, and the development of specialized frameworks for sector-specific sustainable innovation.

title: M3-DP: Measured Metrics Mapped Data Preparation for Gain Crop Yield Prediction
authors: Virendra Pal Singh, Manoj Kumar Sachan
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Advanced Computing and Emerging Technologies (ACET)
abstract: The agricultural industry not only powers the economies of many countries, but it also contributes significantly to their food security. This paper proposes a novel structural mathematical modeling method for creating datasets essential for forecasting grain crop yield. To ensure accurate modeling of the desired datasets, the methodology has been tested with wheat ear images that have been processed using a pre-trained Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) for essential feature extraction. To improve relevance, further phases imply thorough preprocessing and focused feature selection. A complete dataset that captures contextual insights and fine-grained information is produced by combining manual measurements with precision derived by machines. The changing nature of agricultural landscapes in terms of crop density and diversity is accommodated by iterative procedures to guarantee adaptability. Using a threshold, the dataset is refined so that only high-quality data is kept. The result is a final dataset that has been thoroughly created and is ready to be fed to a model to forecast grain crop yield. By combining manual measurements, machine learning, and precision agriculture, this method represents a major advancement in the field.

title: A Novel Three-Satellite Cooperative System for Wildfire Detection: Autonomous Task Management and Quick Response Framework
authors: Hu Qiu, Dongfusheng Liu, Xinwei Zhao, Wei Wan, Honghai Wang, Heng Zhang
abstract: Current satellite wildfire monitoring faces critical limitations: long revisit periods leading to slow response, poor fire detection accuracy due to uncalibrated sensors, and significant gaps from cloud interference. This paper proposes a novel three-satellite cooperative system to solve these problems using a survey–calibration–observation framework. The constellation operates in a Sun-synchronous polar orbit at approximately 800 km altitude with three satellites (B, A, C) in the same plane, separated by optimized phase differences of 3° (B–A) and 20° (A–C). Satellite B performs a wide-area survey and initial fire detection. Satellite A acts as the control center, performing infrared calibration and onboard AI-based autonomous task planning. Satellite C conducts flexible, high-resolution follow-up imaging. This design, combined with fast inter-satellite links and a dual-priority data transmission strategy, ensures high responsiveness. Simulation tests, validated using the Satellite Tool Kit (STK) environment with realistic orbital parameters, show significantly faster alert times (reduced from 45–60 minutes to less than 30 minutes) and better detection accuracy compared to existing systems, with calibration errors staying below 2 percent. This scalable design advances intelligent and responsive global environmental monitoring.

title: An AI-Augmented Framework for Early-Stage Product Discovery
authors: Mohana Sudha Karumuri
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: Traditional product discovery methods rely on surveys, focus groups, and manual analysis, which are timeconsuming and often fail to capture latent user needs. This paper proposes an AI-augmented framework leveraging large language models (LLMs), natural language processing (NLP), and clustering algorithms. Our framework integrates automated analysis of user feedback, search trends, and online forums to identify unmet needs, perform market segmentation, and generate product ideas. Through semi-simulated evaluation based on expert-defined ground truth, we demonstrate that AI-augmented approaches have potential to reduce discovery time by 67% while identifying 43% more latent needs, with 89% coverage rate and $890 cost-per-insight versus $4,200 for traditional approaches. Real-world validation through industry deployment remains critical future work.

title: A VR Interactive Motor Skill Evaluation System Based on BOT-2 Assessment
authors: Yuhan Xu
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Science, and Information Processing (AICSIP)
abstract: Motor skills play a crucial role in children's perceptual, cognitive, and socio-emotional development. This paper presents a VR-based motor ability assessment system integrating the second edition of the Bruininks-Oseretsky Test of Motor Proficiency (BOT-2). The system evaluates four key motor parameters: strength, bilateral balance, manual dexterity, and agility. Utilizing an MPU-6050 sensor and an ESP32 microcontroller, it enables real-time motion data collection. The software, developed using the Unity engine, provides interactive scenarios such as boxing, figure skating, painting, and rowing to assess users' abilities. Experimental results indicate that VR technology enhances user engagement and evaluation accuracy. The system employs gamified assessments to improve participation and data reliability, particularly in children. The integration of high-precision wearable sensors ensures accurate motion tracking with minimal interference. Furthermore, the system introduces a new approach to rehabilitation training by offering precise, personalized assessments for therapists and educators. This VR-based assessment method has significant applications in sports education and rehabilitation medicine. It provides objective, data-driven insights into motor development, enabling tailored training plans. With the continued advancement of VR and wearable technology, this system offers a scalable solution for future research and clinical applications.

title: AI-Enabled Adaptive Channel Estimation and Precoding for Next-Generation MIMO Wireless Networks in Dynamic Fading Environments
authors: Madhu Kumar Vanteru, Chakradhar Adupa, K. Shashidhar, G. Deepthi
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Advanced Computing and Emerging Technologies (ACET)
abstract: Accurate channel state information (CSI) is indispensable for high-performance MIMO wireless systems. This work proposes an AI-based framework combining score-based generative modeling for channel estimation with reinforcement learning (RL) for adaptive precoding. The generative estimator infers CSI from limited pilot observations using gradient-based density learning, while the RL module dynamically optimizes transmission strategies based on predicted CSI. Simulations across standardized 3GPP channel models demonstrate enhanced reliability and throughput, achieving significant reductions in BER, NMSE, and pilot overhead compared with LS and MMSE methods.

title: River Water Quality Prediction Based on Spatiotemporal Graphical Attention Networks
authors: Weida Zhu, Shuxin Du, Yi Qiu, Aoyu Ren, Zhirong Wang, Yiming Shi
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Science, and Information Processing (AICSIP)
abstract: The water quality of a region is of great significance for water resources management and environmental protection, and this study focuses on the water quality of the Ou River in southern Zhejiang Province, and the water quality of the Ou River in southern Zhejiang Province is predicted. A water quality prediction model based on spatio-temporal graphical attention network is proposed. The water quality data were obtained from the China Environmental Testing Center (CETC), and six cross sections of the Oujiang River were selected, and the cross section data were time, PH, dissolved oxygen, hypermobilized index, ammonia nitrogen, total phosphorus, and total nitrogen. According to the correlation between the river sections and the upstream and downstream structure of the river, the model realizes the prediction of river water quality by constructing the spatio-temporal map structure of the river and utilizing the attention mechanism of STGAT to capture the complex connection of the water quality parameters of multiple sections in the spatio-temporal dimension. The experimental results show that the model has a good performance in water quality prediction accuracy.

title: ArabicNumBench: Evaluating Arabic Number Reading in Large Language Models
authors: Anas Alhumud, Abdulaziz Alhammadi, Muhammad Badruddin Khan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: We present ArabicNumBench, a comprehensive benchmark for evaluating large language models on Arabic number reading tasks across Eastern Arabic-Indic numerals (0-9 in Arabic script) and Western Arabic numerals (0-9). We evaluate 71 models from 10 providers using four prompting strategies (zero-shot, zero-shot CoT, few-shot, few-shot CoT) on 210 number reading tasks spanning six contextual categories: pure numerals, addresses, dates, quantities, and prices. Our evaluation comprises 59,010 individual test cases and tracks extraction methods to measure structured output generation. Evaluation reveals substantial performance variation, with accuracy ranging from 14.29% to 99.05% across models and strategies. Few-shot Chain-of-Thought prompting achieves 2.8x higher accuracy than zero-shot approaches (80.06% vs 28.76%). A striking finding emerges: models achieving elite accuracy (98-99%) often produce predominantly unstructured output, with most responses lacking Arabic CoT markers. Only 6 models consistently generate structured output across all test cases, while the majority require fallback extraction methods despite high numerical accuracy. Comprehensive evaluation of 281 model-strategy combinations demonstrates that numerical accuracy and instruction-following represent distinct capabilities, establishing baselines for Arabic number comprehension and providing actionable guidance for model selection in production Arabic NLP systems.

title: Advancing Web-Accessibility: Systematic Review of Machine Learning Approaches for Web Content Summarization and Accessibility for the Visually Impaired
authors: Fatima Ezzahrae Massdar, Adnane El Amrani, Yousra Chtouki
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: Web accessibility remains a significant digital inclusion front for the visually impaired. This systematic review explores the convergence of web accessibility and machine learning, as applied to text summarization and speech synthesis technology. In line with PRISMA guidelines, we survey the current assistive technologies, and we determine there are significant failings of screen readers in managing unstructured web content. Our assessment indicates that deep learning-based summarization models have higher performance ratings (ROUGE-1: 0.46-0.55) compared to traditional approaches and have higher user satisfaction ratings (4.1/5) from visually impaired users. We proceed to analyze the development of speech synthesis from early concatenative to neural approaches like WaveNet and Tacotron, offering better naturalness and expressiveness. This research sets a future research agenda by pointing to essential gaps in real-time summarization, multilingual support, and personalized accessibility solutions, milestones toward designing more inclusive digital spaces for visually impaired users.

authors: Dandan Zhu, Xueying Yan, Haoran Zhang, Yi Han, Tieniu Zhao, Zengqunzong Dan, Chenxi Fan, Peizhen Jiang, Yuxuan Wang, Yuqing Mu, Xiaoyu Wang

title: Multi-Modal Plagiarism Detection in the Era of AI: A Comprehensive Review and Novel Framework Proposal
authors: Anshu Kumar, Purushottam Choudhary, Anirban Dutta, Aanchal Vij
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Advanced Computing and Emerging Technologies (ACET)
abstract: The most modernized traditional systems also cannot keep abreast with the new forms of evasion by converting texts to images, making submissions manually, and machine-written articles. Recent commercial systems show that they fail dramatically in performance, the quality decreases by up to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{4 5} \boldsymbol{\%}$</tex> on multi-modal input, and the failure rates become high, up to 73 % on AI generated content. This paper gives a comprehensive overview of these constraints and talks of an integrated seven step multi-modal plagiarism detection framework. It is proposed to use a combination of deep learning-based Optical Character Recognition (OCR) and transformer-based Natural Language Processing (NLP) architecture to allow analyzing texts, scans, and handwritten documents in a comprehensive manner. A hybrid similarity analysis methodology based on a combination of lexical, semantic and structure-oriented features enhance resistance to paraphrasing and automated generation. To be extensible, the system must be designed to achieve over 95 % of detection and lowering the false positives when compared to the current systems. Defining the future-oriented architecture and defining the critical research gaps, the research opens the path to the next generation of academic integrity systems which would accommodate the changing needs of AI-enhanced plagiarism.

title: LLM–Interactive Optimization for Multi-Sensor Cooperative Target Detection
authors: Hongye Zhang, Zheyuan Wang, Feng Zhang, Jun Sun, Zili Liu
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Science, and Information Processing (AICSIP)
abstract: This study aims to develop a collaborative detection planning framework that integrates a multi-stage, multi-objective optimization algorithm with large-model interactive capabilities, thereby enhancing both system efficiency in task allocation and overall user experience. To address conflicts among objectives, we propose a three-stage optimization process that employs OR-Tools to generate a Pareto solution set, enabling visualization of the decision space and flexible selection. At the system level, an LLM-driven intelligent agent interaction workflow is introduced to seamlessly integrate natural language input parsing, visibility determination mechanisms, and the optimization solving pipeline. This design allows users-without requiring specialized programming skills-to define tasks, adjust parameters, and receive feedback through conversational interaction. Simulation experiments demonstrate that, compared to random planning methods, this framework achieves significant improvements in coverage, resource utilization, and switching efficiency. The contributions of this paper are twofold: first, it enables controllable Pareto-optimal outputs for multi-equipment, multi-objective scheduling problems; second, it explores an interactive paradigm for the collaboration between LLMs and traditional planning tools, offering a novel direction for intelligent decision-making system design.

title: Machine Learning Approaches for Phishing Detection: A Systematic Literature Review
authors: Aimaan Abdulla, Ibrahim Thorig
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Innovations in Engineering, Science and Technology for Sustainable Development (ICEST)
abstract: Phishing and online scams are growing cybersecurity threats worldwide. In the Maldives, online scams and fraudulent activities have exponentially increased in recent years. Victims are targeted through social media impersonation, card fraud, compromised email accounts, spoofed SMS, fraudulent merchandise sales, fake foreign currency exchanges, and more recently, Telegram account hijacking via phishing links. Traditional rule-based and blacklist methods are proving inadequate against the evolving sophistication of these schemes, prompting greater interest in Machine Learning (ML) for phishing detection. The current study presents a systematic literature review of ML-based phishing detection methods published recently, covering website, email, URL, and messaging-platform attacks. The review categorizes studies by algorithm type, feature extraction methods, datasets, and performance metrics, identifying Support Vector Machines (SVM), Random Forests (RF), and deep learning models such as Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) and Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks as leading approaches. Public datasets like PhishTank, as well as custom domain-specific datasets, play a central role in model training. This scientific review reveals significant research gaps in addressing region-specific scam patterns and in deploying lightweight, real-time detection for low-resource network environments similar to Maldives. Recommendations are included for developing localized ML-based phishing detection systems, supported by region-specific datasets, to address the Maldives' unique scam patterns such as social media impersonation, Viber, Facebook and Telegram account hijacking.

title: Code Invaders: Detecting Malicious Activity in Embedded Systems
authors: Aryan Panwar, Deeksha Dhaka, Avichal Tomar, Ayush Tyagi, Akash Rajak
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Advanced Computing and Emerging Technologies (ACET)
abstract: As of today more embedded and IoT (Internet of Things) devices are being used in the world today and deployed for in the world's most critical applications, therefore the need for lightweight, affordable, and highly effective ransomware training and detection tools adapted for specialized resource-scarce environments for employees to work safely from their devices is highly critical. To address these needs, this research proposes the first cross-architecture integrated hardware and software solution of its kind designed to use Hardware Performance Counters (HPCs) in tracking low-level processor events such as cache hits, cache misses and branches, and using those to help to detect the continuing use of ransomware. Different from the highly accurate tracking signature-based methods in the computers of today, this is a tracking signature-free, operational real-time method. this makes it highly abnormal, unrecognized and unknown from the unrecognizable ransomware common. It uses the extraction of real-time data characteristics from the HPC and machinelearning tracking signature Barns and counterparts to identify benign processes and aligned ransomware to malicious behavior. The use of the affordable general-purpose embedded computer, Raspberry Pi, showed that the operational support of the tracking signature method was kept within the acceptable limits of the computer.
