title: When Movable Antenna Meets URLLC Systems: A Deep Reinforcement Learning Approach
authors: Jingrong Liu, Jin Guo, Jianpeng Xu, Yaoyuan Zhang, Xiaolin Liang
abstract: Movable antenna (MA) represents a promising technology for boosting wireless communication performance by enabling the repositioning of antennas within the given region. In this paper, we investigate one MA enabled uplink ultra reliable and low-latency communication (URLLC) system. Firstly, we characterize an optimization problem that seeks to acquire the maximization of the system's weighted sum rate via jointly optimizing the MA positions as well as transmit power at the users. Subsequently, we employ the twin-delayed deep deterministic policy gradient (TD3), a deep reinforcement learning algorithm, to address the weighted sum rate maximization problem. At last, simulation results validate that the proposed TD3-based algorithm exhibits notable advantages in enhancing the weighted sum rate compared to other benchmark algorithms including the fixed-position antenna algorithm.

title: A Novel Compact Printed Inverted-F Antenna Integrated into Biker Helmets for Consumer Internet of Vehicles
authors: Kapil Gangwar, Kundan Kumar Suman, Som Pal Gangwar, Ravi Kumar Gangwar
abstract: This paper introduces a novel Planar Inverted-F Antenna (PIFA) design optimized for biker’s helmets, aimed at enhancing rider safety by facilitating effective communication systems. Road traffic injuries, a significant global health concern, disproportionately affect motorcycle riders, with mobile phone usage while riding increasing this risk. A helmet model, developed in CST software, serves as a testbed for evaluating antenna characteristics. The presented PIFA design, covering a frequency range from 2.36-2.6 GHz, offers a 9.8% impedance bandwidth around the 2.45 GHz ISM band and maintains high efficiency and stability despite variations in helmet materials and placements. Detailed specific absorption rate (SAR) analysis is done to ensure compliance with safety protocols. A link budget analysis examines the communication feasibility between helmet-mounted and mobile phone antennas, as well as between different helmet users. This study contributes significantly to the field of antenna-integrated helmet technology, offering a viable solution to enhance rider safety through improved communication capabilities.

title: Beyond Support Samples: Incorporating Unlabeled Queries for Few-Shot Semantic Segmentation
authors: Yuanwei Liu, Nian Liu, Tao Jiang, Yi Wu, Xiwen Yao, Junwei Han
publish: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
abstract: Few-shot semantic segmentation (FSS) often struggles with the intra-class diversity issue between query and support images, caused by the category-biased information provided by limited annotated support images for matching objects. While increasing the number of annotated support images could mitigate this bias, it is impractical within the few-shot learning framework. Therefore, our proposed Unlabeled Query Integration Few-Shot Segmentation (UQI-FSS) tackles this challenge by incorporating unlabeled query images into the learning paradigm. This approach aims to achieve a more comprehensive category representation, which is essential to enhance segmentation accuracy in various scenarios. However, integrating unlabeled query images directly requires careful management to prevent the dilution of vital information from the annotated support set. To address this issue, we present an Unlabeled Query Integration Network (UQINet), which adaptively extracts beneficial and suppresses detrimental information from the unlabeled query images. Specifically, we first introduce an Information Bridging Module to close the gap between support and unlabeled query features, generating a pseudo-support set enriched with additional category data. Next, we introduce a Query Fusion Module to incorporate query information from both prototype and pixel levels into the pseudo-support features, thus improving their adaptability to the query. Finally, we propose an Adaptive Selection Module to select effective category information and combine the pseudo-support features, thereby activating target objects for precise segmentation prediction. Experimental results show considerable performance improvements over previous methods on various FSS benchmarks. The application of this method to four challenging scenarios further underscores its versatility and practical value. The code is available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/LIUYUANWEI98/UQINet</uri>

title: Hardware-Rooted Device Identity for IoT: Integrating Silicon PUFs and DICE in RISC-V Embedded Systems
authors: Silvia Sisinni, Lorenzo Ferro, Enrico Bravi, Pablo Navarro-Torrero, Eros Camacho-Ruiz, Macarena C. Martínez-Rodríguez, Piedad Brox, Antonio Lioy
abstract: The security of Internet of Things (IoT) and embedded systems critically depends on establishing trustworthy, unclonable device identities from the earliest boot stages. Conventional approaches often rely on externally stored secrets, which remain vulnerable to extraction and cloning, or heavyweight hardware modules, which are unsuitable for constrained platforms. In this work, we present a lightweight Hardware Root of Trust (HRoT) architecture that integrates silicon-based Physically Unclonable Functions (PUFs) with the Device Identifier Composition Engine (DICE) directly into the boot ROM of RISC-V embedded systems. Unlike existing RoTs, our design eliminates the need for persistent key storage by generating manufacturer-unknown identities on demand, and it extends trust across the system through DICE-based layered attestation.We further introduce lifecycle-aware mechanisms for identity provisioning, renewal, and secure zeroization, ensuring long-term device trustworthiness. The proposed architecture is implemented and evaluated on the open-source SPIRS RISC-V platform, embedding hardware accelerators for cryptography and PUF-bound secrets. Experimental results demonstrate strong resistance against physical and software attacks, compliance with IEC 62443 SL2+ requirements, robust lifecycle support, and negligible performance overhead (<1% increase in boot time). By bridging unclonable hardware identities with standardized attestation, this work establishes a scalable and interoperable foundation for secure authentication and continuous trust in IoT and embedded devices.

title: Sickle cell anemia prediction system in machine learning based on clinical data
authors: Jorge Gómez Gómez, Claudia Madrid Rosales, Soraya Isabel Montaño
abstract: Confirmatory diagnosis of sickle cell anemia (SCA) traditionally requires specialized tests that are not widely available in low-resource settings. We propose an automated prediction system based exclusively on structured Complete Blood Count (CBC) variables and demographic data. We analyzed the clinical records of 786 patients (425 with normocytic anemia and 361 with SCA) and evaluated seven classifiers using stratified 10-fold cross-validation with fold-wise SMOTE. Random Forest and XGBoost achieved the best performance (mean accuracy = 0.961, mean F1 = 0.957, mean AUC = 0.995). A global Friedman test indicated significant differences among the classifiers (χ² = 29.52, p = 1.16 × 10⁻⁴). Post-hoc Wilcoxon tests adjusted by Holm did not identify pairwise significant contrasts at α = 0.05; however, paired effect sizes (Cohen’s d) revealed large practical differences favoring the ensemble tree methods. SHAP interpretability identified PCV (hematocrit), HGB, RDW and Age as the main contributors. The system offers a low-cost, interpretable screening tool that prioritizes patients for confirmatory testing. However, external validation and multimodal integration are required before it can be clinically deployed.

title: A Low Noise Multipath Chopper-Stabilized Operational Amplifier using Dynamic Element Matching Gain-Boosting and Low Input Current Adaptive Clock-Boosting Chopper Switch
authors: Mookyoung Yoo, Gibae Nam, Minhyeok Son, Manhyuck Choi, Inju Yu, Dahyun Jin, Euiyoung Lee, Hajun Bin, Hyoungho Ko
abstract: This paper presents a low noise multipath operational amplifier with chopper-stabilized and gain-boosted low-frequency path (LFP) amplifier. The LFP amplifier adopts the gain-boosting using dynamic element matching (DEM) and buffered adaptive clock boosting input chopper techniques. The gain-boosting techniques increase the gain of LFP, which allows for higher stabilization of offset and low frequency noise from high-frequency path (HFP) amplifier. To mitigate the switching artifacts from input chopper switches, the NMOS switch with buffered adaptive clock-boosting and dummy switch is proposed. The VGS of the input switch is kept constant, and a buffer within the switch drives the boosting capacitor, which prevents it from drawing current from the input terminal. Ripple generated by the chopper operation is attenuated by a gain-boosted integrator and an AC-coupled gain-boosted ripple reduction loop (RRL). The amplifier was fabricated using a 180-nm complementary metal–oxide–semiconductor (CMOS) process. The amplifier operates with a 1.8V supply and consumes a DC current of 200.8 μA. The amplifier exhibits a minimum PSRR of 93 dB and a minimum CMRR of 130 dB at 1 Hz. The measured unity-gain bandwidth (UGBW) with 40 dB closed-loop gain is 4.076 MHz. The measured input-referred noises of the amplifier are from 2.3 nV/√Hz to 3.3 nV/√Hz with 10 chips. The measured noise efficiency factors (NEF) are from 1.96 to 2.81. The measured input currents are from 12 pA to 88 pA.

title: High-Fidelity 3D Facial Avatar Synthesis with Controllable Fine-Grained Expressions_supp1-3674337.mp4
authors: WEI WANG

title: 20 Years of TES Gamma-Ray Microcalorimeters
authors: Shannon E. Kossmann, Mark P. Croce
abstract: In the last 20 years, transition edge sensor (TES) gamma-ray microcalorimeters have evolved from the first proof of concept single pixel detectors to large, multiplexed arrays of hundreds of TES pixels used to collect ultra-high resolution gamma spectra. For example, the Spectrometer Optimized for Facility Integrated Applications (SOFIA) detector of 256 pixels is able to achieve coadded spectra with resolution as good as 59 eV full width at half maximum (FWHM) at 97 keV or 70-90 eV FWHM for high-rate plutonium measurements. Much of the research and development of these detectors over the last 20 years has focused on improving the achieved resolution of individual TES pixels, creating arrays with a sufficiently high yield of pixels, developing methods to readout large numbers of individual pixels in an array without providing too much heat load at the very cold temperatures (from wires to room temperature), how to process the data from each TES individually, and how to calibrate and coadd the pixel data to form a spectrum without much degradation of resolution. TES gamma-ray microcalorimeter arrays are now used for improvement of fundamental nuclear data and to collect ultra-high resolution spectra that can resolve peaks that overlap in high purity germanium (HPGe) spectra for better characterization of nuclear material. Current areas of development include increasing the size of arrays with new form factors, improving user experience with more mature systems, integration of these detector systems into facilities with nuclear material to improve nuclear material control and accounting with significantly improved resolution of complex spectra compared to HPGe detectors, and ongoing research and alteration of the TES detector design.

title: WAVES: Fine-grained Water Type Mapping via Vision-Language Alignment under Supervision without Pixel-level Labels_supp1-3674221.docx
authors: Haigang Sui
abstract: Fine-grained classification of surface water types is crucial for capturing the functional diversity of aquatic environments, enabling more targeted environmental regulation, hydrological modeling, and land-use planning. However, existing water mapping efforts remain limited to binary segmentation, largely due to the lack of pixel-level annotations and the semantic ambiguity among visually similar water types. In this study, we propose WAVES (Weakly-supervised Alignment for Vision-Language Enhanced Segmentation), a novel framework that combines binary water masks and image-level captions to enable fine-grained water type classification. We design a novel caption-labeling pipeline that combines LLM-generated water-type descriptions with CLIP-based filtering to obtain high-confidence, context-aware supervisory signals. Built upon a CLIP backbone with visual prompt tuning, WAVES incorporates a cross-modal Transformer decoder to produce caption-specific segmentation masks. To bridge the gap between global text and spatial regions, we design a three-part vision-language alignment loss collection, which aligns each caption with its most relevant water region using embedding similarity, enabling water type segmentation without pixel-level annotations. Experiments on the GID dataset show that WAVES achieves a FWIoU of 0.735 from the raw model output, which increases to 0.810 after applying the post-processing module, outperforming both visual-based fully and weakly supervised baselines, especially in complex categories such as aquaculture water and irrigation channels. In a large-scale application in Hubei Province, WAVES maps nine water types classification results across diverse landscapes including agricultural, urban, and mountainous regions. These results demonstrate that WAVES offers a practical solution for detailed water type mapping, capable of handling real-world, large-area applications.

title: IEEE Power Electronics Society Information
authors: N/A

title: LIMR: Intent-Aware Mashup API Recommendation via LLM-Augmented Multi-Scale Fusion
authors: Yao Zhang, Yude Bai, Minhong Dong, Keqing Cen, Ji Zhang, Qiang Hu, Wei Ma, Yongqiang Lyu, Ruitao Feng, Xiaohong Li, Junjie Wang, Lingxiao Jiang, Yang Liu
abstract: The increasing availability of Web APIs has amplified the complexity of mashup creation, where developers must identify compatible and functionally relevant APIs based on often ambiguous natural language descriptions. Traditional methods also fall short in capturing hierarchical semantic cues, modeling compatibility, and aligning with developer intent. Although large language models (LLMs) offer strong generalization capabilities, they remain unreliable in mashup recommendation due to hallucinated outputs, limited controllability, and token-length constraints when dealing with large-scale API repositories. To overcome these limitations, we introduce LIMR, an intent-aware mashup recommendation framework that combines LLM-augmented semantic reasoning with structured, multi-scale neural modeling. LIMR first prompts a LLM to extract high-level intent from user requirements, which serves as a global semantic signal. This intent is fused with low-level, multi-scale features extracted by a convolutional encoder, which are designed to capture fine-grained lexical/phrasal patterns at different granularities and provide precise semantic grounding for API matching. These heterogeneous representations are further contextually refined through a Transformer-based interaction module. To handle nonlinear semantic dependencies and compositional complexity, LIMR integrates a Kolmogorov-Arnold Network (KAN) with learnable activation functions, enhancing the model's capacity to capture intricate feature interactions. The entire framework is optimized via LLM, incorporating auxiliary objectives such as mashup category prediction and API quality estimation to guide generalization and reduce overfitting. Comprehensive experiments on the ProgrammableWeb and APIBench datasets show that LIMR significantly outperforms state-of-the-art baselines, which the ranking-oriented metrics, including NDCG and mAP, achieves improvements of 17.1%–34.2% over the strongest competitors. These results confirm the effectiveness of LIMR's hybrid design in delivering precise, robust, and intent-aware mashup API recommendations, especially in scenarios where LLMs alone fail to meet accuracy and scalability demands.

title: GOT-JEPA: Generic Object Tracking with Model Adaptation and Occlusion Handling using Joint-Embedding Predictive Architecture
authors: Shih-Fang Chen, Jun-Cheng Chen, I-Hong Jhuo, Yen-Yu Lin
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: The human visual system tracks objects by integrating current observations with previously observed information, adapting to target and scene changes, and reasoning about occlusion at fine granularity. In contrast, recent generic object trackers are often optimized for training targets, which limits robustness and generalization in unseen scenarios, and their occlusion reasoning remains coarse, lacking detailed modeling of occlusion patterns. To address these limitations in generalization and occlusion perception, we propose GOT-JEPA, a model-predictive pretraining framework that extends JEPA from predicting image features to predicting tracking models. Given identical historical information, a teacher predictor generates pseudo-tracking models from a clean current frame, and a student predictor learns to predict the same pseudo-tracking models from a corrupted version of the current frame. This design provides stable pseudo supervision and explicitly trains the predictor to produce reliable tracking models under occlusions, distractors, and other adverse observations, improving generalization to dynamic environments. Building on GOT-JEPA, we further propose OccuSolver to enhance occlusion perception for object tracking. OccuSolver adapts a point-centric point tracker for object-aware visibility estimation and detailed occlusion-pattern capture. Conditioned on object priors iteratively generated by the tracker, OccuSolver incrementally refines visibility states, strengthens occlusion handling, and produces higher-quality reference labels that progressively improve subsequent model predictions. Extensive evaluations on seven benchmarks show that our method effectively enhances tracker generalization and robustness. The code will be available at https://github.com/chenshihfang/GOT.

title: A –89.2-dB Crosstalk, 24.5-nV/${\surd}$Hz Noise Floor, High-Efficiency Bio-Potential Recording AFE With Nested Frequency-Phase-Division-Multiplexing Structure
authors: Wenfei Cao, Longjie Zhong, Wenxiu Jian, Shuhang Li, Ling Wang, Zhangming Zhu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems I: Regular Papers
abstract: This paper proposes the nested frequency-phase-division-multiplexing (Nested FPDM) based analog-front-end (AFE) for multi-channel signal recording of the bio-potential. The AFE employing the nested FPDM structure significantly reduces the crosstalk. In the multi-channel signal recording AFE, there are two design challenges: 1) noise performance limitation due to the large parasitic capacitance in the front-end instrumentation amplifier, 2) significant chip area consumption due to the large capacitance in the back-end programmable gain amplifier. To address the challenges, the operational amplifier capacitance matching technique and the miller-effect-reduction technique are employed. The proposed 4-channel nested FPDM-AFE is fabricated in a commercial <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$0.18\mu $</tex-math> </inline-formula>m BCD process, consuming <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$17.7~\mu $</tex-math> </inline-formula>A at a single 1.8-V supply voltage. Compared to other similar reported multiplexing bio-potential recording AFEs, this work achieves the best crosstalk of −89.2 dB, a competitive power efficiency in terms of NEF (4.29) and a compact chip area of 0.118 mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>/channel.

title: Global Sensitivity Analysis for Cyber-Physical Power Distribution Network
authors: Dongmeng Qiu, Suhan Zhang, Tianyu Luo, Xin Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Cyber-Physical Systems
abstract: Cyber-physical power systems (CPPS) operate under uncertainties from both the power and communication layers, yet there are limited systematic methods to evaluate uncertain cyber-physical impacts. To address this gap, we build a CPPS model by coupling a power distribution network represented by differential–algebraic equations (DAEs) and a discrete-event communication network with node-specific communication delays. On this basis, global sensitivity analysis (GSA) is introduced to quantify the impact of cyber-physical uncertainties on the power distribution network, thereby identifying the most critical factors that affect CPPS operation. Probabilistic models are used to simulate load, photovoltaic (PV) generation, and communication delays, and a Monte Carlo framework is built to estimate Sobol' sensitivity indices. Finally, a CPPS digital co-simulation platform is developed which integrates OPAL-RT power system simulator and OMNeT++ communication network simulator via shared memory mechanism to validate the CPPS framework. Case studies demonstrate the quantitative relationship between the sources of uncertainty and their impacts within the CPPS, indicating that PV generation dominates the variance of bus voltages, whereas communication node delays mainly affect local voltage stability in the test system.

title: Driving Economic Growth through AI-Enabled Smart Transportation
authors: Farheen Akram, Muhammad Abrar-ul-Haq, Jayendira P Sankar, Vinodh K Natarajan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: Fast-growing smart transportation dominates global sustainability and urban development by integrating modern technology and management strategies to improve efficiency, safety, and environmental impact. So, smart transportation is essential for various applications, including commuting, goods movement, personal travel, and public services. Through enhancing traffic management, infrastructure, electric vehicle support, safety systems, and environmental monitoring, smart transportation aims to reduce travel time, fuel consumption, vehicle maintenance costs, and emissions while boosting productivity and economic growth. Also, the study explores opportunities and challenges for smart transportation in developing countries, focusing on economic benefits and digital solutions. Further, advanced technologies like AI and IoT optimize traffic control, accident detection, parking, and route planning, enhancing urban mobility and infrastructure reliability. Despite significant progress, gaps in localized studies, regulatory frameworks, and public-private partnerships need to be addressed to realize the full potential of smart transportation systems. Thus, these efforts are crucial for fostering sustainable urban development and meeting the demands of increasing urban populations.

title: Multi-modal Fake News Detection
authors: Moataz S. Elfoly, Ahmed M. Fahmy
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: False information poses a significant risk to society by altering public perception and eroding trust in reliable sources. With the rise of misinformation in various text and image forms, the demand for advanced multi-modal detection systems is growing. This study introduces a multi-modal framework for detecting fake news that integrates NLP, ML, and GNNs, tested on two datasets: a multi-class text-only dataset and a binary text+image dataset. Text embeddings (TF-IDF, GloVe, FastText, BERT) are combined with classifiers (Logistic Regression, Decision Trees, XGBoost, Random Forests) and graph models (GCN, GraphSAGE) to understand structural relationships. An adapted BERT model additionally integrates numerical, categorical, and textual features for enhanced classification. In the binary context, a visual module based on YOLOv11 identifies fake images, and late fusion integrates the top text and vision models into a single classifier. Findings indicate that the suggested framework surpasses unimodal benchmarks: the adjusted BERT secures the highest text accuracy, GraphSAGE excels with graphs, and YOLOv11 fusion enhances detection for visually backed news, underscoring the efficiency of multi-modal fake news detection in practical applications.

title: Integration of IoT and AI Technologies for Predicting the Ecological Status of Black Sea Water
authors: Tamar Lominadze, Lily Petriashvili, Nino Topuria, Gia Surguladze, Taliko Zhvania, Givi Gavardashvili
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: This study explores opportunities to advance sustainable water management systems through innovative technological approaches. It emphasizes the potential of modern technological platforms for monitoring the ecological status of water bodies, with a particular focus on the Black Sea ecosystem. An integrated system is presented that combines IoT sensors and Artificial Intelligence (AI) predictive models using Azure Machine Learning AutoML. The system enables real-time collection and analysis of simulated telemetry data. The study demonstrates that, through the use of simulated IoT data and AI, it is possible to monitor and predict the ecological state of water bodies in real time. The simulated telemetry data includ essential parameters for assessing water quality and were processed on the Azure IoT Central platform. Dissolved oxygen (DO) prediction models were developed using Azure Machine Learning AutoML, achieving the following initial evaluation metrics: R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> = 0.11, MAE = 0.73 mg/L, RMSE = 0.86 mg/L, and Spearman correlation = 0.49. The results indicate that the model effectively captures general water quality trends; however, its accuracy is currently limited due to the small size of the dataset and the simulated nature. This approach demonstrates the innovativeness of the developed methodology and provides a foundation for future predictive models incorporating real IoT and meteorological data. The proposed system will support real-time management of the ecological condition of the Black Sea region. The work is carried out within the framework of the grant NATO SPS G6028 Control of Black Sea Safety and Pollution Risks Using Numerical Models /2023-2025/)

title: A General Model for Standard Knowledge Representation and Empirical Research- A Case Study of Port Container Operation as an Standards
authors: Tao Wang, Bin Xu, Yixuan Li, Hongjun Sun, Rongchao Wang, Bojun Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Science, and Information Processing (AICSIP)
abstract: This paper focuses on the general model for standard knowledge representation and its empirical research. Based on relevant studies and practices both domestically and internationally, a general model for standard knowledge representation is proposed, along with a method for extracting standard knowledge. An empirical validation is conducted using standards from the port handling domain. Finally, the paper explores the application of standard digitalization based on standard knowledge, aiming to provide methodological and experiential references for future research.

title: Energy-Efficient Distance-Controlled (EEDC) Clustering for Bottleneck Avoidance in Wireless Sensor Networks
authors: Ahmad Abuashour
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: Clustered Wireless Sensor Networks (WSNs) are widely used for their scalability and energy efficiency; however, cluster heads (CHs) located near the Base Station (BS) often experience excessive relay traffic, causing rapid energy depletion and creating a persistent energy bottleneck around the BS. This imbalance significantly reduces network stability and overall lifetime. To address this issue, this paper introduces EEDC (Energy-Efficient Distance-Controlled Clustering), a distance-aware clustering mechanism that regulates CH density and transmission power based on each node’s distance from the BS. EEDC increases the number of CHs in the vicinity of the BS to distribute the forwarding load more effectively, while using fewer CHs in distant regions to conserve energy. In addition, CHs dynamically adjust their transmission power to enable distance-proportional communication and minimize redundant energy consumption. Simulation results demonstrate that EEDC significantly outperforms existing clustered protocols such as LEACH, HEED, DEEC, SEP, and EECS. Specifically, EEDC improves the stability period by up to 42 %, extends overall network lifetime by 23 %, and increases average residual energy by 13 to 29 %. These results confirm that distance-controlled CH selection and adaptive transmission power effectively mitigate the BS bottleneck and enhance the energy efficiency and operational longevity of clustered WSNs.

title: Research on the Optimal Flood Control Operation Model for Cascade Hydropower Stations Based on Piecewise Linearization
authors: Chunlan Cao, Jinlin Chen, Feixiong Chen, Junjie Lin
publish: 2025 4th Asia Power and Electrical Technology Conference (APET)
abstract: To address the complex relationships among multiple objectives in watershed flood control operation, such as ensuring dam safety, reducing downstream flood peaks, and storing water for beneficial use, this paper proposes an efficient optimal flood control operation technique. By constructing a flood control optimization scheduling model for cascade reservoirs during the flood season, the model meticulously considers constraints such as hydraulic connections between reservoirs, storage capacity, and downstream safe discharge volumes, effectively reducing reservoir flood peaks. To efficiently solve this multi-objective optimization problem, the model employs a weighting approach to transform it into a singleobjective problem, thereby generating an optimized scheduling plan that balances system safety and benefits. Case studies on a cascade hydropower system in southern China demonstrate that this technical solution not only significantly enhances the flood control safety margin of the cascade but also provides a scientific approach for achieving flood resource utilization, validating its feasibility and practical value in cascade reservoirs' flood warning and fine scheduling during the flood season.

title: Implementation of Kakeibo Method in Personal Finance Management Application
authors: Muhammad Dzaky Nashshar, Risna Sari, Rizki Elisa Nalawati, Hata Maulana, Iwan Sonjaya
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: Facing the financial management challenges encountered by millennials and Gen Z, coupled with the lack of planning and budgeting features in existing financial applications, this research has successfully designed and built a functional and relevant Android-based personal finance management application. This application innovatively implements the Japanese Kakeibo method to not only record transactions but also to facilitate users in creating category-based budgets, setting savings targets, and, most importantly, conducting structured monthly financial reflections. Developed using the Waterfall methodology, the application implements clean architecture with MVVM using Kotlin and Jetpack Compose on the client side, and Laravel for the REST API. It has undergone comprehensive testing. The results show 100% functionality as expected, a good usability level with an average System Usability Scale (SUS) score of 70.07, and strong content validity, evidenced by a Scale-Content Validity Index Average (S-CVI/Ave) score of 0.91 and a Universal Agreement (S-CVI/UA) score of 0.80 from expert evaluation. Thus, this research successfully developed a tested, user-accepted, and relevant technological solution that helps users achieve their financial goals in a more structured, planned manner.

title: FPGA Optimized Pipelined Modulo Computation Architecture Leveraging Primitive Instantiation and Placement Constraints for Efficient Logic Packing
authors: Suprabhat Bhattacharjee, Ayan Palchaudhuri
publish: 2026 39th International Conference on VLSI Design &amp; 25th International Conference on Embedded Systems (VLSID)
abstract: Modulo arithmetic constitute an essential component of many cryptographic algorithms. In this paper, we have proposed a fast and compact architecture for <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$X \bmod Z$</tex> computation tailored for FPGAs. One of the primary challenges in an FPGA based design is improving upon the logic packing density which has been carefully taken care of by appropriate configuration of logic elements. Primitive instantiation technique is adopted to effectively utilize the internal FPGA slice architecture by optimal configuration of look-up tables and use of fast carry chain. Pipelining is introduced to reduce critical path delay and placement constraints are applied to maximize logic resource usage within a configured FPGA slice, thereby improving area efficiency. The designs are crafted to ensure a good fit into modern day FPGA families, thereby throwing light on generic datapath design techniques to extract high performance.

title: CNN-Based Exposure Time and ISO Optimization via Image-Parameter Fusion in Underwater Optical Camera Communication
authors: Jiongnan Lou, Lianxin Hu, Zhan Wang, Yiqian Qian, Haifei Shen, Xun Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Science, and Information Processing (AICSIP)
abstract: In the area of Underwater Optical Camera Communication (UOCC), image quality-and consequently, communication performance-is highly dependent on camera exposure time and ISO settings. To address this sensitivity, we propose a CNN-based joint regression framework that optimizes exposure times and ISO settings by leveraging both visual content and associated metadata. The model employs a ResNet50 backbone for deep feature extraction and integrates a parameter-aware regression head to jointly predict the optimal exposure time and ISO under diverse underwater conditions. By fusing image features with parameter information, the model achieves robust performance across varying environments. Experimental results demonstrate a root mean square error (RMSE) of 0.96 in parameter prediction. Furthermore, the average synthetic signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) improves from 4.16 dB to 6.91 dB, highlighting the model's generalization capability and practical value. This approach offers a reliable solution for automatic camera parameter tuning in UOCC systems, enhancing adaptability and communication reliability in complex underwater scenarios.

title: Fake News Detection via AI Model
authors: Muzna Salim Al-Hattali, Rawad Abdulghafor, Wasin AlKishri, Yousuf Al Husaini, Mohammed Abdulla Salim Al Husaini, Mohammad Abrar
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: The spread of fake news in digital space is a serious challenge to the social stability, citizens trust, and informed citizens. Although manual approaches to fact-checking are useful, they are by their very nature limited in scale and time, which justifies the need to develop automated detection tools. The work introduces a fake news detector based on the idea of deep learning, which utilizes Natural Language Processing (NLP) methods to detect fake news with a high degree of accuracy. To obtain the linguistic and semantic features, such as word frequency, sentiment polarity, and readability indices, a labelled dataset of authentic and forged news articles was pre-processed to obtain them. Several models were tested and one of them was a Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) neural network, which reached the highest classification rate of 92%. The findings show that the model is good at identifying misleading content. Additional improvements in the future will include incorporation of image analysis, user behavior profiling as well as implementation as easy to use web or browser-based tool to make it even more practical in the real world.

title: Empowering Sustainable Agriculture in the UAE: An IoT-Driven Smart Greenhouse
authors: Nedaa Al Barghuthi, Hussain Al-Aqrabi, Jawaher Al Mehrzi, Maitha Yousef
publish: 2025 Advances in Science and Engineering Technology International Conferences (ASET)
abstract: Agriculture and farming are critical sectors that underpin human health, societal development, and economic growth. However, in the United Arab Emirates (UAE), several challenges, such as a dry climate, scarce water resources, and insufficient fertile land, obstruct agricultural output. This research paper proposes an Internet of Things (IoT)-based smart greenhouse monitoring system to address these challenges and promote sustainable agriculture within the UAE. The proposed system leverages IoT technology to create a controlled environment agriculture setting that optimizes indoor parameters, such as light exposure, ventilation, humidity, temperature, and carbon dioxide levels, for optimal plant growth. Sensors, actuators, and a cloud platform are utilized to dynamically adjust environmental factors like humidity, temperature, soil moisture, and lighting, ensuring healthy plant development. This research paper also provides an overview of the current state-of-the-art IoT-based applications in smart greenhouses, highlighting the technology's benefits and opportunities in agriculture. By applying these innovative solutions to the UAE's agricultural challenges, this research aims to contribute to reducing food imports and enhancing food security in the region. Overall, the proposed system offers a promising approach to promoting crop diversity, improving resource efficiency, and supporting sustainable agricultural practices in the UAE.

title: Deep Analysis of Sequential and Parallel CNN-LSTM Architectures for Energy Time Series Forecasting
authors: Jerry I. Teleron, Shem L. Gonzales, Arnel C. Fajardo
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: This study performed a time series forecasting of total energy load using deep learning architectures by comparing sequential and parallel Convolutional Neural Network-Long Short-Term Memory (CNN-LSTM) models. The dataset went through a comprehensive preprocessing, including datetime conversion, extraction of time-based features in hour, day of week, month, and year, and the creation of 24 lagged features for total load. Missing values were handled using forward and backward filling, followed by MinMaxScaler normalization. Input sequences were generated with a 24-step look-back window, and the data was split into 80% training and 20% testing sets before feeding to the Deep Learning models. The sequential CNN-LSTM model processed CNN-extracted features directly into the LSTM layer. For the parallel CNN-LSTM architecture, which featured distinct CNN and LSTM branches, whose outputs were concatenated before feeding into a final dense layer. Evaluation using metrics such as Mean Absolute Error (MAE), Root Mean Squared Error (RMSE), and R-squared (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>) revealed that the parallel CNN-LSTM architecture generally achieved superior percentage difference in performance compared to its sequential counterpart having 5.42 for MAE, 3.37 for RMSE, and .09% for R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>. This implies that allowing CNN and LSTM branches to learn distinct features independently before concatenation and fusion can lead to a more robust representation and improved forecasting accuracy.

title: Agrocare: Iot-Based Crop Recommendation and Automated Irrigation
authors: Arpan Jain, Ali Meyan, Shaili Gupta, Vanshita Verma, Gaurav Dubey, Arti Sharma
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Advanced Computing and Emerging Technologies (ACET)
abstract: With alarming challenges in agriculture, such as unpredictable weather conditions and scarcity of resources, smart farming solutions are integral to crop production in sustainable agriculture.In this work, an IoT system that combines crop suggestion and automated irrigation. The system uses Random Forest to predict the best crops as a function of soil and environmental conditions such as <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{p H}$</tex>, temperature, and nutrients. The Comparative analysis identified that Random Forest had 96 % accuracy and precision 0.95, which was higher compared to the Support Vector Machine (SVM) and K-Nearest Neighbor (KNN) models. The intelligent irrigation module based on realtime soil moisture sensing recorded a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$30-40 \%$</tex> decrease in water wastage compared to conventional irrigation techniques. The field test was shown to produce a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{0. 5 - 1. 2}$</tex> tons / hectare gain, which confirmed the viability of the system in increasing agricultural output, resource use efficiency and sustainability.

title: Deep Learning Approach for the Classification of Chest X-rays in Pneumonia Detection
authors: Omar Abahussain, Nabil Hewahi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: Artificial intelligence is increasingly transforming healthcare diagnostics by enabling rapid and reliable analysis of medical images. Pneumonia remains a leading cause of morbidity worldwide, and early detection through chest X-rays is essential for timely treatment. Manual interpretation is time consuming, subjective, and constrained by radiologist availability, particularly in resource limited settings. This study investigates lightweight convolutional neural networks for pneumonia detection from chest X-rays using transfer learning. First, a MobileNetV2 baseline is developed on the public Kaggle pediatric dataset with standardized preprocessing and augmentation. The baseline experiment reports learning curves and a confusion matrix and achieves 88.0% test accuracy with 96.0% recall for pneumonia, emphasizing clinical sensitivity. To evaluate efficiency and stability under identical conditions, a comparative experiment retrains MobileNetV2 alongside MobileNetV3Small and MobileNetV3Large using the same dataset split, preprocessing, augmentation, and training configuration. The comparative results show that MobileNetV2 attains the highest accuracy and macro F1 score among the three architectures and delivers an average inference time of 0.0068 seconds per image on an NVIDIA GPU with 16 GB VRAM, indicating real time feasibility for screening scenarios. These findings demonstrate that a compact MobileNetV2 model can balance diagnostic performance and computational efficiency for point of care use. The primary limitation is the use of a single dataset. Future work will extend evaluation across additional pneumonia datasets and apply statistical testing to assess generalizability with greater rigor.

title: PKTORA: Integrated PSO-Kmeans for Joint Task Offloading and Resource Allocation in Satellite-Terrestrial Networks
authors: Jialong Xu, Hongxiang Li, Guanxi Li, Jialin Xie, Song Hui
abstract: Satellite networks enhance terrestrial networks, covering remote areas and integrating edge computing and distributed storage for low-latency, high-reliability services. However, these advantages also bring new challenges. In this paper, we introduce a Satellite-Terrestrial Network (STN) with multi-layer local-edge-cloud collaboration, which integrates multi-level resources of geostationary orbit satellites (GEO), low orbit constellations (LEO) and ground users (GUs). We define a problem to jointly optimize offload decisions, user uplink power, and satellite computing resource allocation, aiming to minimize the total system cost. In order to address this optimization problem, we propose an integrated PSO-Kmeans for joint task offloading and resource allocation (PKTORA) algorithm. The simulation results reveal that the proposed algorithm can effectively decrease energy consumption and task delay while maintaining a high task success rate.

title: Dynamic Grouping-Driven Cheating Mitigation Framework for Online Asymmetric Adversarial Games: A False-Positive Resilient Approach
authors: Kai Shi, Xin Li, Hai Yu, Zhiliang Zhu
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Science, and Information Processing (AICSIP)
abstract: Online asymmetric games like Werewolf and Identity <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\boldsymbol{V}$</tex> struggle with a unique form of cheating called collusion, where players secretly share hidden game information using external tools like messaging apps. Unlike traditional cheating methods (e.g., hacking), collusion mixes legitimate teamwork with rulebreaking, making it hard to detect. Current anti-cheat tools often mistake skilled play for cheating or fail to track cross-platform coordination, leading to unfair bans and player frustration. Worse, collusion spreads through social networks, encouraging more cheating and driving honest players away. To address this, we propose a new dynamic grouping framework that focuses on protecting the game community rather than chasing perfect cheat detection. Our solution introduces a Cheating Risk Score (CRS) that evaluates players using their social connections, in-game behavior, and community reports. Instead of banning players, we sort them into three matchmaking groups: Pure (low-risk players), Observation (medium-risk), and Isolation (high-risk), reducing collusion's impact while avoiding harsh punishments. This approach offers a practical way to combat collusion in social deduction games while keeping gameplay fair and enjoyable.

title: Nationwide Assessment of Ambient Fine Particulate Matter Using Lowcost Sensors: Practicality and Feasibility
authors: Noora Khaleel, Michael H. Bergin, Prakash V. Bhave, Thameem A. Razzaq, Siti J. B. M. Jani, Md F. Khan
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Innovations in Engineering, Science and Technology for Sustainable Development (ICEST)
abstract: Urban air quality in the Maldives remains understudied, partly due to a lack of capacity to establish reference air quality monitoring stations. This study assessed the practicality and feasibility of measuring fine particulate matter, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{PM}_{2.5}$</tex>, using low-cost sensors (LCS), BlueSky 8134, across 8 locations in the Greater Male' Region and 9 locations in the other atolls. Sensors were colocated to harmonize the measurements before deployment. Linear regression for the harmonization revealed high inter-unit reliability, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}$</tex> ranging from 0.952 to 0.999. Calibration against a reference station in the neighbouring country, with similar climate conditions, resulted in a moderate to strong correlation (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R^{2}$</tex> of 0.685). Harmonisation and calibration data were used to correct a yearlong dataset from the sensor network. An average of 87% of valid hourly data was obtained. Across the Maldives, the annual average concentration in all the islands exceeded the WHO standard. The highest annual average (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$17.9 \mu ~\mathrm{g} / \mathrm{m}^{3}$</tex>) was observed in the northernmost island, while the lowest <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left(11.1 \mu ~\mathrm{g} / \mathrm{m}^{3}\right)$</tex> was measured in the southernmost island. <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{P M}_{2.5}$</tex> concentrations gradually decrease from north to south, indicating the impact of transboundary pollution. Seasonal impacts are evident across all the sites. Operational challenges faced included accessibility to sites due to the geographical constraints, power outages, and wifi connectivity issues. Despite the challenges, continuously assessing <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{P M}_{\mathbf{2. 5}}$</tex> across the Maldives was achieved using the lowcost sensor network. This study proved the potential of LCS in providing extensive and real-time data to better assess air quality for effective air quality management.

title: Efficient DDoS Detection Under Distribution Shift: Window Summaries, Augmentations, and Chronological Validation
authors: Vikas Shukla, Santosh Kumar Sharma, Kuldeep Pal, Pawan Kumar
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Advanced Computing and Emerging Technologies (ACET)
abstract: Distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) detection at flow level must be accurate under distribution shift and efficient enough for inline operation. We present a deployment-aware pipeline on CIC-DDoS2019 that standardizes heterogeneous columns, engineers compact rates/ratios and TCP-flag features, and groups flows by source and protocol-family into fixed windows that capture burstiness. To address class imbalance and improve generalization, we apply four augmentations: random oversampling, physics-preserving jitter/scale/warp, sequence MixUp, and a class-conditional VAE. Evaluation spans chronological vs. randomized splits and binary vs. multiclass targets, with seven transparent baselines (Linear SVM, kNN, GaussianNB, Decision Tree, Random Forest, Extra Trees, Gradient Boosting) trained on mean-std window summaries. The analysis shows how augmentation interacts with protocol heterogeneity and scenario choice, and establishes strong resource-conscious anchors that future sequence encoders can surpass without relying on optimistic splits. In the hardest temporal multiclass setting, the best macro-F1 is 0.226 with GaussianNB, whereas in the randomized binary setting Gradient Boosting reaches 0.976 accuracy, 0.957 macro-F1, and 0.979 macro average precision.

title: The Collapse of Safety Guardrails in Lightweight Models: A Similarity-Based Study on the Impact of Full-Parameter Fine-Tuning on Safety Alignment
authors: Jianyu Lin, Peng Ran, Xuliang Ning, Lei Chen, Cancan Chen, Kai Yang, Shen He, Li Su
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Science, and Information Processing (AICSIP)
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs), especially small-parameter ones, face safety degradation during finetuning due to semantic similarity between fine-tuning data and upstream safety datasets. Their limited parameters, particularly in full-parameter fine-tuning, make them vulnerable to safety sample replay attacks. This study proposes a framework integrating multi-granularity similarity detection with hierarchical features and contrastive learning, alongside dynamic safety supervision. It adjusts loss weights and learning rates based on real-time similarity monitoring. Evaluations across four small models show superior performance over baselines, ensuring secure deployment of resource-constrained models.

title: Cryptocurrencies and Cybersecurity: A Study of Scope and Association
authors: Abdullah Al Dhaheri, Mohammad Amin Alkrisheh, Tayil Shiyab, Ibrahim Al Nuaimi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Communication, Networking and Services (ICCNS)
abstract: This research addresses the issue of the illicit use of digital cryptocurrencies, considering it one of the most pressing contemporary legal challenges facing legislative systems, particularly given the unique technical features of these currencies-such as encryption, decentralisation, and anonymity. The significance of the study lies in examining the legal impact of cryptocurrencies on cybersecurity and analysing the adequacy of the legal framework in the United Arab Emirates in confronting crimes arising from their use. The study's core problem lies in the absence of a comprehensive legislative framework that regulates the use of cryptocurrencies and limits their exploitation in cross-border crimes, such as money laundering and terrorist financing. It also lies in the technical difficulties of tracking digital transactions and the lack of well-established legal concepts regarding the possession of cryptocurrencies and the liability of those dealing with them. The research adopts an analytical methodology through the study of relevant legal texts, in particular, Federal Decree-Law No. 20 of 2018 on Anti-Money Laundering and Combating the Financing of Terrorism and Illegal Organisations; Federal Law No. 34 of 2021 on Combating Rumours and Cybercrimes; and Law No.4 of 2022 on the Regulation of Virtual Assets in the Emirate of Dubai. The study concludes that the current legislative structure is advanced at the regional level but requires further specialisation and technical flexibility. The study recommends issuing a federal law addressing digital assets, enhancing international cooperation in information exchange, adopting technical solutions such as artificial intelligence to track digital crimes, and equipping the competent authorities with advanced legal tools to regulate transactions in this field.

title: Improving Arithmetic Skills in Students with Intellectual Disabilities Through AI in the UAE
authors: Aisha Issa Al-Shehhi, Mohamed Elsayd El-Zeiny, Ibtehal Mahmoud Aburezeq, Suad Abdalkareem Alwaely
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Communication, Networking and Services (ICCNS)
abstract: This study explored the effectiveness of an artificial intelligence (AI) application in supporting ninth-grade students with mild intellectual disabilities who are integrated into special education classrooms in Ras AI Khaimah, UAE. The aim was to enhance their arithmetic skills. The study sample consisted of six students of the same age and academic level; all enrolled at Well Spring Private School. The participants were divided into two groups: an experimental group (3 students) that received instruction using the AI-based program, and a control group (3 students) that received traditional instruction. The study was conducted during the second semester of the 2025 academic year and consisted of thirty instructional sessions. To assess the impact, both groups were evaluated using a test that measured eight arithmetic skills and the total test score. The results revealed statistically significant differences in favor of the experimental group in the second, fourth, fifth, and sixth skills, as well as in the total test score. No statistically significant differences were found in the remaining skills (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{p}>0.05$</tex>). These findings suggest that the AI-based instructional program had a notable positive impact on the development of arithmetic skills among the students with intellectual disability. Furthermore, the calculated effect sizes demonstrate the practical significance of these results, despite the small sample size.

title: Leveraging IoT Technology Concept for Smart Traffic Lights in Jakarta
authors: Kenny Kenedy, Gunawan Wang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: Jakarta's ongoing problem of traffic congestion is made worse by traditional fixed-time traffic light systems that are unable to adjust to shifting traffic circumstances. An Internet of Things (IoT)-based smart traffic light system that dynamically modifies signal phases in response to current vehicle density is proposed in this study. Sensors are used to gather vehicle counts from each junction approach, which are then analyzed by a microcontroller. Traffic conditions are divided into two categories using a threshold-based judgment system: normal and congested. Green light priority is given to the lane with the greatest number of vehicles during congestion, whereas signals normally run sequentially. A scaled prototype was created and assessed using real-time monitoring and simulation. The findings show that, in comparison to fixed-time signals, the suggested approach increases traffic flow efficiency and decreases vehicle waiting times.

title: Deep Learning Based Framework for Real-Time Detection and Classification of Driver Drowsiness States
authors: Anuj Kumar, Aditya Gupta, Sonam Tyagi, Vishal Jayaswal, Tinku Singh
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Advanced Computing and Emerging Technologies (ACET)
abstract: Road accidents remain a major public safety concern in India, with driver drowsiness identified as one of the leading contributing factors. Fatigue and sleep deprivation significantly increase the likelihood of collisions, particularly among drivers aged 18–45 years, who constitute the majority of road accident victims. Studies indicate that 10–15% of crashes are directly linked to tiredness, highlighting the urgent need for an effective detection mechanism. In this study, we propose a convolutional neural network (CNN)-based real-time driver drowsiness detection system. Initially, the system recognizes faces using the Haar Cascade classifier before using Dlib to identify the areas of the lips and eyes. Features such as the eye opening angle and the mouth's height-to-width ratio are extracted and combined with CNN predictions to classify four states: eyes open, eyes closed, yawning, and not yawning. A dataset of 2890 images was preprocessed through resizing, normalization, and augmentation before being used to train the CNN model. The trained model, consisting of four convolutional layers and two fully connected layers, achieved an accuracy of about 97.93%. Real-time implementation with webcam input demonstrated that the system can reliably recognize drowsiness and trigger timely alerts, outperforming conventional classification techniques.

title: The Impact of Digital Supply Chain on Location-Based Services in The Technology Sector in Jordan: The Mediating Role of Mobile Applications
authors: Ahmad Y. A. Bani Ahmad, Firas Tayseer Ayasrah, Mahmoud Allahham, Wasef Almajali, Khaleel AlArabi, Sheikha AI-Buraiki, Badriya Alsadrani
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Communication, Networking and Services (ICCNS)
abstract: The study investigates the effect of the digital supply chain on LBS in the technology industry in Jordan, with mobile applications as a mediator. While these technologies have seen increasing adoption over the years, little research has examined the contextual effect of these advancements on the effectiveness of LBS. Although previous research has delved into significant factors, including the quality of data, the integration of systems, and technological adaptability, the influence of mobile applications on improving these services has not been as thoroughly investigated. The study proceeds to reflect on the mediating role of mobile applications in digital supply chain efficiency and location-based services (LBS) performance, underscoring the significant aspects of mobile applications to reinforce the effectiveness of digital supply chains and enable seamless service delivery LBS performance via real-time accessibility, operational agility, and service personalization. The results reveal that a successful digital supply chain can considerably improve the performance and responsiveness of location-based services. On top of that, mobile apps facilitate the exchange of information, interaction with users, and decision- making, thus adding an extra layer to the partnership. Drawing from these perspectives, the contribution highlights key recommendations for enhancing the performance of LBS via digital supply chain innovations and mobile application functionalities.

title: Sales Forecasting for the Tile Market Using Time Series and Regression Techniques
authors: Sachini Rathnayake, PPG Dinesh Asanka
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Innovations in Engineering, Science and Technology for Sustainable Development (ICEST)
abstract: The dynamic nature of the tile market requires precise demand forecasting to ensure inventory optimization, cost control, and enhanced customer satisfaction. This study focuses on developing a robust forecasting model that integrates time series and regression techniques to predict tile sales. Utilizing historical sales data, economic indicators such as GDP growth, inflation, and interest rates, and customer preferences, the research evaluates the performance of various machine learning models, including CatBoost, ARIMA, and Gradient Boosting. The results indicate that models integrating multiple variables significantly outperform those relying on individual factors, with Gradient Boosting and CatBoost demonstrating the highest accuracy for both monthly and quarterly forecasts. These findings highlight the value of combining historical sales data, market trends, and economic indicators to achieve reliable sales predictions, thereby enabling more informed decision-making in the tile industry.

title: Modeling and Experimental Validation of Anisotropic Damping in 3C-SiC Resonators for MEMS Applications
authors: Annamaria Muoio, Angela Garofalo, Saverio De Luca, Francesco La Via
publish: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Metrology for Green Technologies, Renewable Energy and Ecological Sustainability (MetroGREENST)
abstract: The simulation of moving or vibrating structures, getting energy loss right makes or breaks the forecast; to accurately capture this dissipative behaviour in practical applications, numerical modeling tools such as finite element analysis (FEA) should be used. This study examines two damping approaches, isotropic and anisotropic implemented within COMSOL Multiphysics environments for 3C-SiC resonators for MEMS applications, such as seismic and volcanic sensing and monitoring. The isotropic model relies on the standard scalar loss factor, while anisotropic damping uses a full symmetric 6×6 matrix, denoted ŋIJ, which captures directional variations in energy dissipation. This study shows that resonators with high internal stress have low damping and isotropic behavior, while wafers with reduced internal stress increased anisotropic dissipation. This suggests that residual stress plays a stabilizing role, suppressing direction-dependent losses. Furthermore, anisotropic damping shifts eigenvalues, alters frequency response curves, and modifies short-time transients.

title: Comparison of Four Classifiers Using Keypoints Technique for BabyPose in-Crib
authors: Neda Khan, Peter Han Joo Chong, Farhaan Mirza
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Micro/Nano Sensors for AI, Healthcare, and Robotics (NSENS)
abstract: Babies aged 6 to 24 months are in a crucial stage of growth and development, engaging in activities such as crawling, walking, twirling, and bouncing. These activities, mainly when performed in a crib, can pose significant health hazards, including potential falls and severe head injuries. This is a concern for parents who prioritize the safety of their children. Our work in this paper is about the safety of babies posing safe or unsafe in-crib by comparing models' accuracy. Our primary objective is to use key points using Human Pose Estimation (HPE) techniques. We aim to evaluate BabyPose by identifying the key points using Mediapipe and feeding them to model the classifiers. Thus, four classifiers, such as Support Vector Machine (SVM), Extreme Gradient Boosting (XGBoost/XGB), Random Forest (RF), and Multilayer Perceptron (MLP), are compatible with trained datasets. Hence, the model achieved an accuracy of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 1} \boldsymbol{\%}$</tex> with Random Forest among all four classifiers based on ADL labeling. This accuracy is improved to 75% when classifiers are modeled using Safe (1) or Unsafe (0) outcome labeling. There is further scope to increase the accuracy of prediction and prevention of potential falls of babies by integrating sensor technology.

title: LLM-Empowered Recommender Systems: Next-Generation Frameworks and Applications
authors: Sudha, Amit Sagu, N.P Singh
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Advanced Computing and Emerging Technologies (ACET)
abstract: This accelerated change in the digital ecosystems has caused recommender systems to evolve to be more than optional utilities, to become core drivers of personalization, affecting how people shop, watch, study, and socialize on e-commerce websites, digital entertainment platforms, online education platforms, and social media feeds. Along with the ever-growing volume, speed, and degree of data, recommender systems have become the critical contact point between human preference construction and massive computational intelligence, converting the flood of information into a consistent and contextuallyaware experience. Expanding upon this growing role, the current work provides a comprehensive investigation of recommender systems in the context of the advances that have been made over recent years, 2022 to 2025, between classical methodological approaches, such as collaborative filtering, content- and knowledge-based modeling, context-aware prediction and graph- or sequence-driven representation learning, and transformative developments in the applications of the LLM, such as cold-start mitigation methods, synthetic data generation, natural-language reasoning, multimodal fusion, agentic planning and privacy-preserving personalization. Representing the resolution of recurrent issues related to sparsity, generalization and adaptability, a hybrid deep-learning model is proposed that combines semantic content features, collaborative latent factors, and fusion-based predictive layers. Strong empirical performance has been shown by experimental validation with the model having a training accuracy of 97.3% and testing accuracy of 94.6% and a significant improvement in NDCG, Recall, and Precision, and a highly decreased RMSE.

title: Chaotic Pseudo-Random Key Generation for AES-Based Image Encryption Using Lorenz Dynamics
authors: Wafaa Al Nassan, Talal Bonny
publish: 2025 Advances in Science and Engineering Technology International Conferences (ASET)
abstract: In this paper, we present a hybrid encryption scheme that integrates a chaotic pseudo-random number generator (PRNG) based on the Lorenz system with the Advanced Encryption Standard (AES) to securely encrypt grayscale images. The proposed method uses the Lorenz attractor to generate a high-entropy key stream by discarding transient dynamics and extracting the first 16 normalized values as the AES encryption key. This approach enhances the unpredictability and security of the encryption process. Experimental results using a standard grayscale image demonstrate the robustness of the proposed system through high entropy (7.9994) of the encrypted image, a structural similarity index (SSIM) near zero between the original and encrypted images, and perfect reconstruction upon decryption. Furthermore, the scheme achieves low computational overhead, making it suitable for real-time image protection. The proposed chaos-enhanced AES encryption method is simple to implement, secure, and computationally efficient, thus offering a promising solution for secure image transmission in sensitive domains such as healthcare and surveillance.

title: Integrating Egyptian Heritage, Medical Tourism, and Technology: A Digital Framework for Sustainable Cultural Development
authors: Yousef Fouad Abdelhakem Abdelkhalek, Abdelrahman Amr Ibrahim Ahmed, Nader Adel Mohamed Ahmed, Mazen Abdallah Ali, Ahmed Ibrahim Kamal, Momen Nabil Ahmed, Dina Darwish
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: By bringing together Egyptian history, medical tourism, and contemporary technology, the objective of this paper is to put up a comprehensive framework that encourages the development of cultural practices that are environmentally responsible. The purpose of this research is to highlight prior studies that have been undertaken on the topic of the preservation of digital heritage, therapeutic tourism in Egypt, and the utilization of new technologies such as virtual reality (VR), augmented reality (AR), and artificial intelligence (AI). The model that has been established aims to promote accessibility, education, and international interaction with Egypt’s cultural and natural aspects by establishing links across a variety of sectors. This will be accomplished through the construction of linkages. The objective of the project is to highlight how digital resources may be utilized to rejuvenate tourism, sustain national identity, and provide support to economic success.

title: Design of a Dual-Band Microwave Antenna for Highly Effective Tumor Ablation
authors: Siqi Liu, Xiue Bao, Yuying Huang, Mariangela Latino, Giovanni Gugliandolo, Nicola Donato, Giovanni Crupi, Weidong Hu
publish: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Metrology for Green Technologies, Renewable Energy and Ecological Sustainability (MetroGREENST)
abstract: A coaxial floating-sleeve antenna, operating at 2.45 and 5.8 GHz in the industrial, scientific, and medical (ISM) band, is proposed for microwave ablation (MWA). The dual resonance is achieved by two slots etched into the outer conductor. The antenna is modeled and tested on the High-Frequency Structure Simulator (HFSS) with a tissue environment. The results of numerical simulation show that the reflection coefficients are −12.8 dB and −29.8 dB at 2.45 and 5.8 GHz, respectively. The simulated specific absorption rate (SAR) patterns of the dual-band antenna confirm its uniform thermal radiation distribution. The different profiles of the ablation areas at each frequency make it a promising candidate for flexible and environmentally friendly tumor therapy with various ablated effects.

title: Study on Coupling Simulation of Transformer Temperature and Electric Field Distribution in High-Altitude Environments
authors: Yueping Yang, Wenlong Liao, Xin Yang, Yu Zhang, Jing Xu, Jian Hao, Zhen Zhao
publish: 2025 4th Asia Power and Electrical Technology Conference (APET)
abstract: The significant diurnal temperature variation in high-altitude areas considerably alters the temperature distribution of transformers and induces electric field distortion. Based on this, a coupling simulation study on the temperature and electric field distribution characteristics of transformers in high-altitude environments is conducted. This study takes a 220 kV oil-immersed transformer as the research object and establishes a coupled simulation model for thermal-fluid field and electric field. Four typical diurnal temperature conditions (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$-15^{\circ} \mathrm{C}, 5^{\circ} \mathrm{C}, 20^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$</tex>, and 35 °C) are designed to analyze the distribution patterns of the thermal-fluid field and electric field. The results indicate that the internal temperature field of the transformer exhibits a significant spatial gradient distribution, and its hotspot temperature shows a strong linear correlation with the ambient temperature. The increase in temperature leads to a decrease in the dielectric constant of insulating paper, causing distortion in the electric field distribution. At 35 °C, the peak electric field modulus is 2.981 <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{kV} / \text{mm}$</tex>, representing an increase of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.021 \text{kV} / \text{mm}$</tex> compared to the condition at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$-\text{1 5}^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$</tex>. This research provides theoretical support for electric field regulation in transformers under high-altitude temperature-varying environments.casings.

title: Risk Assessment of Cascading Failures in Power Systems and Vulnerability Analysis of Key Lines
authors: Xin Hu, Xiyu Wang, Peng Li, Yunzheng Chen
publish: 2025 4th Asia Power and Electrical Technology Conference (APET)
abstract: To address the growing risk of cascading failures in modern power systems and ensure safe, stable grid operation, this paper proposes a probabilistic vulnerability assessment method for power grids based on large-scale Monte Carlo simulation. Using the international standard IEEE 14-bus test system as the research object, 10,000 random initial fault scenarios were simulated to dynamically model the propagation process of system cascading failures and evaluate the operational vulnerability of each transmission line. The results show that lines (5 → 12),(8 → 13), and (2 → 3) are the key vulnerable links in the system, exhibiting the highest comprehensive failure rates during cascading failures. Statistical analysis further reveals that the distributions of system failure scale and load loss exhibit typical heavy-tail characteristics, meaning most fault scenarios have limited impact, but a few high-risk extreme events can trigger large-scale blackouts. The conclusions of this study not only identify the weak links in the test system but also provide a basis for power grid planning, operation, and risk control based on dynamic simulation and probabilistic statistics.

title: PIDArc: Physics-Informed Deaggregated Architecture for Enhanced Aging-Aware Leakage Power Estimation for FinFET Logic Cells
authors: Mohammad Rehan Akhtar, Zia Abbas
publish: 2026 39th International Conference on VLSI Design &amp; 25th International Conference on Embedded Systems (VLSID)
abstract: FinFET technology has emerged as a solution to mitigate the short-channel effects in CMOS technology. However, with continued technology scaling, leakage power is now emerging as a primary contributor to total power consumption. Additionally, aging effects, compounded by process, voltage, and temperature (PVT) variations, significantly degrade leakage behavior over time, resulting in high data variance and posing challenges for conventional machine learning models to generalize aging-induced leakage trends. This paper proposes a physics-informed machine learning architecture based on a deaggregation-based methodology that effectively captures the complex, non-linear relationship between aging and leakage power across PVT, incorporating both NBTI and HCI effects, by leveraging pattern-oriented knowledge. Experimental evaluations on 7 nm and 10 nm high-performance FinFET standard cells demonstrate mean absolute percentage error (MAPE) improvements of 52.71% and 54.44%, respectively, compared to state-of-the-art standalone boosting and deep learning models. The proposed method presents a robust and computationally efficient alternative to traditional simulation-based approaches for agingaware power estimation in digital circuit design.

title: English Please: Evaluating Machine Translation with Large Language Models for Multilingual Bug Reports
authors: Avinash Patil, Siru Tao, Aryan Jadon
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: Accurate translation of bug reports is critical for efficient collaboration in global software development. In this study, we conduct the first comprehensive evaluation of machine translation (MT) performance on bug reports, analyzing the capabilities of DeepL, AWS Translate, and large language models such as ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, LLaMA, and Mistral using data from the Visual Studio Code GitHub repository, specifically focusing on reports labeled with the english-please tag. To assess both translation quality and source language identification accuracy, we employ a range of MT evaluation metrics—including BLEU, BERTScore, COMET, METEOR, and ROUGE—alongside classification metrics such as accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score. Our findings reveal that while ChatGPT (gpt-4o) excels in semantic and lexical translation quality, it does not lead in source language identification. Claude and Mistral achieve the highest F1-scores (0.7182 and 0.7142, respectively), and Gemini records the best precision (0.7414). AWS Translate shows the highest accuracy (0.4717) in identifying source languages. These results highlight that no single system dominates across all tasks, reinforcing the importance of task-specific evaluations. This study underscores the need for domain adaptation when translating technical content and provides actionable insights for integrating MT into bug-triaging workflows. The code and dataset for this paper are available at Github. https://github.com/av9ash/English-Please.
