title: A Pipelined Probabilistic Computing Accelerator for Sparse Max-Cut with Parallel Updating p-Bits
authors: Kiran Magar, Utsav Banerjee
publish: 2026 39th International Conference on VLSI Design &amp; 25th International Conference on Embedded Systems (VLSID)
abstract: Probabilistic computing is an emerging quantuminspired paradigm for solving large-scale computationally hard problems such as combinatorial optimization. Probabilistic computers consist of stochastic units called probabilistic bits (p-bits) which are interconnected to construct probabilistic circuits (p-circuits) based on the parameters of the problem being solved. In this work, we present a high-performance configurable max-cut accelerator which integrates 8192 p-bits on an AMD Virtex UltraScale+ FPGA. We propose an algorithm for grouping sparsely connected p-bits to enable their parallel updates without compromising accuracy. Further, we propose a pipelined architecture to realize this algorithm while balancing critical path delays across pipeline stages and boosting the overall system throughput. Using our FPGA-based hardware accelerator, we are able to efficiently solve large-scale max-cut problems on G-Set benchmark graphs with up to 8,000 nodes. We demonstrate average accuracy close to 99 % across all these standard graphs while achieving the best known solutions for several graphs. Compared to previous hardware implementations of max-cut, our approach offers comparable accuracy and performance while achieving improved FPGA resource utilization.

title: Secure Quantization of Large Language Models Based on Gaussian Naive Bayes Classifier
authors: Wei Zhao, Hong Wen, Wenjing Hou, Juntao Mao, Wenjun Luo, Yuzheng Chen
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Science, and Information Processing (AICSIP)
abstract: This paper proposes a quantitative evaluation framework based on the Gaussian Naive Classifier, which is proposed for the quantitative evaluation of the security of Large Language Model (LLM) under adversarial sample attack. Techniques like principal component analysis (PCA) and linear discriminant analysis (LDA) serve as optimization approaches to enhance the efficiency and stability of classifiers. Through simulation experiments based on the open-source security score dataset, the classifier can effectively reduce the high-dimensional feature data to low latitude and improve the classification efficiency. The experimental results show that under the limited pre-training dataset, the optimized high-Gaussian Naive Bayesian classifier can still give relatively clear classification decision boundaries, providing a reliable solution for the security assessment of LLMs.

title: Efficient Fusion for Multimodal Sentiment Analysis: The DSOAN Approach
authors: Ruiqi Tian, Xin Kang, Kazuyuki Matsumoto, Minoru Yoshida
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Science, and Information Processing (AICSIP)
abstract: Multimodal sentiment analysis seeks to understand human emotions by integrating textual, acoustic, and visual information. However, effectively fusing these heterogeneous modalities while minimizing redundancy and computational overhead remains a key challenge. We propose the Dynamic Sparse Order-Aware Network (DSOAN), a novel and efficient framework for multimodal fusion. DSOAN incorporates three key components: a Modality Order-Aware Module (MOAM) for context-sensitive modality ranking, a Dynamic Modal Selection and Lightweight Fusion (DMSLF) module to fuse only the most informative modalities, and a Residual-Projection-LayerNorm (RPL) block to enhance generalization and training stability. Experiments on CMU-MOSI, CMUMOSEI, and UR-FUNNY show that DSOAN achieves competitive accuracy (e.g., 80.3 % on MOSI) and an F1-score of 83.5 %, outperforming prior state-of-the-art models with fewer parameters and faster inference. Ablation studies further demonstrate the critical roles of MOAM, DMSLF, and RPL in improving performance and robustness.

title: Impact of Microlearning on Learning Performance and Enjoyment in Higher Education
authors: Akshana Wickramasinghe, Kaushalya Yatigammana, Nadeesha Dasanayaka
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Innovations in Engineering, Science and Technology for Sustainable Development (ICEST)
abstract: With the rise of smartphones, social media, and the constant stream of information from platforms, the reduction in attention, especially in higher education, has become a growing concern. In order to address this issue, microlearning has been explored as a technology-based instructional method that offers short learning activities in small, self-contained content that can be easily understood. This study investigated the impact of microlearning modules on undergraduates' learning performance and enjoyment in the “Internet of Things” course module at the University of Kelaniya, Sri Lanka. The research compared two content delivery methods: a YouTube-based microlearning module and a traditional document-based lesson, using a quasiexperimental design with 32 second-year undergraduates. The findings revealed that the microlearning group significantly outperformed the traditional group, demonstrating a meaningful improvement in learning performance. Additionally, the microlearning group reported significantly higher enjoyment, reflecting a notable improvement in student engagement. The study revealed that microlearning positively influenced both learning performance and learning enjoyment. The study further recommended that incorporating microlearning into higher education, especially for complex practical subjects, will illuminate a greater learner satisfaction with sound learner performance and enjoyment in higher education. Therefore, incorporating microlearning into higher education will generate tremendous benefits for the learners in memorizing the learning concepts with sound performance and enjoyment.

title: Global Logistics 4.0 Adoption: Technologies and Influencing Factors Across Economies
authors: K K D K T Yashodara, Ruwan Wickramarachchi, Chathumi Ayanthi Kavirathna
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Innovations in Engineering, Science and Technology for Sustainable Development (ICEST)
abstract: Logistics 4.0 represents the digital transformation of logistics, integrating advanced technologies to build smart, connected, and autonomous supply chains. This study conducts a systematic literature review (SLR) to analyse core technologies, adoption challenges, and influential frameworks for Logistics 4.0. It also evaluates specific adoption patterns across regions. Findings highlight the importance of context-aware strategies, human capital development, and leadership commitment for successful implementation. The study concludes with recommendations for industry, policymakers, and academia to foster inclusive and effective digital logistics transformation. The study also stresses the need for flexible regulations, cross-sector collaboration, and greater investment in cybersecurity and sustainability. These measures will help build scalable and resilient logistics systems that can withstand global disruptions and adapt to future technologies.

title: Emerging Technologies and Smart Models for Precision Agriculture and Rice Cultivation: A Comprehensive Survey
authors: Pankaj Singh, Ajay Rana, Pushpendra Singh
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Advanced Computing and Emerging Technologies (ACET)
abstract: Precision agriculture (PA) uses digital tools and smart systems to improve farming. It applies WSN, AI, ML, IoT, big data, and nanotechnology. These technologies help increase yield, save resources, and cut costs. New models like fog, edge, and SDN add extra support to PA. Rice is a vital food crop grown worldwide. It faces problems such as soil degradation, climate change, and crop diseases. Leaf diseases are common and reduce both yield and profit. AI, image processing, and IoT are used for quick and accurate disease detection. This paper reviews new methods for detecting rice diseases. It groups earlier studies into three areas: artificial intelligence (AI), image processing (IP), and the Internet of Things (IoT). The review also looks at common rice diseases and the datasets used in research. It explains how hyperparameter tuning affects model accuracy. The study indicates that fine-tuning is important for better results. Most of the studies highlights current solutions, research gaps, and future directions using AI, image processing, and IoT for sustainable farming.

title: A Cross-Generational Evaluation of SQL Aggregation Efficiency on Oracle Exadata Engineered Systems
authors: Chaitanya Kulkarni, Chandrashekhar Medicherla, Bharadwaj Vulugundam, Tejas Pravinbhai Patel, Sandeep Shivam, Amit Kumar Padhy
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: This paper presents a comparative performance analysis of SQL aggregate functions across three generations of Oracle Exadata Database Machine: X8M, X9M, and X11M. Utilizing generation-aligned Oracle Database configurations (19c, 23ai, and 26ai), we executed a suite of TPC-H-derived decision support queries covering foundational aggregates (COUNT, SUM, AVG) and advanced OLAP operations (GROUP BY, ROLLUP, CUBE). The test environment was designed to quantify the cumulative impact of platform evolution integrating architectural enhancements such as Smart Scan offloading, Persistent Memory (PMEM), and RDMA over RoCE with database engine optimizations on query execution efficiency. The results reveal substantial generational improvements in response time and CPU offload efficiency. X9M delivered higher throughput through optimized Smart Scan and PMEM integration, while X11M consistently achieved up to 41.7% lower query latency compared to X8M. These gains are attributed to the synergy between high-bandwidth RDMA interconnects and storage server I/O offload rates exceeding 96.4%, which enable near-linear scalability for complex aggregations. These findings provide actionable insights for database architects and performance engineers evaluating Exadata refresh strategies. By quantifying the return on investment (ROI) in analytical workload acceleration and server resource optimization, this study informs planning decisions for mission-critical systems requiring high-throughput, low-latency SQL aggregation.

title: Dual-Loop Feedforward Control for a Hybrid DC Bus AC Line Conditioner Under Wide Input Voltage Fluctuations
authors: Chenlong Zhang, Weimin Wu, Houqing Wang, Xiaodong Lv, Huiyang Zhang, Junling Gao
publish: 2025 4th Asia Power and Electrical Technology Conference (APET)
abstract: To meet the stringent requirements of sensitive electronic equipment for high-quality power amidst wide-range input voltage fluctuations, this paper proposes a dual-loop feedforward control strategy for a transformerless, hybrid DC bus AC line conditioner. This strategy is designed to provide a stable and reliable 220 V AC supply to the load, even amidst significant fluctuations in the input voltage. To validate the proposed scheme, a single-phase system simulation model with a rated power of 2.2 kW and an output voltage of 220 V was developed in MATLAB/Simulink. The simulation results clearly demonstrate that under input voltage conditions of a 195 V sag and a 245 V swell, the proposed topology and control strategy achieve precise voltage regulation, proving the effectiveness and advanced nature of the solution.

title: AI-Based Online Learning Platform With Smart Board & LLM Tools
authors: Sankara Mahalingam M, Nossam Madhan Kumar Reddy, Pakanati Hemanth Reddy, Ramireddy Lakshmi Pujitha, N.Suresh Kumar, Shaik Jafar Sadique
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Advanced Computing and Emerging Technologies (ACET)
abstract: The emerging bloom in the welcome of the eLearning business has led to a number of such issues coming into existence, and involving the lack of interactivity, the lack of personalization, and real-time support of learners. Like usual, existing systems use conventional content delivery systems, thus reducing the chances of interaction among students, hence burdening teachers with grading, content development, and tracking learners. These are the issues that would provide them with a push in the development of an intelligent. and versatile platform that could simulate a digital experience similar to the classroom to a greater level, and is accommodating. The paper suggests an online lesson powered by AI and using Smart Board technology and Large Language Models (LLMs) that would make an online classroom interactive and personalized. On one side, the Smart Board can be used to collaborate in real time and to actively visualize, and on the other side, it is possible to tutor in real time, provide adaptive feedback, and create intelligent content with LLM usage. This is then optimized using multimodal input processing, adaptive analytics, and cloud scale to improve engagement, teaching, learning, and access. There is some emerging evidence to support the idea that Smart Board interactivity and AI powered customization can greatly improve the learning process and become the foundation of the next generation of eLearning.

title: Applications of Artificial Intelligence in Sports: Advances in Biomechanics, Injury Analytics, and Tactical Intelligence
authors: Digvijay Rautela, Kunwar Singh Vaisla
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Advanced Computing and Emerging Technologies (ACET)
abstract: Artificial Intelligence (AI) is rapidly transforming the sports industry by enhancing performance analysis, injury prevention, tactical planning, and fan engagement. Advances in machine learning, computer vision, and data science provide deep insights into player behavior, game dynamics, and audience interaction. AI-driven systems analyze real-time and historical data to evaluate player movements, biometrics, and decisions, helping coaches optimize training and performance. Wearable sensors and video tracking powered by AI enable precise feedback tailored to individual athletes. In injury prevention, predictive analytics assess risk factors from biomechanical and physiological data, allowing early interventions that improve athlete longevity and reduce costs. Tactically, AI supports coaches in analyzing opponent strategies, simulating game scenarios, and making informed real-time decisions. This is especially impactful in complex sports like soccer, basketball, and football. Additionally, AI enhances fan experiences through personalized content and interactive technologies. Despite its potential, AI also brings challenges such as data privacy, ethics, and algorithmic bias. This paper reviews current applications of AI in professional and amateur sports, highlights recent innovations, addresses key concerns, and outlines future research directions aimed at responsible and effective <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$A I$</tex> integration in sports.

title: From Idea to Impact: Leveraging Disruptive Technologies for Scalable Entrepreneurial Solutions
authors: Priyanka Chauhan, Sunny Prakash, Sanjay Kumar, Shweta Saxena, Richa Sharma
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Advanced Computing and Emerging Technologies (ACET)
abstract: The paper describes the business-based framework to turn high-potential ideas at the early stages into scalable entrepreneurial solutions based on the incorporation of disruptive technologies at each innovation lifecycle phase. The given model has brought together a high-velocity idea generation/low-code prototyping, AI-powered market assessment, and cloud-native deployment, to speed up the time to market and low risks during venture creation. The use of tools like generative AI, predictive analytics, and microservice architectures will help the system be able to empower entrepreneurs with the ability to validate user needs, resource planning, and scale the product with minimal overheads. The framework was actually introduced in pilot projects in the fintech, edtech and cleantech industries, and it showed drastic impact on the development velocity, user involvement, implementation effectiveness. The outcomes hold validity with regard to the viability of a tech-first data-driven method of the entrepreneurial process which can be effectively utilized as a repeatable projectile that can take a concept to a market-ready impact.

title: Machine Learning Framework for Efficiency and System Optimization in Solar-Powered Absorption Cooling Systems
authors: Naif Khalaf Al-Shammari, Abubakar D. Maiwada, Fahad Jibrin Abdu, Jamilu Usman, Muhammad Sani Gaya, Sani I. Abba
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Sustainable Energy and Net-Zero Emissions (SENZE)
abstract: The growing demand for sustainable cooling technologies has motivated the integration of solar energy into absorption systems. The study focuses on predicting the coefficient of performance (COP) of a solar-powered vapor absorption refrigeration system (VARS) integrated with latent heat energy storage, designed for the climatic conditions of Riyadh, Saudi Arabia. The input parameters considered were incident solar radiation (Ir), evaporator cooling capacity (Qe), absorb efficiency (effa), and heat exchanger load (Qhx), while COP served as the system's output variable. A comprehensive dataset of 8,760 hourly records was analyzed using statistical and machine learning methods. Preprocessing steps included the treatment of missing values, retention of zero values, and dimensionality reduction via Principal Component Analysis (PCA). Correlation analysis revealed strong interdependencies among Ir, Qe, and Qhx, while highlighting absorber efficiency as the most influential factor governing COP. The Random Forest (RF) regression model was trained with 70 % of the data and tested on the remaining 30 %. Results showed outstanding predictive performance, with RMSE values of 0.00032 (training) and 0.00043 (testing), and MAPE values below 0.01 %. The findings confirm the potential of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R F}$</tex> as a reliable predictive tool for system optimization, offering accurate forecasting and practical insights for improving absorber and heat exchanger performance.

title: Composable AI Stack for Intelligent Agents: Modular Orchestration Using Context Routing, Memory, and Tools
authors: Angshuman Rudra, Manan Agrawal
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: The rise of composable AI agents has transformed automation orchestration across diverse domains. Rather than relying on monolithic pipelines, these agents enable modular workflows using routing, memory, and tool APIs for real-time task execution. This paper introduces a Composable AI Stack demonstrated through marketing automation, with generalizability to finance, healthcare, industrial automation, and intelligent transportation. The architecture employs memory-augmented agents, context-aware routing, and API function-calling to manage insights, personalization, and performance optimization. We present comprehensive ablation studies isolating each module’s contribution, scalability benchmarks for enterprise deployment, and governance considerations for responsible AI. Experimental evaluation confirms significant improvements: CTR +38%, CPA -24%, and ROAS +48% over monolithic alternatives, with zero system failures compared to 9% crash rate in baseline systems.

title: Investigating Student Perceptions of Conversational Recommender Systems for Academic Major Selection
authors: Abeer Khaled Dhaher Alfadhli, Abdel-Rahman Hedar, Mamdouh F. Mohamed, Mostafa K. Osman
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: Selecting an appropriate academic major is a critical decision that shapes future career trajectories of students. Despite its importance, many first-year students struggle with inadequate guidance during their transition to higher education. This study investigates student perceptions on the potential impact of Conversational Recommender Systems (CRS) on academic decision-making processes. A quantitative approach was used using a structured questionnaire administered to 395 newly enrolled students at Kuwait University during the 2023-2024 academic year. Linear regression analysis reveals a statistically significant positive relationship between student’ perceptions of CRS capabilities and their confidence in selecting appropriate majors (R = 0.329, R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> = 0.109, p < 0.001). The findings indicate that while students view intelligent conversational systems as valuable tools for enhancing academic guidance, these systems are most effective when utilized as complementary support to traditional human advising methods. The study contributes empirical evidence supporting the integration of AI-driven guidance technologies in higher education while emphasizing the continued importance of human advisor involvement.

title: Optimization of Nickel Contents for Ni-Based Liquid Metal Strain Sensors
authors: Jing Zhang, Xiaoyang Zou, Ziqi Li, Colin Pak Yu Chan, King Wai Chiu Lai
publish: 2025 IEEE 18th International Conference on Nano/Molecular Medicine &amp; Engineering (NANOMED)
abstract: Liquid metals (LM) exhibit high electrical conductivity and excellent ductility, making them highly promising for applications in flexible, stretchable, and wearable sensors. This paper proposes a novel method to overcome the challenge of patterning liquid metals on flexible substrates, which is hindered by their high surface tension. By adding Ni particles to the LM, the surface tension is reduced while maintaining its flowability, enabling easy patterning on flexible substrates. When <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$12 \text{wt}. {\%} \text{Ni}(40 \mu \mathrm{m})$</tex> is added to LM, a gauge factor (GF) of 5.17 can be achieved at 300%. In addition, the mechanism analysis of different GF resulting from varying Ni contents from 0 to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$20 \text{wt}. {\%}$</tex> was investigated. The strain sensor with a Ni content of 12 wt.% was subjected to a cyclic performance test of 1000 cycles at 150% strain. The strain sensor proposed in this paper, which adopts a magnet-patterned LM structure, has significant application potential in the field of wearable robots.

title: Opportunities and Challenges of Solar-Wind-Powered Hydrogen in Emerging Economies: A Spatial and Techno-Economic Analysis
authors: Sassi Rekik, Imed Khabbouchi, Dorra Lounissi, Mohamed Khaleel, Delmaria Richards, Lioua Kolsi
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Sustainable Energy and Net-Zero Emissions (SENZE)
abstract: This study evaluated the spatial and technoeconomic feasibility of green hydrogen large-scale production from hybrid wind-solar energy systems in Tunisia by multi-criteria decision-making based on GIS. <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$15.91 \%\left(21,185 ~\text{km}^{2}\right)$</tex> of Tunisia is suitable for hybrid wind-solar energy systems, of which <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1,110 ~\text{km}^{2}$</tex> is within areas of exceptional suitability in the central western, south western, south eastern, and coastal areas of Tunisia. Techno-economic modeling using site data from Skhira shows that a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0 ~ M W}$</tex> photovoltaic solar plant (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 0. 5 \%}$</tex> capacity factor) and a wind farm (30.9 % capacity factor) can produce approximately <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1,800,722 ~\text{kg}$</tex> of green hydrogen annually. The wind power infrastructure amounts to the vast majority of capital investment (66.14 % of the total $56.66 million USD), followed by solar PV systems (17.64%) and infrastructure for electrolyzer components <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(13.76 \%)$</tex>. The Levelized Cost of Hydrogen is calculated to be <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\$ 3.96 / \text{kg}$</tex>, which is competitive with other countries in the MENA region. The results of the sensitivity analysis show that the most critical parameters that affect LCOH are system efficiency, capital recovery factor, and wind CAPEX. A cash flow analysis shows substantial economic feasibility, as indicated by a discounted payback time of approximately seven years and four months. The findings indicate that Tunisia can be a participant in the regional green hydrogen market.

title: A Novel Latch Reset Technique Enabling Sub- $6 \mu ~\mathrm{W}$ Operation in Double-Tail Comparator
authors: Aditya Dubey, Sunil Rathore, Navjeet Bagga
publish: 2026 39th International Conference on VLSI Design &amp; 25th International Conference on Embedded Systems (VLSID)
abstract: The comparators are crucial components in data converters, e.g., analog-to-digital converters (ADCs), sensors, and control systems to enable rapid binary decisions derived from specific thresholds in analog signals. This paper proposes a novel latch reset technique for a double-tail comparator to attain lowpower operations. To reset the latch, the additional pull-up (PMOS) transistors are included in the pre-amplifier stage, which are switched by the derived clock signal (MCLKB) from the baseline clock (CLK). The delayed version, i.e., MCLKB, resets the latch to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$V_{D d}$</tex>. Therefore, the proposed method enhances the energy efficiency of the comparator by minimizing dynamic power consumption. Using optimized timing and control of the latch stage, the simulation has been performed for the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{4 5 ~ n m}$</tex> CMOS process, demonstrating that the comparator achieves a power consumption of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$5.85 \mu ~\mathrm{W}$</tex> at a 1 V supply. Monte-Carlo simulation has been done for 200 samples, showing the average power distribution with a standard deviation of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.8 \mu ~\mathrm{W}$</tex>. The effective layout area of the proposed design is <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$60 \mu ~\mathrm{m}^{2}$</tex>. Thus, the proposed technique is needed to design the low-power analog front ends.

title: BHARAT-TPM: Micro-Architecture Design of Scalable Hardware Random Number Generator for a RISC-V Trusted Platform Module
authors: Soumya G Hosmani, Raja Sekar K, Aneesh Raveendran, Vivian Desalphine, Hari Babu P, S D Sudarsan
publish: 2026 39th International Conference on VLSI Design &amp; 25th International Conference on Embedded Systems (VLSID)
abstract: Trusted Platform Modules (TPMs) are widely used in modern digital systems to provide a Root-of-Trust and support for various security applications. Random number generation plays a crucial role in secure applications such as message authentication, digital signatures, pseudo-random number generation, and cryptographic key derivation. Dedicated hardware accelerators for random number generation enhances the performance of cryptographic operations in TPMs. With the rise of RISC-V open-source architectures, integrating cryptographic accelerators into TPMs is vital for secure embedded designs. In this context, this paper presents a high-performance, AXI enabled Random Number Generator (RNG) hardware accelerator is integrated into an indigenous RISC-V based, named BHARAT-TPM. The RNG micro-architecture has been validated for compliance with NIST SP800-90B and supports three output widths (128, 256, and 512 bits). The accelerator interfaces with the system through AXI-4 compliant control and data registers, ensuring seamless integration within RISC-V-based TPMs. The RNG accelerator is implemented in Verilog RTL and verified with NIST test vectors. The proposed accelerator is integrated with the BHARAT-TPM is prototyped on Xilinx Virtex UltraScale VCU118 FPGA devices and validated for TCG 2.0 commands. The proposed RNG accelerator achieves a frequency of 370 Mhz, demonstrating and suitability for high-speed cryptographic operations in TPM based security applications.

title: Bridging the Gaps in Distributed Deadlock Detection
authors: Surendra Kumar Shukla, Vishan Kumar Gupta, Paras Jain, Tarandeep Kaur Bhatia, Devanshi Ramani, Riju Patidar
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Advanced Computing and Emerging Technologies (ACET)
abstract: The need for performance in today's computer systems has led to the use of automatic. The problem of identifying and handling deadlocks in distributed systems is rather unsolved, given the fact that many processes operate concurrently during their run time, accessing resources of other nodes. Although several algorithms have been developed over the years, a critical issue remains: the latter most do not have strict formal validation, which means that errors and low performance are probable. Based on the literature, this paper provides an overview of the existing techniques for deadlock detection and identifies the research lacuna. Some of these gaps are: The lack of formal correctness proofs, Performance analysis that is mostly done with message counts and Forgetfulness of real-world characteristics. Furthermore, the present literature lacks enough research regarding deadlock detection or at least the solutions to the problem. This work therefore reviews related work in several areas and discusses the lack of viable techniques for testing real systems in the following areas of distributed databases, multithreaded applications, and object systems. In this paper, we utilize temporal logic to construct a formal verification approach for proving the precision of deadlock identification procedures. In addition, we consider ASTs as a value-added solution in identifying deadlock issues in multithreaded program development, based on source code analysis. This review intends to guide future research to support the creation of stronger and flexible deadlock detection and prevention solutions that must address existing modern distributed systems.

title: Priv-DATA: Privacy-Aware Dataset Anonymization Tool for Autonomous Vehicles Research
authors: Abdulrahman Ahmad, Abdelmoamen Nasser, Nadya Abdel Madjid, Murad Mebrahtu, Bilal Hassan, Jorge Dias, Majid Khonji
publish: 2025 Advances in Science and Engineering Technology International Conferences (ASET)
abstract: The release of large-scale autonomous vehicles (AV) datasets has become essential for advancing perception models capable of generalizing across diverse environments. However, visual data captured in public settings often contains identifiable information about pedestrians and vehicle owners, raising privacy concerns and compliance issues with data protection regulations. Since AV datasets are inherently non-consensual, anonymization becomes a necessary step before public data distribution. In this paper, we introduce Priv-DATA, an open-source tool for AV dataset anonymization. It supports: (1) Automatic anonymization of privacy-sensitive content (e.g., faces, license plates) using state-of-the-art detection models with Gaussian blurring in a systematic way that while anonymization, it minimizes any resultant visual artifacts; and (2) User-friendly validation via a GUI for manual correction and artifact removal. We validate Priv-DATA on the Emirates Multi-task dataset and discuss challenges in balancing privacy and data utility. The code is publicly available on GitHub<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</sup><sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</sup>https://github.com/AV-Lab/Dataset_Anonymization.

title: HC-MBNet: A Hybrid Collaborative Multi-Branch Network for Spatiotemporal Gastrointestinal Disease Classification
authors: Muhammad Abdullah, Tanzila Kehkashan, Faheem Akbar, Roko Milošević, Nikola Ivković, Adnan Akhunzada
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: Gastrointestinal diseases are a critical health issue in the world with an estimated 2.8 million cancer cases being diagnosed each year and the need to have reliable early disease detection methods. The current classification methodologies usually focus on either the spatial or the temporal attributes isolated, thus ignoring what a combination of multi-scale spatiotemporal information and collaborative learning paradigm can offer. To this end, this paper presents a new architecture, the Hybrid Collaborative Multi-Branch Network (HC-MBNet), that has spatial attributes, time dynamics, and long-range contextual relationships to comprehensively classify gastrointestinal diseases. HC-MBNet consists of four professional branches, which process shared ConvNeXt backbone features, which further adapt distinct spatial-attention modules, time-shift operations, dual-pathway multi-scale streams, and transformer encoders designed to exchange features through cross-branch attention. Significant testing on the HyperKvasir video sampling shows that HC-MBNet has a classification probability of 99.3, which is better than state-of-the-art approaches both in precision and recall. The model also provides clinically interpretable predictions based on attention-based visualizations, thus creating a reliable computer-aided diagnostic tool that can be used in endoscopy centers in the real-world.

title: A Novel Speckle-Aware Dynamic Vision Transformer Deep Learning Model for Retinal Disease Detection
authors: Vedaant Agarwal, Yelena Yesha
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: Visual impairment affects over 2.2 billion people globally and results in an estimated $3 trillion in economic loss, with retinal diseases such as Choroidal Neovascularization (CNV), Diabetic Macular Edema (DME), and Drusen contributing significantly. Optical Coherence Tomography (OCT) is the standard for retinal imaging, however its diagnostic potential is hindered by speckle: an interference pattern caused by coherent light scattering within heterogeneous retinal tissue. Most existing deep learning models treat speckle as noise and suppress it, often at the cost of losing biologically relevant information. To address this, we propose a Speckle-Aware Dynamic Vision Transformer (SA-DVT), a novel deep learning model that leverages speckle as a diagnostic feature rather than suppressing it. The model integrates a robust speckle map generator with a learnable gating mechanism that dynamically blends structural and speckle content. This enhanced representation is then passed to a modified Swin Transformer backbone adapted for single-channel OCT inputs, enabling classification of CNV, DME, Drusen, and Normal scans. The SA-DVT was evaluated across seven public OCT datasets and a balanced 60,000-image split. It achieved over 96% accuracy with high per-class precision and recall. These results demonstrate strong generalizability across varied imaging conditions and diseases. SA-DVT offers a biologically informed and clinically robust solution for retinal disease classification from a single OCT scan by treating speckle as signal rather than noise.

title: A Two-Step Model-Free Predictive Current Control Strategy for a Four-Level NNPC Inverter Based on Sliding Mode Observer
authors: Yusheng Fang, Xianyang Cui, Baichuan Ye, Yilu Wang, Minxin Lin, Tao Jin
publish: 2025 4th Asia Power and Electrical Technology Conference (APET)
abstract: To address the issues of heavy computational burden and high sensitivity to system parameters in traditional Model Predictive Control (MPC) for four-level NNPC (Neutral-Point Piloted Clamping) inverters, this paper proposes a Model-Free Predictive Current Control (MFPCC) strategy based on a sliding mode observer. This approach effectively eliminates the reliance on accurate model parameters. The proposed method first establishes an ultra-local model and uses a sliding mode observer to estimate the lumped disturbance of the four-level NNPC inverter. A two-stage vector optimization method based on a logical function is then developed for the subsequent vector selection, which simultaneously achieves the control objectives of current tracking and DC-link capacitor voltage balancing without the use of weighting coefficients. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed strategy exhibits high robustness and can operate properly even with changes in model parameters, showing significant advantages over conventional methods and proving its feasibility and effectiveness.

title: Trusted Medical Imaging: A Blockchain Enabled Deep Learning Framework for Secure Healthcare
authors: Hunar Sajjan Agrawal, Vaibhav Bhatnagar, Ashish Kumar, Kanika Singla
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Advanced Computing and Emerging Technologies (ACET)
abstract: The evolution of medical imaging technologies has significantly improved clinical outcomes by enabling early and precise diagnosis through modalities such as MRI, CT, PET, and X-ray. However, the rapid growth of high dimensional imaging data has raised fundamental challenges involving data security, centralized storage bottlenecks, regulatory compliance, and privacy. Storage architectures are inadequate to provide the desired levels of auditability and resilience and leave sensitive diagnostic information exposed to breaches. At the same time, deep learning has emerged as a backbone for medical image analysis, outperforming other approaches in disease classification, segmentation, and anomaly detection. While strong, traditional AI models tend to exist in silos without interoperability, traceability of data, and trust. To overcome these weaknesses, this paper suggests a single, decentralized platform that leverages AI powered diagnostic analysis with blockchain based data governance. Architectures like Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs), Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs), and Transformers are used to carry out image enhancement, classification, and report generation. Blockchain integration uses smart contracts, hashing, and IPFS for decentralized storage, providing tamper proof audit trails, access control, and immutable records of consent. Through combining leading edge AI analytics with verifiable, decentralized storage, the proposed framework provides end-to-end trust, transparency, and compliance for medical imaging applications.

title: A Multi-Factor Fusion Network With Transfer Learning for Magnetic Core-Loss Prediction
authors: Longsheng Xu, Liping Zhang, Kaiming Chen, Xie Jingyi, Lebin Wang, Yu Chen, Tingfeng Shao, Junmin Hu
publish: 2025 4th Asia Power and Electrical Technology Conference (APET)
abstract: Accurate prediction of magnetic core loss is crucial for evaluating and optimizing magnetic components in power converters. However, many models struggle to capture coupled relationships among flux density, frequency, temperature, and waveform, which limits generalization. This paper proposes a multi-factor fusion network comprising a Transformer encoder for single-cycle flux-density features and a fusion-projection head that aligns environmental scalars (temperature, frequency) with encoded representations for sequence-to-scalar regression. For unseen materials, we adopt transfer learning with multi-stage fine-tuning to adapt a model pretrained on known materials. Experiments on public (MagNet) data and laboratory measurement datasets with limited samples for soft-ferrite cores under zero DC bias show that, compared with LSTM and plain Transformer baselines, the proposed approach yields lower mean relative error (MRE) and 95th-percentile error, and maintains stable accuracy on unseen materials within the studied operating regime, demonstrating practical value for engineering applications.

title: Enhancing the Surface Properties of Bacterial Cellulose for Sustainable Electronic Applications
authors: Francesca Pescosolido, Mattia Scagliotti, Matteo Rapisarda, Luigi Mariucci, Antonio Valletta
publish: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Metrology for Green Technologies, Renewable Energy and Ecological Sustainability (MetroGREENST)
abstract: Bacterial cellulose (BC) is a promising, green, biocompatible polysaccharide that can be used to produce flexible, eco-friendly electronic devices. However, its high surface roughness and porosity can hinder uniform deposition of the material, which compromises device fabrication and performance. These limitations have been addressed by combining mechanical treatments with the application of natural polymeric coatings. Additionally, a chemical cleaning process is introduced to remove residual compounds such as NaOH, chalk, and CaCO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf>, which are typically used during BC sheet processing. The effectiveness of the treatments was evaluated by using FT-IR spectroscopy, optical microscopy, and SEM/EDX analysis. Finally, inkjet printing is employed to assess the enhanced printability of the treated BC, showcasing its viability as a substrate for printing electronic components.

title: An Optimized DenseNet-201 Model in Classifying Cocoa Pod Maturity using Bayesian Optimization
authors: Hermoso J. Tupas, Ariel M. Sison, Ruji P. Medina
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: The timely and accurate identification of cocoa pod maturity is a critical determinant of crop yield and quality; however, traditional manual inspection is inefficient and inconsistent. Deep learning offers a non-destructive solution, yet the efficacy of Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) is strictly limited by suboptimal hyperparameter configurations. To address this, this study uses Bayesian Optimization to automate the hyperparameter search for a DenseNet-201 backbone, targeting optimal classification accuracy on the CocoaMFDB dataset. The proposed framework successfully identified a streamlined architecture that achieved a classification accuracy of 99.20%, significantly surpassing the 80.00% accuracy of the baseline model. Furthermore, comparative analysis revealed that the optimized DenseNet-201 outperformed standard pre-trained networks such as MobileNetV2, ResNet-50, and VGG16. These results validate Bayesian Optimization as a powerful tool for reducing computational overhead while maximizing diagnostic precision in precision agriculture.

title: SmartFactCheckBot: A Dual-Interface AI Platform for Real-Time Misinformation Detection with Explainable Predictions
authors: Baskaran Jeyarajan, Vigneshwaran Jagadeesan Pugazhenthi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: The rapid spread of misinformation across digital platforms has created a critical need for accessible, real-time fact-checking tools. Existing fact-checking systems are typically web-only, limited to research prototypes, or lack public accessibility and real-time responsiveness. This paper presents SmartFactCheckBot, a dual-interface AI misinformation detection platform deployed through a Telegram bot and a web-based interface. The system uses a DistilBERT classifier fine-tuned on the Kaggle Fake News Classification dataset and incorporates an explainability engine to increase public trust. The backend is deployed on an AWS EC2 t2.small instance in us-east-1, with a production-grade stack including FastAPI, Nginx, systemd services, and HTTPS. Real-world usage analytics collected via Google Analytics 4 (GA4) and Amazon CloudWatch show 125 bot users and 135 unique web users during the initial deployment phase. Experimental results demonstrate an accuracy of 94.7%, F1-score of 94.1%, and an inference time of 72 ms, making the system suitable for public, real-time use. SmartFactCheckBot demonstrates a practical, scalable AI solution to support society in mitigating misinformation and serves as an open-source public-benefit technology.

title: Comparative Evaluation of Vision-Language Models in Skin Diseases Recognition
authors: Alexandr Ovsyannikov, Maksim Airapetian, David Barboza, Noor Jamal Alkhateeb, Luqman Ali
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: Generative AI models have seen remarkable advancements with innovations in data comprehension and reasoning. Vision-Language Models (VLMs) offer new opportunities for dermatology screening through zero-shot classification. This study provides a comparative benchmark for nine modern VLMs in skin disease classification and highlights key limitations that must be addressed before reliable clinical deployment. Three prompt-engineering strategies and two dataset variations (original and brightness-augmented images) were used to assess the models’ robustness to context and visual noise. Results show that Gemini 2.5 Pro consistently outperforms all other models, while smaller and GPT-based models display reduced accuracy and sensitivity to lighting variations. Prompt refinement improves prediction stability across multiple models, confirming the importance of well-structured instructions in zero-shot dermatology tasks.

title: A Review of Various Buoyancy Control Mechanisms for Underwater Vehicles
authors: Karim I. Sadek, Mohamed A. Elwahsh, Nesma K. Shaaban, Mohamed M. NoorEldeen, Ahmed N. Donia, Omar A. Mohamed, Seif A. Alsayed, Hazem A. Sayed, Ahmed M. Hamza, Irene S. Fahim, Ahmed G. Radwan, M. Hamoud
publish: 2025 Advances in Science and Engineering Technology International Conferences (ASET)
abstract: The exploration and monitoring of marine environments are of paramount importance due to their potential for resource discovery, territorial delineation, and ecological preservation. These tasks present inherent challenges, particularly in extreme environments, where human intervention is impractical. Consequently, the scientific community increasingly relies on unmanned underwater vehicles, especially autonomous ones, for these purposes. Recent advancements in buoyancy engine technology have garnered significant attention due to their potential to enhance the energy efficiency and operational capabilities of these vehicles. This paper provides a comprehensive review of recent developments in buoyancy engines across various types, with a specific focus on methods that offer low energy consumption and high efficiency. The primary objective is to offer insights that can guide future developments in underwater robotics, enabling more effective ocean exploration and monitoring efforts. The review encompasses a comparative analysis of different buoyancy engine types, including water intake-discharge, pneumatic, hydraulic, thermal, hybrid-powered, and Reversible Fuel Cell systems. Each type is evaluated based on its strengths, weaknesses, and suitability for different operational scenarios.

title: Unmanned Air Vehicles for Coastal Surveillance: A Review of Design and Environmental Constraints in the Maldives
authors: Yoosuf Nizam
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Innovations in Engineering, Science and Technology for Sustainable Development (ICEST)
abstract: This study presents a modeling and feasibility analysis of solar-electric Unmanned Air Vehicles (UAVs) for coastal surveillance in the Maldives. A model UAV design is developed and evaluated using simulations of endurance, coverage, and environmental constraints, including local wind, solar irradiance, humidity, and salt aerosol exposure. Results indicate that solar-assisted fixed-wing UAVs can achieve approximately <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3 0}-\mathbf{3 6}$</tex> hours of endurance under typical Maldivian irradiance conditions. Coverage analysis suggests that a coordinated fleet of six UAVs could provide nearcontinuous monitoring of the Maldivian Exclusive Economic Zone (EEZ). Physical prototypes are under construction, and flight testing is planned to validate the modeled predictions. The study provides a framework for UAV-based coastal surveillance and design guidelines for tropical island environments.

title: Multi-Context Protocol (MCP) Hosts provide secure and real-time integration of AI tools
authors: VasanthRao Jadav
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: Real-time intelligent workflows require AI agents to securely interact with external tools, REST APIs, and data sources—capabilities that static, model-only systems cannot provide. This paper presents a secure and modular framework built on the Multi-Context Protocol (MCP), enabling AI agents to execute external tools through permission-gated and auditable workflows. The architecture integrates MCP Clients, Servers, and Hosts, coordinated via an API Gateway enforcing TLS 1.2, OAuth 2.0, and enterprise authentication.An MCP Inspector validates tool behavior prior to deployment, while the MCP Host interprets user intent, selects the appropriate tool, and combines tool outputs with embedded LLM-generated reasoning. The system supports internal scripts, enterprise services, and third-party APIs, with all executions requiring explicit user approval.Demonstrations using a real-time Weather Tool and File Explorer Tool highlight dynamic tool discovery, secure execution, and LLM-assisted response generation. Results show that MCP enables scalable, secure, and compliant AI-agent workflows, outperforming conventional agent frameworks by preventing unauthorized actions and minimizing data leakage through built-in guardrails.

title: A Comparative Analysis of Machine Learning Models for Chronic Kidney Disease Prediction
authors: Hussain AlSalman
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: Chronic Kidney Disease (CKD) is an urgent and most serious health problems in the world, as it usually grows silently until it reaches an advanced stage. For effective treatment and improved patient outcomes, early detection and timely intervention are required. Machine learning (ML) algorithms show potential in CKD detection, but their performance on detection and prediction regarding the nature of clinical data features needs to be explored. In this paper, a comparative analysis approach is proposed for chronic kidney disease prediction using a number of machine learning models. The approach develops and investigates seven ML methods, which are Support Vector Machine (SVM), Logistic Regression (LR), K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN), Decision Tree (DT), Random Forest (RF), Naive Bayes (NB), and Deep Neural Network (DNN). Unlike a lot of previous studies, all methods are trained and evaluated using a standardized analytical pipeline for certifying fair comparison. A public medical dataset, containing various categorical and numerical attributes of patients, is used to conduct the experiments of the study. For validating the stability of developed models and generalizing their performance, a 10-fold cross-validation and a 70:30 train-test split are applied and assessed based on several metrics, including accuracy, F1-score, recall, precision, and Area Under the Receiver Operating Characteristic (ROC) Curve (AUC). The experimental results reveal that LR achieves the highest overall Macro Avg. F1-score result, followed by DNN and NB. The findings of the research indicates that simpler LR model can be highly effective due to linear separability nature of CKD dataset features, contributing to growing the body of ML research CKD deployment and screening.

title: Machine Learning Enhanced High-Utility Sequential Pattern Mining
authors: Gunjan Saxena, Rajiv Kumar, Yajnaseni Dash
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Advanced Computing and Emerging Technologies (ACET)
abstract: A significant subfield of data mining called HighUtility Sequential Pattern Mining (HUSPM) finds useful patterns in datasets where each item has a utility value, such as weight, relevance, or profit. Two major issues with traditional HUSPM algorithms are that they produce a lot of repetitive patterns or lose important information as a result of aggressive pruning. A machine-learning-based HUSPM system is presented in this study, which labels mined patterns based on a specified utility threshold and transforms them into numerical feature vectors based on utility statistics. After that, a Support Vector Machine (SVM) classifier is trained to differentiate between patterns of high and low utility. The suggested approach reduces redundancy and improves scalability while achieving stable accuracy, according to experiments conducted on the SIGN dataset. A feasible path to creating effective and intelligent data analysis systems is the combination of machine learning and traditional HUSPM methodologies. In our future work, we hope to expand the framework with additional datasets, sophisticated models such as LSTM and Transformers, and experiments in noisy environments to evaluate resilience because we think this approach presents promising prospects.

title: Design and Manufacture of CNC Foam Cutting Machine
authors: Laith Al-Juboori, Fatma Rashed Mohamed, Shouq Ahmed Abdulla, Mariam Mohammed Saleh, Fatma Rashed Salem, Marwa Saeed Abdulla
publish: 2025 Advances in Science and Engineering Technology International Conferences (ASET)
abstract: This work designs and manufactures a prototype of lowcost CNC Hot Wire Foam Cutting Machine to fabricate accurate and customized 3D foam shapes. A 3D printing machine used to print many elements of designed CNC cutting machine. Three stepper motors are used to control (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{X}, \mathrm{Y}, \mathrm{Z}$</tex>) axis. The system cuts 3 cm thick using a temperature of 200° C and with a speed of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$150 ~\text{mm} / {\min}$</tex>. The maximum error is 2 mm with a temperature of 220 °C.

title: A CRISP-DM Based Hybrid Machine Learning Approach for Predicting HIV/AIDS Spread Patterns in Indonesia
authors: Stephen Krisdy, Gunawan Wang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: HIV/AIDS remains a serious health problem globally and in Indonesia. The increasing trend of cases in Indonesia remains a main challenge and indicates that prevention strategies are not yet optimal. HIV/AIDS prevention policies in Indonesia also still uses conventional statistical methods that are unable to produce predictive insights and have not utilized machine learning despite the availability of abundant Indonesian health data. This study aims to build a Machine Learning model with a hybrid approach combining clustering and classification using the CRISP-DM Framework to predict the HIV/AIDS spread patterns in Indonesia, especially mapping risk zones. Using the "Indonesian Health Profile" data from 2014 to 2024, which contains information on disease cases, sociodemographic, and health in Indonesia. The clustering approach is used to determine and label three classes of "risk zones" (low, moderate, high) that will be used as targets in the classification. The lagging technique, namely the use of the year t-1 mechanism for predictions in year t, is carried out at the classification stage and uses SMOTE to balance the training data. There are 2 target classes in the classification, namely HIV and AIDS, separated because each disease has its own characteristics and so that the resulting model is specific to each disease. There are 4 algorithms that were tested and compared their performance, namely Random Forest, Decision Tree, LightGBM, and XGBoost. The results found that the best model for HIV and AIDS was different, Random Forest was the best model for HIV with an F1-Score of 94%, while for AIDS, LightGBM was the best model with an F1-Score of 87.3% and outperforming the other models. Then the best model was used for deployment to identify and group Risk zones from 38 provinces and found 2 provinces for HIV and 1 province for AIDS that were included in the "High Risk" zone for 2025. SHAP analysis was also carried out to determine the variables that were the dominant factors influencing the prediction of the high-risk zone.

title: Design and Realization of a High-Speed Multi-Channel Pressure Scanning Valve Concentrator
authors: Zhigang Wu, Zhonghua Liu
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Micro/Nano Sensors for AI, Healthcare, and Robotics (NSENS)
abstract: This paper presents the software and hardware design and implementation of a high-speed, multi-channel pressure scanning valve concentrator. The concentrator supports a high-density layout of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3 2} \boldsymbol{\times} \mathbf{6 4}$</tex> channels, totaling <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2, 0 4 8}$</tex> individual pressure measurement points. By employing highprecision analog-to-digital conversion, parallel acquisition technology, and optimized data transmission protocols, the device ensures high acquisition rates and data integrity even with a large number of channels. Challenges related to channel synchronization, signal integrity, and system integration are effectively addressed through the use of high-precision clock synchronization protocols and low-noise signal transmission technologies. The system's flexibility and configurability are enhanced by a modular <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$4 \times 8$</tex> architecture, which simplifies subsequent expansion and maintenance. The concentrator is capable of real-time data processing from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2, 0 4 8}$</tex> channels through optimized acquisition algorithms. Data is transmitted to the control end via high-speed data transmission protocols, such as Ethernet UDP/IP. Additionally, the software supports real-time calibration and compensation functions, ensuring the accuracy of measurement results. The high-speed, multichannel pressure scanning valve concentrator not only meets the demands of high channel counts and high acquisition rates but also significantly improves the system's accuracy, reliability, and applicability. It provides a high-performance solution for applications in industrial inspection, wind tunnel experiments, and environmental monitoring.

title: Financial Distress Predication based on Deep Learning Techniques: A survey
authors: Magdi El-Bannany, Belma Ramic-Brkic, Ahmed M. Khedr
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: Deep learning (DL) is changing the way we analyze financial problems. By leveraging DL, we can build models that are significantly more efficient and accurate at identifying signs of financial distress. This work explores recent advancements in the use of DL for financial forecasting, especially on text data sourced from reports, websites, and news articles. It fills the gap in existing literature by providing a critical examination of the DL techniques in analyzing financial text to address the Financial Distress Prediction (FDP) problem, and provides key recommendations for future research. It reviews existing studies, discusses applications and limitations, and outlines future directions and challenges based on articles published between 2020 and 2025.

title: Security-aware Performance-Optimized Computation Offloading Under Near Memory Processing
authors: Simran Preet Kaur, Asutosh Kumar Sarma, Satanu Maity, Manojit Ghose
publish: 2026 39th International Conference on VLSI Design &amp; 25th International Conference on Embedded Systems (VLSID)
abstract: With the emergence of resource-intensive applications and the rising popularity of cloud computing, resource sharing has become a common practice, primarily to improve system performance. However, the sharing of resources introduces various threats, including cache side-channel attacks. This paper presents a strategy called SecNMP, which utilizes an emerging computing paradigm known as near memory processing (NMP), and performs a fine-grained security-aware computation offloading to mitigate these cache side-channel attacks by restricting the use of shared cache memory. The framework begins with a static program analysis to automatically identify the sensitive regions of an application. Next, the framework effectively executes non-sensitive regions of the application on the multicore processor, while sensitive regions are offloaded to the NMP cores for execution. Experiments conducted using a set of standard simulators show that our approach achieves a 36 % speedup compared to the state-of-the-art policy. Additionally, SecNMP offloads 18 % fewer instructions than the state-of-the-art approach and reduces energy consumption by 6 %. Furthermore, the proposed strategy outperforms the conventional approach, reducing off-chip data transfer by 53 %.

title: LSTM Optimization for Battery Temperature Prediction and Cooling Efficiency
authors: Yomna Abdalla, Mohammad Alkhedher, Mohamad Ramadan
publish: 2025 Advances in Science and Engineering Technology International Conferences (ASET)
abstract: Long short-term memory (LSTM) networks have proven to be extremely effective when managing sequential data, making them well-suited for time series forecasting and predictions based on system dynamics. As it pertains to battery thermal management systems (BTMS), temperature forecasting is critical, as forecasting aids the ability to deliver battery efficiency, lifespan, and safety. As such, this paper examines different hyperparameters and their influences on the performance of LSTM models in the context of BTMS, assessing parameters such as number of units, layers, batch size, learning rate, dropout rate, sequence length, optimizer, and loss function. In assessing these parameters and their influences, and the relationship between parameters and effects on prediction accuracy, this paper provides insight into the design process and gives recommended best-practices in tailoring configurations for LSTM model settings, with the goal to improve prediction accuracy and performance, eventually assisting researchers and engineers in building more accurate and effective LSTM models, specific to BTMS. This work reviews and analyzes the current literature on LSTM hyperparameter optimization for BTMS applications, highlighting achievements, identifying challenges, and outlining recommended practices for future implementations.

title: A Comparative Review of Lossless Text Compression Algorithms: From Classic Techniques to Hybrid Models
authors: Mahesh Kumar Sharma, Bosco Paul Alapatt, Vandana Sharma, Manjula Shanbhog
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Advanced Computing and Emerging Technologies (ACET)
abstract: The lossless text compression is an essential part of data transmission and storage that allows using resources effectively without losing data integrity. This paper combines the most recent research with the important discoveries of benchmark research that focuses on essential algorithms such as Huffman LZW and Shannon-Fano and advanced hybrid algorithms such as LZW and Burrows-Wheeler Transform (BWT -based algorithms. The trade-offs between compression ratio, speed and adaptability can be observed comparing how algorithmic concepts, operational stages and empirical performance evolve in different datasets. This evaluation ends with recommendations on how to choose algorithms as well as future research recommendations.

title: Modular PV Panel Cleaning Robot: Affordable Automation for Solar Farms in Developing Countries
authors: Khalid Mahmud Niloy, Md Shahriar Kabir, S. M. Imrat Rahman, Md. Ismail Hossain Mofazzol, Isha Das, Mohammad Tanvir
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Innovations in Engineering, Science and Technology for Sustainable Development (ICEST)
abstract: Photovoltaic (PV) panel cleaning using robotic mechanisms is an advanced technology, but its adoption in developing countries remains limited due to high manufacturing and installation costs. Although robotic cleaning systems are commercially available, they are expensive, costing over # 2,500 per unit, making them difficult to afford. Therefore, an efficient robotic cleaning mechanism was designed with a strong focus on cost-effectiveness. To reduce the cost, the cleaning robot was developed from scratch, which involved circuit simulation and printed circuit board (PCB) design, chassis layout in AutoCAD, 3D modeling, component assembly, sensor calibration, firmware programming, and thorough testing before installation. The proposed system employs a six-wheel design to maintain stability on inclined PV panels, featuring a rain sensor to conserve water during rainfall and an ultrasonic sensor for efficient movement across the surface. The hardware implementation of a per-unit robot costs $ 398, with a predicted 5-year operational cost of #407 (#81 yearly). The cost analysis shows $0.57 per kW annually required for cleaning PV panels using the proposed cleaning robots. Within the first year, the robot's benefit-cost ratio reached 1.13, demonstrating it to be a practical solution for underdeveloped countries. The proposed design may be helpful to manufacturers and stakeholders in guiding them to implement cost-effective design solutions that minimize energy losses and promote the use of renewable energy.

title: Glove-Type Interface Facilitated by Graphene-Based Yarn Strain Sensor for Hand Reconstruction in Virtual Diagnostic Training
authors: Xiaoyang Zou, Xiaoting Li, Jing Zhang, Colin Pak Yu Chan, Zhou Zhao, King Wai Chiu Lai
publish: 2025 IEEE 18th International Conference on Nano/Molecular Medicine &amp; Engineering (NANOMED)
abstract: To address the issues of bulkiness, signal drift, and limited application scenarios in existing hand-tracking devices, we developed a glove-type interface based on graphene-based yarn strain sensors. Combined with IMUs integrated into a compact control circuit, the glove enables unrestricted finger motion and hand position tracking, facilitating accurate hand reconstruction in the virtual world. The glove-type interface leverages graphene-based strain sensors to precisely map hand finger flexion and extension. Additionally, a virtual world was developed, allowing doctors to wear the glove-type interface for patient diagnostic training in the virtual world This demonstrates the significant potential and importance of the glove-type interface in digital healthcare.

title: Counterfeit Product Identification Using Blockchain Technology: A Comprehensive Framework for Supply Chain Authentication
authors: Sumit Sharma, Ravi Sharma, Ashish Kumar
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Advanced Computing and Emerging Technologies (ACET)
abstract: Counterfeit consumer products have become a serious global issue effecting several industries like medicines, electronics, luxury goods and fast moving consumer goods. Existing supply chain management systems offer no transparency and traceability creating vulnerabilities for counterfeit goods to access legitimate marketplaces. This paper proposes a complete blockchain-based framework for counterfeit goods detection and authentication. Our proposed framework uses Ethereum blockchain technology, smart contracts programmed in Solidity and a user-friendly ReactJS UI to create product authentication system that is immutable and transparent. The software allows producers to register authentic products with unique identifiers (UIDs) such as QR codes or serial numbers on the blockchain along with product metadata such as manufacturer, manufacture date, batch data, and product specifications. Consumers and vendors can utilize the UI to authenticate product claims for trust verification. As consumer and vendor users are using the UI, they can even verify authenticity with the real-time capability to interact with the blockchain, while also ensuring data integrity through user tampering and illegal changes. The UI is utilizing the Truffle framework for creating and deploying a smart contract on the Ethereum blockchain, Ganache local blockchain simulator, and web3.js for being able to connect from frontend to the blockchain. Experimental results show a significant improvement in authenticating goods, decreased verification time and increased transparency through the use of our product authentication system. The proposed framework represents a viable solution to significant challenges in product authentication while providing scalability, security and cost effectiveness for global acceptance and use.

title: Explainable and Multimodal AI Approaches for Early Detection and Prognosis in Autoimmune Liver Diseases
authors: E. Ben George, Samuel Giftson, Jeba Roseline, Teresa K. George, Gnana Rajesh, Shaima Al-Wahaibi, Abraham Vargese, Giri Ramadoss, Fatma Al Junaibi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: Autoimmune liver diseases are conditions with challenging prognosis features because of the overlapping clinical features and complicated pathophysiology. Recent developments in Artificial Intelligence, especially Machine Learning and Deep Learning, have shown a high potential of early detection, classification, and prognosis in Autoimmune liver diseases. The current review presents AI techniques utilizing multimodal data, such as clinical, serological, imaging, genomic, and unstructured text data, to enhance the accuracy of diagnosis and treatment planning. In addition to predictive modeling, the analysis of electronic health records and pathology reports can be done with the help of Natural Language Processing methods and Explainable AI methods can be used to improve their interpretability and clinical trust. Moreover, new AI Agent models coordinate multimodal integration, which allows real-time and patient-centered decision support. With these developments, issues of standardization of data, generalizability and regulatory compliance still exist. Building on these foundations, this paper proposes a comprehensive framework that systematically collects, preprocesses, and fuses multimodal hospital data including laboratory results, imaging, pathology, and clinical text into predictive machine and deep learning models. The framework integrates natural language processing for unstructured text, explainability methods for transparency, and an AI agent for evidence retrieval and real-time orchestration, thereby providing an opportunity to pursue interpretable and precision-driven hepatology, to intervene earlier, provide personalized care, and achieve better clinical outcomes.

title: Fault Diagnosis of Mechanical Equipment Based on Heterogeneous Intelligence
authors: Shaoping Zhu, Leping Zhu, Xunrou Ye, Zejian Huang, Meiqi Zhao
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Science, and Information Processing (AICSIP)
abstract: In response to the limitations of traditional signal processing methods when handling complex nonlinear signals, as well as the insufficient detection accuracy for single-modal signals in mechanical equipment fault diagnosis and their susceptibility to noise interference, this paper proposes a novel method which based on heterogeneous intelligence for mechanical equipment fault diagnosis. This method establishes a heterogeneous intelligent fault diagnosis model that integrates multi-source data fusion and multi-algorithm collaboration. By combining diverse source signals such as images, vibrations, and currents, this framework employs Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) for autonomous deep feature abstraction, while incorporating cross-entropy loss optimization to refine multimodal feature fusion. Consequently, this approach enables real-time, accurate, and efficient diagnosis of mechanical equipment faults. Empirical validation on the Paderborn bearing dataset confirms that the proposed heterogeneous intelligence-based fault diagnosis method, which incorporates multi-source data, can achieve end-to-end fault diagnosis with a diagnostic accuracy rate of up to 98.5%.

title: Enhanced Medical Data Diagnosis using an Optimized Soft Computing Model and Feature Selection Algorithms
authors: Asha Rani Borah, Ginni Nijhawan, K.Pushpa Rani, Dinesh Kumar Yadav, Rusul Najm, Mohammed Sahal Mohamed Salim Shaikh, M. Chithirai Pon Selvan
publish: 2025 Advances in Science and Engineering Technology International Conferences (ASET)
abstract: The present paper offers the fast soft computing model of the cardiovascular disease prediction based on the new multi-stage feature selection technique. It uses a combination of filter, wrapper and embedding which contains Chi-square, correlation analysis, Recursive Feature Elimination (RFE), Random Forest, LASSO, and LightGBM to determine the most pertinent medical features of a dataset comprising 70,000 patient records. A True condition satisfaction mechanism is adopted to choose features that are repeatedly confirmed among various methods. The classifiers generated by XGBoost, Random Forest and Naive Bayes are then tested using precision, recall, and the f1-score, and Gmean. A higher accuracy of 95 % was recorded among the proposed model and XGBoost was the best performing prediction model in terms of classification accuracy and performance. The framework provides a scalable and robust solution on early disease detection and can be stretched in other uses including bio informatics and financial analytics.

title: Improving the Statistical Quality and Security of Pseudo-Random Number Generators by Using an Adder Accumulator
authors: Munther Gdeisat, Mohammad Al Sa'd, Walid A. El-Jammal, Asif Nawaz, Jawdat Asous, Arif Judi, Ahmed Khodary, Yacouba Moumouni, Yonatan Melaku, Petros Kassahun, Tsegazeab Zegeye, Amanuel Koye, Biruh Chalie, Yohannes Abate
publish: 2025 Advances in Science and Engineering Technology International Conferences (ASET)
abstract: This study suggests using an adder accumulator to improve a pseudo-random number generator's (PRNG) statistical properties and security. This idea is tested using the linear congruential generator (LCG) and the Mersenne Twister popular PRNGs. Both PRNGs are heavily utilized in various software packages and programming languages like C++, MATLAB, Java, Python, and R. The statistical properties of both PRNGs are evaluated using two famous tests: PractRand and TestU01. Experimental results confirm that the statistical properties are improved by appending an adder accumulator to those PRNGs.

title: Design and Integration of Intelligent Agents for Automated Threat Intelligence in Suricata-Based Intrusion Detection Systems
authors: Guy Munn, Kingsley C. Nwosu, Musbah Abdulgader
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: The rapid evolution of cyber threats has exposed critical limitations in traditional intrusion detection and prevention systems such as Suricata, which depend on static rules and signatures that require constant manual updates. This reliance often results in delayed detection, excessive false positives, and alert fatigue, ultimately reducing the effectiveness of security operations. To overcome these challenges, this work proposes the integration of intelligent agents for automated threat intelligence. By leveraging machine learning and real-time data, intelligent agents can dynamically update threat knowledge and prioritize alerts, enabling faster and more accurate detection of emerging attacks. The proposed solution was implemented within a controlled virtualized environment comprising multiple Ubuntu and Windows virtual machines, where network traffic was generated using tools such as Nmap, curl, ping, and SSH. Suricata monitored the traffic and produced structured JSON alerts, which were used to train a Random Forest model for classifying events as benign or malicious. Experimental results demonstrate that the intelligent agent significantly reduced false positives and improved classification accuracy, achieving an average detection accuracy of 84%. These findings highlight the potential of automated intelligence-driven approaches to enhance the adaptability and responsiveness of intrusion detection systems, reduce human workload, and strengthen organizational security postures against evolving cyber threats. Experimental results show that the trained Random Forest classifier achieved a 96.7% accuracy, while the intelligent agent processed 84% of alerts in real time, reflecting its reduction of analyst workload rather than classification accuracy.

title: 2025 Global Conference on Sustainable Energy and Net-Zero Emissions (SENZE)
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Sustainable Energy and Net-Zero Emissions (SENZE)
abstract: 2025 Global Conference on Sustainable Energy and Net-Zero Emissions (SENZE).

title: Attention Redundancy Reduction for Image Super-Resolution
authors: Yican Liu, Jiacheng Li, Yuhao Jiang, Delu Zeng, Zhou Wang
abstract: Transformer-based models have demonstrated great promises in single image super-resolution (SISR), but our investigations find significant redundancy in terms of high mutual information across the attention maps, negatively impacting both the quality and efficiency performance of SOTA models. To address the problem, here we propose a low redundancy attention network (LRAN). First, to mitigate the redundancy among heads, we introduce in the self-attention computation a multi-element mechanism, which allows for the incorporation of various types of self-attention, thus increasing inter-head diversity. Second, to address the redundancy among blocks, we propose the encapsulated architecture, in which enhanced local perception unit and gated multi-layer perceptron are designed to capture local information. Specifically, this architecture incorporates a single self-attention layer between several MLP layers. Subsequently, the proposed gated multi-layer perceptron significantly enhances the SR quality. Extensive experiments demonstrate that LRAN outperforms SOTA models in the task of lightweight SR, achieving a better trade-off between quality and speed. For instance, the proposed LRAN-light surpasses SwinIR-light by 0.32dB PSNR in ×4 SR on Urban100, while running ×4 faster.
