title: Metrics Gathering for AI-based Trust Assessment
authors: Lorenzo Ferro, Flavio Ciravegna, Francesco Zaritto, Antonio Lioy, Luis Landeiro Ribeiro
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Communication, Networking and Services (ICCNS)
abstract: Technological advances have increased the complex-ity and volume of system operations. As a result, today systems face broader attack surfaces due to the larger amount of code executed. Ensuring the reliability of applications and operations in such environments requires well-defined security criteria, but establishing them is a non-trivial task. Trusted Computing offers mechanisms to establish trust in a non-repudiable manner, yet it faces inefficiencies and scalability challenges when applied to complex scenarios. In fact, defining a flexible verification method for a complex system is a challenging task. This is especially true when the process focuses solely on verifying individual actions rather than on the overall system's behaviour. AI-based techniques offer a viable countermeasure, since modelling a sys-tem's behaviour provides an adaptable approach to determining reliability. An AI model can also analyse a larger amount of data. However, collecting, securely storing, and provisioning relevant information is still a required feature. This work proposes two approaches for the secure collection of system events. The objective is to facilitate the provision of an AI model while ensuring the integrity and authenticity of the data.

title: Tree-Based Machine Learning Algorithms for Alzheimer's Disease Prediction
authors: Vikas Sharma, Aditya Pratap Singh, Harsh Vardhan Kumar Mishra, Esha Khanna, Tamanna Sachdeva
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Advanced Computing and Emerging Technologies (ACET)
abstract: Alzheimer's disease (AD) is the leading cause of dementia among elder people above the age of 65 years. Currently, there is no definitive treatment for AD. Early detection of AD is essential, as it facilitates effective tracking and management of the disease. AD primarily affects cognitive abilities along with memory loss. This study investigates the application of tree-based machine learning (ML) methods for detecting AD. Various ML-based techniques have been evaluated and compared using standard assessment measures. Results indicate that the Light GBM (LGBM) method achieved the highest accuracy of 96%. Experimental results highlight the potential of tree-based models—particularly LGBM—as valuable tools for an effective and early diagnosis of AD.

title: Research on the Design and Operation Strategy of the “Light Storage Charging” Smart Demonstration Project
authors: Wu Jungang, Dou Junhao, Bai Yong, Zhang Shiling
publish: 2025 4th Asia Power and Electrical Technology Conference (APET)
abstract: Integrated of the “photovoltaic power generation, energy storage application, electric vehicle charging, car battery testing, and battery swapping” integrates five functions, with the photovoltaic capacity of 400 kW, the energy storage equipment of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$960 ~\text{kW} / 1720 \text{kWh}$</tex>, and 12 charging piles <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(160 ~\text{kW}$</tex>, one machine, two guns). Install 12 sets of 160 kW electric vehicle one machine two gun DC fast charging piles, which can charge 24 vehicles at the same time, including 18 small vehicles and 6 large vehicles. In addition, there is also one V2G charging station installed, which can provide the low-cost and flexible energy storage resources for power grid in reverse, and help electric vehicles obtain additional auxiliary services or peak valley price benefits through electricity market trading. Furthermore, Weibull distribution probability density function is applied to fit and analyze distribution function of the wind power output. When there is sufficient sunlight, it switches to MPPT mode to improve power generation efficiency. On basis of proportional declaration method, adjust declaration plan output by tracking the changes in load. The typical 400 MW user-side energy storage in practical applications exhibits regular charging and discharging characteristics throughout year, with a 365-day cycle. LSTM model using only historical data performs better than the Seq2Seq with the Attention model that integrates historical context and future weather forecasts.

title: Beyond Pixel Agreement: Large Language Models as Clinical Guardrails for Reliable Medical Image Segmentation
authors: Jiaxi Sheng, Leyi Yu, Haoyue Li, Yifan Gao, Xin Gao
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Science, and Information Processing (AICSIP)
abstract: Evaluating AI-generated medical image segmentations for clinical acceptability poses a significant challenge, as traditional pixelagreement metrics often fail to capture true diagnostic utility. This paper introduces Hierarchical Clinical Reasoner (HCR), a novel framework that leverages Large Language Models (LLMs) as clinical guardrails for reliable, zeroshot quality assessment. HCR employs a structured, multistage prompting strategy that guides LLMs through a detailed reasoning process, encompassing knowledge recall, visual feature analysis, anatomical inference, and clinical synthesis, to evaluate segmentations. We evaluated HCR on a diverse dataset across six medical imaging tasks. Our results show that HCR, utilizing models like Gemini 2.5 Flash, achieved a classification accuracy of 78.12 %, performing comparably to, and in instances exceeding, dedicated vision models such as <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\operatorname{ResNet50}(72.92 \%$</tex> accuracy) that were specifically trained for this task. The HCR framework not only provides accurate quality classifications but also generates interpretable, step-by-step reasoning for its assessments. This work demonstrates the potential of LLMs, when appropriately guided, to serve as sophisticated evaluators, offering a pathway towards more trustworthy and clinically-aligned quality control for AI in medical imaging.

title: User-Friendly Robotic Mitten for Severe Hand Motor Impairments: Technical Evaluation and Design Validation
authors: Adam Ibrahim, Waleed Ahmed, Hassan Abdulmouti, Fatima Albaity, Adel Al-Jumaily, Fady Alnajjar
publish: 2025 Advances in Science and Engineering Technology International Conferences (ASET)
abstract: This research introduces a portable robotic mitten designed for individuals with severe hand motor impairments, including post-stroke patients, traumatic brain injury survivors, and post-immobilization recovery cases. The mitten-style assistive device provides a user-friendly solution for daily activities and rehabilitation through its simplified design. The combined finger compartments allow users to put it on quickly and effortlessly-a particular advantage for those with limited hand dexterity and strength. Compared to traditional robotic gloves requiring individual finger placement, our mitten significantly reduces cognitive and physical load during application. The device enables coordinated finger movement, delivering a robust grip essential for everyday tasks while facilitating fundamental finger flexion and extension movements for rehabilitation. The proposed design minimizes cost and maintenance compared to complex multifinger systems. The mitten is comfortable for extended wear, encouraging longer use. With low power consumption and minimal training needed, it provides a practical, affordable solution for patients seeking rehabilitation and functional assistance. Technical evaluation demonstrated successful grip functionality and superior usability compared to conventional robotic gloves. This simplified approach addresses key barriers to assistive device adoption in severe motor impairment populations, potentially improving quality of life through enhanced independence in activities of daily living.

title: Robustness Evaluation of ESO Using ADRC and PR-SMC Controllers for Robotic Manipulators
authors: Ahlam Mohammed Ali, Omar Mohamed Gad, Sofiane Khadraoui
publish: 2025 Advances in Science and Engineering Technology International Conferences (ASET)
abstract: This paper presents a comprehensive evaluation of two robust control strategies, Active Disturbance Rejection Control (ADRC) and Power Rate Sliding Mode Control (PRSMC) integrated with an Extended State Observer (ESO), for trajectory tracking in a 3-DOF Omni Bundle robotic manipulator. The study focuses on enhancing performance under nonlinear dynamics, parametric uncertainties, and external disturbances. Both controllers are tuned using Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO) to improve tracking accuracy, reduce control effort, and increase robustness. Simulations in MATLAB/Simulink consider three scenarios: no disturbances, combined sinusoidal and step disturbances, and ramp disturbances. Results show that while ADRC achieves strong tracking and disturbance rejection, PR-SMC with ESO consistently delivers faster convergence, lower steady-state and estimation errors, and more effective compensation of both sudden and gradual disturbances. However, PR-SMC with ESO is more complex to design and implement and is sensitive to parameter tuning. The findings confirm the effectiveness of PRSMC with ESO for high-precision control of nonlinear robotic systems.

title: Design of Centralized Monitoring System of Embedded DC Converter Stations
authors: Deng Kai, Guo Tao, Xu Weigang, Xu Bing, Gu Mingqing, Zhao Huilong, Liu Xiaofan
publish: 2025 4th Asia Power and Electrical Technology Conference (APET)
abstract: The embedded DC technology effectively enhances the interface transmission capacity within regional power grids. Adopting a “localized operation” maintenance approach would face challenges, including insufficient human resources, inadequate monitoring intensity, and deficient management granularity. The “centralized control” model serves as an effective solution to alleviate these issues. Taking the ongoing embedded DC project in Jiangsu province as a case study, this paper discusses the necessity of establishing a centralized control center for converter stations. It conducts analysis and research on the practical conditions of converter stations from both technical and managerial perspectives, proposing targeted solutions and recommendations. This study provides actionable insights for transitioning conventional DC operation and maintenance models.

title: Effect of Armature Resistance on Delivered Power in a Non-Salient Pole Synchronous Generator
authors: Ali Ridha Ali
publish: 2025 Advances in Science and Engineering Technology International Conferences (ASET)
abstract: In power system stability studies, the maximum power delivered by synchronous machines is a fundamental parameter known as the static or steady-state stability limit. Due to its small value, the armature resistance is often neglected, resulting in a synchronous impedance that is purely reactive. This simplification arises because the ratio of synchronous reactance to armature resistance is very large, leading to a power factor angle close to 90°. Consequently, the mathematical expressions for active and reactive power become significantly simpler while maintaining high accuracy. However, it is insightful to examine the original expressions when armature resistance is included. This study investigates the influence of armature resistance on delivered power at different load angles, derives the conditions for maximum power delivery, and evaluates the absolute percentage error at the maximum power point. The results help validate the generality of the classical formulas presented in the literature.

title: Converging Deep Learning and Cloud Computing: A Scalable and Efficient Approach for Modern AI Infrastructure
authors: Priyanka Gupta, Suhasini Monga, J. Logeshwaran, Damandeep Kaur
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Advanced Computing and Emerging Technologies (ACET)
abstract: Deep learning has proven to be a powerful approach to solving challenging problems, ranging from natural language processing, speech recognition, to computer vision. The proposed model has capable of matching the expanding amount of facts as well as complexity of the algorithms is demands of deep researching methodologies. These demands cannot be met with traditional computing environments. This is why cloud computing technologies have developed, offering a scalable and affordable alternative for executing deep learning algorithms. Cloud computing platforms provide the resources necessary to run deep learning workloads including compute, storage, and networking. This means you no longer have to spend lots of money on expensive hardware and makes it easier for teams and researchers to deploy and train deep learning models faster. Cloud computing has ushered in a new era of deep learning developments by mobilizing the power of specialized hardware, notably GPUs, to accelerate the training and performance of deep learning models.

title: Robotic Optimal Path Planning via Multi-Objective Dolphin Echolocation Optimization (DEO)
authors: Muaz Al Radi, Abdulrahman Ahmad, Abderrahmene Boudiaf
publish: 2025 Advances in Science and Engineering Technology International Conferences (ASET)
abstract: Path planning is a well-known NP-hard problem that frequently arises in the design and operation of intelligent systems, including mobile robots and unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs). Owing to its wide-ranging applications across diverse engineering domains, efficient path planning remains a critical research focus. Metaheuristic algorithms have demonstrated significant potential for addressing complex optimization problems, especially in scenarios where conventional methods become computationally infeasible. This study investigates the application of the Dolphin Echolocation Optimization (DEO) algorithm to the mobile robot path planning problem, explicitly considering environmental obstacles and associated risk factors. The problem is mathematically formulated as a multi-objective optimization task, encompassing objectives such as path length, smoothness, and risk minimization. Experimental results and comparisons with classical path planning algorithms (such as <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$A^{*}$</tex>) demonstrate that the DEO algorithm is capable of generating optimal paths that effectively balance these objectives. The findings are thoroughly analyzed and discussed, highlighting the advantages of the proposed approach and suggesting avenues for future research and enhancement.

title: Classification of Alzheimer's Disease Using CNN Extractors and Machine Learning
authors: Saijal Dahiya, Kadambri Agarwal, Shweta Roy, Sandhya Avasthi
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Advanced Computing and Emerging Technologies (ACET)
abstract: A neurological condition which mostly affects senior citizens, Alzheimer's disease first damages the brain cells that operate your memory and progressively it spreads to hamper basic cognitive functions. It happens due to erosion of neurons and connections between them. To identify the disease, we initially used manual imaging techniques like CT, MRI, etc. but they have been proven to be time-taking and ineffective for early diagnosis. This gave rise to the use of faster and precise Deep Learning techniques. In this paper, supporting the increasing demand for accurate and automated diagnosis, we propose a study to investigate the use of hybrid models that follow the pipeline of using pre-trained CNN as deep feature extractors followed by classifying the medical images using the five traditional algorithms: Random Forest (RF), Support Vector Machine (SVM), Multi-layer Perceptron (MLP), Boost (XGB)and Gaussian Naïve Bayes. The proposed model used a publicly available multi-class dataset to classify AD into four classes to evaluate various model's performance. In this paper, we have achieved the accuracy of 93.1 % from the hybrid of DenseNet121 and MLP with the second highest being <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{8 2 \%}$</tex> using DenseNet121 with boost. The results showed that proposed hybrid approaches demonstrate large potential for adaptable, flexible and accurate medical image classification.

title: Reliability Assessment of Multi-Component Systems Considering Shock Correlation
authors: Ruida Wang, Li Ma, Hao Lyu, Michael Pecht
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Micro/Nano Sensors for AI, Healthcare, and Robotics (NSENS)
abstract: In some systems, numerous, if not all, components share the same system shock, or at least transfers shock loads to the other component. Since each component has a certain resistance to shock loads, the system shock can be divided into three zones according to the magnitude of the shock, i.e., a safe zone, a damage zone, and a failure zone. In this paper, a non-homogeneous Poisson process is used to describe the number of shock arrivals, from which a developed reliability model can be implemented to describe the dependent competitive failure processes. The developed process and model are then applied to a microelectromechanical system developed at Sandia National Laboratories in the USA.

title: GenAI-Powered Clinical Libraries: Enhancing Patient-Centric Care with Retrieval-Augmented Generation and Multimodal Search
authors: Shravya Bussari, Ritesh Ruparel
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: Healthcare organizations are now producing enormous volumes of unstructured data in many forms—clinical guidelines, PDFs, presentations, and even diagnostic images. Existing clinical libraries largely depend on keyword-based search, which often yields long, unfocused lists that clinicians must sift through before finding relevant information. This work introduces a Clinical Library powered by generative AI. The system combines Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) [1], multimodal content ingestion, and large language models (LLMs) to create a more intuitive search experience. Instead of wading through document lists, clinicians can ask natural-language questions and receive concise, multilingual answers backed by citations. Safeguards against hallucinations are built in, ensuring that outputs remain evidence-based and trustworthy for clinical practice.

title: Heuristic Scheduling Optimization for Agricultural Aircraft Spraying Tasks Under Resource and Time Constraints
authors: Ameera Albadawi, Mahdi Jemmali, Zaher Al Aghbari
publish: 2025 Advances in Science and Engineering Technology International Conferences (ASET)
abstract: Agricultural aerial vehicles, particularly unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs), are increasingly utilized in various agricultural tasks due to their efficiency, precision, and maneuverability. Nonetheless, scheduling tasks for UAVs continues to pose challenges owing to practical limitations, such as the limited flying time due to the need for refueling and the preparation time needed before tasks. This study examines the problem of assigning spraying tasks to agricultural UAVs while taking into consideration the real-world limitations of the refueling time and the preparation time needed, with the objective of minimizing maximum completion time. The algorithms created and assessed are based on three approaches: Shortest Flying Time (SFT), Longest Flying Time (LFT), and Lower-bound (LB). The research systematically examines various algorithms across different real-world scenarios that are reflected through the dataset generated, which includes varying quantities of tasks, durations, and UAV availability, demonstrating how particular scheduling methods can significantly improve operational efficiency, decrease expenses, and elevate overall productivity. Extensive experiments and comparative analyses yield significant insights into the most effective methods. Using a dataset of 630 different instances, the experimental results demonstrate that the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$L F T_{2}$</tex> algorithm achieves superior performance, attaining the highest rate of optimal solutions (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{4 9. 5 2 \%}$</tex>) and the smallest average gap (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{0. 0 1 1 8)}$</tex>.

title: Research on Performance Evaluation Indicators and Benchmark for Artificial Intelligence Chip
authors: Yue Zhao, Jun Luo, Qiuzhen Zhang, Zhizhe Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Science, and Information Processing (AICSIP)
abstract: With the explosive development of artificial intelligence (AI) technology, as the fundamental support for computing power, AI chips are facing an ever - more - pressing requirement for standardized performance evaluation. The benchmark suite for AI chip testing is not merely a tool for performance evaluation; rather, it serves as a central driving force in technological iteration and the establishment of an industrial ecosystem. Beginning with the performance metrics of AI chips and the characteristics of benchmark test suites, this paper analyzes and synthesizes existing AI chip evaluation tools and benchmark test suites. We delve into the AI computing capabilities of chips to conduct a comprehensive assessment of current test benchmark suites. Consequently, the research presented herein provides crucial support for the fullspectrum optimization of AI chips, spanning from laboratory development to industrial deployment.

title: Micro-Nano Hierarchical Structure Fabricated by Femtosecond Laser on Lithium Niobate Surface for Second Harmonic Generation
authors: Ye Qiu, Xiaoduo Wang, Haibo Yu, Jianchen Zheng, Jingang Wang, Lianqing Liu, Wen Jung Li
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Micro/Nano Sensors for AI, Healthcare, and Robotics (NSENS)
abstract: Lithium niobate <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left(\text{LiNbO}_{3}\right)$</tex> is a colorless, transparent material renowned for its photorefractive, nonlinear, electro-optic, and ferroelectric properties. These characteristics make it a cornerstone in photonic applications, including electro-optic modulators, optical resonators, and optical frequency combs. However, traditional processing techniques often face limitations in precision and scalability. On the other hand, femtosecond laser (fs-laser) ablation has emerged as a versatile and high-precision method for three-dimensional micro- and nano-scale fabrication. This study investigates the fabrication of micro-nano hierarchical structures on the surface of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{LiNbO}_{3}$</tex> using fs-laser ablation. By varying the laser's repetition frequency, micro-nano structures with diverse morphologies were successfully created. Notably, the results reveal a strong second harmonic generation (SHG) effect associated with the pronounced micro-nano hierarchical structures on the LiNbO3 surface. This approach demonstrates significant potential as a straightforward yet powerful method for advancing research in areas such as nonlinear optical integration and quantum photonic devices.

title: Graphene and Copper Nanoparticles Based Electrochemical Sensors for the Determination of Glyphosate in Water
authors: Uswah Yasin, Federico La Barbera, Stefano Boscarino, Antonino Scandurra, Francesco Ruffino, Felice Torrisi
publish: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Metrology for Green Technologies, Renewable Energy and Ecological Sustainability (MetroGREENST)
abstract: Glyphosate, a widely used herbicide, poses significant environmental and health risks due to its persistence in soil and water. The demand for scalable and sensitive detection methods has led to the exploration of advanced sensing materials. Here, we present a flexible and sustainable graphene–copper nanoparticle (G: Cu) sensor fabricated via a spray-coating process of liquid-phase exfoliated graphene ink (~0.7 mg/mL) on polyimide and graphene paper, followed by physical vapor deposition of ultra-thin Cu films and Intense Pulsed Light (IPL) photonic de-wetting to form uniform nanoparticles. This route enables precise nanoparticle size control and yields electrodes with low sheet resistance (~300 Ω/sq.) and high mechanical stability, even after electrochemical cycling. Multiple cyclic voltammetry tests in 1 mM glyphosate solutions exhibited distinct redox peaks, indicating a strong interaction between the analyte and the electrode surface. Unlike previous graphene-Cu systems, this work combines solution-processable graphene deposition, scalable nanoparticle patterning, and flexible substrate integration, offering a low-cost, robust platform for real-time environmental monitoring of glyphosate contamination.

title: Enhancing Truth with AI: Evaluating ML, LLMs, and RAG in Combating Misinformation
authors: Manisha, Manisha Jailia
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Advanced Computing and Emerging Technologies (ACET)
abstract: In the digital age, misinformation is a new danger. It impacts how people talk about things, harms democracy, and changes how people think about the government. This study answers the important demand for better ways to find false information by comparing classic ML models with the newest LLMs and then creating a hybrid framework that contains the best parts of both. Unfortunately, classic classifiers like Naive Bayes and the Passive-Aggressive Classifier often miss content that is both hostile and full of context. LLMs like GPT-3.5 and GPT-4, on the other hand, are very accurate and know what words mean, but they need a lot of processing power and can make stuff up in their outputs. Adding Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) to the LLM pipeline fixes these issues. Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) plays a crucial role by grounding LLM outputs in verified external knowledge, reducing hallucination and improving factual accuracy. By combining retrieval with generation, RAG ensures that misinformation detection becomes more reliable, contextaware, and aligned with real-world evidence. Our hybrid technique works better than existing algorithms on benchmark datasets including LIAR, and Buzz Feed. It has an F1 score of 95.9% and an accuracy score of 96.3%. It also keeps inference times in check. Also, SHAP analysis and attention weight visualisation make things easier to comprehend. This study looks at the good and bad sides of the present ways of identifying misinformation and suggests a solution that is easy to comprehend and can be applied in the real world.

title: Implementation of the Multi-Objective Optimization on the Basis of Ratio Analysis Method for Determining Tuition Fee Assistance Eligibility
authors: Euis Oktavianti, Farhan Adi Nugraha, Ahmad Muharam Jiddan Wahono, Ayres Pradiptyas, Ariawan Andi Suhandana
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: The Single Tuition Fee (UKT) is a mandatory payment required from students to support both academic and operational activities within higher education institutions. The amount of UKT varies according to predetermined criteria and is grouped into several categories. At Public Universities, a merit-based tuition fee reduction program is implemented as a form of institutional recognition for students with outstanding achievements. Each semester, a large number of applications are received, requiring the Student Affairs Department to conduct a careful verification and validation process to identify eligible recipients. To make this process more accurate and objective, this study proposes a Decision Support System (DSS) that applies the Multi-Objective Optimization on the Basis of Ratio Analysis (MOORA) method. Three evaluation criteria are used: Competition Level (C1), Achievement (C2), and Number of Participants (C3). Among eleven student candidates, five obtained the highest optimization scores—A2, A3, A4, A1, and A5—with values ranging from 6.062 (highest, achieved by A2) to 1.835 (lowest, achieved by A10). The results show that the MOORA method provides a clear, transparent, and objective mechanism for ranking applicants. It supports institutional decision-making by promoting fairness, consistency, and efficiency in the selection of tuition fee assistance recipients.

title: Your EV Is Talking Behind Your Back: How Token-Based Security Can Protect Driver Privacy
authors: Prem Kumar Akula, Karthik Pappu, Akshay Mittal
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: Electric vehicles (EVs) generate unprecedented volumes of user data, including driving patterns, location histories, and charging behaviors, raising significant privacy concerns. This study introduces a novel token-based security framework for EV charging infrastructure that leverages hardware-backed tokenization to protect personally identifiable information (PII) while maintaining grid efficiency. The framework employs AES-256 encryption with hardware security modules (HSMs) to generate single-use tokens and implements controlled state-of-charge (SoC) reporting with ±2% random offset to prevent pattern inference while enabling grid optimization. Compatible with Open Charge Point Protocol (OCPP) 2.0 and Open Charge Point Interface (OCPI) 2.2 standards, this solution ensures inter-operability across heterogeneous charging networks and complies with global privacy regulations, including GDPR, CCPA, PIPL, and DPDP.

title: MaSVet: A Flexible Framework for Statistical Modelling and Synthesis of Macroscopic Vehicular Trajectory Datasets
authors: Dipankar Mandal, Arnab Sarkar, Arijit Mondal
publish: 2026 39th International Conference on VLSI Design &amp; 25th International Conference on Embedded Systems (VLSID)
abstract: Artificial Intelligence (AI)-driven mobile Cyber-Physical Systems (CPSs) such as autonomous vehicles, robots, and wearable devices require large volumes of mobility trace data for training, validation, and testing. These traces are often region-specific, and real-world datasets for them may be limited due to cost, privacy, and accessibility constraints. While tools like Simulation Of Urban MObility (SUMO) support microscopic traffic generation, solutions for flexible macroscopic trajectory generation remain rare and cumbersome. This paper presents Macroscopic Synthetic Vehicular Traffic Generator (MaSVeT), a versatile tool for generating large-scale vehicular trajectory datasets using high-level statistical characterisations. MaSVeT features an interactive GUI for easy input of city maps, landuse annotations, population and ownership densities, vehicle type distributions, and spatio-temporal traffic flow parameters. It supports diverse vehicle classes, embedded physical properties, and congestion-aware traffic modelling over multiple days. The tool has been evaluated over real city maps and applied in multiple CPS contexts, including High Definition (HD) mapassisted edge routing, dynamic Electric Vehicle (EV) routing under congestion and range constraints, pollution-aware vehicle routing, and grid-aware EV charging demand estimation. MaSVeT enables efficient, controllable synthetic data generation and supports reproducible AI-based Intelligent Transport Systems (ITS) research across scales and scenarios.

title: Design and Simulation Analysis of a High-Voltage Power Supply System Based on Phase-Shifted Full-Bridge Topology
authors: Yuanjie Zhu, Yiyun Huang, Shengmin Pan, Congguo Gong, Kai Yu, Xiaolong Li, Hulin Feng, Denghui Wang
publish: 2025 4th Asia Power and Electrical Technology Conference (APET)
abstract: This paper proposes a high-voltage power supply system based on a phase-shifted full-bridge topology, developed to satisfy the stringent requirements for high stability and rapid dynamic response in China's nuclear fusion facilities. The system employs the classical proportional-integral (PI) control algorithm, using it to implement closed-loop control of the output-voltage regulation loop. A comprehensive circuit and control model was constructed in Simulink to investigate the system dynamics through time-domain simulations. The simulation results demonstrate that the output reaches a stable steady state within a short rise time and exhibits only minor voltage ripple, indicating that the system possesses excellent dynamic response behavior and robust voltage regulation capability. The results offer practical guidance for engineering and performance enhancement of power supply systems serving China's large-scale high-power, highvoltage scientific installations.

title: RISC-V Unified: A Cross-Platform Generation Framework from Architecture to Implementation
authors: Mohamed Badawy, Jiulong Wang, Paritosh Kumar Sinha, Wolfgang Ecker
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: Register-Transfer Level (RTL) generation is foundational to hardware design, converting high-level specifications into synthesizable descriptions for physical implementation. Conventional RTL generators produce platform-agnostic designs to enable flexibility across diverse targets (e.g., FPGA or ASIC), however this approach is problematic for designs with technology-specific components (e.g., memory macros), where generic constructs—suitable for simulation and verification—fail to leverage native hardware resources, leading to either non-implementable designs or inefficient synthesis and degraded power, performance, and area (PPA) metrics. Extensive manual adaptations are thus required, eroding automation benefits and widening the design productivity gap. This paper proposes a cross-platform methodology that automates the generation of RTL code and constraints tailored to both ASIC and FPGA platforms, enabling optimal native resource utilization. Applied to a RISC-V subsystem, it enables seamless synthesis and implementation across targets, forming an end-to-end workflow from specification to deployment. Evaluations on an AMD Zynq UltraScale+ FPGA and a 22 nm ASIC process demonstrate substantial improvements: up to 3.66× higher maximum frequency, 3.44× area reduction, 5.99× power savings, and 18.80× faster implementation run times compared to platform-agnostic baselines, affirming its robustness for modern embedded systems.

title: Integrated IoT-LSTM Framework for Real-Time Vehicle Dynamics Monitoring and Lane Change Intention Prediction
authors: Umer Mukhtar Andrabi, Ehsan Wadood, Muhammad Salman Saeed, Raman Saurabh, Sanjeev Kumar Ojha, Aamir Mehmood
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: The rapid developments in the interconnected car technologies has shown promising improvements concerned to the road and driver safety. This security is further enhanced by integrating smart driver assistance systems in these technologies. This paper presents a novel mathematical based system that combines wireless Internet of Things sensors with Long Short Term Memory neural networks to support real-time traffic monitoring, velocity control and detection of intentions to change lanes. In contrast to the traditional methods which mostly use the visual sense modalities, our system employs distributed Internet of Things devices to record multidimensional data pertaining to vehicle. It also uses the vehicle’s telemetric data such as velocity, acceleration, heading angles, and positioning to improve the output. Our system is a hybrid Long Short Term Memory architecture that can process temporal sequences generated by Internet of Things sensor streams to forecast lane changing intentions. The system can predict such intentions up to 2.5 seconds prior to the actual maneuver performed by the vehicle. The achieved results reveal that the IoT-based framework achieves an accuracy of 94.3% in lane change detection at 0.5 seconds before maneuver initiation, which is 3.2% higher than baseline LSTM models. Furthermore, this paper also includes an experimental validation which compares our proposed system to standard machine-learning baseline models and demonstrates the computational efficiency. Overall efficiency of this lightweight integrated framework revealed that it takes only 45 milliseconds to complete an entire prediction cycle on resource-constrained IoT gateways. This paper contributes towards the field of intelligent transportation systems by developing scalable, distributed sensor system architecture for understanding vehicle behavior. It also provides a feasible approach to real-time, scalable vehicular safety systems to make it robust and more reliable.

title: Flexible Electronic Sensing Systems for Motion Recognition in Sports Training: A Badminton Application
authors: Jian Li, Junhui Gong, Yuliang Zhao
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Micro/Nano Sensors for AI, Healthcare, and Robotics (NSENS)
abstract: Efficient data capture and real-time feedback in sports training rely heavily on advancements in wearable flexible electronics. This paper introduces an intelligent badminton racket handle embedded with multi-node MEMS sensors for precise motion recognition and performance optimization. The sensor system, consisting of three flexible MEMS accelerometer nodes, accurately captures key motion data such as grip force distribution, swing trajectory, and impact force. Data is transmitted wirelessly via a Bluetooth module, enabling real-time monitoring and processing. The system achieves a classification accuracy of 99 % across ten distinct badminton actions, setting a new benchmark for sports data acquisition. Compared to traditional external wearable sensors, this system is lightweight, seamlessly integrated into the racket handle, and does not interfere with the athlete's natural movements. When combined with a virtual reality (VR) platform, it offers real-time feedback and immersive training environments, significantly enhancing engagement and efficiency during practice sessions. This study highlights the potential of flexible electronics in intelligent sports equipment, paving the way for new applications in sports training, rehabilitation, and human-machine interaction.

title: Electrochemistry as a Bridge to Oxidative Stress and Neurotoxicity Mechanism Elucidation: From Rhanterium Suaveolens Plant Based Assays to Mouse Model
authors: Meryam Chelly, Sabrine Chelly, Giuseppe Nania, Tahia Boudawara, Giovanni Neri, Hanen Bouaziz-Ketata
publish: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Metrology for Green Technologies, Renewable Energy and Ecological Sustainability (MetroGREENST)
abstract: This study investigates the neuroprotective potential of Rhanterium suaveolens (RS) extract by integrating in vitro electrochemical assays and in vivo histopathological analysis in a mouse model exposed to oxidative stress. RS extract was initially characterized via UV-Vis spectroscopy to identify its phenolic and flavonoid content. Multiple antioxidant assays (CUPRAC, FRAP, H<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>, OH·, NO·) were employed to quantify its redox properties. A screen-printed carbon electrode (SPCE) was then modified with RS extract to assess its ability to detect oxidative stress-related compounds such as H<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>, Fe<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3+</sup>, and Cu2+. The electrochemical sensor played a pivotal role in translating the antioxidant capacity observed in vitro into a measurable analytical signal, enabling real-time detection of oxidative markers. This analytical approach provided a sensitive and selective platform to monitor redox-active species which are directly implicated in neurodegenerative processes. In vivo validation using an acetamiprid-induced mouse neurotoxicity model confirmed the extract's ability to reduce oxidative damage in brain tissues. The sensor's performance not only supported the biochemical findings but also offered a practical tool for bridging molecular-level antioxidant activity with physiological outcomes. This work emphasizes the synergy between green electrochemical sensing and biomedical application, bridging in vitro findings with real-world biological outcomes.

title: DemandLens: Enhancing Forecast Accuracy Through Product-Specific Hyperparameter Optimization
authors: Srijesh Pillai, M. I. Jawid Nazir
publish: 2025 Advances in Science and Engineering Technology International Conferences (ASET)
abstract: DemandLens demonstrates an innovative Prophet-based forecasting model for the mattress-in-a-box industry, incorporating COVID-19 metrics and SKU-specific hyperparameter optimization. This industry has seen significant growth of E-commerce players in the recent years, wherein the business model majorly relies on outsourcing Mattress manufacturing and related logistics and supply chain operations, focusing on marketing the product and driving conversions through Direct-to-Consumer sales channels. Now, within the United States, there are a limited number of Mattress contract manufacturers available, and hence, it is important that they manage their raw materials, supply chain, and, inventory intelligently, to be able to cater maximum Mattress brands. Our approach addresses the critical need for accurate Sales Forecasting in an industry that is heavily dependent on third-party Contract Manufacturing. This, in turn, helps the contract manufacturers to be prepared, hence, avoiding bottleneck scenarios, and aiding them to source raw materials at optimal rates. The model demonstrates strong predictive capabilities through SKU-specific Hyperparameter optimization, offering the Contract Manufacturers and Mattress brands a reliable tool to streamline supply chain operations.

title: Cellular Mechanobiology in 3D Heterogeneous Cross-Linking Hydrogels*
authors: Xiaolu Zhu, Fengliang He, Zheng Wang, Xi Yin, Yanping Lu, Xuan Zhang, Xiangyan Pan
publish: 2025 IEEE 18th International Conference on Nano/Molecular Medicine &amp; Engineering (NANOMED)
abstract: The mechanobiological behavior of cells is often challenging to regulate in a rational and predictable manner, limiting their effectiveness in specific therapeutic applications. To address this limitation, we propose a strategy based on composition-tunable hydrogels with engineered structural heterogeneity. These hydrogels are fabricated via a cross-linker-clustering (CLC) approach that introduces nanoscale heterogeneity, enabling precise micro- to nano-scale modulation of gel stiffness. This tunability facilitates the rational control of cellular mechanobiological responses within 3D hydrogel niches, as demonstrated by monitoring cell morphology and quantifying cell mechanical properties using atomic force microscopy (AFM) and a thermosensitive microactuator-based method.

title: An Intelligent Digital Microfluidic Biochip System with GUI and Deep Learning-Based Automation
authors: Sanju De, Tamal Mandal, Sudip Roy
publish: 2026 39th International Conference on VLSI Design &amp; 25th International Conference on Embedded Systems (VLSID)
abstract: This paper introduces an intelligent digital microfluidic biochip (DMFB) system that integrates deep learning for automated droplet manipulation with an intuitive interface for manual control. Our approach combines a custom, low-cost <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$8 \times 8$</tex> electrode array fabricated on a printed circuit board (PCB) with a real-time vision system. The system utilizes copper-coated electrodes with cost-effective dielectric and hydrophobic layers, driven by a microcontroller-based high-voltage actuation circuit. Real-time, autonomous droplet manipulation is enabled by a deep learning-based pipeline that features a fine-tuned You Only Look Once (YOLOv11n) model for robust droplet detection and an intelligent routing algorithm for generating optimal, collision-free routing paths. Experimental validation demonstrates high system performance, with the automation pipeline achieving a 90% routing success rate and the YOLOv11n detector attaining a mean Average Precision (mAP@0.5) of 0.993 on a custom-created dataset. Furthermore, the system incorporates a graphical user interface (GUI) for manual control, which exhibited 98% task accuracy. By successfully integrating affordable hardware with advanced computer vision, this work presents a scalable and accessible platform for automated biochemical assays, demonstrating significant potential to advance applications in point-of-care diagnostics, drug discovery, and personalized medicine.

title: Fedsig+: Trust-Aware Federated Mapping of Suspicious File Behaviour
authors: V Ragavarthini, Surendar R, Ranjani S, Narmadha B, Mathew Hayden
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Advanced Computing and Emerging Technologies (ACET)
abstract: Federated Learning (FL) offers a new way to improve cybersecurity by allowing distributed clients to work together on file threat intelligence while keeping data private. Unlike centralized detection systems, FL enables endpoints to contribute without revealing raw data, which helps meet regulatory and operational needs. However, most current FLbased detection frameworks depend on complicated predictive models, which can lead to issues with understanding, resource use, and real-world application. This paper presents FedSIG+, a clear, rule-based FL framework that maps suspicious file behavior through smart group aggregation. The proposed model includes a dynamic trust-scoring system that stops trust from deteriorating and selectively shares signatures. This ensures that only valid inputs build the global security knowledge base. Built in Python with the Flower FL framework, FedSIG+ was tested on real-world datasets and showed a detection accuracy of 92 to 94 % with a low false positive rate. The system also proved to be very efficient, using less than 10% CPU, making it suitable for low-power devices. Our comparison against predictive FL and centralized solutions shows that FedSIG+ keeps competitive detection performance while excelling in understanding, trust management, scalability, and ease of deployment. These results suggest that FedSIG+ provides a practical, scalable, and clear solution for real-world cybersecurity issues.

title: Cyber Attacks in Digital Banking: Security Challenges and Threats, Risk Mitigation
authors: Shiny Bhattacharjee, Mir Mehdi Ali Jafri, Samaya Pillai, Pankaj Pathak, Bhavana Kamarthapu, Venkatesh Iyengar
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Advanced Computing and Emerging Technologies (ACET)
abstract: This is the era of digital banking, where the customer prefers online transactions. Nowadays, every industry is trying to go digital, and so is the banking sector, where a bank can be operated online, and processes that used to occur traditionally are digitalized. Digital banking consists of online banking like Internet and mobile banking, cards like debit and credit, UPI Ids, wallets, etc. The advancement of the internet has enhanced activities in cyberspace, where everyone has access to a wide range of worldwide data. Because digitalization has made computers and I.T. become an important part of our life, we are now inseparable from our electronic devices, and we can not go a single day without our computers, laptops, phones, etc. Due to this habit of humans, nowadays, cybercrime and information security are intrinsically linked with each other. This raises a risk of cybercrime and unauthorized access of information of bank details to fraudsters. The paper covers the issue of cybersecurity attacks in digital banking and, the risks vulnerabilities of cybersecurity, and how many types of cybercrime or cyber threats can occur in the digital banking industry despite having guidelines and frameworks. This paper also discusses the measures and recommendations to avoid or prevent digital banking organizations and customers from using e-banking.

title: Transforming Code Development with Large Language Models
authors: Alex Kaplunovich
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Communication, Networking and Services (ICCNS)
abstract: In recent years, a plethora of code generation tools, products, and models have been released, varying in price, size, integration capabilities, and the resources required for their utilization. In our study, we explored a range of tools, from IDE-integrated “helpers” to open-source models, to identify their advantages, drawbacks, successes, and failures. Our research aims to assist developers and data scientists in navigating the evolving landscape of code assistants and preparing for potential challenges. We have explored several key methods and techniques, including LLM finetuning, code dataset selection, the use of quantized models to minimize GPU memory requirements, and the implementation of techniques like LoRA, QLoRA, and PEFT for efficient tuning. Additionally, we explored llama.cpp for running advanced models on moderate CPU instances. Our research compared existing AI coding methods, commercial models and open-source LLMs, evaluating their performance, benefits, and limitations. We have also identified the missing tools and possible future trends. Our findings are intended to guide the software community through the rapidly advancing realm of Generative AI for coding, helping people to select solutions that best fit their specific needs, budget, and technological experience.

title: Research and Application of Virtual Joints for Effectively Increasing the Length of High-Voltage Cable Segments
authors: Xingmin Hou, Haisheng Song, Shengxiexian Xuan, Hanghui Ji
publish: 2025 4th Asia Power and Electrical Technology Conference (APET)
abstract: The intermediate joint of high-voltage cables is a relatively weak link in the cable line. In order to improve the reliability of system power supply and reduce cable failure rates, the length of a single section of cable can be increased, thereby reducing the number of intermediate joints. However, the increase in the length of high-voltage cable sections will lead to a linear increase in induced voltage on the metal sheath of the cable. Based on the analysis of the influencing factors of induced voltage, this article studies the “intermediate grounding” scheme, “virtual joint” process and three control argon arc welding process suitable for long-distance cable use. The “intermediate grounding” scheme can directly reduce the induced voltage of the cable by half, and the “virtual joint” process can minimize the damage to the cable body while meeting the requirements of effective electrical connection. The “virtual joint” technology in this article has been successfully applied in practical engineering, completing the installation and operation of a single 220 kV land cable of 1715 meters, setting a new record for the longest length in China.

title: Research on Parameter Estimation of the Starlink Downlink Signal
authors: Xinrui Hu, Yongfeng Wang, Runhui Zhao, Wenjin Hou, Hong Wen, Hongzhi Liu
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Science, and Information Processing (AICSIP)
abstract: Starlink satellites have garnered global attention due to their global coverage capabilities, high-speed and lowlatency service features, and unique advantages in providing communication in remote areas. However, since SpaceX has not disclosed the technical details of its physical layer, the identification and analysis of Starlink signals face significant technical challenges. Therefore, research on parameter estimation of Starlink signals has become particularly urgent, yet relevant studies in this field remain relatively scarce. Given that the signal system of Starlink employs OFDM signals, this paper mainly discusses how to utilize OFDM signal parameter estimation algorithms for the identification of Starlink signals. Simulation experiments are conducted using data from the Starlink downlink signal to investigate OFDM parameter estimation algorithms. The research results indicate that the joint estimation algorithm strategy proposed in this paper can successfully perform blind parameter estimation on the Starlink downlink signal.

title: Image-based Waste Classification Using Convolutional Neural Networks on Diverse Image Data
authors: Mohammad I. Daoud, Sulafa Mubarak, Fatima Inuwa Yau, Farah Abu Teer, Hana Khedr, Mohammad A. Jaradat
publish: 2025 Advances in Science and Engineering Technology International Conferences (ASET)
abstract: Efficient waste sorting is critical for improving recycling rates and reducing environmental impact. However, manual sorting processes are often slow and prone to errors. Deep learning provides powerful tools to automate waste classification, offering a more accurate and scalable alternative. This study compares four well-established convolutional neural network models: AlexNet, ResNet-50, VGG16, and VGG19. A comprehensive custom dataset was created by merging six publicly available datasets. It includes six waste categories: PET, HDPE, Mixed Plastics, Metal, Paper, and Food Organics. The dataset captures a broad range of shapes, backgrounds, and contamination levels, closely approximating real-world conditions that lack controlled setups. Each model was evaluated using macroaveraged metrics, including accuracy, recall, precision, and F1score, along with detailed per-class results. VGG19 achieved the highest performance, with macro-averaged values of 97.6 % accuracy, 92.2 % recall, 92.6 % precision, and 92.5 % F1-score. These strong results were obtained using images captured by standard cameras, without specialized sensors or ideal acquisition conditions. This highlights the potential of VGG19 for practical, cost-effective waste sorting systems. Future work will expand the dataset and explore advanced deep learning models to further improve classification and generalization.

title: Surface Topographic Features-Assisted Droplet Manipulation in Magnetic Digital Microfluidic Platform
authors: Xuyang Hu, Xiaosheng Zhang, Yi Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Micro/Nano Sensors for AI, Healthcare, and Robotics (NSENS)
abstract: Magnetic digital microfluidics (MDM) is a discrete droplets-based microfluidics technology that manipulates droplets on an open surface. The MDM is capable of various droplet operations, such as moving, merging, mixing, and release, which provides advantages in device flexibility and low costs for biomedical applications. However, traditional systems relying on flat hydrophobic surfaces and microparticle often struggle with precise liquid handling and operational versatility. In this work, we propose surface topographic features to conform different droplet manipulation in MDM, incorporating three-dimensional surface designs fabricated through thermoforming and 3D printing. The thermoformed and 3D-printed features exhibit unique advantages in device construction and droplet manipulation. The effectiveness of these surface topographic features was validated through droplet movement, droplet mixing, microparticle extraction, and droplet dispensing. Our work provides an enhanced and efficient solution for droplet manipulation in MDM, broadening the potential application scenarios of this technology.

title: Evaluating the Kinetic Parameters Involved in the Pyrolysis of Palm Waste Cellulosic Fibers to Produce Sustainable and Renewable Energy: ANN Optimization
authors: Ahmed Belaadi, Soumia Atoui, Djamel Ghernaout
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Sustainable Energy and Net-Zero Emissions (SENZE)
abstract: Biomass has emerged as a promising renewable energy source due to declining fossil fuel reserves and environmental issues linked to their use. Understanding the characteristics of biomass pyrolysis is crucial, as it provides valuable information for developing and improving the pyrolysis process. A thermogravimetric analysis (TGA) conducted in a nitrogen atmosphere examined the pyrolysis features and kinetic parameters of palm waste cellulosic fibers (PWCFs). The temperature ranged from 25 to 1000°C, with heating rates (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\beta$</tex>) of 25, 45, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$60^{\circ} \mathrm{C} \cdot \min^{-1}$</tex>. The study investigated the kinetic and thermodynamic properties, pyrolytic products, and thermal degradation behavior of PWCFs using TGA and Fourier transform infrared analysis in an inert atmosphere. Thirty-six kinetic models for four key stages of the solid-phase reaction were tested with the Coats-Redfern method. The pre-exponential factor of the reaction model, which ranged from 7.566 to 7.214 for all <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\beta$</tex> values, was identified as the most suitable, based on assuming random nucleation and subsequent growth. Activation energy values of 217, 239, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$261 ~\text{kJ} \cdot ~\text{mol}^{-1}$</tex> were obtained at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\beta$</tex> of 25,45, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$60^{\circ} \mathrm{C} \cdot \min^{-1}$</tex>, respectively. The kinetic parameters were used to calculate the thermodynamic properties (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\Delta \mathrm{H}, \Delta \mathrm{G}$</tex>, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\Delta \mathrm{S}$</tex>). The thermodynamic data indicate that the pyrolysis of PWCFs is an endothermic process that is not spontaneous. The kinetic and thermodynamic properties derived from the best-fit model (ANN27) were compared with those predicted by three model-free approaches: Flynn-Wall-Ozawa, Kissinger-Akahira-Sunose, and Starink.

title: Leveraging Electronic Health Records for Improved Healthcare Delivery in the Digital Era
authors: Samaya Pillai, Manik Kadam, Pankaj Pathak, C.G. Balaji, Sri Krishna Kireeti Nandiraju, Vikash Yadav
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Advanced Computing and Emerging Technologies (ACET)
abstract: Healthcare digitization is now a worldwide necessity rather than a pipe dream. The Electronic Health Record (EHR), is a technology that has developed from basic data storage to an intelligent, networked platform. It influences the direction of healthcare. EHRs have become essential in recent years for improving care coordination, expediting clinical procedures, and providing real-time access to patient data. This is true for patient data across borders and institutions. As healthcare systems around the world faced previously unheard-of difficulties, the COVID-19 pandemic further increased need for interoperable, secure, and scalable EHR systems. However, despite tremendous advancements in the technology and EHR enduring obstacles still exist. Usability issues, data privacy worries, and interoperability gaps still prevent EHRs from being widely adopted and optimized. This study examines the global importance of EHR in the modern era, emphasizing how it advances patient-centered care, fortifies public health infrastructure. The newer advancements through cutting-edge technologies like blockchain, artificial intelligence, and mobile health also play a crucial role in the shaping of EHRs. In order to create robust, data-driven healthcare systems, the study ends by highlighting the main issues, policy considerations, and future research avenues.

title: The Impact of Digital Technology on the Green and Low-Carbon Development of SME
authors: Ming Guo, Kai Qi
publish: Proceedings of the 2025 2nd International Conference on Economic Data Analytics and Artificial Intelligence

title: Physics-Informed Modeling of Coupled Electro–Thermo–Mechanical Degradation in ADAS Interconnects Under Self-Heating and Bias Temperature Instability
authors: Shih-Lin Lin
publish: IEEE Transactions on Components, Packaging and Manufacturing Technology
abstract: Reliability degradation in nanoscale automotive interconnects is increasingly governed by coupled electrical, thermal, and mechanical effects. Here, a physics-informed electro– thermo–mechanical framework is developed to predict the lifetime of 5-nm ADAS interconnects subjected to electromigration (EM), bias temperature instability (BTI), and vibration-induced fatigue. The model extends the classical Black and Basquin relations to capture concurrent electrothermal and mechanical degradation mechanisms under self-heating and bias compensation. Analytical results reveal that thermal–BTI coupling accelerates EM failure by approximately seventeenfold compared with the nominal baseline. A competing-failure formulation is further introduced to quantify the lifetime trade-off between electrical and mechanical degradation, enabling the determination of design envelopes that satisfy 20-year automotive reliability targets. Finally, a physics-informed neural network (PINN) is trained to extrapolate beyond the simulated stress range, embedding the governing EM– vibration equations as soft constraints. This hybrid physics–AI framework offers a transferable approach for predicting reliability in nanoscale automotive and electronic materials.
