title: Characterization of the Mechanics of Single Cell-Cell Junctions
authors: Timothy Goldsmith, Bahareh Tajvidi Safa, Ruiguo Yang
publish: 2025 IEEE 18th International Conference on Nano/Molecular Medicine &amp; Engineering (NANOMED)

title: Electrospun Carbon Black-Incorporated Nanofibers for Enhanced Facial Sebum Adsorption
authors: Princess Joy E. Naig, Zih-Yin Kuo, Yang-Shun Wu, Kuo-Yung Hung
publish: 2025 IEEE 18th International Conference on Nano/Molecular Medicine &amp; Engineering (NANOMED)

title: Beyond Likes and Shares: Gamification and Machine Learning as Drivers of Enhanced Social Media Brand Engagement on Gen Z
authors: Asmita Gaikwad, Geeta Srinivas Rao, Monica Goud
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Innovations in Engineering, Science and Technology for Sustainable Development (ICEST)
abstract: Generation <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{Z}$</tex> are the individuals born between 1997 and 2012 and represents a distinct group with unique demographic characterized by digital nativity and distinct consumption patterns. The researchers explore the integration of gamification elements with machine learning algorithms to enhance brand engagement strategies on social media platforms w.r.t. Generation Z, and also proposes a novel framework which combines adaptive gamification technicalities with predictive analysis by analyzing behavioral patterns, engagement metrics and technology. The experimental results demonstrated a 47% increase in engagement rates and 32% improvement in brand loyalty metrics when implementing ML-driven gamified experiences compared to traditional social media marketing approaches. The research exposes that personalized achievement systems, dynamic reward mechanisms, and realtime behavioral adaptation significantly influence Generation Z's brand perception and purchasing decisions. The results offer valuable insights for marketers and technology developers seeking to create more effective digital engagement strategies for the Generation Z Consumers.

title: Measurements and Integrated Strategies for Energy Efficiency in Hospitality Facilities
authors: Andrea Fioravanti, Simone Mari, Andrea Silvestri, Haseeb Shams, Federico Centi, Edoardo Fiorucci
publish: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Metrology for Green Technologies, Renewable Energy and Ecological Sustainability (MetroGREENST)
abstract: Hospitality buildings, among the highest energy consumers in the tertiary sector, are increasingly subject to regulatory and operational pressure to improve their energy efficiency. This paper presents a methodological analysis of efficiency strategies, combining a technical review of best practices with field-based measurements. A case study conducted on the University Residence in L’ Aquila enables a comprehensive evaluation of the operational performance and energy use of such facilities under real-world conditions. Results demonstrate considerable potential for energy, economic, and environmental impact reduction in the Italian hospitality sector, supporting the validity of the proposed measurement-driven approach.

title: Wireless Sensor Network Design for Cultural Heritage Monitoring
authors: Marco Sento, Davide Sgrò, Christian Dellisanti, Anna Laura Tassi, Laura Santagostini, Alberto Vallan
publish: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Metrology for Green Technologies, Renewable Energy and Ecological Sustainability (MetroGREENST)
abstract: The conservation of cultural heritage artifacts and monuments requires rigorous environmental monitoring to mitigate the risk of degradation caused by adverse environmental conditions. In cultural heritage preservation, chemical and physical deterioration processes, biodeterioration, and material instability phenomena are appearing with increased frequency and severity, enabled by pollutant and microclimatic fluctuations that compromise the preservation conditions established by conservation science. In this scenario, Wireless Sensor Networks (WSNs) offer a scalable, non-intrusive solution to monitor the most critical environmental parameters in real time. This paper illustrates the application of a WSN in the context of environmental quality monitoring in cultural sites, highlighting the metrological challenges posed by current standards in the field. The proposed architecture is based on sensing nodes composed of a general-purpose structure, featuring long range communication capabilities, specialised by means of front-ends conditioned for the measurement of hazardous quantities, in accordance with international regulations. The proposed architecture combines innovative metrological management with modern Internet of Things (IoT) design principles, with the aim of providing a comprehensive and remotely manageable monitoring solution for sensitive cultural heritage environments.

title: Synergizing CNN's Features with Fuzzy C-Means for Accurate Brain Tumor Segmentation
authors: Preeti Chaudhary, Lisa Gopal, Noor Mohd, Yogendra Narayan Prajapati, Dev Baloni
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Advanced Computing and Emerging Technologies (ACET)
abstract: Detecting tumors in brain remains one of the most critical and crucial factors in medical images, as it directly impacts patient survival and treatment planning. This work feature out the key difficulties, recent technological advances, and current directions in tumor's identification. For early detection, radiologist depends on image based techniques such as: Computed-Tomography and Magnetic-Resonance Imaging. Traditionally, all the interpretation was carried out manually by radiologists, but the inclusion of DL especially “CNN's” has transformed the process. With access to big and clear datasets these models can improve diagnostic accuracy while also helpin g to reduce the workload on healthcare professionals. Even as CNN models in our research achieved a staggering 97.87% accuracy, outstanding problems persist. These are the lack of high-quality datasets and difficulty in making AI-driven decisions more transparent. Our research aims at the core objective of classifying image pixels as either normal or abnormal through the use of statistical and texture-based analysis.

title: Shaping the Future of Education Through Technology: A Conceptual Framework for Bahrain
authors: Mahmood Akbar, Aalia Mehboob, Hasan Razzaqi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)

title: Convergence-Enhanced KLMS Algorithm: A Fixed-Point Perspective for Nonlinear System
authors: Tingsen Zhang, Dongjie Bi, Xifeng Li, Yongle Xie
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Micro/Nano Sensors for AI, Healthcare, and Robotics (NSENS)
abstract: This paper reconstructs the kernel least mean squares algorithm (KLMS) from the perspective of the fixed-point strategy, thereby introducing the fixed-point kernel least mean squares algorithm (FPKLMS). Specifically, we reformulate the updating rules and demonstrate the superiority of the proposed algorithm in two applications: nonlinear channel equalization and nonlinear time series prediction. Experimental results demonstrate that FPKLMS achieves significantly improved convergence speed and accuracy compared to KLMS, while also eliminating dependence on the initial learning rate. This study not only deepens the theoretical understanding of KLMS but also introduces a novel convergence proof method, providing new insights into the design and analysis of kernel adaptive filtering algorithms.

title: Current Integration Method as a Highly Reliable Tool for Measuring Dielectric Properties
authors: Yoshimichi Ohki, Naohiro Hozumi, Yasuhiro Tanaka
abstract: Explore the current integration method, a revitalized, versatile, and practical tool for examining dielectric behaviors and evaluating insulation integrity, particularly of polymeric insulators and devices that use them.

title: Design of a PSM High-Voltage Power Module with Boost Circuit
authors: Zhibin Sun, Jian Zhang, Yiyun Huang, Wendi Fan, Rui Guan, Chenglong Huang, Linkai He, Yu Zhou
publish: 2025 4th Asia Power and Electrical Technology Conference (APET)
abstract: As nuclear fusion experiments progress, fusion devices impose increasingly stringent demands upon their high-voltage power supplies. Existing high-voltage power systems suffer from issues including substantial footprint, excessive numbers of power modules, and non-adjustable output voltages. This paper incorporates a boost circuit topology into existing PSM(Pulse Step Modulator) power modules. By completing the design of this power module, it fulfils the requirements for the fusion device's high-voltage power supply system: reduced overall volume and continuous, adjustable output voltage within a specified range for individual modules. This work provides a reference for the subsequent holistic design of the power supply system.

title: RIS with Minimal Actuation: Budgeted Phase-Only Control under QoS and Fairness
authors: Wafaa M. R. Shakir, Jinan Charafeddine, Zeinab Mhanna
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)

title: Highly Efficient Solid State Organic Electrochemical Transistor-Based Tactile Sensor
authors: Rupesh Kumar, Ritika Jajoriya, Akshay Moudgil
publish: 2025 IEEE 18th International Conference on Nano/Molecular Medicine &amp; Engineering (NANOMED)
abstract: Organic electrochemical transistors (OECTs) in solid-state configurations present an attractive approach for improving tactile sensor technology, offering low operating voltages, high sensitivity, and robust mechanical flexibility. In this work, we demonstrate a highly efficient solid-state OECT-based device. The resulting sensor achieves ultra-high pressure sensitivity at sub-1V operating voltage, exhibiting a low limit of detection, outstanding durability, and stable response over prolonged operation. Compared to previous liquid electrolyte OECT and other solid-state sensor technologies, our device significantly advances tactile transduction, enabling new possibilities for large-area sensor arrays in soft robotics, health monitoring, and human-machine interface applications. This study illustrates the underlying contact-modulated ionic doping mechanism and demonstrates the practical integration of the sensors for high-fidelity detection of static and dynamic pressure signals.

title: A Novel Temperature and Aging Invariant Voltage Droop Monitor Architecture with 96.94% Accuracy
authors: Anup Deka, Shobhit Tyagi, Manoja D
publish: 2026 39th International Conference on VLSI Design &amp; 25th International Conference on Embedded Systems (VLSID)
abstract: Modern CPU needs fast voltage and current monitors to maximize performance-per-watt. Various techniques used for power performance trade-off like dynamic voltage and frequency scaling, dynamic task scheduling, voltage emergency predictions needs fast voltage/current data to be efficient in lowering overall Vmin guardband. Faster sensing also helps in improving system robustness to power virus attacks. Existing voltage droop monitors have higher latency, require post silicon trimming for multiple voltage and temperature and their accuracy is severely impacted by HCI/BTI degradation over the lifetime. This paper presents a highly accurate temperature/aging (HCI/BTI) invariant architecture eliminating complicated multivoltage post-silicon trim, have substantially lower response time per readout 5 ns at 10% lower area. This enables a robust, lowlatency power management, paving the way for more efficient and reliable CPU operation.

title: Economic Load Dispatch Model Revisited
authors: Ali Ridha Ali
publish: 2025 Advances in Science and Engineering Technology International Conferences (ASET)
abstract: The economic load dispatch (ELD) package in energy management systems (EMS) is crucial for any electric power system. Ensuring optimal operation can save millions of dollars. Based on this, many techniques have been presented in the literature and promoted as the best ELD solvers, with some numerical results provided as evidence of their superiority. However, there remains doubt about the ELD mathematical optimization model itself. For example, all known techniques are built on the assumption that each unit is connected to one specific busbar, whereas, in reality, multiple units can be connected to one common busbar, as seen in most thermal power stations. Therefore, the entire ELD model needs to be revisited and modified with some essential corrections and realistic constraints, which is the main goal of this paper. If this realistic model is correctly coded, it can be used with any existing <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$n$</tex>-dimensional optimization algorithm presented in the literature. Implementing this non-ordinary ELD model could increase profitability by identifying realistic solutions that satisfy both optimality and feasibility criteria.

title: GAN-Driven Synthetic Data Generation for Enhancing Insider Threat Detection Models
authors: Monali Gulhane, Nitin Rakesh, Alavikunhu Panthakkan, Majida Appathil, Balamurugan Balusamy
publish: 2025 Advances in Science and Engineering Technology International Conferences (ASET)
abstract: Organizations are at great risk from insider threats because they come from trusted people who are allowed to access private data and systems. Because these dangers are usually hard to spot because they are hidden and it's hard to tell the difference between normal user behaviour and bad behaviour, traditional cybersecurity measures often fail to find them. This study looks into how Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) can be used to create fake data to make insider threat detection models better. By creating precise fake datasets that mimic the patterns of both upper and immoral conduct, generative adverse networks (GANs) may provide enormous amounts of various information that may be hard to get in real life. The have a look at seems into how GAN-generated fake statistics could be capable of assist machine getting to know fashions do their jobs better, in particular when it comes to finding minor insider danger moves that might not be picked up in ordinary datasets. The method starts with teaching a GAN to create fake data about user behaviour. This data is then added to common models for finding anomalies, like Random Forest, Support Vector Machine (SVM), and Deep Neural Networks (DNNs). We check how well this mixed method works by looking at model performance measures such as F1-score, accuracy, precision, and recall. The results show that adding fake data not only improves the model's ability to spot insider threats more accurately, but it also makes the model more stable by expanding the training dataset, lowering the risk of overfitting, and making the model better at generalizing. This method can also make it easier to get labelled data for training reasons, especially in rare but important insider danger situations.

title: Efficient AI model deployment using quantization analysis tool
authors: Dwith Chenna, Kanishka Macherla
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: As deep learning models are increasingly deployed on resource-constrained devices, the demand for efficient model optimization techniques continues to grow. Effective deployment of AI models on edge and low-power platforms requires optimization methods that reduce model size and computational cost while maintaining high accuracy. This paper presents Quantization Analysis Tool, a practical system designed to streamline quantization workflows and support performance-efficient model deployment. Built on the ONNX framework for broad interoperability, the tool provides detailed layer-wise sensitivity analysis, visualization of weight and activation distributions, and insights to guide precision selection. By identifying layers that are resilient or sensitive to reduced precision, the tool enables developers to make informed trade-offs between model size, latency, and accuracy. Experimental evaluations across multiple neural network architectures demonstrate that the tool effectively improves the quantized accuracy, leading to improved efficiency in real-world deployment scenarios. The tool also provides developers valuable insights into the effects on quantization on the model and its accuracy. This work highlights the tool’s capabilities, practical applications, and its role in enabling efficient AI model deployment through robust quantization analysis.

title: Implementation of a Robust Penalty-Interior-Point Method for Optimal Power Flow Analysis
authors: Wei Cui, Junheng Li, Xiaowei Ma, Meng Wang, Cong Wang, Penghan Li, Chao Duan
publish: 2025 4th Asia Power and Electrical Technology Conference (APET)
abstract: With the continuous expansion of power system scale and the increasing requirements for safety, operational efficiency, and power quality, in-depth research on optimal power flow (OPF) algorithms has become increasingly important. In this paper, a mathematical model of the OPF problem is first established, including the objective function, equality constraints, and inequality constraints. Then, building upon the traditional interior-point method, a penalty parameter is introduced, and the theoretical foundation of the penalty-interior-point method is systematically derived. This derivation covers the principles for calculating the search direction and step size, termination conditions, and other related aspects. Subsequently, the overall algorithmic flow is outlined. Finally, a power flow program implementing the proposed method is developed in <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{C}++$</tex> and validated on the Three-Machine Nine-Bus system. The results demonstrate that the solutions obtained are consistent with those of the well-established solver IPOPT, while achieving a significant reduction in generation costs, thereby demonstrating the soundness and effectiveness of the proposed algorithm.

title: Customer Feedback Sentiment Analysis of Social Media Text Using Machine Learning Classifiers
authors: Sonica Upadhyay, Yogesh Kumar Gupta
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Advanced Computing and Emerging Technologies (ACET)
abstract: Customer Feedback Sentiment (CFS) analysis is a process for exploratory text data analysis of customers' feedback text sentiments that was stored on the web. The customer's feedback text sentiments for a specific product are categorized into three distinct categories: positive, negative, or neutral, based on customer reviews. Due to the widespread use of social media platforms, people express their feelings in various forms, including textual data, audio, and video on the web, resulting in a massive amount of unstructured data generated on the internet every day. It becomes increasingly essential to analyse or review customer feedback sentiments digitally and automatically. This automatically digitized feedback text review system is beneficial for understanding consumers' overall feelings or moods towards a specific product or company. CFS analysis is now widely used for classifying customer feedback sentiments in various product reviews, movie review ratings, and other applications. In this research study, we examine and classify customer feedback text sentiments for Flipkart products using multiple Machine Learning (ML) classifiers for multiclass text sentiment analysis.

title: Decoding Communication: Confidence Assessment Through CNN-Based Speech Signals Analysis
authors: Kshitiz Srivastav, Mayank Srivastava, Kshitiz Singhal, Shanu Sharma
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Advanced Computing and Emerging Technologies (ACET)
abstract: A person's level of self-assurance plays a very important part in the development of their attitude as well as their communication abilities. The mind of a human being is inherently conditioned to evaluate the self-assurance of another person who is speaking. Thus, confidence in speech plays a crucial role while communicating or interacting with others. A problem of low self-esteem is a major issue in individuals during interviews or professional settings. A platform for self-evaluation of confidence may help individuals assess their confidence and further practice better communication. The speech signals convey a variety of features to characterize the quality of speech. If characterized efficiently, the speech signals can be classified as confident and non-confident accordingly. In this article, the speech signals are thoroughly analyzed to extract the significant features for the development of automated speech signal classification, as confident and not confident. The study analyzes various studies on speech signal processing, identifying key observations, data collection, acoustic feature extraction for confidence assessment, such as pitch, prosody features, etc., and machine learning-optimized modeling of these features. After analysis, the MFCC features are extracted from the speech dataset, and the spectrograms are used to train the CNN-based classifier. The model is trained and tested on self-collected and labeled data and has shown an accuracy of 94 percent.

title: Real-Time Monitoring and Behavioral Analysis for Sustainable Fish Farming
authors: Omaima Anwar Jarndal, Alyaziyah Metaeb Alwahshi, Malaz Abdelmoneim Ahmed, Saeed Abdallah
publish: 2025 Advances in Science and Engineering Technology International Conferences (ASET)
abstract: Sustainable fish farming faces challenges such as inefficient feeding, inadequate health monitoring, and poor water quality management, which reduce productivity and harm the environment. To address these issues, this paper proposes a comprehensive solution leveraging automation, sensor networks, and artificial intelligence (AI). The system integrates an automated feeding mechanism that distributes food evenly, minimizing waste and improving fish nutrition. A sensor array monitors critical parameters, including water temperature, pH levels, light intensity, water level, and Total Dissolved Solids (TDS). The data collected by these sensors is transmitted to a mobile application, providing users with real-time updates. Additionally, AI techniques are employed to detect abnormal fish behaviour using real-time footage captured by a camera connected to the system. The AI analyses fish movements and patterns to enable timely intervention. The physical prototype is equipped with sensors and a camera connected to a microcontroller, which gathers real-time data and transmits it to the application for visualization and decision-making. This solution enhances productivity, reduces risks, and establishes a foundation for sustainable aquaculture practices, contributing to environmental conservation and food security.

title: Strategic Analysis and Actionable Suggestions for Addressing Shortcomings of Traditional Data-Driven Method on Sustainable Manufacturing
authors: Juncheng Pan, Junjie Pan
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Sustainable Energy and Net-Zero Emissions (SENZE)
abstract: Data-driven methodologies are indisputably essential for achieving sustainable outcomes and optimizing resource usage within the manufacturing sector. However, there are at least two dilemmas that still constrain the applicability of research in this area: existing methods either suffer from limited “generalizability” due to overly specific, narrow application scopes, or they propose high-level theoretical frameworks that fundamentally omit discussion of “engineering feasibility” and real-world implementation challenges-this gap between theory and practice hinds the applicability for industry adoption for effective green transformation. Therefore, this work initiates a strategic analysis by providing unique insights into the core limitations of traditional sustainability research from a practical operational business perspective. This foundational analysis allows us to subsequently derive and propose robust, data-driven operational guidelines specifically designed to conquer these inherent structural shortcomings. Following that, we proceed to a deep analysis of the potential engineering challenges based on real-world manufacturing situations, which is often overlooked by traditional data science engineering researchers whose primary focus is on the performance of algorithms on specific aspects of their target application. In light of this analysis, we provide corresponding actionable suggestions that will shed light on the solution for problems closely related to real-world data-driven manufacturing settings. We hope this brief perspective can offer a feasible and valuable suggestion for future researchers who rely on it and could extend beyond it to address real-world limitations and deliver actionable results.

title: Partial Product Grouping-Based Approximate Multipliers for Deep Learning Inference
authors: Boggu Priestly Samarpana, Siddharth R.K., Geeta Shet, Nithin Kumar Y.B., Vasantha M.H.
publish: 2026 39th International Conference on VLSI Design &amp; 25th International Conference on Embedded Systems (VLSID)
abstract: This paper proposes a GPU-accelerated, hardware-faithful framework for evaluating Approximate Carry Disregard Multipliers (ACDMs) in AI and signal processing pipelines. Custom CUDA kernels emulate three multiplier designs—Conventional, Proposed Design 1, and Proposed Design 2—enabling direct integration into CNN models. Experiments with ResNet18 and MobileNetV2 on TinyImageNet, and ShuffleNetV2 on CIFAR-10, assess inference throughput, Top-1/Top-5 accuracy, and mean relative error distance (MRED). Proposed designs achieve up to 32% higher throughput with minimal accuracy degradation, particularly in ResNet18 and ShuffleNetV2. Image resizing tasks further confirm high visual fidelity (PSNR <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$>30 ~\text{dB}$</tex>, SSIM <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$>0.93$</tex>). Results highlight proposed ACDMs' suitability for power- and latency-constrained platforms, owing to their structural simplicity, timing predictability, and robustness to quantization.

title: Coordinated Restoration of Power-Transportation-Information Networks Considering UAV Relay Communication
authors: Shichuang Li, Le Liu, Xiaoning Kang, Xuming Chen, Jinliang Fu, Chenjun Fan, Xi Huang, Qi Tong
publish: 2025 4th Asia Power and Electrical Technology Conference (APET)
abstract: Flood disasters, among the most frequent and destructive natural hazards, often submerge and damage distribution facilities, disrupt communications, and trigger widespread power outages, severely impacting power supply and emergency response in affected areas. To mitigate these challenges, this paper proposes a UAV-assisted relay communication-based coordinated restoration method for distribution and communication networks, enhancing post-disaster load recovery via integrated pre-disaster deployment and post-disaster scheduling. First, flood impacts on distribution and transportation networks are analyzed to generate fault scenarios. Then, a two-stage optimization model is developed for pre-disaster deployment and post-disaster restoration, based on the worst-case scenario and disaster traits. Leveraging UAV relay communication's flexibility and cost benefits, a connectivity-gated framework unifies communication coverage recovery with distributed resource scheduling. Case studies on the IEEE 33-bus system validate the method's effectiveness and underscore UAV-assisted communication's key role in boosting load restoration performance.

title: Performance Evaluation of Pattern-Based Prediction in Modern AI Systems with Limited Reasoning Budgets
authors: Muhammad Badruddin Khan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) exhibit strong natural language understanding but encounter substantial difficulties with pattern-based prediction when constrained from expressing explicit step-by-step reasoning. This study examines pattern-based predictions under reasoning suppression using a custom dataset derived from 8 arithmetic formulas. Four advanced LLMs—GPT-5, GPT-5.1, Claude Opus 4.1, and Claude Sonnet 4.5—were evaluated through controlled prompts via the Poe API, with outputs restricted to final numeric answers exclusively. The four modern AI systems were evaluated using accuracy and consistency as the main assessment criteria. Based on the data, Claude-Opus 4.1 consistently outperforms all models with approximately 79% accuracy across all three experiments, while Claude-Sonnet 4.5 achieves around 68% accuracy, significantly surpassing both GPT-5 (~42%) and GPT-5.1 (~50%). Moreover, Claude-Opus 4.1 and Claude-Sonnet 4.5 demonstrated perfect consistency with 0% zero-consistency errors (the worst consistency level in this study), while GPT-5 and GPT-5.1 showed concerning inconsistency rates of 22.3% and 24.2% respectively, indicating they fail to maintain stable responses. However, it's important to note that these experiments were conducted under limited token budgets, which may have disproportionately impacted the GPT models' performance. In unconstrained environments with higher token allowances, GPT systems are expected to perform significantly better as they could have more capacity for reasoning and generating comprehensive responses, potentially narrowing the performance gap observed in these constrained settings.

title: A Deep Generative Convolutional Autoencoder Image Colourizer
authors: Hagar Shalabi, Gerard T. McKee, Walid Hussein
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)

title: Research on Ecological English Teaching System Based on Distance Technology of Computer Network
authors: Xiuying Zhou
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Science, and Information Processing (AICSIP)
abstract: This article attempts to construct an ecological teaching system for English classes in information-based universities from the perspective of educational ecology. It mainly focuses on a multimodal teaching paradigm of English class in the multimedia network environment and is expected to improve the teaching effectiveness of college English, thus enhancing students' interests in English learning. At the same time, it is expected to construct an online ecological teaching system for English teaching. The research focuses on the overall structure of the college English teaching system, the design of the core modules of the system, the critical technology, and the realization based on the intrinsic characteristics of computer network distance technology. Research results demonstrate that the constructed ecological teaching system could promote the interaction between teachers and students before, during, and after class. The application of the constructed system in English class can enhance students' interests in English learning and effectively improve the teaching quality.

title: Analyzing Data Integrity and Transmission Patterns in a Smart Oyster Mushroom Cultivation
authors: Shem L. Gonzales, Chrysler Van D. Conde, Rex C. Legaspi, Jerry I. Teleron, Jenie L. Plender-Nabas
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: This study presents an exploratory data analysis (EDA) of Internet of Things (IoT) sensor data collected from an Oyster Mushroom cultivation environment in Surigao del Norte State University - Mainit Campus, Philippines, focusing on temperature, pH, and humidity parameters. The dataset, comprising 272,378 entries, underwent rigorous preprocessing, including the conversion of updated_at and date columns to datetime objects, revealing significant data characteristics such as a high prevalence of 0-second time differences (80.68% of readings). Notably, these instances corresponded to 12,119 unique combinations of sensor values, indicating a high-resolution, potentially multi-sensor, or rapid sampling data acquisition system. Descriptive statistics revealed distinct profiles for each parameter. Temperature maintained a mean of 29.13°C (±1.72°C), pH exhibited remarkable stability with a mean of 5.73 (±0.17), while humidity averaged 82.89% (±7.29%). Time-series visualizations corroborated these findings, showing continuous fluctuations in temperature and humidity, contrasted by the exceptional stability of pH with only occasional, brief deviations. Anomaly detection using the Interquartile Range (IQR) method further highlighted these patterns. Temperature recorded no anomalies, humidity showed a small number of deviations with 438 data points (0.16%), but pH presented a significant proportion of anomalous readings with 38,578 data points (14.16%). The findings suggest a well-monitored cultivation environment where the IoT system effectively captures dynamic environmental shifts. The pronounced stability of pH despite frequently detected anomalies warrants further investigation into sensor calibration, control mechanisms, or specific environmental events. This analysis provides foundational insights for optimizing environmental control, refining sensor deployment strategies, and improving predictive analytics in IoT-enabled agricultural systems.

title: A Real-Time Spatiotemporal Classification System for Assisting Visually Impaired Individuals
authors: Loubna Bougheloum, Mounir Bousbia-Salah, Maamar Bettayeb
publish: 2025 Advances in Science and Engineering Technology International Conferences (ASET)
abstract: Assistive navigation systems for visually impaired individuals must both detect obstacles and interpret their behavior in dynamic environments. This paper presents a realtime spatiotemporal classification framework that integrates DINOv2 (DIstillation of knowledge with NO labels v2) transformer-based embeddings with a Convolutional LSTM (Long Short-Term Memory) network to distinguish static from dynamic obstacles. Unlike conventional methods that separate detection from behavior analysis, our model jointly performs both tasks directly from raw video sequences. DINOv2 extracts robust spatial features, while ConvLSTM captures temporal motion patterns for behavior-aware classification. Trained on 6,800 annotated urban clips, the system achieved 91.7% accuracy, 91.6% F1-score, and outperformed threshold-based tracking by 13.2 %, while sustaining real-time performance at 22 FPS. Usability testing with six visually impaired participants showed a 42.3 % gain in navigation confidence, high perceived alert accuracy <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(4.7 / 5)$</tex>, and low cognitive load <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(2.1 / 5)$</tex>, highlighting the system's practical value for safe, real-time navigation in complex urban environments.

title: Overview of Poisoning Attacks and Defence Against Large AI Models
authors: Wenjing Hou, Hong Wen, Juntao Mao, Wenjun Luo, Peng Zhang, Xinchen Xu
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Science, and Information Processing (AICSIP)
abstract: Large language models (LLM) based on artificial intelligence (AI) have changed people's lifestyles and improved production efficiency, but they also face security challenges such as impersonation, deception attacks, and data poisoning attacks. Data poisoning attacks can seriously threaten the security and reliability of AI models. Therefore, it is urgent and necessary to explore the mechanism of poisoning attacks in large model environments and develop corresponding defense measures. Now research mainly focuses on image processing or classification scenarios, with little consideration given to online learning. This paper mainly discusses the development and challenges faced by poisoning attacks and defense methods under large models.

title: Least-squares model-reference adaptive control: extension to higher relative degree plants
authors: Ramon R. Costa
abstract: A Lyapunov-based least-squares modelreference adaptive controller (LS-MRAC) was recently developed for plants with relative degree one. The algorithm exhibits a remarkable tracking error transient performance while also ensuring fast parameter convergence. In this paper, we generalize this algorithm to the more complex and challenging case of plants with higher relative degree. The key elements for the design procedure are the Strictly Positive Real (SPR) principle and the backstepping technique. The idea here is to employ the backstepping technique to retrieve the control law from its filtered signal. This solution, however, requires the derivative of the tracking error, which is provided by a first-order passive observer. The SPR principle, so fundamental to the controller design, is also exploited in the observer design. The proposed SPR observer is the key to replicating the analysis performed for the controller. As a result, least-squares update laws can be employed for both.

title: Compact Dual-Band Balanced MIMO Antenna for Wi-Fi 6/6E Applications in Tablet Computers with Metal Housing
authors: Yilin Gao, Hongbin Zhu, Ning Ma, Changjiang Deng, Hongda Lu, Zhengpeng Wang, Steven Gao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Antennas and Propagation
abstract: A compact dual-band balanced MIMO antenna is presented in this article for Wi-Fi 6/6E applications in metal-housed tablet computers. To address the challenges of small clearance and metal housing, a systematic evolution of the proposed design, derived from a slot antenna pair with zero edge-to-edge spacing, highlights four key contributions. Firstly, two balanced slot modes, are deliberately excited to support consistent coverage and maintain robust performance against environmental detuning. Secondly, coupled-loaded C-shaped rings (CSRs) are first employed to simultaneously achieve a 59.5% size reduction and generate a transmission zero (TZ) via Fano-type resonance in the 6-GHz band. Thirdly, an additional TZ is realized through modal cancellation, using a chip capacitor, to enhance isolation in the 5-GHz band. Consequently, dual-band coverage and decoupling across Wi-Fi 6/6E bands (5.15–5.83 GHz and 5.925–7.125 GHz) are achieved within a compact antenna footprint of 0.21×0.07 λL2. Finally, an 8×8 MIMO antenna system prototype is fabricated and experimentally verified in a metal-housed tablet platform. Measured results confirm a –6 dB impedance bandwidth (IBW) from 4.3 GHz to 7.5 GHz. Within Wi-Fi 6/6E bands, measured total efficiency from 53.0% to 73.1% and isolation over 12.9 dB are obtained, with acceptable diversity performance.

title: Geometry-Aware Physics Informed PointNet (GeoPIPN) for Fast Thermal Distribution Prediction in Additive Manufacturing of Unseen Part Geometries
authors: Amirul Islam Saimon, Raghav Gnanasambandam, Zhenyu Kong
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: This paper proposes a Geometry-Aware Physics Informed PointNet (GeoPIPN), a deep learning-based fast prediction model for solving Partial Differential Equations (PDE) across unseen additive manufacturing (AM) geometries. Thermal distributions in AM parts are crucial and governed by diffusion models in the form of PDEs, which dictate their spatial and temporal behavior. Proper thermal distribution control ensures desired material properties, uniform microstructure, and dimensional accuracy – key to AM part quality and reliability. Existing thermal modeling primarily utilizes numerical solvers, namely, the Finite Element Methods (FEM), which are computationally costly for the fast-paced, iterative geometric design phase. Furthermore, these methods are tailored to a specific part geometry, requiring rerunning for each new geometry, making them inefficient for designs that change frequently. Our GeoPIPN considers various part geometries, represented as point clouds, as input, and is trained in a physics-informed setting where the learning objective combines PDE residual loss, boundary condition loss, and measurement-based loss from sparse sensor locations. During the training, both geometry-aware global and boundary features, as well as point-wise local features, are learned. The resulting GeoPIPN can predict the thermal distribution for a new AM part geometry at inference time without rerunning the FEM simulations. In the case studies, we demonstrate the superior performance of our GeoPIPN over benchmark methods on 2D AM test problems. Consequently, GeoPIPN offers an efficient solution for fast and accurate AM design iteration in 2D settings and provides a foundation for future extensions to fully 3D AM problems.

title: Multi-scale Temporal Causal Network for Speech Emotion Recognition
authors: Sijia Fei, Qiang Feng, Fei Gao
abstract: Speech Emotion Recognition (SER) has a wide range of applications, as it analyzes acoustic features in speech signals to infer the speaker’s emotional state and enhance interaction experiences. For speech sequences with varying lengths and complex emotional characteristics, relying solely on fixed-scale feature information and temporal causal characteristics makes it challenging to accurately identify the speaker's emotional state. To address this issue, a multi-scale temporal causal network (MSTCN) is proposed to effectively capture multi-scale feature representations and long-range temporal dependencies in speech signals. Specifically, the multi-scale semantic feature extraction block performs semantic feature extraction at different receptive field scales for speech sequence features, capturing multi-scale semantic information within the speech sequence to establish a local-to-global feature learning pathway. Furthermore, the temporal-sensing block employs dilated causal convolutions to achieve bidirectional sequence feature modeling, capturing bidirectional semantic information and time-causality details within the speech signal to represent spatial-temporal characteristics of emotional changes. Simultaneously, a one-dimensional efficient attention (ECA) mechanism is introduced to reduce noise interference, enhancing the capture of effective speech sequence feature details. Finally, by integrating multi-layer forward-backward features, the proposed MSTCN precisely characterizes emotional change trends, achieving high-precision perception of emotional details. Experimental results on six language datasets (EMODB: 96.64%; EMOVO: 93.53%; RAVDESS: 93.13%; SAVEE: 91.25%; CASIA: 95.17%; IEMOCAP: 71.33%) demonstrate that the proposed method exhibits highly competitive emotion recognition capabilities compared to state-of-the-art techniques, indicating promising practical applications.

title: Estimating Accident Clearance Time: A Comparative Study of Genetic Algorithm-Optimized Neural Network and Random Forest Approaches
authors: Igor Betkier, Mateusz Oszczypała
abstract: This study applies artificial intelligence (AI) techniques to develop and evaluate predictive models for road accident clearance time - an essential element in traffic management and public safety. Using an initial dataset of 1,867 accidents from Poland in 2023, which after validation and removal of incomplete records yielded 1,497 fully validated observations for machine learning, two models - Multilayer Perceptron (MLP) and Random Forest (RF), were assessed for their accuracy in forecasting clearance durations. The dataset, compiled from three national agencies, includes 90 variables describing accident, infrastructure, weather, and traffic conditions. Feature importance analysis revealed that a compact subset of 15 predictors preserved nearly the full explanatory signal. Among them, average traffic flow, population density, and emergency response delays were most influential. RF demonstrated higher stability across data splits, achieving a Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE) of 22.25%, indicating strong predictive capabilities and practical potential for crisis management. The MLP model also showed good performance, with a MAPE of 23.44% across the full dataset, suggesting adaptability and room for further tuning. A sensitivity analysis revealed a notable threshold effect: clearance times in low-density areas were approximately half those in urban zones, highlighting the need for context-aware response strategies. These findings support the application of machine learning in road safety systems and underscore the value of tailored resource allocation. The study demonstrates that both models can effectively synthesize complex data and adapt to dynamic road environments. It also suggests that with refinement, such models can support real-time decision-making in accident response without significant accuracy trade-offs.

title: Impact of AI Literacy on Self-Efficacy Among Student Translators: Serial Mediating Roles of Self-Regulated Learning and Technology Acceptance
authors: Jianyu He, Jian Wang
abstract: The recent advancement of artificial intelligence (AI) technology has revolutionized the language translation industry and unavoidably exerts impact on student translators in multiple aspects. In this context, this study investigated the antecedents of student translators’ self-efficacy in AI-enhanced contexts, an underexplored area in current academic research. Data were collected via validated quantitative scales from 446 undergraduate student translators across eight public universities in China, who self-reported their AI literacy, technology acceptance, self-regulation, and self-efficacy in leveraging AI tools for translation tasks. Results indicate that AI literacy, technology acceptance, and self-regulation positively predicted translation self-efficacy. Furthermore, AI literacy served as a positive predictor of technology acceptance and self-regulation in AI-assisted translation, while technology acceptance positively predicted self-regulation. Additionally, technology acceptance and self-regulated learning (SRL) acted as partial mediators between AI literacy and translation self-efficacy, and the serial mediation pathway “AI literacy → technology acceptance → SRL → translation self-efficacy” was confirmed. This research offers theoretical and practical insights for translation education research and pedagogy.

title: Improving Single-Frequency PPP with Multilayer Perceptron-based Ionospheric Delay Model
authors: Yuxuan Tan, Xin Li, Xingxing Li, Jiande Huang, Yuxuan Zhou, Junjie Han
publish: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
abstract: Ionospheric delay, as a significant error source in GNSS raw observations, deserves rigorous handling in single-frequency precise point positioning (SF-PPP). Currently, SF-PPP commonly employs global ionospheric maps (GIM) from IGS analysis centers, obtaining slant delays through spatiotemporal interpolation and oblique projection. However, constrained by inaccuracies in phase-smoothed pseudorange-derived slant total electron content (STEC) and truncation errors during fitting procedures, GIM products achieve only 0.32-1.28 m equivalent ranging accuracy at GPS L1 frequency, offering limited enhancement for SF-PPP. In this paper, we propose a lightweight multilayer perceptron (MLP)-based ionospheric modeling approach to enhance SF-PPP. The proposed method utilizes undifferenced and uncombined PPP to generate raw slant ionospheric delays as training labels. The input features are constructed from satellite-related parameters (e.g. time, locations of the ionosphere pierce points, elevation and azimuth angles) that are readily available during precise positioning computation. By leveraging the exceptional nonlinear fitting capability of MLP, the proposed model can effectively capture the relationship between the slant ionospheric delay and satellite-related parameters to enhance SF-PPP. Experimental results demonstrate that the MLP-based approach achieves slant delay mean absolute error below 0.3 m. Compared to final ionospheric products from Center for Orbit Determination in Europe (CODE), the proposed method demonstrates a 14.9% to 52.6% reduction in slant ionospheric delay root mean square error across different time periods. Furthermore, the proposed method significantly reduces convergence time while enhancing positioning accuracy, with 95th percentile horizontal and vertical positioning errors below 0.527 m and 0.831 m respectively - corresponding to 41.5% and 43.3% reductions compared to CODE GIM-enhanced SF-PPP.

title: Breathing to Heartbeat: Virtual Acoustic Enhancement for Cardiac Spike Extraction via Smartphone
authors: KounKou Vincent, XiYue Tan, Ke Sun, Tao Gong, Lyusha Cheng
abstract: Continuous monitoring of vital signs is crucial for daily health management, enabling the early detection of conditions such as premature heartbeats, atrial fibrillation, and cardiac hyperactivity. However, subtle chest movements associated with cardiac activity are often obscured by respiratory artifacts and multipath interference, presenting significant challenges in tracking minute cardiac-induced vibrations using conventional acoustic sensing. This paper introduces Acoustic-Augmented (AcAg), a novel non-contact monitoring framework that leverages smartphone-generated acoustic signals to extract cardiac signatures from respiratory-modulated chest movements. Our key innovation lies in Virtual Acoustic Enhancement (VAcE), a paradigm-shifting approach that mathematically reconfigures multipath propagation to transform destructive interference into constructive patterns, revealing previously obscured cardiac features. We develop a comprehensive signal processing pipeline that combines this virtual enhancement with phase-coherent analysis to capture submillimeter cardiac displacements. Furthermore, we leverage AcAgNet, a variational autoencoder that corrects signal artifacts and recovers fine-grained waveform characteristics while providing a precise heart rate estimate. Experimental results demonstrate a mean absolute error of 0.3431 bpm across our datasets, establishing a significant advancement over state-of-the-art acoustic methods for non-contact cardiac monitoring.

title: Toward Safety-Critical Driver-Automation Collaboration: Blending Data and Physics for Polynomials-Empowered Steering Control Systems
authors: Xuekuan Chen, Xiang-Peng Xie
abstract: Driver-automation collaboration of steering control systems remains brittle in dynamic authority allocation and managing conflicts under safety-critical driving conditions. This article introduces a safety-critical collaboration framework that systematically blends data-driven perception with physics-aware control synthesis. To start with, an enhanced authority allocation strategy is proposed, which integrates conditional variational autoencoder-based probabilistic trajectory prediction for risk awareness with an interval type-2 fuzzy inference engine. This is further augmented by a novel driver reactive capability assessment module to ensure smoother authority transitions by modulating control shares based on real-time driver state and context. The collaborative dynamics and driver-automation authority allocation strategy are encapsulated within a unified Takagi–Sugeno fuzzy framework, which natively represents the shared steering characteristics. A switched memory-gain-scheduling controller is synthesized, building upon the core of the polynomials-empowered scheme, and explicitly accounts for transmission failures with probabilistic uncertainty. This strategy reduces design conservatism while ensuring enhanced robustness and adaptability to driving modalities. Driver-in-the-loop experiment validates the effectiveness of the proposed approach, demonstrating notable improvements in risk management, tracking accuracy, and system resilience under both typical and extreme driving conditions.

title: DISC: Disentangling Spurious Correlations for Multibehavior Recommendation
authors: Tongxin Xu, Chenzhong Bin, Cihan Xiao, Zhixin Zeng, Tianlong Gu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computational Social Systems
abstract: Multibehavior recommender systems are proficient at constructing precise representations of users and items by leveraging a variety of interaction behaviors, e.g., click, add-to-cart, and purchase. However, they are still facing the following challenges: 1) there are a large number of spurious correlations existing in auxiliary behaviors (i.e., noise unrelated to the target behavior preference); and 2) spurious correlations may be unintentionally introduced and amplified during the representation learning process in traditional multibehavior recommenders. Hence, we propose a novel framework-disentangling spurious correlations (DISC) to measure and disentangle the spurious correlations of the multibehavior recommendation. In particular, to precisely identify the intent representations for each user, we devise a cross-behavior self-attention layer incorporating lightweight graph convolution networks to encode graph nodes under specific behaviors, thereby enabling the model to supervise the subsequent spurious correlation disentanglement. Subsequently, to disentangle the spurious correlations among multiple behaviors, we design a time-sensitive Jaccard coefficient to dynamically measure the users’ spurious correlations. Building on this, we propose a dual mutual information (MI) bound structure to disentangle spurious correlations existing in auxiliary behaviors and transfer genuine correlated semantic information to the target behavior, thus alleviating the data sparsity. Extensive experiments on three real-world datasets demonstrate the consistent improvements obtained by DISC over 11 state-of-the-art baselines by effectively disentangling the spurious correlations.

title: GLIDE: Generative LLM-Driven Data Augmentation for Meta-Services With Applications to E-Commerce Scenarios
authors: Yudan Lyu, Jingwei Lu, Jinshuo Guo, Zheng Jing, Haoyu Qiu, Junzhe Ouyang, Lefei Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computational Social Systems
abstract: In this article, a novel data augmentation method is developed for Meta-Services based on large language models (LLMs) and requirement-functional-logical-physical (RFLP) models, referred to as generative LLM-driven data augmentation (GLIDE), and an application of GLIDE to e-commerce recommendation systems is presented. First, the framework of GLIDE is designed under Meta-Services, including a generalized encoder and decoder architecture, as well as designers, which captures the idea of “designers in the loop” in Meta-Services. Furthermore, the generalized encoder and decoder architecture is established based on RFLP models in systems engineering, aiming to translate requirements and knowledge into prompts and obtain ideal LLM outputs under physical constraints. Subsequently, the application of the GLIDE method to e-commerce recommendation systems is given in detail, which is committed to enriching users’ behavioral data and providing an effective way to decouple data augmentation and recommendation modules. Finally, recommendation experiments are performed on real-world and synthetic behavior data, and the empirical results demonstrate the effectiveness of our GLIDE method.

title: Exploring the conditional effect of RLF on handover failure based on ns-3 under stochastic channel condition_supp1-3672646.pdf
authors: SHANKAR GHOSH
abstract: A Key component of Handover failure (HOF) in Fifth generation (5G) cellular network is the underlying radio link failure (RLF) event; existing model based analyses of HOF have not adequately explored this dependency. Moreover, HOF as a function of user mobility necessitates models that incorporate spatio-temporal correlation that has been largely ignored. In this work, based on ns-3 simulation, we characterize the relationship between RLF and HOF considering the effects of handover parameters (i.e., hysteresis (Hys), time-to-trigger (TTT), A2 threshold, A4 threshold) and RLF parameters (i.e., out-of-synch threshold (Qout), out-of-synch indication (N310), insynch indication (N311) and RLF timer (T310)) for correlated RSRP samples. The study has been carried out for different kinds of handovers in Non-Standalone (NSA) deployment of 5G. Our study reveals that optimal settings of handover parameters and RLF parameters to optimize HOF are actually constrained by the correlation characteristics of the prevailing channels. Comparison of simulation results with an existing semi-analytic model based analysis shows the novelty of the proposed ns-3 simulation methodology in capturing the cumulative impact of all the aforementioned factors in causing HOF. This study will help the mobile operators in choosing optimal RLF and handover parameters to minimize HOF under different UE velocities and fading scenarios.

title: Outage Probability Analysis for Link Aggregation-Enabled Hybrid LiFi-WiFi Network
authors: Saswati Paramita, Vivek Ashok Bohara, Arani Bhattacharya, Anand Srivastava
abstract: Li-Fi and Wi-Fi operate in non-overlapping spectrum, allowing users to receive data simultaneously from both access points (APs). Rising demand for high-speed, reliable indoor communication has spurred the development of hybrid Li-Fi/Wi-Fi networks. To assess the performance of link aggregation (LA) in such hybrid networks (LA-HLWNs), we propose an analytical model to derive rate outage probability. The model accounts for LA-overhead and benchmarks LA-HLWNs against standalone Li-Fi and Wi-Fi systems. Results show that LA-HLWNs consistently achieve lower outage probabilities across diverse scenarios, proving LA’s effectiveness in combining Li-Fi and Wi-Fi strengths to meet growing indoor communication demands.

title: Safety-Aware Intrusion Response System Based on Safe Reinforcement Learning for Cyber-Physical Systems
authors: Lin Chen, Yingxu Lai, Peng Zhao, Baoshan Xie, Yan Zhang
abstract: The growing interconnectivity of consumer Cyber-Physical systems (CPS) has substantially elevated cybersecurity risks. The Intrusion Response System (IRS), a crucial component of CPS security, is essential for mitigating cyber threats. Ineffective response strategies can pose direct threats to user privacy, property security, and physical safety. However, current research mainly focuses on network threats and lacks a systematic method for assessing safety risks in the physical domain. Although Safe Reinforcement Learning (Safe RL) has shown promise in addressing constrained optimization problems, its application in the complex, multi-layered CPS risk environment remains largely unexplored. This study introduces an IRS framework based on the Constrained Markov Decision Process (CMDP) to address these challenges. The approach includes a safety cost function that continuously measures risk based on sensor threshold breaches. It employs a hierarchical policy optimization algorithm to develop response strategies prioritizing safety and effectiveness. Experiments in a simulated CPS testbed demonstrate that, compared with existing IRS-based methods, SAIRS reduces the safety cost from 5.07 to 0 and improves the defense success rate by 7% relative to other safe RL approaches; additionally, it surpasses existing MPC-based methods in computational efficiency and defense clearing efficiency by 39.23 and 1.45 times, respectively.

title: Distributional Reinforcement Learning with Information Bottleneck for Uncertainty-Aware DRAM Equalization
authors: Muhammad Usama, Dong Eui Chang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Components, Packaging and Manufacturing Technology
abstract: Equalizer parameter optimization is critical for signal integrity in high-speed memory systems operating at multi-gigabit data rates. However, existing methods suffer from computationally expensive eye diagram evaluation, optimization of expected rather than worst-case performance, and absence of uncertainty quantification for deployment decisions. In this paper, we propose a distributional risk-sensitive reinforcement learning framework integrating Information Bottleneck latent representations with Conditional Value-at-Risk optimization. We introduce rate-distortion optimal signal compression achieving 51 times speedup over eye diagrams while quantifying epistemic uncertainty through Monte Carlo dropout. Distributional reinforcement learning with quantile regression enables explicit worst-case optimization, while PAC-Bayesian regularization certifies generalization bounds. Experimental validation on 2.4 million waveforms from eight memory units demonstrated mean improvements of 37.1% and 41.5% for 4-tap and 8-tap equalizer configurations with worst-case guarantees of 33.8% and 38.2%, representing 80.7% and 89.1% improvements over Q-learning baselines. The framework achieved 62.5% high-reliability classification eliminating manual validation for most configurations. These results suggest the proposed framework provides a practical solution for production-scale equalizer optimization with certified worst-case guarantees.

title: Book Reviews
authors: Nihal Kularatna
abstract: Our AC power grid is very old but still growing with a mix of renewable systems such as solar and wind energy. High-voltage transmission links and high-voltage transformers are the basis for long distance links within the complex power networks worldwide. Insulation as applied to these high-voltage systems within the power network plays a key role in the reliability of the power networks. This 2026 book, in the form an edited volume of relevant publications, provides the reader with a new direction for materials for high-voltage insulation based on polymer micro and nanocomposites.
