title: Toward a Theoretical Mapping of Internet Addiction Subtypes and AI Detection Models
authors: Elham Eskandarnia
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: The increasing prevalence of Internet Addiction (IA) highlights the necessity for detection frameworks that thoughtfully integrate psychological theory with advanced computational methodologies. IA is a heterogeneous phenomenon comprising several subtypes, such as social media addiction, online gaming disorder, and generalized compulsive internet use, each defined by specific behavioral indicators—ranging from time distortion and compulsive engagement to social withdrawal and challenges in emotional regulation. This paper introduces a comprehensive theoretical framework that aligns IA subtypes with appropriate Artificial Intelligence (AI) detection models. Leveraging existing scholarship, the study illustrates how Natural Language Processing can identify patterns of validation-seeking and sentiment in social media, how time-series analysis and ensemble methods may effectively model gaming behaviors, and how clustering algorithms can uncover complex patterns in broad internet usage. The framework delineates both commonalities and distinctions across IA subtypes, establishing a structured intersection be-tween behavioral addiction theory and AI-driven analysis. This theoretical approach provides a foundation for future empirical and applied research aimed at developing subtype-sensitive AI detection systems. The paper concludes by discussing the ethical and societal considerations involved in implementing AI-based digital addiction monitoring, with particular attention to privacy, transparency, and family well-being. Accordingly, this work is positioned as a conceptual framework paper

title: A Unified Analytics Framework for Cloud Governance, Cost Optimization, and Security Compliance Using Azure CCO Insights
authors: VasanthRao Jadav, Akshay Mittal, Karthik Pappu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: Enterprise cloud operations are increasingly fragmented across independent consoles for cost management, governance enforcement, and security monitoring, forcing organizations to perform manual reconciliation across tenants and subscriptions. This paper presents a unified analytics framework for cloud operations that consolidates governance, cost optimization, and security posture into role-based dashboards through connector-based architectures. We demonstrate this framework through analysis and experimental validation of Azure Continuous Cloud Optimization (CCO) Insights, an open-source implementation that uses custom Power BI connectors authenticated via the Azure Management API to ingest real-time metadata from Azure Resource Graph, Defender for Cloud, and Cost Management. The framework delivers two integrated dashboards: a Governance Dashboard exposing policy compliance, tagging standards, regulatory posture, and blueprint usage; and an Infrastructure Dashboard surfacing idle resources, security alerts, compute utilization, RBAC roles, NSG configurations, and orphan assets. We validated the framework’s technical architecture through experimental deployment in a controlled Azure subscription environment, confirming connector functionality, API integration, and dashboard rendering capabilities. Analysis of published production case studies demonstrates the framework’s enterprise scalability, with documented deployments reporting high tagging compliance rates, significant cost optimization outcomes, and substantial reductions in reporting cycles. The architectural principles generalize to multi-cloud environments, with future extensions including cross-platform federation and ML-driven anomaly detection for cost and policy drift.

title: Camouflage-Inspired Fitness Optimization UsingBio-Inspired Techniques for Real-Time Posture Correction
authors: Garima Panwar, Deepshikha Bhargava, Harsh Khatter
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Advanced Computing and Emerging Technologies (ACET)
abstract: The need for intelligent devices that can identify and adjust human posture in real-time has expended due to the rising incidence of musculoskeletal illnesses brought on by bad posture. This analysis offers a state-of-art examination of several bio-inspired algorithms specifically adapted for a concealed fitness algorithm intended to identify and noninvasively modify human posture. These algorithms, which draw inspiration from natural systems such as evolution, swarm intelligence, brain feedback, immune protection, and animal camouflage, offer a range of learning, adaptation, and optimization processes. The strengths, limitations, and suitability for real-time biomechanical correction of Genetic Algorithms, Artificial Neural Networks, Particle Swarm Optimization, Reinforcement Learning are thoroughly evaluated. The recent idea of camouflage-inspired methods, which enable subtle and flexible corrective systems that fit in perfectly with the user's natural surroundings and behaviour, is given particular attention. The analysis indicates that a hybrid approach that uses neural networks for pattern recognition, evolutionary algorithms for optimization, and reinforcement learning for adaptive correction is the most promising framework for developing trustworthy, contextaware posture correction systems. The present study provides the groundwork for future research on the implementation of bio-inspired, human-cantered fitness gadgets that are smart and blend in with daily life.

title: Latency-Optimized Microservices Orchestration for Real-Time E-Commerce in Multi-Cloud Environments
authors: Amit Kumar Padhy, Chandrashekhar Medicherla, Bharadwaj Vulugundam, Chaitanya Kulkarni, Tejas Pravinbhai Patel, Sandeep Shivam
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: Real-time e-commerce platforms are increasingly based on distributed microservices deployed across multi-cloud environments, but due to high variability in the network and complicated inter-service dependencies, the latency of inter-service communication often becomes a suboptimal performance indicator that degrades user experience. The main goal is to create latency optimized orchestration framework to provide low response time, scalability and reliability across the federated cloud infrastructures. The proposed system involves service mesh-based traffic shaping, adaptive autoscaling with reinforcement learning, end-to-end observability with distributed tracing, and a latency-aware scheduling algorithm that makes dynamic decisions on the placement of services using the realtime network metric. Experimental evaluation across AWS, Azure, GCP multi-cloud deployments showing substantial performance improvements such as a reduction of the internal average end-to-end latency by 32%, 27% improved request throughput and 22% improved peak load stability in compare to baseline orchestration strategies. The proposed latency-optimized orchestration framework significantly improves the real-time responsiveness and operational efficiency of multi-cloud e-commerce platforms in a scalable way for the next-generation digital commerce systems.

title: A Review on an Optimized Knowledge Distillation Framework with Mamba-U Net Based on SAR for Brain Tumor Detection
authors: Bhawna Sachdeva, Jeebananda Panda
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Advanced Computing and Emerging Technologies (ACET)
abstract: In Brain the abnormal or unbridled increase of the neurons or cells is brain tumor and it also known as tumor with in skull, which is one of most harmful and diseases. Every brain cancers are tumor, but not every tumors are cancerous. Some of noncancerous brain tumors are curable at early stage. Brain tumors are divided into four stages. Tumors classified as grade 1 and grade 2 are considered benign, meaning they are noncancerous and typically exhibit slow growth. Then, the grade 3 and grade 4 brain tumor is known as malignant tumor which grow more quickly and harmful disease. A better response to treatment after an early diagnosis of a brain tumour leads to a higher patient survival rate. Locating and classifying brain tumours in enormous collections of medical images involves a lot of time and effort when performed manually during standard clinical tasks. The benign tumor initially started to form with the brain and the malignant tumor forms in any location or organ of body which spread towards the brain [1]. Benign tumors include categories such as fibromas and adenomas, while malignant tumors are grouped into low-grade and high-grade types [2].

title: Machine Learning based Security Mechanism Against DDoS Attacks in Mobile Edge Computing
authors: Omar H. Ramadan, Saddaf Rubab
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: Mobile Edge Computing environments, become very important topic in the field of networking and telecommunication by providing low latency service with their structure and resources. Distributed Denial of Service attacks are particularly vulnerable which can severely Freezing the services, affect the infrastructure, and slowing down network performance. These threats are often fail to addressed through traditional security frameworks. This research describes different categories of solutions aimed at improving resilience in MEC networks while considering their limits and constraints. The research assesses the performance of thirteen models, including machine learning, deep learning, unsupervised, hybrid, and transformer-based approaches for DDoS attack detection. In the realm of ML, Random Forest takes the crown with Accuracy (99.92%) to Boosting and Decision Tree with (99.85%) and (99.86%) respectively. CNN-LSTM in the hybrid deep learning also performed well with 99.69% accuracy, while BiLSTM trailed at 98.74%. K-Means and DBSCAN also proved to be helpful with 94.21% and 95.95% accuracy, K-Means being the least accurate among the unsupervised methods. These models can help reduce latency in Mobile Edge Computing by detecting DDoS attacks quickly and directly at the edge of the network.

title: Comprehensive Wheat Leaf Disease and Pest Diagnosis Using Convolutional Neural Networks
authors: Mohamed Hadadi, Yousra Chtouki
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: Wheat productivity is increasingly threatened by numerous foliar diseases and pest infestations, making timely and accurate diagnosis essential for sustainable crop management. Although deep learning-based computer vision approaches have shown promise in plant disease recognition, most existing studies focus on a limited set of disease types or rely on datasets that do not capture real field diversity. This paper presents a comprehensive multi-class image classification study utilizing the Wheat Plant Diseases dataset, which comprises fifteen distinct categories covering major wheat diseases, insect pests, and healthy plants. Leveraging MobileNetV2 and VGG16 as baseline convolutional architectures, we investigate standardized training pipelines and extensive data augmentation protocols tailored to the complexities of wheat stress classification in realistic field conditions. Experimental results demonstrate high efficacy for both architectures: VGG16 achieved a classification accuracy of 96.28%, while the lightweight MobileNetV2 reached a competitive 93%. Notably, training dynamics revealed little to no overfitting, validating the robustness of the proposed data augmentation strategy. The study further provides a comparative analysis of model efficiency suitable for resource-constrained environments. This work establishes new baselines for comprehensive wheat disease and pest classification and provides actionable guidance for practical computer vision systems in precision agriculture.

title: Research on Multilingual Off-Line Handwritten Signature Verification Methods and Application
authors: Nuerbiye Xiemuxiding, Binlei Zhang, Chaohong Wang, Mukaram Mahaiti, Aynuram Tuersun, Yanchao Lou
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Science, and Information Processing (AICSIP)
abstract: Handwritten signature verification has been widely used in all aspects of daily life. Offline handwritten signature verification is the most popular biometric tool in biometrics because it is not only low-cost, but also widely used, at present, the research on multilingual off-line handwritten signature verification is still developing. This paper studies multilingual off-line handwritten signature verification, the data is made up of self-created data sets and public data sets such as the Chinese Xinjiang minority language Uyghur, Kazakh, kerkiz, Mongolian and simplified Chinese characters in the common Chinese language, English and Bengali alphabet, some common methods of multilingual off-line handwritten signature and the application of multilingual signature research are also introduced.

title: Experimental and Numerical Investigation of Nano-Coated Solar Dryers Integrated with Silica Gel Dehumidification
authors: Shek Rahman, Salah Issa, Ahmed Hachicha, Ali Radwan
publish: 2025 Advances in Science and Engineering Technology International Conferences (ASET)
abstract: This study investigates the design, implementation, and performance analysis of a solar-assisted drying system incorporating a silica gel dehumidifier and a nanocoated absorber plate. The system, developed as a sustainable solution to minimize post-harvest crop losses, operates on forced convection and utilizes a nanomaterialenhanced flat plate solar collector to improve thermal performance. Experimental results demonstrate that the incorporation of silica gel successfully reduces moisture content by up to 57.6%, and tray rearrangement further enhances drying uniformity. The thermal efficiency of the collector reaches an average of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{6. 2 5 {\%}}$</tex>, aligning with similar technologies reported in the literature. Computational fluid dynamics (CFD) simulations validate airflow distribution and provide insights into thermal gradients, while life cycle analysis (LCA) confirms the environmental and economic viability of the proposed dryer. Among several drying models, the Logarithmic model best describes the drying kinetics. This work confirms the potential of integrating nanocoating and desiccant-based dehumidification in compact, solar-powered drying technologies.

title: Unlocking Biomedical Insights: Hierarchical Attention Networks for High-Dimensional Data Interpretation
authors: Rekha R Nair, Tina Babu, Alavikunhu Panthakkan, Hussain Al-Ahmad, Balamurugan Balusamy
publish: 2025 Advances in Science and Engineering Technology International Conferences (ASET)
abstract: The proliferation of high-dimensional datasets in fields such as genomics, healthcare, and finance has created an urgent need for machine learning models that are both highly accurate and inherently interpretable. While traditional deep learning approaches deliver strong predictive performance, their lack of transparency often impedes their deployment in critical, decision-sensitive applications. In this work, we introduce the Hierarchical Attention-based Interpretable Network (HAIN), a novel architecture that unifies multi-level attention mechanisms, dimensionality reduction, and explanation-driven loss functions to deliver interpretable and robust analysis of complex biomedical data. HAIN provides feature-level interpretability via gradientweighted attention and offers global model explanations through prototype-based representations. Comprehensive evaluation on The Cancer Genome Atlas (TCGA) dataset demonstrates that HAIN achieves a classification accuracy of 94.3 %, surpassing conventional post-hoc interpretability approaches such as SHAP and LIME in both transparency and explanatory power. Furthermore, HAIN effectively identifies biologically relevant cancer biomarkers, supporting its utility for clinical and research applications. By harmonizing predictive accuracy with interpretability, HAIN advances the development of transparent AI solutions for precision medicine and regulatory compliance.

title: Investigating the Detrimental Implications of Screen Exposure on Young Adults: A Research Survey
authors: Neil Dwivedi, Radhika Gupta, Upen Manocha, Bhavya Munjal, Vidushi
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Advanced Computing and Emerging Technologies (ACET)
abstract: The rising incidence of screen exposure among young adults in the current digital era has sparked serious worries. Using laptops, tablets, smartphones, and other gadgets on a daily basis has become essential. The consequences of screen time on young adults are examined in this descriptive research survey, with particular attention paid to screen habits, sleep quality, and wider implications for their overall wellbeing. A cross-sectional survey examined screen habits (e.g., daily screen hours, device types), sleep quality (e.g., sleep duration, latency, wakefulness), and other adverse impacts. The data underwent pre-processing, exploratory data analysis (EDA), and further analysis to extract key insights. Findings revealed unfavorable outcomes associated with excessive screen time, such as impaired sleep quality (e.g., reduced sleep duration and efficiency) and adverse physical and mental health effects. These included fatigue, behavioral changes, and reduced daily functionality. The study highlights the wide-ranging consequences of screen use, suggesting that its effects go beyond simple recreation or communication. Balancing screen time with other activities is essential to reducing potential risks to health and overall well-being. A significant portion of the responses came from younger individuals, with many reporting physical discomfort such as posture issues and vision concerns. A notable share also experienced mental or behavioral shifts, such as reduced focus, emotional fluctuations, and organizational challenges. Other reported effects included difficulties in social interactions, memory issues, and reduced self-confidence.

title: Enhancing Cybersecurity Systems through PGP Integration
authors: Ala Samy El-Bardini
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: this study examines the incorporation of pretty good privacy (pgp) encryption inside contemporary cybersecurity frameworks to enhance data confidentiality, integrity, and authenticity. notwithstanding its robust cryptographic architecture, pgp experiences restricted acceptance owing to usability difficulties, intricate key management, and constrained interaction with contemporary technologies. a design science research (DSR ) methodology, along with mixed qualitative and quantitative approaches, was utilized to develop and assess a scalable pgp-based framework. the solution, developed with kleopatra and openpgp standards, was evaluated through simulations, usability testing, and surveys of general users and cybersecurity experts. the results validated pgp's technological efficacy in safeguarding both textual and multimedia communications, although underscored obstacles to its extensive use. the paper presents a pragmatic approach that integrates robust cryptographic functionalities with a design centered on usability, thereby enhancing secure digital communication in both organizational and personal settings.

title: FitPredictor: AI-Driven Fashion Size Recommendation Extension
authors: Himanshu Masand, Judhi Prasetyo
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: The rise of online fashion retail has introduced convenience but also significant challenges, particularly around clothing size accuracy. High return rates due to poor fit negatively impact customer satisfaction, profitability and environmental sustainability. FitPredictor is a machine-learning-driven clothing size recommendation system designed to address inconsistent sizing across fashion brands. The system predicts four essential body measurements: chest, waist, neck, and hip using only five user inputs: age, height, weight, gender and body type. This paper presents the complete methodology of the FitPredictor, Dataset development, preprocessing, machine learning modeling, brand-specific adjustment logic, and evaluation metrics. The system uses Random Forest and Gradient Boosting regression models, enhanced by brand-bias correction layers and size-chart mapping automation. Results demonstrate that FitPredictor can improve fit accuracy, reduce size-related returns, and serve as a scalable solution for global e-commerce platforms.

title: Compute-Efficient, Calibrated, and Explainable Brain Mri Tumor Classification with External Testing
authors: Adarsh Kumar, Bitul Pratap Singh, Harsh Kumar, Harshendra Kumar Singh, Mahima Shanker Pandey
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Advanced Computing and Emerging Technologies (ACET)
abstract: Deep learning models for brain magnetic resonance imaging tumor classification often show limitations in external testing performance, calibration reliability, and readiness for real clinical deployment. This study presents a comprehensive evaluation of an efficient MobileNetV2 based approach with a strong emphasis on external validation and deployment feasibility. A MobileNetV2 feature extractor was trained in combination with classical machine learning classifiers using a primary dataset consisting of over fourteen thousand brain MRI images belonging to four categories including glioma, meningioma, pituitary tumor, and normal brain tissue. The proposed system achieved strong internal testing performance with high classification accuracy and balanced class level performance. To evaluate real world generalization, external testing was conducted on images collected from independent sources and compared against end to end fine-tuned models and deeper baseline architectures. Calibration techniques were applied to improve prediction reliability, while visual explainability methods were used to enhance clinical interpretability. External testing revealed a significant performance degradation caused by domain shift, particularly affecting glioma detection. Simple domain adaptation strategies based on external recalibration and threshold optimization resulted in notable improvements in overall performance and glioma sensitivity. End to end fine tuning demonstrated overfitting and reduced robustness on unseen data. Efficiency analysis showed low inference latency and high processing throughput, making the system suitable for practical clinical deployment. The results highlight the importance of external evaluation and demonstrate that lightweight models combined with simple adaptation strategies can achieve reliable and deployable performance for brain tumor classification.

title: Motivations and Challenges in Deploying Battery Energy Storage Systems (BESS) Within Smart Grids
authors: Nikhil Jayaraj, Sacithra Anandaraj
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Sustainable Energy and Net-Zero Emissions (SENZE)
abstract: The climate change mitigation strategies call for the adoption and inclusion of renewables for electricity generation and mobility. The intermittency in energy generation using renewable sources cannot be handled by conventional grids, leading to power quality issues. A flexible energy management system is, hence, required to support the increasing incorporation of renewables in the system. Smart grids can enable the adoption of renewables by facilitating the management of electricity through automated control and continuous monitoring. Batteries in the smart grid can charge during times of low demand when energy is generated in excess, and discharge during times of high demand. Hence, battery energy storage systems (BESS) are an integral part of smart grids. The study analyses the role of BESS in empowering smart grids and the motivations and barriers to the adoption of BESS. Case studies of BESS-integrated smart grids are also analysed to understand the motivations and barriers.

title: Decentralized Identity Management Using Blockchain for Secure Cross-Border Digital Services
authors: Ankit Kumar Dubey, Khushbu Kesharwani, Shubneet, Anushka Raj Yadav, Navjot Singh Talwandi, Ram Mohan Polam
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Advanced Computing and Emerging Technologies (ACET)
abstract: On the other hand, the buzz around modern technology like decentralized identity management (DCI) is somewhat frozen in time on a blockchain to ensure its security and efficiency for cross-border digital services. The traditional way of identity management relies on a centralized authority and, therefore are very vulnerable to data breaches, extremely inefficient, and continuously quite hard to control. Concepts of self-sovereignty such as self-sovereign identity, decentralized identifiers, and verifiable credentials drive the DCI evolving way where persons can control their own personal data and usually share it selectively by themselves without the mediation of any third party. Cryptographic techniques such as zero-knowledge proof, are highly relevant to privacy and security, whereas a non-repudiable blockchain ledger is the security and necessarily provides auditability, ensuring authentication and non-repudiation. This paper examines how a blockchain Digitalized decentralized identity can serve to resolve some pertinent issues with regard to regulatory compliance in cross-border digital transactions, therefore thwarting identityrelated frauds and facilitating their international interoperability. The practical cases would showcase leaps in technology speed, one trust of the user, and regulatory harmonization with regard to the travel biometric credential systems being currently exhibited with reference to the e-Residency program in Estonia. Scalability and key management, though still some issues linger, the trend shows that upcoming innovations in the blockchain protocol-game and in the regulatory framework contribute to DCI being one of the major pillars of the trusted user-centric secure digital identity solution of the future global economy.

title: A Holistic Deep Learning Framework for Cardiovascular Disease Diagnosis
authors: Naga Durga Saile. K, S Yamuna, Yadala Sucharitha, Kumbham Bhargavi, Patil Yogita Dattatraya, U.S.B.K. Mahalaxmi
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Advanced Computing and Emerging Technologies (ACET)
abstract: A crucial part of illness management is identifying and predicting the likelihood of developing cardiovascular-diseases(CVD) in otherwise healthy patients. There is great promise in the extensive health data on CVD that is now stored in hospital databases for the timely detection of CVD, which could have a positive effect on disease outcomes. Thus, there is great potential for clinical practice to develop in the management of CVDs with the use of deep learning technologies. This study suggests creating new deep learning algorithms called CNN and LSTM to automatically select important features and detect early-stage heart disease. The goal is to improve timely anticipation and intervention for CVDs. The algorithms should be robust, operative, and efficient. An F1-score of around 97.54% and an mean accuracy of 96.84% are produced by the suggested Catboost model. Consequently, it amplified classification performance with improved accuracy and F1-score compared to several other existing traditional methodologies.

title: An FPGA-Based Secure and Privacy-Aware RISC-V SoC with a CNN Accelerator for Edge AI
authors: Priyanshu Tyagi, Rhythm Patel, Sparsh Mittal, Rekha Singhal
publish: 2026 39th International Conference on VLSI Design &amp; 25th International Conference on Embedded Systems (VLSID)
abstract: We propose a privacy-aware RISC-V SoC for edge inference, integrating a pipelined RV32IMC CPU, a quantized FPGA-based CNN accelerator, and a lightweight hardware security module. The accelerator employs parallel 8-bit MAC and max-pooling units. The design also features inter-layer pipelining in addition to intra-layer parallelism. This allows partial feature maps to be sent to the next layers without waiting for full completion. This approach significantly cuts down on inference latency. Moreover, we enforce security through custom CNN instructions embedding challenge-response authentication, while a noise injection unit ensures output privacy. Implemented on an Xilinx Zynq-7000 FPGA, the accelerator achieves inference latencies of 0.25 ms (MNIST) and 15.93 ms (CIFAR-10), delivering more than <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$10 \times$</tex> speedup over the sequential baselines. The system delivers peak throughput of 10.56 GOPS and 2.48 GOPS, with energy efficiency reaching <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$60.3 \text{TOPS} / \mathrm{W}$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$10.3 \text{TOPS} / \mathrm{W}$</tex> for MNIST and CIFAR-10, respectively. The results demonstrate the viability of our work for secure edge AI workloads. The code is available at https://shorturl.at/hb5NU.

title: Enhancing Fluid Dynamics Simulations: Integrating Neural Operators into Visual Models
authors: Qizhen Yang
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Science, and Information Processing (AICSIP)
abstract: Fluid dynamics simulations, a cornerstone for lots of scientific fields, often require solving partial differential equations (PDEs), e.g. Navier-Strokes equations. Traditional methods such as computational fluid dynamics (CFD) are typically computationally intensive and have limitations on real-time simulations and extreme boundary conditions. This research discovers the potential of U-Net-like deep learning architecture, as a visual model, to simulate fluid motions and integrates neural operators, particularly Fourier Neural Operator (FNO) and Adaptive Fourier Neural Operator (AFNO), into its architecture to enhance and specialize its capability in capturing features in fluid motions and provides an alternative approach. Experiments are done on shallow water equations (single property) and Navier-Strokes equations (multiple properties). The modified architecture showcases a significant improvement compared with the baseline model, especially in single-property simulations and shows promise in multi-property scenarios, with improvements up to 80% in the error.

title: A Literature-Based Assessment of the Impact of Emerging Technologies on Urban Spaces
authors: Rahaf Nidal Kanaan, Mohammad Shamsuzzaman, Emad Mushtaha, Salah Haridy, Hamdi Bashir
publish: 2025 Advances in Science and Engineering Technology International Conferences (ASET)
abstract: Building sustainable and livable cities has become a major priority due to the increasing challenges posed by environmental degradation, infrastructure demands, and rapid population growth. The Internet of Things (IoT), artificial intelligence (AI), big data analytics (BDA), digital twins (DT), blockchain, and cyber-physical systems (CPS) are examples of Industry 4.0 (I4.0) tools that provide effective solutions to these issues. This paper follows the PRISMA 2020 guidelines and specific criteria focused on urban uses of 14.0 technologies to choose 154 peer-reviewed articles (2010-2025) from an original set of 468 Scopus-indexed papers for a systematic literature review (SLR) and bibliometric analysis. Important conclusions demonstrate how these technologies greatly improve public safety, infrastructure resilience, mobility, energy efficiency, and governance. Real-time infrastructure monitoring, automation, data-driven governance, and smart energy systems are some of the thematic clusters. But common challenges like digital inequality, governance flaws, and data privacy show ongoing implementation difficulties. For policymakers, researchers, and urban planners looking to execute technology-driven urban transformation, this study provides useful insights and unifies disparate scholarly debates.

title: A Fusion Method for Attitude Angle Detection Based Micro-Pressure Array and Inertial Data
authors: Lina Chen, Haoyang Sun, Yang Yang, Shuisen Chen, Chulin Huang, Guangyi Shi
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Micro/Nano Sensors for AI, Healthcare, and Robotics (NSENS)
abstract: This paper introduces a method for detecting the attitude angle of a seven-hole probe in complex flow fields. The method is based on the acceleration and angular velocity data from an Inertial Measurement Unit (IMU), combined with aerodynamic parameters (such as flow velocity, total pressure, and static pressure) collected by the seven-hole probe. An improved Kalman fusion algorithm is used to achieve high-precision attitude angle estimation. By feeding back the attitude and position information to the correction process of the seven-hole probe's measurement values, the method effectively eliminates systematic errors caused by angle deviations and changes in the external environment, significantly improving measurement accuracy and stability. The proposed method has been validated under various flight attitudes to ensure the timeliness and accuracy of the data. Additionally, a model based on deep neural networks (Convolutional Neural Networks, CNNs) has been designed. This model takes the raw data or preprocessed features from gyroscopes, accelerometers, and pressure sensors as inputs and directly outputs the attitude angle, further optimizing the performance of attitude angle detection. Experimental results show that the calibration method proposed in this paper outperforms traditional single-sensor solutions under various flight attitudes. It not only enhances measurement accuracy and stability but also demonstrates good real-time performance and adaptability, providing an efficient and reliable solution for the attitude angle detection of seven-hole probes in complex flow fields.

title: Bidirectional DC-DC Buck Boost Converter for Enhanced Energy Transfer in Solar Battery Systems
authors: Abdullah Ehsan, Vidhya Viswambaran, Anu Malpan Lonappan
publish: 2025 Advances in Science and Engineering Technology International Conferences (ASET)
abstract: This paper presents the design and simulation of a bidirectional DC-DC buck boost converter integrated with a photovoltaic system and a lithium ion battery for efficient energy management in off grid and unstable grid environments. Unlike conventional unidirectional converters, the proposed system enables bidirectional power flow, operating in buck mode to charge the battery when PV voltage exceeds a predefined threshold and in boost mode to power the load when PV voltage falls below the threshold. The converter is modelled and implemented entirely in MATLAB Simulink using logic based control, eliminating the need for external microcontrollers. A threshold of 300 V governs the mode transitions, maintaining duty cycle of approximately 90 percentage in buck mode and 20 percentage in boost mode. Simulation results indicate an inductor current ripple of approximately 80 A and a load voltage regulation of approximately 100 V with a tolerance of plus or minus 5 V. The state of charge of the battery increased from 70 percentage to 70.4 percentage during charging and slightly decreased to 70.2 percentage during discharge, with an overall system efficiency of around 93 percentage in both operating modes. These findings demonstrate that a simple threshold based control approach can effectively manage bidirectional power flow, ensuring stable operation of the PV battery system. The proposed design is thus well suitable for embedded applications and small scale photovoltaic installations where the use of complex controllers may be impractical or cost effective.

title: Research on a Novel Overvoltage Suppression Circuit and its Application in Enhancing the Overvoltage Withstand Capability of SVG Devices
authors: Chao You, Xueting Lei, Xin Chen
publish: 2025 4th Asia Power and Electrical Technology Conference (APET)
abstract: The Static Var Generator (SVG) is a key piece of equipment in Flexible AC Transmission Systems (FACTS), and the reliability of its core component, the Insulated Gate Bipolar Transistor (IGBT), is highly susceptible to internal turn-off overvoltage spikes. The superposition of this spike with external faults constitutes the critical bottleneck limiting the overall overvoltage withstand capability of the SVG. Traditional suppression schemes are difficult to apply in high-frequency, highpower applications because they introduce high device turn-on loss during continuous switching; whereas the Metal Oxide Varistor scheme, though having a minor impact on device switching loss, suffers from insufficient non-linearity, leading to poor suppression of conventional turn-off overvoltages. To resolve this contradiction, this paper proposes a novel Overvoltage Withstand Enhancement Circuit based on the series combination of a Gap and an MOV. This circuit utilizes the Gap's breakdown characteristic to precisely engage the MOV during the IGBT's turn-off transient, thereby mitigating the insufficient non-linearity of the traditional MOV and effectively avoiding the restriction imposed by the MOV on the IGBT's permissible voltage. The paper first conducts a detailed comparative analysis of the switching loss and suppression effect of existing schemes, proving the limitations of current technology. Subsequently, by establishing an SVG device-level simulation model, the proposed scheme's effectiveness in enhancing the device's overvoltage withstand capability is verified.

title: AI-Based Personal Protection Equipment Detection and Face Recognition for Automated Access Control
authors: Mariam M., Aoosh M., Reem S., Alyazy S., Irfan Ahmed, Moath Awawdeh, Anees Bashir, Ali Altowati
publish: 2025 Advances in Science and Engineering Technology International Conferences (ASET)
abstract: This paper presents an AI-powered automated access control system designed to enhance workplace safety and operational efficiency by minimizing entry delays and ensuring compliance with personal protective equipment (PPE) requirements. The proposed system integrates real-time video feed analysis with an intuitive user interface, enabling security personnel to monitor and manage access seamlessly. Leveraging the Roboflow dataset, we train a YOLOv8 (You Only Look Once) pre-trained deep neural network to accurately detect essential PPE items such as helmets and gas detectors. For identity verification, the system employs the dlib face_recognition framework, which achieves a high recognition accuracy of 99.38 %. The combined use of PPE detection and facial recognition facilitates a robust and automated access control mechanism that can be deployed in industrial and high-risk environments to ensure both safety compliance and secure entry.

title: Key Technologies and Applications for Planning and Control of Phase Shifter Engineering in Transmission Grids
authors: Quanquan Wang, Jinjiao Lin, Xu Wang, Xingning Han, Xiaodong Liu, Wanchun Qi, Zhiwei Wang
publish: 2025 4th Asia Power and Electrical Technology Conference (APET)
abstract: With the fast development of the new power system, uneven power flow distribution restricts the enhancement of transmission capacity in certain corridors, and the issue of accommodating new energy sources becomes increasingly prominent. Traditional methods for controlling power flow, such as constructing new transmission lines or adjusting generator outputs, are costly and have limited effectiveness. Phase shifter is an economic compact trend control device, can fully tap the power supply capacity of the grid, has a broad application prospect, many places in the country are carrying out phase shifter selection and construction projects. This paper condenses China's first set of transmission grid phase shifter technology research experience, describes the phase shifter siting and capacity, operation control, protection and transformation of key application technologies, and provides an overview of engineering applications and effectiveness data, in order to provide reference for the subsequent development of phase shifter technology and engineering applications.

title: Three Dimensional Surface Fitting and Prediction of Wind Power Photovoltaic Hydropower Energy Storage and Load Curve, and Joint Optimization Strategy for Energy Storage
authors: Zhang Shiling, Wu Jiaxing, Wu Jungang, Jia Yun
publish: 2025 4th Asia Power and Electrical Technology Conference (APET)
abstract: Output characteristics of wind power, photovoltaic power, and hydropower are different and require quantitative analysis and research. Taking 8760 hours of annual data from wind, solar, and water storage in a certain region as an example for the analysis and calculation, the annual output characteristics of wind, solar, water storage are analyzed by annual operation data. Photovoltaic power generation and wind power generation have the significant output characteristics, with wind discharge output power ranging from 25 MW and fluctuating in the range of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 0 - 2 5 M W}$</tex>. Photovoltaic power generation fluctuates within the range of the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0-80 \text{MW}$</tex>, and the fluctuation range is between 6080 MW, so the output of the new energy wind and the solar power has significant volatility. For the wind power generation, its 12 cluster curves show a complex process of change, with an overall trend of low in the middle and high on both sides, and there is a curve with the zero output characteristics. Similarly, the output curve of the photovoltaic power generation presents an inverted “U”-shaped distribution curve, which complements output curve of wind power. However, the smoothness of photovoltaic power generation shows the significant differences. The long short-term memory neural network LSTM network structure is set with 100 hidden neurons in hidden layer and 100 iterations. This indicates that after 100 iterations, the wind solar water storage new energy data can gradually converge to the objective function value, with an overall running time of 56 seconds. And the loss value of the overall objective function can converge to around 0, while the RMSE value also converges to around 0.2.

title: Standby Power of Appliances and Its Measurement Technique
authors: Yogeshkumar Prajapati, Akanksha D. Singh
publish: 2025 Advances in Science and Engineering Technology International Conferences (ASET)
abstract: The conservation of electrical energy has become a critical global priority, with many nations emphasizing the efficient generation and utilization of power. The increasing demand for comfort and improved living standards has led to widespread use of household electrical appliances, many of which consume standby power even when switched off. Although the consumption per device is minimal, its cumulative impact contributes to significant energy waste. Reducing such unnecessary consumption is essential for improving energy efficiency and supporting sustainability goals. The international standard IEC 62301 defines methodologies for measuring standby power, with the selection of techniques and instrumentation dependent on the specific device under test. While manual sampling methods are widely applied, they often involve complex calculations. This paper highlights the necessity and challenges associated with the measurement of standby power. It presents a case study with a detailed comparative analysis of various measurement methods and techniques, offering insights for selecting the most suitable approach for accurate measurement. Furthermore, the paper addresses regional regulations to raise awareness of compliance requirements. The study also demonstrates improvements in measurement precision and efficiency achieved with modern methods compared to traditional approaches.

title: AI-Powered Smart Glasses to Enhance Navigation for Individuals with Visual Impairments
authors: Abdullah Shahid, Adam Burhanpurwala, Karim Elsayed, Mustafa Ashraf, Mohammad I. Daoud
publish: 2025 Advances in Science and Engineering Technology International Conferences (ASET)
abstract: This paper presents a cloud-enabled, AI-powered smart glasses system designed to enhance situational awareness and provide navigational support for individuals with visual impairments. The proposed framework integrates five advanced deep learning models within a modular, cloud-based architecture. These models include a custom-made network for human activity recognition, YOLOv11 for object detection, MiDaS for monocular depth estimation, FaceNet for facial recognition, and BLIP for scene description. The system is deployed on Vuzix Blade 2 smart glasses, which capture user input through voice-based instructions. These instructions are processed by the GPT-4o-mini language model to identify the requested task. The corresponding computer vision operation is executed on a cloud server, enabling real-time, off-device inference. The outputs are then synthesized into natural language feedback and delivered to the user via the glasses' audio interface. Experimental validation confirms the system's functional reliability, with task-specific performance metrics and round-trip latency measurements demonstrating its suitability for real-time assistive applications. Overall, the proposed solution represents a promising platform for intelligent mobility assistance designed to support individuals with visual impairments.

title: DeepCardioNet: A CNN-GRU-Based Diagnostic Engine with Feature Optimization for Early Heart Disease Detection
authors: Febeena Ezhil Jothi S, E Anbalagan, M. Babu, Mahmoud Odeh, Bolagani Balaji
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Advanced Computing and Emerging Technologies (ACET)
abstract: Despite the fact that heart disease continues to be the largest cause of morbidity and mortality across the globe, early identification is essential to an improvement in patient outcomes. In order to enable accurate and timely identification of early-stage cardiovascular conditions from electrocardiogram (ECG) and multimodal clinical data, DeepCardioNet is a novel diagnostic engine that combines convolutional neural networks (CNNs) and gated recurrent units (GRUs) with an advanced feature optimization module. This innovative diagnostic engine is known as DeepCardioNet. Our architecture starts with a CNN-based feature extractor that processes raw ECG waveforms to collect spatial properties such as QRS shape, ST segment abnormalities, and arrhythmic patterns. This is the foundation of our architecture. Next, these attributes are input into a GRU-based recurrent block, which is responsible for modeling the temporal dependencies that are inherent in pulse sequences as well as the fluctuation of heart rate across time. A feature optimization layer that incorporates both genetic algorithm-driven selection and correlation-based filtering helps to reduce input redundancy while maintaining discriminative power. This layer is designed to increase interpretability and performance. Furthermore, the results of our ablation investigation demonstrated that feature optimization enhances the speed of convergence and reduces the complexity of the model by as much as forty percent without compromising diagnostic accuracy. With its excellent diagnostic accuracy, reduced computing cost, and better interpretability, DeepCardioNet is a viable end-to-end framework for early cardiovascular disease screening. In conclusion, DeepCardioNet offers such advantages. The modular design of this device allows for its adaption to different modalities, which could potentially drive clinical implementation in applications including real-time monitoring and telemedicine settings.

title: Metrological Characterization of Low-Cost Sensors for Real-Time Environmental Monitoring Systems in Mobile Applications
authors: Antonio Cannuli, Fabrizio Freni, Antonino Quattrocchi, Giovanni Briguglio, Sarah Ben Haj Fraj, Roberto Montanini
publish: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Metrology for Green Technologies, Renewable Energy and Ecological Sustainability (MetroGREENST)
abstract: This work presents the preliminary metrological characterization of a low-cost environmental monitoring system designed for mobile applications, such as scooters and boats. The system integrates compact commercial sensors for environmental conditions (temperature, humidity, barometric pressure) and air quality evaluation (concentrations of carbon dioxide - CO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>- and carbon monoxide - CO -), interfaced with a Raspberry Pi for real-time data acquisition. GPS tracking is included to enable georeferenced environmental mapping. The focus of this study is the experimental evaluation of sensor performance under controlled laboratory conditions using reference precision instruments. Temperature and humidity sensors were characterized in a climatic chamber, while gas sensors were tested in a customized flow chamber with controlled gas mixtures. Uncertainties were estimated according to the GUM guidelines, highlighting systematic deviations and linearity issues in some low-cost models. These results represent key steps toward the development of reliable, portable and cost-effective vices for dense and dynamic environmental monitoring.

title: Dynamic Aggregation Model of Flexible Resources Based on Bi Level Optimization
authors: Zilun Shen, Ye Tian, Taiyu Gu, Rui Guo, Ying Chai
publish: 2025 4th Asia Power and Electrical Technology Conference (APET)
abstract: In order to deal with the operational uncertainty and volatility caused by the high proportion of renewable energy access, this paper proposes a flexible resource dynamic aggregation optimization model based on two-level optimization. The model uses recurrent neural network (RNN) to model the time sequence characteristics of multiple types of flexible resources (distributed generation, energy storage, adjustable load) in order to achieve output prediction and aggregate capacity evaluation. The upper layer aims to minimize the total system cost, and the lower layer dynamically aggregates flexible resources and generates output constraints based on the RNN prediction results. Through the construction of IEEE 41 bus system example, the multi scenario simulation verification is carried out. The results show that the model is significantly better than the traditional single-layer optimization model in terms of operation cost, network loss and renewable energy utilization rate, in which the total system cost is reduced by 58%, the network loss is reduced by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{7 4 \%}$</tex>, and the renewable energy penetration is increased to 80%. This study provides a theoretical basis and engineering implementation path for the construction of flexible dispatching under the new power system.

title: EPSP-SQL: Enhancing Text-To-SQL by Explicit Prompt-Based Self-Play Fine-Tuning
authors: Ye Liu, Yufei Yang, Taipeng Zhu, Yingwei Liang, Yue Chen, Liang Yan
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Science, and Information Processing (AICSIP)
abstract: Self-Play Fine-Tuning (SPIN) has attracted significant attention in recent years, as it enables large language models (LLMs) to iteratively improve their performance through simulated self-interactions, effectively transforming a weaker model into a stronger one. However, applying SPIN to text-to-SQL tasks remains challenging, primarily due to the model's limited ability to capture implicit schema relationships among database tables during training. To address this limitation, we propose a novel SPIN-based finetuning framework tailored for text-to-SQL tasks, termed EPSP-SQL. Our approach leverages in-context learning to construct explicit prompts that highlight the relationships between database schemas, thereby guiding the model to better understand table interactions during self-play. Experimental results on the Spider dataset demonstrate that EPSP-SQL significantly outperforms existing state-of-the-art methods, achieving superior performance in execution accuracy.

title: Explainable Graph Reasoning for Substation Construction Safety
authors: Liang Chen, Yiting Yin, Bo Huang, Shilun Zeng, Shen Ma
publish: 2025 4th Asia Power and Electrical Technology Conference (APET)
abstract: Safety management at substation construction sites is a critical component for ensuring the stable operation of power systems. However, existing methods that rely on manual labor or rule-based systems exhibit significant limitations, including insufficient discriminative capability, poor generalizability, and a lack of interpretability when dealing with complex work scenarios. To address these deficiencies, this paper proposes a Multi-scale Contrastive Reasoning Framework for Safety (MCTR-Safe), which integrates heterogeneous knowledge graphs and Graph Neural Networks. First, a multi-source heterogeneous knowledge graph encompassing entities such as personnel, equipment, operations, and regulatory clauses is constructed based on one year of construction logs and safety regulation texts. Then, a multi-scale contrastive learning mechanism-operating at the node, subgraph, and path levels-is designed and combined with rule consistency constraints to enhance the model's ability to identify highly similar violation behaviors effectively. Furthermore, an attention-based path mechanism and GNNExplainer are introduced to achieve structured attribution and visual explanation of violation causal chains. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method outperforms traditional rule-based reasoning, GCN, and R-GCN models in terms of accuracy and F1-score, while also providing transparent and traceable decision-making evidence. The research findings provide effective support for the intelligent monitoring and interpretable diagnosis of power construction safety, holding significant theoretical and practical value for safety management in high-risk industrial scenarios.

title: Harnessing Bio-Oil Pyrolysis for Sustainable Aviation Fuel: Technological Advances, Challenges, and Opportunities
authors: Mohamed Dafalla, Abrar Inayat, Chaouki Ghenai, Haif Aljomard, Abdallah Shanableh
publish: 2025 Advances in Science and Engineering Technology International Conferences (ASET)
abstract: The aviation industry is facing pressure to decarbonize, with Sustainable Aviation Fuel (SAF) as an important solution for reducing greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions. Current SAF technologies are limited by feedstock quantity and availability. The pyrolysis route for the conversion of bio-oil derived from biomass stands out due to its raw material flexibility, renewable nature, and ability to integrate with existing refinery infrastructure. The current review provides an overview of the pyrolysis-based production of biooil and its use in SAF synthesis. It addresses the basic principles of the process of pyrolysis, the physical-chemical properties of produced bio-oils, and new upgrading technologies such as hydrodeoxygenation and catalyst cracks. In addition, the review mentions technical-economic analysis and environmental benefits. Challenges on feedstock uncertainty, product instability, and certification barriers are presented, and developing opportunities for innovation and policy assistance arise. The paper aims to act as a reference point for researchers who are interested in upscaling pyrolysis-based SAF production and contributing to a carbon-neutral aviation sector.

title: Explainable Skin Condition Classification Using GCViT with Grad-CAM Visualization: A Multi-Class Analysis of Eczema and Related Dermatoses
authors: Maher Alrahhal, Talal Bonny, Abir Jaafar Hussain, Mohammad Ahmad Qasim Al-Shabi, Mawieh Hamad, Nabeel Al Yateem
publish: 2025 Advances in Science and Engineering Technology International Conferences (ASET)
abstract: This study presents an interpretable deep learning framework for multi-class classification of eczema and related dermatological conditions using a Global Context Vision Transformer (GCViT) integrated with Gradient-weighted Class Activation Mapping (Grad-CAM). The proposed model processes dermoscopic and clinical images of 17 eczema-related categories, leveraging hierarchical attention encoding to extract both local and global contextual features. The framework incorporates a post-hoc explainability module that visualizes salient regions contributing to classification, thereby enhancing clinical trust and transparency. The evaluation was conducted on a curated dermatological dataset comprising 1,299 labeled images, partitioned using a 90:10 training-to-validation ratio. The model achieved an average classification accuracy of 98.66%, recall of 82.22%, and AUC of 0.9076, outperforming conventional CNN-based architectures. Grad-CAM visualizations confirmed alignment between model focus areas and clinical lesion regions such as erythema, vesicles, scaling, and lichenification. The results demonstrate the effectiveness and interpretability of the GCViT-XAI pipeline, affirming its potential as a reliable decision-support tool in dermatology and telemedicine applications.

title: Numerical Analysis and Performance Optimization of Paraboloidal Solar Dish Receiver Using Finite Element Analysis and Advanced Thermal Materials
authors: Ahana Fatima Alex, R Vinoth, Ravishankar Dudhe
publish: 2025 Advances in Science and Engineering Technology International Conferences (ASET)
abstract: An extensive overview of the developments in paraboloidal solar dish reception technology is given in this research article. A key element of concentrating solar power systems are paraboloid solar dish receivers, which use focused sunlight to produce high-temperature heat for a variety of uses, such as the production of electricity and industrial processes. The design concepts, functional traits, current technological advancements, and prospective uses of paraboloidal solar dish receivers are covered in this study. The study focuses on the most important scientific discoveries, obstacles, and potential paths forward for this exciting new solar energy technology. Solar dish receiver was designed using finite element analysis tool to observe its effectiveness over conventional receivers. The data obtained through simulation study was compared with published data for validation. The study further incorporated various receiver geometric evaluations to determine the stability of the structure and thermal efficacy. Thermal durability and emissivity were also monitored by analysing the materials of the receiver under different temperature loads. Investigation on integration potential of the dish and its coating technologies were done along with precision of tracking system and its effect on focus accuracy. Heat transfer fluids and its role on performance of the solar dish were studied by and large. This comprehensive study not only scale up the understanding of paraboloidal solar dish receiver dynamics but also add in as a guide to future research with details on maximum energy conversion efficiency and the cost viability in solar thermal systems

title: Identifying Core Innovation Dimensions via Semantic Clustering with a Novel Pairwise Validation Metric
authors: Sarah Malaeb, Imad Saleh, Maroun Jneid
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: Innovation capability frameworks remain fragmented, with inconsistent taxonomies that constrain maturity assessment and comparability across academic, industrial, and policy contexts. Addressing this challenge requires the systematic identification of core innovation dimensions from heterogeneous textual sources. This task is difficult, as it relies on clustering short, sparse, and semantically ambiguous texts, where ground-truth labels are absent and existing validation metrics fall short. Traditional internal and external indices rely on geometric properties or labeled data, while semantic measures improve interpretability but fail to assess cross-method stability. To overcome this gap, we propose the Pairwise Clustering Match Ratio (PCMR), a novel validation metric that quantifies cross-method agreement. PCMR serves a dual role: as a validation tool assessing stability and interpretability of clustering results, and as a model selection tool identifying the most robust algorithm. Applied within a hybrid methodology combining unsupervised clustering (K-means, Agglomerative Hierarchical Clustering) with a semi-supervised semantic classification using Sentence-BERT embeddings and cosine similarity, PCMR achieved a global mean score of 0.79 and identified K-means as the best-performing method. Through iterative validation and pruning of weak categories, we consolidated a stable set of 14 core innovation dimensions. These findings provide a semantically coherent, empirically validated dimensional structure to support unified innovation taxonomies, organizational maturity assessment, and the evaluation of innovation-related educational programs.

title: GenAI-Powered Contextual Micro-Learning Recommender for Personalized Education
authors: Akshy Mittal, Anusha Nagineni, Krishna Kandi, Vamsi Alla
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: This paper introduces a novel framework integrating Machine Learning (ML), Natural Language Processing (NLP), and Generative AI (Gen AI) to deliver personalized micro-learning content for e-learning platforms. The system leverages NLP for real-time learner context analysis, ML for adaptive content recommendation, and Gen AI for dynamic content generation, ensuring accessibility and scalability. Evaluated on simulated datasets, the framework achieves high engagement and personalization accuracy, addressing IEEE/ICCA'25 themes of AI, e-learning, and knowledge extraction. A case study demonstrates its efficacy in multilingual STEM education, promoting global education equity. The framework's design incorporates recent advancements in AI to overcome limitations in traditional systems, providing a scalable solution for diverse educational environments.

title: Adaptive Multimodal Biomarker Fusion: A Machine Learning Framework for Personalized Mood Disorder Management
authors: Shirin Abdallah Alimour, Dina Naser Tahat, Mohammad Alrabeei, Suhair Naser Alrawash, Wedad Alakal, Jassim Al Hammadi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Communication, Networking and Services (ICCNS)
abstract: Mood disorders such as bipolar disorder and major depressive disorder represent persistent challenges in psychiatric medicine due to their episodic nature, diagnostic subjectivity, and biological complexity. This paper introduces AMP-Fuse, a modular, cyber-physical architecture for adaptive multimodal biomarker fusion, aimed at transforming psychiatric diagnostics and real-time intervention. The proposed system integrates a neuro-symbolic reasoning pipeline that dynamically fuses behavioral rhythms (actigraphy), neuroelectrical activity (EEG), and biochemical markers into a unified, context-aware psychiatric interface. At its core, an adaptive inference engine employs attention-guided learning and bidirectional neuro-computational feedback loops to personalize psychiatric decision-making across temporal scales. A human-in-the-Ioop co-adaptation mechanism ensures that algorithmic learning remains clinically aligned and ethically grounded. The resulting output is a real-time Cognitive Digital Twin-a living, interpretable simulation of patient mood dynamics-designed for seamless integration in ambulatory, clinical, and home-based environments. AMP-Fuse lays the foundation for the next generation of precision psychiatry systems: scalable, explainable, and continuously evolving with clinician feedback.

title: A Blockchain-Monitored Agentic AI Architecture for Trusted Perception–Reasoning–Action Pipelines
authors: Salman Jan, Hasan Ali Razzaqi, Ali Akarma, Mohammad Riyaz Belgaum
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: The application of agentic AI systems in autonomous decision-making is growing in the areas of healthcare, smart cities, digital forensics, and supply chain management. Even though these systems are flexible and offer real-time reasoning, they also raise concerns of trust and oversight, and integrity of the information and activities upon which they are founded. The paper suggests a single architecture model comprising of LangChain-based multi-agent system with a permissioned blockchain to guarantee constant monitoring, policy enforcement, and immutable auditability of agentic action. The framework relates the perception conceptualization-action cycle to a blockchain layer of governance that verifies the inputs, evaluates recommended actions, and documents the outcomes of the execution. A Hyperledger Fabric-based system, action executors MCP-integrated, and LangChain agent are introduced and experiments of smart inventory management, traffic-signal control, and healthcare monitoring are done. The results suggest that blockchain-security verification is efficient in preventing unauthorized practices, offers traceability throughout the whole decision-making process, and maintains operational latency within reasonable ranges. The suggested framework provides a universal system of implementing high-impact agentic AI applications that are autonomous yet responsible.

title: Exploring Real-World Applications on Peer-to-Peer Energy Trading and Sharing Systems
authors: Ayman Akila, Ahmed Abo-Khalil, Ramesh C. Bansal
publish: 2025 Advances in Science and Engineering Technology International Conferences (ASET)
abstract: Peer-to-peer (P2P) energy trading and sharing systems have emerged as a disruptive innovation in modern electricity markets, enabling direct energy transactions between prosumers and consumers without reliance on centralized utilities. By leveraging technologies such as blockchain, smart contracts, and IoT-enabled smart meters, P2P energy trading fosters energy decentralization, cost efficiency, and renewable energy integration. This paper explores the fundamentals of P2P energy trading, highlighting its real-world applications through case studies, including the Brooklyn Microgrid (USA), Power Ledger (Australia), and SOLshare (Bangladesh). This paper also explores various projects on P2P energy trading systems with their unique objectives. Despite its economic and environmental benefits, challenges such as regulatory constraints, cybersecurity risks, scalability issues, and consumer adoption barriers hinder widespread implementation. Future advancements, including AI-driven trading optimization, vehicle-to-grid (V2G) integration, and policy reforms, are expected to accelerate the transition toward sustainable, decentralized energy systems. As a review-oriented study, this paper does not present new experimental or modeling results, but instead synthesizes insights from real-world implementations to guide future research and development. By addressing key challenges and fostering innovation, P2P energy trading can play a pivotal role in reshaping global energy markets and enhancing energy resilience.

title: A Review of Electric Vehicle Hosting Capacity Assessment in Distribution Networks: Methods, Mathematical Models, and Enhancement Strategies
authors: Yanfang Mao, Xiaoxiang Lv, Zhujian Ou, Yu Zong, Ting Qin, Zhonghao Qian
publish: 2025 4th Asia Power and Electrical Technology Conference (APET)
abstract: The exponential proliferation of electric vehicles (EVs) represents a paradigm shift for distribution networks (DNs), transforming them from passive energy delivery systems into active, constraint-limited environments. The uncoordinated integration of high-penetration EV charging loads imposes multidimensional technical challenges, including voltage limit violations, thermal overloading of transformers, and the propagation of harmonic distortion. Consequently, the accurate quantification of electric vehicle hosting capacity (EVHC)—defined as the maximum magnitude of EV charging load a network can accommodate without violating operational performance indices—has become a prerequisite for resilient grid planning. This paper provides a comprehensive review of the state-of-the-art in EVHC assessment. It systematically categorizes methodologies into deterministic, stochastic (including Monte Carlo simulation, point estimate methods, and Gram-Charlier expansion), and datadriven approaches. Furthermore, it critically analyzes the mathematical formulations governing these assessments, with a specific focus on emerging constraints such as harmonic distortion and voltage unbalance. Finally, the paper evaluates enhancement strategies, ranging from traditional grid reinforcement to advanced soft solutions like soft open points (SOPs), dynamic network reconfiguration, and vehicle-to-grid (V2G) coordination, providing a comparative techno-economic analysis to guide future research and industrial deployment.
