title: Innovation and Practice of Hydropower Station Management Model of Data Distributed Federations
authors: Saixiao Huang, Jiao Li, Jin Wu, Fan Zhou
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Science, and Information Processing (AICSIP)
abstract: The traditional management model of hydropower stations faces issues such as system fragmentation, information isolation, and security risks during the transition to intelligent operations, which impede operational efficiency and economic benefits. An innovative intelligent management framework for hydropower stations based on a distributed integrated concept is proposed. This framework centers on constructing a unified watershed data resource pool and employs standardized, high-performance technical components. Through federated data collaboration among multiple nodes, centralized management and collaborative application of global data are achieved. A hierarchical architecture is adopted, integrating diverse data sources and building a hot and cold tiered storage system. A unified computing engine provides flexible data service interfaces, and a center-edge collaborative model is deployed. The core innovation lies in the collaboration mechanism, which enables elastic allocation and sharing of data services, storage resources, and computing resources. The results indicate that this framework effectively overcomes the challenges of a fragmented system architecture, information barriers, and technological dependence. Through consistent technical standards and processes, inter-system linkage for production monitoring and safety management is realized. The research conclusions facilitate the digital transformation of hydropower stations from data silos to intelligent decision-making, ultimately significantly enhancing operational efficiency and economic benefits.

title: Examining the Mediating Role of Sustainable Innovation in the Impact of Cybersecurity Measures on Enhancing Supply Chain Resilience: Insights from the Electronics Manufacturing Sector
authors: Hamzeh Alhawamdeh, Mahmoud Allahham, Wasef Ibrahim Almajali, Ahmad Y. A. Bani Ahmad, Firas Tayseer Ayasrah, Sajead Mowafaq Alshdaifat, Ala Mohammad Shdouh, Fadhli Ranuharja, Dony Novaliendry
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Communication, Networking and Services (ICCNS)
abstract: This study explores the relationship between cybersecurity in the electronics manufacturing sector and resilience in supply chain management, focusing on sustainable innovation as a mediating factor. This study applies the Resource-Based View (RBV) and Dynamic Capabilities Theory (DCT) to examine how processes driven by cybersecurity employment-based practices underpin sustainable innovation while increasing the ability to adapt supply chains. For segment by Application, the sub-segments are Electronics Logistics Service Providers, Electronics Component Manufacturers, Electronics Manufacturing Services(EMS), and Original Design Manufacturers (ODMs). The relationships among cybersecurity measures, sustainable innovation, and supply chain resilience are tested using the partial least squares structural equation modeling (PLS-SEM) approach. The findings provide insight into how critical intermediate outcomes, as exemplified by improvements in sustainable innovation induced by robust cybersecurity, serve to increase supply chain resilience. Cybersecurity and sustainable innovation have a crucial common interest that emphasizes the need to implement cybersecurity frameworks that can effectively respond to Supply Chain for operational effectiveness and long-term sustainability. The findings suggest that firm actions that consider cybersecurity preparedness and innovation-driven adaptability are important for strengthening such chain resilience in an increasingly digital and complex marketplace. This paper contributes to the literature through the empirical assessment of how sustainable innovation mediates the relationship between cybersecurity tools and supply chain resilience. In this blog post, we aim to share these insights for industry practitioners who can leverage cybersecurity, in turn, as a driver of sustainable and resilient supply chain strategies in the electronics manufacturing industry.

title: Distributed Energy Resources Operation with Stationary Battery Storage Cooling in Southeast Asian Islanded Distribution Networks
authors: Ruoxuan Leng, Nan Zhou
publish: 2025 4th Asia Power and Electrical Technology Conference (APET)
abstract: This study presents a new operation optimization method for distributed energy resource (DER) units in islanded distribution networks (DNs) of Southeast Asia, a region featured by high solar irradiance, consistently high temperatures and unpredictable weather patterns. First, a holistic thermal model for stationary battery energy storage systems (ESSs) is proposed with a detailed analysis of internal cooling power consumption. Then, to deal with voltage fluctuations caused by intermittent photovoltaic (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{P V}$</tex>) power generation caused by the region's volatile weather, the PV inverter oversizing is modeled to enable reactive power support while accounting for inverter lifespan degradation. Finally, the entire optimization problem is modeled by two stage stochastic programming, and the solution approach based on model linearization and relaxation is developed. Simulation tests on an IEEE-34 bus three-phase DN demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed method.

title: Few-Shot Brain Tumor Classification from MRI Using a Meta-Learning-Based CNN Framework
authors: Maher Alrahhal, Abdul-Kadir Hamid, Talal Bonny, Samar Mouti
publish: 2025 Advances in Science and Engineering Technology International Conferences (ASET)
abstract: Accurate classification of brain tumors from magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) is vital for early diagnosis and treatment planning. Conventional deep learning models require large, annotated datasets, which are often unavailable in clinical practice due to privacy and labeling constraints. We propose FSMeta-CNN, a few-shot meta-learning convolutional neural network designed to adapt rapidly to unseen tumor classes with minimal labeled samples. The framework employs an episodic training strategy with inner-outer loop meta-optimization, where each episode simulates <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$K$</tex>-way, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$N$</tex>-shot diagnostic tasks. <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$A$</tex> lightweight CNN backbone is used for feature embedding, and the meta-learner is optimized to generalize quickly across episodes. Evaluation was conducted on a multi-source MRI dataset with glioma, meningioma, pituitary, and no-tumor classes, using patient-level splits to ensure robust generalization. FSMeta-CNN achieved macro-averaged accuracy, precision, and F1-scores above 97%. These results highlight the model's capacity to operate effectively in low-data regimes, supporting scalable and dataefficient deployment in clinical neuro-oncology workflows.

title: Development of Educational Trainer Using 8051, Arduino, ESP8266, Raspberry Pi for Multi-Platform Microcontroller Learning
authors: Jerry I. Teleron
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: This study presents the design, development, and evaluation of a dual-module educational microcontroller trainer system aimed at improving embedded systems and IoT instruction for Computer Engineering students. The trainer comprises two integrated modules: (1) the Embedded Module utilizing AT89C2051/AT89C4051, which represents the Intel 8051 family, and (2) the IoT Module featuring Arduino UNO, ESP8266, and Raspberry Pi. The Embedded Module provides learners with practical exposure to low-level programming, machine code execution, and microcontroller interfacing using classic 8051 architecture, supporting core topics such as timers, serial communication, interrupts, and digital I/O control. In parallel, the IoT Module focuses on higher-level development, covering sensor interfacing, real-time clock implementation, wireless data transmission, and Python-based IoT automation. A total of twelve hands-on experiments were developed for the Embedded Module, while ten were designed for the IoT Module, ensuring structured and progressive learning. The system was implemented and evaluated by engineering and IT students, and the feedback indicated a high satisfaction rate of 97.5% in terms of usability and learning outcomes. The trainer's modularity, cost-effectiveness, and alignment with CHED CMO No. 87, s. 2017 outcomes position it as a scalable and practical instructional tool for embedded and IoT education.

title: Automated Feature Extraction and UML Modeling from Real-World Unstructured Egyptian Arabic Text
authors: Farah El Degheidy, Raghda El Ebrashi, Hany Abdelghaffar, Mervat Abu-Elkheir
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: Conventional requirements engineering (RE) is often a manual process that is tedious, time-consuming, susceptible to errors, and hard to scale in case of continuous updates. Recent advances in natural language processing (NLP) transformed the RE process via automation. However, automation works better with structured data and formal requirement formats, limiting the ability to elicit and extract requirements from unstructured data; the rapid growth of digital platforms accentuates the need for treating user posts, comments, opinions and feedback as primary data sources for requirements mining and modeling. However, research conducted on unstructured Egyptian Arabic text is largely underexplored, due to its inherent linguistic challenges. This paper fills a critical gap in Arabic NLP, by providing an automated feature extraction and UML modeling pipeline utilizing state of the art embedding models, density-based clustering algorithm, and Large Language Models (LLMs), specifically engineered for handling Egyptian-Arabic text. The proposed pipeline demonstrates strong positive results, producing quality features and meaningful use case diagrams, and further confirms the feasibility of applying this advanced NLP-based pipeline to real world Egyptian dialectal data.

title: Performance Optimization of LLM-Based Agentic Workloads in Kubernetes Environments: Bottlenecks, Root Causes, and Solutions
authors: Akshay Mittal, Goutam Tadi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: Large Language Model-based AI agents deployed in Kubernetes containerized environments face unique performance bottlenecks that differ fundamentally from traditional applications. This paper presents a comprehensive analysis of critical bottlenecks affecting LLM agent workloads, including GPU memory constraints, container orchestration overhead, storage I/O limitations, and inter-agent communication inefficiencies. Through systematic evaluation of production deployments and optimization strategies, we identify root causes and provide actionable solutions. Our findings demonstrate that proper GPU memory management, storage optimization, and intelligent workload orchestration achieve 2-5x throughput improvements, 33x faster cold starts, and 60

title: Research on Ice Detection Method of Wind Turbine Blades Based on Fusion Convolutional Neural Network
authors: Wang Tingting, Xing Zuoxia
publish: 2025 4th Asia Power and Electrical Technology Conference (APET)
abstract: To address the reduced detection accuracy of wind turbine blade icing caused by wind condition variations, this study proposes an ice detection method based on fused convolutional neural networks under variable wind conditions. First, the XGBoost algorithm is utilized to calculate feature weights in SCADA data variables of wind turbines, selecting strongly correlated variables to reduce computational load and improve diagnostic efficiency. Second, considering both blade icing detection and the impact of wind condition changes on accuracy, a multi-convolutional network fusion method is developed. The framework includes: multiple sub-convolutional network units for extracting fault signal features under specific wind conditions; a weight allocation unit to adjust feature distribution across different wind conditions; and a feature fusion unit responsible for integrating fault features, thereby enabling effective detection of blade icing under unknown wind conditions. Finally, a cross-validation dataset is established to validate the proposed method, achieving an average detection accuracy of over 97 % for blade icing, demonstrating its effectiveness.

title: Design and Implementation of Bluetooth-Enabled Multi-Sport Scoreboard with Dual ESP32 Controllers and Android App
authors: Al D. Horteza, Jerry I. Teleron
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: The researchers created a scoreboard system that works for basketball, volleyball, and also badminton. The system uses two ESP32 microcontrollers and a custom Android app, which connects through Bluetooth for remote control. The hardware includes a clear In-line LED display that shows the score, time, fouls, time-outs, set wins, and service indicators in real time. The Android app allows the official to switch quickly between different sport modes. The system was tested during actual games and performed well, showing fast response and easy operation. Five IT professionals evaluated the system. They noted its clear display, reliable Bluetooth connection, simple mobile interface, portability, and good overall function. Their ratings ranged from very good to excellent. This suggests the scoreboard system is cost-effective and user-friendly, making it suitable for schools, sports clubs, and community events. The study shows that low-cost microcontrollers and mobile apps can be combined to make flexible and affordable scoreboard systems. The researcher recommends improving the LED’s visibility under sunlight, increasing Bluetooth range for bigger venues, and adding surge protection for stable AC use. With these improvements, the system could be used more widely in both educational and professional sports.

title: Self-Heating Aware Performance Investigation of Vertically and Sideway Stacked GAA Nanowire FETs at Sub-5nm Technology Node
authors: Guruprasad Reddy, Jagan V, Shashidhara M, Shobhit Srivastava, Abhishek Acharya
publish: 2026 39th International Conference on VLSI Design &amp; 25th International Conference on Embedded Systems (VLSID)
abstract: The aggressive scaling of semiconductor devices has led to significant advancements in transistor technology. Gate-All-Around (GAA) nanowire FETs are promising candidates for sub- 5 nm nodes due to their superior electrostatic control and scalability. However, self-heating effects (SHE) present a major challenge, degrading device performance and reliability. This work employs calibrated Sentaurus TCAD simulations to quantify SHE in vertically and sideways stacked GAA nanowire FETs. For the vertically stacked configuration (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$4 \times 5 ~\text{nm}$</tex> wires), SHE raises the peak lattice temperature to approximately 430 K, causing an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{8. 4 \%}$</tex> reduction in ON-current (from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{1 0 8} \mu \mathrm{A}$</tex> to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$99.6 \mu ~\mathrm{A}$</tex>) and a 4 mV threshold-voltage shift (from 0.315 V to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.311 ~\mathrm{V})$</tex> at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$V_{\text{GS}}=V_{\text{DS}}=1 ~\mathrm{V}$</tex>.The peak temperature difference between channels is <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim 17 ~\mathrm{K}$</tex>. In contrast, the sideway-stacked design (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2 \times 2$</tex> array) experiences a higher peak lattice temperature near 490 K, but with a 12.5 % degradation of ON-current (from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{1 4 4} \mu \mathrm{A}$</tex> to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{1 2 8} \mu \mathrm{A}$</tex>) a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{5 ~ m V}$</tex> threshold-voltage shift (from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{0. 3 2 5 V}$</tex> to 0.320 V) at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$V_{\text{GS}}=V_{\text{DS}}=1 ~\mathrm{V}$</tex>,while maintaining a significantly lower leakage current of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{1. 3 ~ p A}$</tex>. The peak temperature difference between channels is <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim 12 ~\mathrm{K}$</tex>. Despite the higher temperature, sideway stacking delivers the highest ON -current among the nanowire configurations, offering an effective trade-off between thermal management and drive current, suitable for future GAA nanowire FET architectures.

title: Generative AI for Intent-Driven Network Management in 6G RAN: A Case Study on the Mamba Model
authors: Md Arafat Habib, Medhat Elsayed, Yigit Ozcan, Pedro Enrique Iturria-Rivera, Majid Bavand, Melike Erol-Kantarci
abstract: With the emergence of 6G, mobile networks are becoming increasingly heterogeneous and dynamic, necessitating advanced automation for efficient management. Intent-Driven Networks (IDNs) address this by translating high-level intents into optimization policies. Large Language Models (LLMs) can enhance this process by understanding complex human instructions, enabling adaptive and intelligent automation. Given the rapid advancements in Generative AI (GenAI), a comprehensive survey of LLM-based IDN architectures in disaggregated Radio Access Network (RAN) environments is both timely and critical. This article provides such a survey, along with a case study on a selective State-Space Model (SSM)-enabled IDN architecture that integrates GenAI across three key stages: intent processing, intent validation, and intent execution. For the first time in the literature, we propose a hierarchical framework built on Mamba-SSM that introduces GenAI across all stages of the IDN pipeline. We further present a case study demonstrating that the proposed Mamba architecture significantly improves network performance through intelligent automation, surpassing existing IDN approaches. In a multi-cell 5G/6G scenario, the proposed architecture reduces quality of service drift by up to <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><mml:math display="inline"><mml:mrow><mml:mn>70</mml:mn><mml:mi>%</mml:mi></mml:mrow></mml:math></inline-formula>, improves throughput by up to 80 Mbps, and lowers inference time to 60–70 ms, outperforming GenAI, reinforcement learning, and non-machine learning baselines.

title: Hierarchical Control Multi-Agent DRL for Vehicle Twin Migration with Workload Prediction in UAV-Assisted Vehicular Metaverses
authors: Junlong Chen, Yingkai Kang, Jiawen Kang, Minrui Xu, Yongju Tong, Fan Wu, Dusit Niyato
abstract: Vehicular metaverses enable immersive digital experiences through seamless Vehicle Twin (VT) services. As vehicles move, VT service instances must migrate between RoadSide Units (RSUs) to sustain low-latency interactions. However, RSUs face significant challenges from dynamic workload fluctuations and uneven geographical distribution. These limitations often result in service degradation during peak demand periods. Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) offer promising solutions to augment fixed infrastructure capacity. Nevertheless, their energy constraints and trajectory optimization create additional complexity for resource management. To address these challenges, we develop a novel framework integrating workload forecasting with coordinated decision-making for VT migration and UAV routing. We first design a long short-term memory-based workload prediction model. This model predicts workload patterns by combining spatial feature extraction with temporal dependency modeling. We enhance the prediction capability through noise-augmented training to improve robustness. Then, we formulate the VT migration and UAV routing optimization as a markov decision process, which captures the sequential nature of decision-making. Finally, we propose a hierarchical control multi-agent deep reinforcement learning algorithm where the upper-layer controller uses multi-agent proximal policy optimization for collaborative decision-making, and the lower-layer controller handles VT migration and UAV routing execution. Simulation results show that the proposed approach reduces average latency by 25.70% and validation loss by 63.70% for workload prediction compared to baseline methods.

title: Model-based Fault-Tolerant Control for Induction Motor Drive under Progressive and Sudden Current Sensor Faults
authors: Magdalena Miniach, Teresa Orlowska-Kowalska
abstract: Ensuring high-quality measurement of stator phase currents is a key objective in contemporary AC drive systems, as measurement errors can lead to instability, reduced efficiency, and degraded control performance. This paper proposes the fault-tolerant control (FTC) based on the novel Current Sensor Measurement Error Removal System (C-SMERS), designed to detect, classify, and compensate for disturbances in measured signals (amplification errors and offsets) and complete sensor faults in the vector-controlled induction motor drive. The method is purely analytical and relies on extracting fault-related symptoms from measured and estimated phase currents sampled at signal peaks. By analyzing the relationship between signals, corrective factors are formulated to compensate for sensor faults, while supplementary coefficients reduce influence of modeling inaccuracies on estimation. C-SMERS enables early detection and effective correction of both progressive and sudden changes in the current signal, accurately identifies the disturbance type, intensity, and polarity. The proposed approach is computationally efficient, and requires no additional diagnostic signals. The experimental results confirm the effectiveness of fault detection and correction under various operating conditions of the drive system, including low-speed operation, contributing to a significant reduction of speed ripples in post-fault operation.

title: YOLO-DMNet: A Dual-Module Enhanced Detector with Multi-Scale Attention Fusion
authors: Lin Shi, Shaoqi Tian, Yafeng Wu, Zhanlin Ji, Ivan Ganchev
abstract: Accurate weed detection in precision agriculture remains a critical challenge due to small target sizes, dense distributions, and complex backgrounds in open-field environments. Existing object detection models often suffer from degraded performance under such conditions. This study presents YOLO-DMNet, a lightweight and high-performance object detection model optimized for field weed scenarios. The proposed model is built upon the YOLOv8n architecture by incorporating two novel module types to enhance feature extraction and multi-scale perception. The C2f with Multi-Directional Perception Attention (C2f_MDPA) module integrates multi-scale dilated convolutions with channel and spatial attention mechanisms, thereby improving the representation of small and occluded targets. The Multi-Scale Enhanced Aggregation (MSEA) module, embedded in the detection head of the proposed model, introduces scale-adaptive convolutions and channel-guided attention to reinforce the perception of dense and morphologically diverse weed instances. Experimental evaluations on three publicly available weed datasets—CWC, CropsOrWeed9, and CottonWeedDet12—demonstrate that YOLO-DMNet achieves substantial improvements according to various indicators, compared to state-of-the-art models, while maintaining a compact architecture suitable for resource-constrained deployment. These results highlight the model’s robustness and applicability in complex agricultural settings, offering promising potential for intelligent machinery and real-time precision weed management in the field.

title: A Compact Image-Rejection LNA Based on Pole-Zero Engineered Colpitts Active Trap
authors: Feng Qiu, Cao Wan, Yuanjin Zheng, Quan Xue
publish: IEEE Transactions on Microwave Theory and Techniques
abstract: This article presents a 24–29.5 GHz image-rejection low-noise amplifier (LNA) in 65-nm CMOS that addresses the tradeoffs between image rejection depth, bandwidth, and in-band loss. Conventional on-chip inductor-capacitor (LC) traps suffer from finite quality factors ( $Q$ -factors) and often require multistage cascading to cover wideband image frequencies, which severely degrades noise figure (NF) and incurs a significant area penalty. To overcome these limitations, a pole-zero engineered active trap is proposed. This topology utilizes a Colpitts-based negative resistance core embedded within a modified pole-zero engineered LC network. By creating a high- $Q$ transmission zero at the image frequency via parasitic neutralization and a parallel resonance pole at the RF signal frequency, the proposed technique achieves deep, wideband image rejection with negligible loading to the signal path. Additionally, a digital-assisted automatic gain control (AGC) loop is integrated in the proposed image rejection LNA to extend the maximum input power handling capability and the upper limit of the dynamic range. Measurement results demonstrate a peak gain of 23 dB and a NF of 2.7–3.7 dB, while consuming 18 mW from a 1.2-V supply. The proposed image rejection LNA yields an image rejection ratio (IRR) of 32–46dB within a compact core area of 0.12 mm2.

title: Path Replanning and Hybrid Fault Tolerant Control for Intelligent Vehicle Under Steering System Faults
authors: Jinheng Han, Junzhi Zhang, Shiyue Zhao, Zhen Han, Yutong Jiang, Henglai Wei
abstract: Steering faults can compromise the lateral control performance of intelligent vehicles, creating a trade-off between tracking performance and degraded actuation capabilities severely. Traditional fault tolerant control methods rely heavily on accurate vehicle models, but inevitable model mismatches in real world scenarios limit their effectiveness. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a replanning based hybrid fault tolerant controller that integrates dynamic path replanning with data driven and model based fault tolerant control strategy. Specifically, a path replanning module is developed by incorporating barrier functions and path tracking objectives to refine the infeasible reference path dynamically. Furthermore, to overcome the limitations of conventional model based fault tolerant controllers and enhance the adaptability of the data driven works to unmodel part, a hybrid fault tolerant path tracking controller is designed by integrating data driven control approach into the model based approach. The model predictive controller provides the fundamental description of the system, while a data driven fault tolerant controller is constructed using online driving data to compensate simulation to reality gap. Then, the fusion strategy is employed to coordinate the different control properties and combine the advantage of both strategy. Extensive simulations and real vehicle experiments validate the superiority and effectiveness of the proposed approach in practical driving scenarios.

title: Optimized Hybrid Deep Learning Models for Realtime Public Wi-Fi Intrusion Detection
authors: Vibhor Puri, Nitish Sawant, Swet Chandan
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Advanced Computing and Emerging Technologies (ACET)
abstract: Public Wi-Fi hotspots in cafés, airports and universities are easy access points to the internet, but expose users and administrators to high cybersecurity risks. This paper presents research on these inadequacies through comparison of multiple algorithms of intrusion detection. This is done using the UNSW-NB15 benchmark dataset, not containing any original data for the original datasets and using official training and testing CSV files to prevent data leakage. Traditional machine learning models including Logistic Regression, Decision Tree, Random Forest, XGBoost, and Naïve Bayes are evaluated with applicable hybrid deep learning models like CNN+LSTM, CNN+BiLSTM, and XGBoost+LSTM. Optimization techniques to improve accuracy and efficiency will include the use of feature engineering, class weighting, and hyperparameter tuning. Evaluation metrics including accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, and confusion matrix analysis will make an argument that hybrid models provide better accuracy than traditional models, dispersion between training and test accuracy models, and minimize false negatives. And lastly, a two-tier detection approach for real-time public Wi-Fi monitoring using lightweight classical models alongside hybrid models of deep learning, an efficient solution with high accuracy intrusion detection.

title: Evaluation of the Stability of Biocidal Molecules Adsorbed on Mesoporous MCM-41 for Use in Air Purification Systems
authors: Anna Laura Tassi, Alessia Santiglia, Vittoria Guglielmi, Laura Santagostini
publish: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Metrology for Green Technologies, Renewable Energy and Ecological Sustainability (MetroGREENST)
abstract: This study focuses on the characterization of a potential passive air purification system based on the controlled release of natural biocides - specifically essential oils - adsorbed onto MCM-41 mesoporous silica particles. Iso-eugenol, linalool, and 4-terpineol were used as representative active compounds due to their bioactivity and favorable physicochemical properties. These compounds, derived from essential oils such as clove, cinnamon, lavender, and tea tree, were incorporated into MCM-41 using physisorption process. To evaluate the loading efficiency and release behavior of the active compounds, a combined analytical approach using Near-Infrared (NIR) spectroscopy and Headspace Gas Chromatography with Flame Ionization Detection (HS-GC-FID) was adopted. NIR spectroscopy, as a rapid and non-destructive technique, was used to estimate the amount of essential oil physically adsorbed onto the silica matrix. Chemometric techniques, including multivariate calibration and principal component analysis, were applied to interpret the complex NIR spectra and enhance quantification accuracy. Meanwhile, HS-GC-FID allowed precise monitoring of compound desorption under various temperature and humidity conditions. This integrated strategy offers a reliable and efficient method for developing eco-friendly air purification systems based on natural bioactive compounds.

title: CubeSat Attitude Determination and Control Testbed Design
authors: Abdalla Saad Elshaal, Khaled Shehadeh, Hazza Alshamsi, Shamma Jamali, Mohamed Okasha
publish: 2025 Advances in Science and Engineering Technology International Conferences (ASET)
abstract: This paper presents the design, development, and validation of a novel Attitude Determination and Control Subsystem (ADCS) testbed for CubeSats. It focuses on creating a quasi-frictionless environment using an air bearing with a porous graphite restrictor. The testbed simulates the microgravity conditions of space, enabling accurate functional testing of ADCS algorithms and hardware before deployment. A comprehensive literature review highlights various ADCS ground testbed configurations, each with distinct advantages and limitations. The proposed system employs a porous graphite air bearing, selected for its cost-effectiveness, machinability, and favorable tribological properties. Design requirements emphasize non-ferromagnetic materials, a load capacity up to 30 kg, seamless integration, and safety features such as overpressure protection and emergency shutdowns. The experimental results show that the graphite restrictor air bearing enables free rotation in yaw and at least <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm 45^{\circ}$</tex> in pitch and roll. Also, it demonstrated superior performance in holding the ADCS system at 0.4 MPa (4 bar) compared to the multiorifice air bearing, which could only hold 2 Kg at the same pressure. The test performance suggests that this bearing can meet system requirements of up to 30 kg (12U CubeSat). This work addresses the gap in research on aerostatic bearings employing porous graphite, demonstrating its viability for highprecision ADCS testing and offering a robust, adaptable platform for future CubeSat development and validation. Future work will focus on several key areas to further validate the developed air-bearing system and expand the quantitative assessment of the air-bearing system to ensure comprehensive characterization and reliability.

title: Video Deepfake Detection in English and Arabic
authors: Rehab Said Al Nassri, Rawad Abdulghafor, Mohammed Abdulla Salim Al Husaini, Wasin AlKishri, Yousuf Al Husaini, Mohammad Abrar
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: The rapid advancement of artificial intelligence has led to the emergence of deepfake technology, which enables the creation of highly realistic synthetic videos that can manipulate human faces and voices. Detecting such manipulations is crucial for maintaining digital integrity and preventing misinformation. This paper presents a proof-of-concept deep learning approach for video deepfake detection using a ResNet-50 convolutional neural network (CNN) applied to still video frames in both Arabic and English contexts. The model is trained and evaluated on a relatively small labeled dataset of real and manipulated video frames, using 10-fold cross-validation. On this dataset, the model achieves high frame-level performance in terms of accuracy, precision, recall, specificity, F1-score, and AUC. However, because the dataset is limited in size and diversity and includes only a subset of deepfake types, these results should be interpreted as optimistic and not as evidence of generalizable, real-world performance. We discuss the risk of overfitting, the potential for train–test leakage when multiple frames from the same video are used, and the need for per-video evaluation and cross-dataset testing. The study highlights the potential of residual CNNs for deepfake detection, but also emphasizes the importance of more rigorous protocols and larger, more diverse datasets in future work.

title: Application of the Power Mutual Support Function of the Jian-Su UHVDC in Jiangsu Power Grid
authors: Wang Qiang, Li Pengbo, Bo Bin, Li Haifeng, Shen Mingkang, Xu Jin, Sha Haoyuan, Wang Zhiwei
publish: 2025 4th Asia Power and Electrical Technology Conference (APET)
abstract: The hybrid cascaded transmission system utilizes a configuration that combines LCC-HVDC and VSC-HVDC in series, integrating their respective advantages for long-distance, large-capacity power transmission. Without altering the DC transmission power or connection topology, the power mutual support function in the multi-terminal VSC-based hybrid cascaded system enables power transfer from one VSC unit to other VSC units, effectively resolving power supply-demand imbalances in local grids.

title: Kidney Disease Predictions Using Machine Learning
authors: Ashish Kumar, Ram Kinkar Pandey, Prabhat Kumar Srivastava
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Advanced Computing and Emerging Technologies (ACET)
abstract: Chronic kidney disease (CKD) is an overwhelming disease worldwide and is included in one of the most important global health problems resulting in millions of fatalities annually. Early prediction of CKD can be important from the standpoint of patient benefit, because it can enable modification or intervention to nullify the specific defects present in patients at early stage of the disease, where if not timeline, probability favors irreversible damage. Prediction of CKD development and progression to ESKD and related outcomes Over recent years, the application of machine learning (ML) approaches led to very good predictive modeling of CKD incidence, of progression to ESRD, and related outcomes. This article provides a general review into existing ML algorithm used for CKD prediction through clinical and technical perspective. We discuss how machine learning (ML) models—ranging from standard classifiers (For instance, support vector machines, decision trees, and logistic regression) to complex ensembles and deep learning—are exploited for predicting CKD risk factors, predicting disease progression, and supporting clinical decisions for patients with CKD. Specifically, machine learning models are favored to leverage complex, high-dimensional health data (i.e., electronic health records [EHRs], lab results) for capturing nonlinear associations that conventional risk scoring approaches may miss, and we summarize the existing literatures and findings on this topic; methods applied; and areas for future research.We address critical challenges of data quality, model interpretability, and integration with clinical workflow, and delineate directions to develop robust explainable and generalizable machine-learning (ML) based CKD prediction applications.

title: An Improved PolarMask Algorithm for Rapid and Accurate Cell Segmentation
authors: Na Liu, Peisen Liu, Lixiang Zheng, Yang Chen, Yue Wang, Tao Yue, Xiaomao Li
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Micro/Nano Sensors for AI, Healthcare, and Robotics (NSENS)
abstract: Shear Flow Deformability Cytometry (SDC), a subset of microfluidics-based deformability cytometry methods, has been widely applied for assessing cellular mechanical properties due to its high throughput. SDC characterizes cells' physical information, including size and stiffness, using automated image processing techniques. However, traditional and convolutional neural network (CNN)-based image processing methods face challenges in achieving rapid and accurate cell segmentation in SDC, primarily due to low image contrast and short exposure times. In response, this paper introduces a novel cell segmentation network based on a PolarMask algorithm. This network leverages contour representation and image enhancement to enhance cell saliency. By incorporating Intersection over Union (IoU) prediction and conducting joint training, the network significantly boosts segmentation accuracy while simplifying network structures to reduce computational resource demands. The proposed network achieves a precision level of 0.925, a remarkable improvement of 0.228 compared to the PolarMask's precision of 0.697. Notably, these accuracy gains are attained while maintaining a mean Frames Per Second (FPS) rate of 12.5.

title: Advancing LLM Agents for Code Generation: Observability, Orchestration, Reliable Performance
authors: Alex Kaplunovich
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Communication, Networking and Services (ICCNS)
abstract: Large Language Model (LLM) agents have gained popularity for constructing complex coding pipelines that incorporate multiple tools, iterative processes, and advanced libraries such as LlamaIndex and LangChain. Recognizing the critical need for observability, we emphasize techniques to monitor and control the process, costs, frequency of model calls, prompt usage, and overall logging. While contemporary agent libraries offer foundational classes for these purposes, many online examples and articles still rely on default implementations with ChatGPT models, allowing agents to invoke generative AI methods without robust flow control, prompt management, or detailed application logging. In our research, we identified effective ways to implement essential observability methods for popular frameworks, including LlamaIndex, LangChain, and CreAI. Our approach introduces workflows, custom model calls, comprehensive logging, and enhanced observability, which improve the reliability, controllability, predictability, and efficiency of LLM agents in software development applications. Through this methodology, we enhanced the architecture and structure of the evolving agentic framework for code generation, achieving a notable 98% accuracy in solving challenging coding tasks via specified workflows.

title: Enhancing Aquaponics Efficiency through IoT Automation and Renewable Energy in Sustainable Agriculture
authors: Zakwan Skaf, Filmon Nezif, EdenYoseph Beyene, Alazar Wake, Hassan Abdulmouti, Wasif Minhas
publish: 2025 Advances in Science and Engineering Technology International Conferences (ASET)
abstract: As the two most important challenges in today's world are food security and environmental health, there is a need for smarter, more sustainable solutions. Among the promising techniques is aquaponics, which is a closed-loop system where fish and plants help support each other: fish waste serves as fertilizer, and plants, in turn, clean the water. While this is a promising method, in practice, such setups are laborintensive and usually costly, always requiring monitoring and manual adjustment. Our research proposes an intelligent fully solar-powered aquaponics system for these challenges. This system is particularly developed at Sharjah campus, United Arab Emirates, through innovative use of renewable energy from solar panels, storing them in batteries to run smooth and sustainable operations. The system consists of an ESP32 microcontroller integrated with relay modules responsible for automating essential functions like watering and operation of lights within the system. It comes complemented with a number of sensors meant to monitor the key environmental factors typical for producing healthy habitats for both fish and plants, like temperature, oxygen, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{p H}$</tex> balance, and circulation. Real-time data monitoring coupled with responsive automated auxiliary mechanisms lessens the need for manual input, reduces consumption of resources, and increases overall productivity. Without compromising natural ecosystems in regard to future food needs, it refers to a much cleaner, more efficient model of producing food. It aims to transport aquaponics into a bright future-lofty agriculture where automated smart, sustainable agriculture is a feasible and popular option.

title: An Adhesive Self-Powered Sensing Patch for Gait Monitoring and Fall Warning
authors: Changjun Jia, Fengxin Sun, Yongsheng Zhu, Yaoxuan Shi, Kun Su
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Micro/Nano Sensors for AI, Healthcare, and Robotics (NSENS)
abstract: Gait information contains abundant physiological and pathological insights that reflect human health status, and therapeutic strategies based on gait analysis contribute significantly to health management. However, current wearable gait sensing devices are generally limited by poor portability, inadequate conformability, and long-term power consumption, severely restricting their clinical application clinical application and development. Here, we propose an adhesive self-powered sensing patch (ASSP) based on a triboelectric nanogenerator (TENG), achieving real-time gait monitoring and fall alarm through a distributed monitoring network. The ASSP primarily consists of Boric Acid, PDMS, Ecoflex, PVDF, and Copper Foil. The synergistic interaction between boric acid and PDMS endows ASSP with ultralong-term skin adhesion (>28 days) and stable interfacial contact, enabling adaptation to pressure changes of the foot during human movement. Through the dielectric enhancement effect from PVDF nanoparticles (1.5 wt% doping), the device achieves a sensitivity of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$6.8 \mathrm{V} / \mathrm{N}$</tex> and a peak power output of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$126 \mu \mathrm{W}$</tex>. Furthermore, we developed a self-powered sensing network comprising four ASSP nodes, which enables real-time acquisition of foot mechanical signals via a low-power Bluetooth module. Clinical validation demonstrates the system's capability to identify gait variations during badminton movements accurately and rapidly provide alerts in simulated fall experiments. The ASSP proposed in this work shows great application potential in the fields of elderly health monitoring, sports medicine evaluation, and intelligent rehabilitation robots.

title: Electro-Thermal-Lifetime Co-Design of DC-Link Capacitor in 3L-NPC Inverters
authors: Liutao Xu, Weimin Wu, Houqing Wang, Xiaodong Lv, Huiyang Zhang, Xinyu Zhang
publish: 2025 4th Asia Power and Electrical Technology Conference (APET)
abstract: Neutral clamp three-level inverters are widely used in high-power applications, among which the performance of DC bus capacitors is particularly critical. To optimize the DC-link capacitor design, a novel comprehensive methodology based on the electro-thermal-lifetime coupling model is proposed. The minimum capacitance value is determined according to the maximum ripple voltage at both ends of the DC bus capacitor, and the circuit model, thermal model and life model of the capacitor are constructed respectively. By analyzing the correlation between these models, a systematic and efficient design process is established. The feasibility of the proposed method is verified by design examples, and the accuracy of capacitor parameter design is further confirmed based on the simulation results of PSIM.

title: Analyzing Performance and Operational Trade-offs in Kong Gateway Deployments on AWS ECS and EKS Platforms
authors: Vinoth Punniyamoorthy, Srivenkateswara Reddy Sankiti, Nachiappan Chockalingam, Akash Agarwal, Aswathnarayan Muthukrishnan Kirubakaran, Bikesh Kumar, Kabilan Kannan, Suhas Malempati
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: Kong Gateway is a cloud-native API management solution widely adopted for securing, managing, and routing microservice traffic. The choice of container orchestration platform such as Amazon Elastic Container Service (ECS) or Amazon Elastic Kubernetes Service (EKS) has a substantial influence on the gateway’s performance, scalability, cost, and operational complexity. This paper presents a comprehensive comparative study of Kong Gateway deployments on ECS and EKS. Real-world workloads were benchmarked across multiple performance dimensions, including throughput, latency, resource utilization, and scaling efficiency, complemented by an analysis of cost and operational overhead. The results reveal clear trade-offs between operational simplicity and architectural flexibility: ECS provides faster provisioning, predictable performance, and lower management overhead, whereas EKS achieves higher peak throughput, more consistent latency under sustained load, and enhanced observability through advanced monitoring capabilities. These findings offer empirical guidance for DevOps engineers and cloud architects in choosing optimal deployment strategies for Kong Gateway based on workload characteristics, organizational maturity, and operational objectives.

title: Project Cost Calculation of Integrated Financial System Development Using Function Point Analysis and COCOMO Methods for Cooperatives in Indonesia
authors: Prasetya Cahya S, Mohamad Heykal, Agustini, Rochania Ayu Yunanda
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Innovations in Engineering, Science and Technology for Sustainable Development (ICEST)
abstract: The development of digitalization has not been felt by savings and loan cooperatives in Indonesia. The large number of savings and loan cooperatives in Indonesia that have not utilized information technology in their operations is one of the causes of the management of savings and loan cooperatives in Indonesia being ineffective and inefficient and lacking transparency, especially in the management of managed funds. This study aims to estimate the cost of a webbased integrated financial system application development project for medium-scale savings and loan cooperatives in Indonesia. The method used is Function Point Analysis (FPA) to estimate the functional size of the software based on user needs, and COCOMO (Constructive Cost Model) to calculate the effort, time, and development costs based on project complexity. The combination of these two methods is expected to produce accurate and realistic cost estimates, considering factors such as project size, team capabilities, and development environment. The results of the study show that this approach is effective in providing cost estimates that can be used as a reference for savings and loan cooperatives in planning an integrated financial system development budget. This study also contributes to the application of the right software project cost estimation method for the cooperative sector in Indonesia.

title: Machine Learning Based Dynamic Pricing Model Using Gradient Boosting Algorithm
authors: Ashmit Ram, Km Meenakshi, Amardeep Gupta
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Advanced Computing and Emerging Technologies (ACET)
abstract: Dynamic pricing has become an important tool for the companies which are planning to maximize their profit, improve their competitiveness, and make their stand in the fluctuating market realities in the real time. Machine learning based model provides us with a strong solution that is by allowing the data based pricing through the real time predictive data and the dynamic algorithms. This research is to create a dynamic price model which will be using machine learning algorithms to study with the past data that will recognize patterns and determine the best price for the product. This method includes the usage of supervised based learning algorithms for the demand prediction of the product. It also uses unsupervised clustering for the customer classification and reinforcement learning for the adapting prices in the real time based on the feedback from the user. With the use of big data, the model can be used to include wider ranging variables like the buying history, product quality, market situations, and the rush hour factors of the day. This model is to improve the price efficiency for the by fixing the revenue and getting the customer satisfaction and their equity. With different findings we came to know that the dynamic pricing by the machine learning gives a static model by enhancing the responsiveness to demand the changes and removing revenue losses. And the model also shows usage across the different industries like e-commerce, travel, and retail where the prices give a better impact to the customer choice. This research represents the importance of gathering the data preprocessing which features engineering and algorithm design in the development of a successful dynamic pricing system.

title: Corrections to “Multi-Channel Multi-Protocol Quantum Key Distribution System for Secure Image Transmission in Healthcare”
authors: Bandana Mallick, Priyadarsan Parida, Chittaranjan Nayak, Tarek Khalifa, Manoj Kumar Panda, Nawaf Ali, Gajanan Uttam Patil, Bibhu Prasad
abstract: Presents corrections to the paper, (Multi-Channel Multi-Protocol Quantum Key Distribution System for Secure Image Transmission in Healthcare).

title: Spatiotemporal Attention Learning Framework for Event-Driven Object Recognition
authors: Tiantian Xie, Pengpai Wang, Rosa H. M. Chan
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Micro/Nano Sensors for AI, Healthcare, and Robotics (NSENS)
abstract: Event-based vision sensors, inspired by biological neural systems, asynchronously capture local pixel-level intensity changes as a sparse event stream containing position, polarity, and timestamp information. These neuromorphic sensors offer significant advantages in dynamic range, latency, and power efficiency. Their working principle inherently addresses traditional camera limitations such as motion blur and redundant background information, making them particularly suitable for dynamic vision tasks. While recent works have proposed increasingly complex event-based architectures, the computational overhead and parameter complexity of these approaches limit their practical deployment. This paper presents a novel spatiotemporal learning framework for event-based object recognition, utilizing a VGG network enhanced with Convolutional Block Attention Module (CBAM). Our approach achieves comparable performance to state-of-the-art ResNet-based methods while reducing parameter count by 2.3 % compared to the original VGG model. Specifically, it outperforms ResNet-based methods like MVF-Net, achieving the highest Top-1 accuracy of 76.4 % (pretrained) and 71.3 % (not pretrained) on CIFAR10-DVS, and 72.4% (not pretrained) on N-Caltech101. These results highlight the robustness of our method when pretrained weights are not used, making it suitable for scenarios where transfer learning is unavailable. Moreover, our approach reduces reliance on data augmentation. Experimental results on standard event-based datasets demonstrate the framework's efficiency and effectiveness for real-world applications.

title: Metaheuristic DG Placement for Resilient Distribution Networks: A Comparative Study
authors: Kehkashan Fatima, Hussain Shareef, Mohd. Shaaban Hussain, Mohd Suhail Husain, Asna Madathodika, Amir Hussain Idrisi
publish: 2025 Advances in Science and Engineering Technology International Conferences (ASET)
abstract: This paper compares four population-based metaheuristic algorithms: Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO), Genetic Algorithm (GA), Gravitational Search Algorithm (GSA), and Grey Wolf Optimizer (GWO), to determine the best location and dimensions for Distributed Generators (DGs) in the IEEE 33 bus radial distribution network. Under normal operating conditions, a composite target that combined a voltage deviation penalty and real-power loss minimization was employed. Convergence behavior, loss reduction, and voltageprofile enhancements were evaluated for cases with one, two, and three DGs. In 20 iterations, PSO and GA achieved the flattest voltage profiles (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\geq 0.99$</tex> p.u. across all busses) and the largest loss reductions (down to 0.0377 MW with three DGs), consistently outperforming GSA and GWO. Due to inadequate DG location, GSA lagged while GWO performed moderately. The findings provide clear guidelines for choosing metaheuristics in real-world distribution planning applications by validating the resilience of GA and the quick convergence of PSO for DG optimization in radial networks.

title: Computational Methodologies for the Design and Investigation of Graphene Oxide-Based Sensors
authors: Damiano Ricciarelli, Simona Filice, Viviana Scuderi, Giuseppe Fisicaro, Gaetano Calogero, Ioannis Deretzis, Ignazio Vacante, Simona Crispi, Rosario Gaetano Viglione, Silvia Scalese, Antonino La Magna
publish: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Metrology for Green Technologies, Renewable Energy and Ecological Sustainability (MetroGREENST)
abstract: We present an ab initio modeling approach for graphene oxide (GO), augmented by machine learning based structural simulations, to extract the optical and electrical properties of the material at different scale levels. The approach addresses a longstanding challenge in GO modeling, crucial for the domain of sensor: capturing the impact on electrical properties provided by variable amount and typology of oxygen functional groups, complex amorphous structure and molecular adsorption of gases and molecules. We show the validation of our model against experimental absorption spectra and photoluminescence data obtained during laser irradiation processes, and we discuss its extension to simulate electrical properties with larger cells of tens of nanometers size, obtained with machine learning interatomic potentials, following preliminary tests we conducted. Breaking with conventional approaches, our framework has the potential to enable electro-optical property predictions on large scales under varying physicochemical scenarios. Those include variable oxidation level, pore formation, and binding with supramolecular species, all of which are common in sensor fabrication processes or during the sensing itself. While this is, indeed, of paramount importance for the mentioned field, further domains of application could be environment, optoelectronics, energy storage, and microelectronics.

title: A Fully Integrated High-PSRR LDO with Cascaded NMOS-PMOS Buffer Architecture in 180 nm CMOS
authors: Kaire Anupama, Patri Sreehari Rao
publish: 2026 39th International Conference on VLSI Design &amp; 25th International Conference on Embedded Systems (VLSID)
abstract: The proposed work introduces a fully integrated low-dropout (LDO) voltage regulator featuring a cascade-assisted multi-buffer architecture that significantly enhances power supply rejection ratio (PSRR) and transient response, which is realized using a 180 nm CMOS process. Unlike conventional LDOs that rely heavily on large off-chip capacitors and simple buffer stages, this design employs a hybrid NMOS-PMOS cascaded buffer system with current mirror enhancements and feedforward ripple cancellation. The regulator is designed to operate from a 1.8 V supply and delivers a regulated output of 1.4 V with a dropout voltage of 0.4 V. The proposed architecture achieves a PSRR of 86.4 dB at 1 kHz and 66.6 dB at 10 kHz making it suitable for low-frequency applications of IoT under full-load condition of 20 mA. Furthermore, the regulator exhibits strong transient performance, with an undershoot of 32.04 mV, an overshoot of 24.82 mV, and a settling time of about 700 ns. The architecture also integrates internal compensation networks and a finely-tuned resistive feedback loop to ensure closed-loop stability across PVT corners.

title: Quantum-Inspired and FNN-Enhanced Needleman–Wunsch Algorithm for Large-Scale Genomic Sequence Alignment
authors: Rowel Facunla, Bobby D. Gerardo
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: This study introduces a GPU-accelerated, quantum-inspired, and FNN-enhanced Needleman–Wunsch (QI-FNN-NW) framework that achieves exact global alignment of multi-million-base genomic sequences while substantially improving runtime, memory efficiency, and robustness to genomic complexity. The method unifies four innovations: (1) a 4-bit binary IUPAC-preserving nucleotide encoding for compact memory representation; (2) chunk-based alignment with overlap-aware stitching for chromosome-scale processing; (3) a quantum-inspired modulation mechanism that broadens dynamic-programming path exploration without altering NW-exact scoring; and (4) a lightweight feedforward neural network that refines local scoring cues and stabilizes pruning.Across 5,000 NCBI scaffolds including GRCh38 and PanTro 3.0, the proposed system reduces memory consumption by ~50%, produces 10–15× faster runtimes than classical NW for 20 kbp sequences, and retains score-identical alignment outputs (Lin ’ s CCC ⩾ 0.987). Whole-genome evaluation further shows higher alignable fraction (91.66%) and percent identity (98.82%) compared to WFA-GPU exact and heuristic modes, while using less VRAM than WFA-GPU exact mode. These results demonstrate that QI-FNN-NW overcomes well-known WFA limitations under high divergence, long repeats, and complex IUPAC ambiguity, offering a scalable and fully exact solution for chromosome-scale, clinical, and pangenome-scale genomic alignment.

title: A Hybrid Heuristic-MILP Warm-Start Framework for Electric Vehicle Charging Station Planning
authors: Yunfan Meng, Yonghui Sun, Chenxu Yin, Liang Zhao, Haoran Dai, Peibiao Chen
publish: 2025 4th Asia Power and Electrical Technology Conference (APET)
abstract: Electric vehicle (EV) charging station (CS) planning has become increasingly complex due to spatially heterogeneous demand, multiple charge pile (CP) types, and stringent service requirements. Conventional mixed-integer linear programming (MILP) formulations are capable of capturing these features. Still, a direct solution from scratch leads to prohibitive computational burdens, especially for large-scale instances and repeated planning runs. In this paper, a hybrid heuristic-MILP warm-start framework is developed for EVCS planning, in which a fast heuristic procedure first generates a feasible yet coarse layout that is subsequently refined by a MILP solver using warm-start techniques. An optional iterative adjustment mechanism is further employed to improve user-oriented performance indicators under given budget constraints. Case studies demonstrate that the proposed framework achieves substantial reductions in computation time while maintaining near-optimal planning costs and stable service levels, compared to static MILP formulations and standalone metaheuristic approaches.

title: Adversarial-Resilient Ids for Iot Devices Via Federated Learning in Resource- Limited Scenarios
authors: Sristi Vashisth, Mayank Kumar Goyal, Anjali Goyal
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Advanced Computing and Emerging Technologies (ACET)
abstract: The prompt expansion of Internet of Things (IoT) de- vices within focused security and resource- constraint settings has amplified the necessity for effective and privacy maintaining Intrusion Detection Systems (IDSs). Conventional centralized IDS approaches are inadequate in fulfilling the modern IoT network requirements for real-time operation, lightweight design, and privacy awareness. In this study, we introduce a Federated Learning (FL)-driven IDS framework tailored for resource-constrained IoT devices operating under adversarial threats, including Denial of Service (DoS), Man-in-the-Middle (MitM), spoofing, and malware injection or data poisoning attacks. The proposed architecture utilizes decentralized model training across distributed IoT nodes while maintaining the confidentiality of locally stored data. Our approach inte- grates lightweight deep learning-based classifiers with resilient aggregation techniques to achieve both high accuracy and operational efficiency. Experimental assessments conducted on standard benchmark datasets indicate superior detection accuracy, minimized communication costs, and robust adaptability against emerging attack patterns, thereby validating the practicality of the proposed FL-IDS in real-world IoT applications.

title: LINK_TIP: An AI-Ready Threat Intelligence Prototype with Machine-Learning-Based Anomaly Detection
authors: Manaf M. Mohamed, Hasan Razzaqi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: Cybersecurity threats continue to grow in scale and sophistication, outpacing purely signature-based defenses. Threat Intelligence Platforms (TIPs) help analysts work with Indicators of Compromise (IOCs), yet many existing solutions either lack machine-learning support or remain too complex and costly for smaller teams. This paper presents the Link Threat Intel Platform (LINK_TIP), an early-stage prototype that accepts analyst-uploaded network logs and applies anomaly detection to highlight suspicious activity. The current implementation uses an Isolation Forest model, exposed through a lightweight architecture with a FastAPI backend, a React dashboard for search/visualization and report export, and Dockerized deployment. Although still under development, LINK_TIP lays groundwork for an AI-assisted TIP. Rather than committing to a single technique, future work will continue to experiment with multiple anomaly-detection algorithms under the same pipeline and select the approach based on empirical results, while the platform matures in correlation and reporting. Overall, this work points toward practical, accessible threat intelligence that can evolve beyond detection toward prediction.

title: Improved IoT Anomaly Detection Through Hybrid Machine Learning and Deep Learning Approaches Using the IoT-23 Dataset
authors: Mariam A. Mohamed, E. Emary, Mahmoud A Attalla
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: The Internet of Things (IoT) has transformed both daily activities and business processes by delivering greater precision, efficiency, and speed. Nonetheless, IoT systems are increasingly vulnerable to cybersecurity threats, such as malicious software and denial-of-service (DoS) attacks, which can undermine the reliability and security of data. Despite the rapid adoption of IoT technology, many implementations lack adequate security measures, exposing them to various risks. This underscores the pressing need for stronger and more effective protective strategies for IoT infrastructure. This paper explores the application of recent machine learning (ML) and deep learning (DL) methods for anomaly detection in IoT systems using the IoT-23 dataset, a comprehensive dataset containing both legitimate and malicious network traffic from various IoT devices. The research assesses the performance of multiple ML and DL models, including decision trees, the Extreme Gradient Boosting (XGBoost) model, the Naïve Bayes model, fully connected neural networks (FCNN), and logistic regression. The results reveal that decision trees achieved the highest accuracy at 98.9%, while also being the fastest to process, making them the most effective model for detecting anomalies in IoT systems. On the other hand, the Naïve Bayes model exhibited the lowest performance, with an accuracy of only 50%. These findings highlight the critical role of selecting the right algorithm to bolster IoT security, ensuring reliable anomaly detection and enhanced resilience against cyber risks.

title: Design of an IoT-Based Monitoring and Control System for Wireless EV Charging with Dynamic Coupling
authors: Paranay Gupta, Pooja Anand, Akanksha Singh, Lipsa Das, Durgesh Kumar Jha, Amit Kumar Saroj
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Advanced Computing and Emerging Technologies (ACET)
abstract: Electric vehicles (EVs) continue to hit the roads in greater numbers, the need for smarter and more efficient charging solutions is becoming more urgent especially to overcome the hassles and limitations of traditional plug-in charging. Wireless charging, especially when combined with dynamic coupling technology, offers a convenient alternative by enabling energy transfer without the need for physical cables, while still working even when the vehicle isn't perfectly aligned or is slightly moving. This project aims to create a smart, IoT-powered wireless charging system that not only delivers power efficiently but also keeps track of charging conditions and adjusts them on the spot in real time. Using a network of built-in sensors, microcontroller and wireless communication modules, the system monitors significant data such as frequent coil alignment, energy transfer rate, battery charge level, temperature and overall efficiency. The system sends real-time data to the cloud, where smart algorithms immediately analyze it and make quick decisions to keep the charging process smooth, efficient and trouble free. Depending on the data obtained, the system can automatically adjust things such as transmission power or magnetic field strength, ensuring that the charging process remains efficient, stable and responsible for the changing conditions. One of the standout characteristics of this system is the ability to adapt to the changing conditions during charging-such as changes in the coil position or variations in magnetic coupling-which are common in dynamic or semi-masters. The system closely monitors these variations and automatically adjusts its operational frequency and resonant parameters to ensure efficient energy transfer and reduce power loss. Inside the electric vehicle, a microcontroller-based control unit works in the sink with primary coil system to make real-time adjustment during charging.

title: A Hybrid Machine Learning and Model-Based Framework for Reliable Fault Diagnosis in Lithium-Ion Batteries
authors: Taha Mohamed Abdelatif Maaradji, Said Alem, Sofiane Berrazouane, Youcef Touati
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Sustainable Energy and Net-Zero Emissions (SENZE)
abstract: This paper delves into the pressing necessity for robust battery management and fault diagnosis within the rapidly expanding domain of lithium-ion battery systems (LIBS), widely applied in electric vehicles and renewable energy storage. The main goal of this work involves bolstering the reliability of fault diagnostic systems by employing a hybrid approach that integrates machine learning and model-based techniques. Through the utilization of structural analysis and the Extended Kalman Filter (EKF), distinct residuals are generated to detect a wide range of anticipated faults within LIBS. The incorporation of a statistical feature extraction process facilitates effective fault detection and isolation, with machine learning classification methods achieving an impressive 98.18% accuracy in discerning fault types, including sensor faults, connection faults, and short circuits. Notably, the study steers clear of conventional threshold-based approaches, diminishing reliance on fixed thresholds and fortifying the system's robustness in detecting and isolating faults. The outcomes underscore the exceptional performance of the Random Forest (RF) method, attributed primarily to its capacity to capture unique patterns and variations from residual data. This comprehensive approach presents promising avenues for early fault detection and precise fault classification, thereby elevating the reliability and safety of LIBS while offering invaluable insights into system behavior.
