title: Multi-hop Knowledge Chain Query on Fine-Grained Fuzzy Spatiotemporal Knowledge Graph by Embedding
authors: Hao Ji, Li Yan, Zongmin Ma
abstract: Performing multi-hop queries on a large-scale Knowledge Graph (KG) presents significant computational challenges. Although subgraph matching-based methods show ability, they return empty/few answers when querying missing KG. An emerging solution involves embedding both entities and multi-hop queries of KG into a vector space, where the proximity between queries and entities reflects their semantic relevance. However, current embedding-based methods fail to adequately address three critical aspects: temporal semantics, spatial semantics, and fine-grained fuzzy semantics in multi relational queries. Thus, we propose a multi-hop query method on fuzzy spatiotemporal KG (STKG) by fine-grained embedding. We first develop an element-level embedding mechanism that maps fuzzy spatiotemporal semantics into each component. We then construct ordered multi-hop relations as composable operations on paths by Hermitian dot products. We finally use a Path-Constraint Resource Allocation (PCRA) to model path reliability. In addition, we validate our method on fuzzy spatiotemporal datasets. Results show that the method outperforms several state-of-the-art models and can effectively handle fuzzy spatiotemporal knowledge chain queries.

title: A Probabilistic Semantics for Defeasible Argumentation in Systems Validation_supp1-3668147.xlsx
authors: Daniel Shapiro

title: Defending PoW Blockchains Against Game-Theoretic DoS Attacks: A Rational Strategy Analysis_supp1-3671880.pdf
authors: Zhiquan Liu
abstract: Game-theoretic denial-of-service (GDoS) attacks exploit rational miners' incentives to degrade the throughput and security of proof-of-work (PoW) blockchains, even when the attacker controls less than 20% of the total hash power. Existing defenses commonly rely on protocol modifications, which risk hard forks and destabilize the system. This paper presents the first rational, protocol-preserving defense against GDoS attacks. We formalize GDoS by unifying selfish-mining-based and blockchain-based denial-of-service variants under a common definition, and establish its theoretical foundation through a dynamic game model with a subgame perfect Nash equilibrium (SPNE). Unlike prior protocol-level defenses, our strategy maintains consensus integrity without introducing any changes to PoW. We propose a cooperative hash-power hopping mechanism in which miners temporarily reallocate hash power to larger pools when under attack to preserve expected payoffs and suppress attacker incentives. To quantify miner utilities under different strategies, we develop a combined game-theoretic and Markov-chain analytical framework and derive closed-form critical profitability thresholds. Simulations calibrated to real-world Bitcoin hash-power distributions show that the proposed strategy reduces attacker revenue gains by more than 20% and prevents throughput degradation across the entire attack range. These results demonstrate that rational, incentive-compatible cooperation can effectively strengthen PoW blockchains against emerging strategic threats.

title: Robust and Reliable Dynamic E2E SFC Placement in Multi-tenant Multi-domain Network Slicing with Guaranteed SLA
authors: Mohammadreza Abedi, Zahra Rashidi, Mehdi Fasanghari, Mohammad Akbari, Nader Mokari, Nizar Zorba
publish: IEEE Transactions on Green Communications and Networking
abstract: Service placement in next-generation networks faces challenges in meeting ultra-low latency demands and managing complex multi-tenant infrastructures. Ensuring that service placement meets stringent quality of service (QoS) standards while addressing these complexities is critical. A key gap exists in delivering consistent network slicing (NS) capabilities within multi-tenant networks, particularly in shared physical infrastructures. This paper proposes a resource provisioning framework for network slices in an network function virtualization (NFV)-based framework. By integrating software-defined networking (SDN) with management and network orchestration (MANO), our approach ensures efficient slice management and QoS compliance, even under unpredictable tenant numbers and resource utilization. The proposed framework maximizes energy efficiency (EE) in multi-tenant, multi-domain NS environments under demand uncertainty, considering end-to-end latency and minimum bit rate as service-level agreement (SLA) constraints. We formulate the problem as a non-convex mixed-integer non-linear programming (MINLP) and solve it using a novel multi-agent deep deterministic policy gradient (MADDPG) algorithm. Compared to a baseline that neglects demand uncertainties, our framework achieves near-optimal solutions with competitive execution time, improving performance by up to 66% over existing benchmarks.

title: HybridFormer: Data-Efficient Deep Learning for High-Dimensional Spatiotemporal Classification with Application to Neural Signal Processing
authors: Ghada Abdelhady, Abdulrahman Ghandoura, Abdullah Alajmi, Ziad Ghazaly
abstract: We present HybridFormer, a hybrid deep learning architecture for data-efficient multichannel spatiotemporal classification, validated on high-gamma EEG motor imagery. The key novelty is an ordered integration pipeline: spatial CNN features are compressed via 4:1 squeeze-and-excitation channel attention before the BiLSTM, and learned Q/K/V temporal self-attention operates after recurrent encoding. This differs from prior hybrids that apply attention only post-recurrence or as simple pooling. On the 128-channel High-Gamma Dataset, HybridFormer achieves 91.2 ± 2.8% within-subject and 78.5 ± 3.4% cross-subject accuracy using stratified 10-fold and leave-one-subject-out protocols, outperforming CNN-LSTM baselines by 6.7% and 5.4% (p < 0.001). Against transformer baselines—EEG-Transformer (BENDR), Transformer-LSTM, and EEG-Conformer—HybridFormer achieves 8.5%, 7.3%, and 6.1% higher accuracy with 2.3× fewer parameters (1.8M). A strict non-overlapping temporal split experiment without augmentation confirms a 5.1% advantage over the best baseline, ruling out information leakage.Cross-dataset validation across 22–128 channels shows consistent generalization. Ablation studies confirm significant contributions from each component (CNN: 8.7%, LSTM: 6.2%, attention: 4.3%). Attention maps correlate with motor cortex activation (r = 0.76, p < 0.001) and remain stable across random seeds (cosine similarity = 0.91 ± 0.03). Real-time inference (15 ms/sample) supports resource-constrained deployment.

title: Revisiting Keyframe Selection in Learning-Based Dense Monocular SLAM
authors: Jaemin Choi, Juhwan Bae, Yun Gu Lee
abstract: Recent learning-based dense monocular SLAM systems, such as VGGT-SLAM and MASt3R-SLAM, leverage powerful feed-forward 3D reconstruction models and achieve state-of-the-art performance even in uncalibrated monocular settings. However, in these systems, keyframe selection is often handled by simple frame-level heuristics based on low-level 2D signals, and the impact of input frame composition on feed-forward reconstruction and overall SLAM performance has not been systematically analyzed. In this paper, we reformulate keyframe selection in learning-based dense monocular SLAM as a problem of configuring the input frame set for feed-forward 3D reconstruction, rather than selecting individual frames. From this perspective, we propose a simple segment-level keyframe sampling strategy that aims to stabilize the composition of input frames. We analyze how this input configuration affects reconstruction stability and camera trajectory estimation. Experiments on public benchmarks demonstrate that the proposed strategy consistently improves camera trajectory RMSE compared to the original VGGT-SLAM and MASt3R-SLAM pipelines. These results highlight the importance of input frame configuration in learning-based SLAM and suggest a new perspective on keyframe selection beyond conventional frame-level decisions.

title: Diving into Epipolar Transformers for Light Field Super-Resolution and Disparity Estimation
authors: Zhengyu Liang, Yingqian Wang, Longguang Wang, Jungang Yang, Yulan Guo, Li Liu, Shilin Zhou, Wei y
publish: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
abstract: Light field (LF) cameras capture the light rays of a 3D scene from multiple views simultaneously, and thus provide a more immersive experience of the real world as compared to traditional cameras. Although significant progress has been made in various LF image processing tasks, it remains challenging to effectively model the non-local spatial-angular correlations inherent in LF images, particularly when dealing with complex disparity variations. In this paper, we focus on orthogonal epipolar geometry of LF images and propose a generic Epipolar Transformer mechanism that incorporates geometrically meaningful correlations along the epipolar lines. Our Epipolar Transformer mechanism enjoys the following benefits: learning effective and diverse LF feature representations, delivering satisfactory results without redundant architectural designs, and enabling flexible extension to various LF-related tasks with simple adaptations. For LF spatial and angular super-resolution, our methods not only achieve state-of-the-art performance on benchmark datasets, but also demonstrate superior and robust performance on large disparity variations. For disparity estimation, we explore the use of geometry information encoded in our Epipolar Transformer to directly regress the disparity results, effectively avoiding the limitation of a fixed maximum disparity. The code and models are available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/ZhengyuLeung/BasicLFSR-plus</uri>.

title: HY-Geo: A Hybrid Mixture-of-Experts and Hypergraph Framework for Intelligent Geological Body Detection in Borehole-Scale Electrical Imaging Logs_supp1-3674235.docx
authors: Chong Zhang
abstract: Electrical imaging logs, as a form of borehole-scale active remote sensing, can provide high-resolution visualization of subsurface structures such as fractures and stratifications. These geological bodies exhibit pronounced cross scale and non-local spatial correlations, and their accurate detection relies on effective modeling of high-order topological relationships. However, existing automatic detection methods based on local convolutional features struggle to characterize complex stratigraphic patterns, resulting in limited accuracy and generalization capability in visually complicated borehole environments. To address these challenges, this study proposes a hybrid deep learning framework for geological body detection in electrical imaging logs, termed HY-Geo. The framework is built upon an enhanced YOLOv9 architecture and integrates a Hybrid Kolmogorov-Arnold Mixture-of-Experts Semantic Processing Module (HKM-SPM) together with a Hypergraph convolution mechanism. This design enables the unified modeling of nonlinear semantic interactions and cross scale, high-order topological dependencies among geological structures. A benchmark dataset containing 10000 Borehole Formation Microresistivity Imaging (FMI) images was constructed for evaluation, covering four representative geological types, conductive fractures, resistive fractures, drilling-induced fractures, and bedding structures. Experimental results demonstrate that HY-Geo achieves a mAP@0.5:0.95 of 96.3%, a precision of 0.962, and a recall of 0.937, consistently outperforming competing methods in both detection accuracy and robustness across multiple geological categories. In terms of efficiency, HY-Geo reaches an inference speed of 43 FPS, while its compact variant achieves up to 89 FPS, satisfying real time interpretation requirements in field applications. These results indicate that HY-Geo provides an accurate and efficient solution for automatic geological body detection in complex borehole environments, offering reliable technical support for downhole structural interpretation and digital logging analysis.

title: IoMT-based proactive approach for detecting stress in physicians to reduce medical errors
authors: Moudy Sharaf Alsahreef, Manal Alfahmi, Eaman Alharbi, Janelle M. Jones, Mona Jaber, Akram Alomainy
abstract: Medical errors, often caused by prolonged stress, remain a critical threat to patient safety. This study introduces a novel, lightweight method for early stress detection in physicians using heart rate variability data from the PARFAIT dataset, which was collected in situ from healthcare professionals using non-invasive wearable sensors. Unlike prior models, our approach incorporates a domain-informed cost-sensitive loss function that prioritizes the avoidance of high-risk misclassifications based on clinical impact. To overcome data scarcity in real-world medical settings, we employ transfer learning to enhance generalization across datasets. The model achieves 98.1% accuracy while reducing the projected cost of stress-related misclassification by more than 50%. Its unobtrusive, real-time design makes it particularly suitable for deployment in Internet of Medical Things systems in hospitals. This work advances the field by aligning machine learning objectives with clinical priorities, offering a practical and scalable solution for reducing stress-induced medical errors.

title: Guest Editorial Special Issue on the IEEE Wireless and Microwave Technology Conference (WAMICON) 2025
authors: Enrique J. González-Carvajal
publish: IEEE Transactions on Microwave Theory and Techniques

title: Prescribed-Time Optimization for Multiagent System With Inconsistent Constraint Sets
authors: Xuening Xu, Zhiyong Yu, Haijun Jiang, Chunxia Zhu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Systems, Man, and Cybernetics: Systems
abstract: In this article, the prescribed-time optimization problem is considered for multiagent systems (MASs) with inconsistent constraint sets. First, the constrained optimization problem is transformed into an unconstrained one using the sliding mode control approach, in which the sliding surface is constructed based on the projection errors. Subsequently, three distinct prescribed-time optimization control protocols are developed by utilizing the sliding mode variables and optimization algorithms. In particular, compared with the existing works in which the gain of each controller approaches infinity at the prescribed instant, this article effectively mitigates singularity by integrating fixed-time theory with the time-domain transformation method. Furthermore, it can be verified that all agents achieve optimization consensus under the three different algorithms by the Lyapunov stability theory and convex optimization analysis. Finally, we provide an application example to verify the validity of theoretical results.

title: Design Trade-offs in Asynchronous BD Pipeline Styles of RISC-V: An FPGA-Based Comparative Study
authors: Valeeprakhon Tamnuwat, Zhen Zhang, Makoto Iwata
abstract: Asynchronous processors adopt diverse pipeline styles, including linear, frequency-adaptive, selective, reduced, and combined styles, to balance performance, energy, and area. However, prior studies remain fragmented because benchmarks, tool support, and design flows differ. Those make fair comparison difficult and prevent a clear understanding of the trade-offs required for choosing pipeline styles for specific applications. Moreover, existing delay-placement flows rely on iterative synthesis or manual engineering change orders (ECOs), which are time-consuming, error-prone, and inefficient. To overcome these limitations, we introduce a morphological delay-placement constraint (MDPC), an automated delay-placement flow for bundled-data (BD) circuits on FPGAs. Compared with conventional flows, MDPC achieves single-pass timing closure with reduced slack deviation by up to 31.66% and LUT utilization by up to 5.56X, thereby enhancing performance by up to 11.94% and energy efficiency by up to 10.42%. Using MDPC, we systematically evaluate five asynchronous RISC-V pipeline styles against a synchronous baseline with typical benchmarks. The frequency-adaptive pipeline achieves the highest speed gains of 1.11%. The selective pipeline delivers the strongest energy savings of up to 65.85%. The reduced pipeline optimizes resource utilization, lowering area by up to 3.12%. The combined pipeline provides moderate improvements with energy reductions of up to 64.40%. In contrast, the linear pipeline exhibits the lowest overall efficiency, with execution slowdowns of up to 37.76%. The results reveal distinct trade-offs: the frequency-adaptive pipeline maximizes speed, the selective pipeline minimizes energy, the reduced pipeline achieves superior resource efficiency, the combined pipeline offers moderate overall gains, while the linear pipeline remains the least competitive.

title: Optimized Constant-Time Implementation of terSIDH
authors: Taehun Kang, Donghoe Heo, Jeonghwan Lee, Suhri Kim, Changmin Lee
abstract: Since supersingular isogeny Diffie-Hellman (SIDH) was broken by a polynomial-time attack, several countermeasures were proposed as it was regarded as the most efficient algorithm among isogeny-based cryptosystems. Among them, terSIDH has been highlighted for its high performance, yet it exposes a side-channel vulnerability. The total degree of isogeny depends on the private key; terSIDH exhibits timing variations during computation, making it vulnerable to timing attacks. In this work, we present the first constant-time design for artificially-oriented isogeny protocols, instantiated with terSIDH. By inserting dummy operations, we remove private-key dependencies in isogeny computations and ensure constant execution time. Specifically for terSIDH, our method equalizes per-prime work using dummy kernels and removes secret-dependent branches at the code level. Since constant-time countermeasures typically incur performance overhead, we introduce an additional optimization technique to mitigate this cost. Furthermore, we provide the first 128-bit secure C implementation of terSIDH. Although our focus is on terSIDH, the proposed technique generalizes to related schemes such as binSIDH and the terSIDH-like component of terSIDH-hyb. We compared our method against CSIDH-4096, a representative isogeny-based key exchange protocol with 128-bit security. Our constant-time key generation is 3.3× faster than constant-time CSIDH-4096, and ≈11% faster than terSIDH’s previous worst case.

title: Design and Development of an Automated School Bell System for Improved Scheduling and Efficiency
authors: Vanessa Gay R. Liabor, Jerry I. Teleron, Niel Carlo D. Asgar
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: This study looks into the development and evaluation of an automated school bell system built around the Orange Pi microcontroller. The project was designed as a practical solution to the day-to-day challenges of manually ringing bells in schools. Test results show that the device is ready for real use, reliably handling scheduled bell times without the need for human intervention. It can also play different tones for specific situations, such as regular class periods, meetings, or emergency alerts. The bell system follows a fully customizable schedule stored in its own database. It also includes an emergency button that allows immediate activation when necessary. To make the system easier to manage, the researchers created a web-based application that works on both Android and iOS devices. This app connects smoothly to the Orange Pi, allowing administrators to monitor the system, change schedules, and trigger alerts remotely using the IP address displayed on the device’s LCD screen. Because the Orange Pi is both dependable and affordable, it offers schools an accessible way to modernize their operations. During a two-week trial in a college setting, the automated bell consistently followed the programmed schedule without failure. The emergency feature also responded instantly when tested. These results highlight the system’s durability and potential to support larger school environments. Overall, the technology shows promise in helping schools manage time and daily routines more efficiently.

title: Scalable and Verifiable Attestation Framework on HPC and Data Center Architecture
authors: Farah Fargo, Sreenadh Kareti, Julien Carreño, Marko Bartscherer, Olivier Franza
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Communication, Networking and Services (ICCNS)
abstract: In recent years, High Performance Computing (HPC) and data center systems have populated the use of millions smart devices that enable novel services and applications. As these technologies become more complex, the need for strong cybersecurity measures has never been more critical. The potential for cyberattacks on HPC and data center systems has called for new solutions to preserve the confidentiality, integrity and availability of the data. Remote attestation is a datacenter security requirement as it provides assurance and allows the client to verify the level of trust and details of the system state before launching their workloads. Integrity represents the characteristics of the data on the platform are in preserved state without being exposed to intentional or unintentional modifications. Firmware integrity is crucial to the security of HPC and data center systems. Most of the existing techniques for firmware attestation require a copy of HPC compute devices firmware. However, it can be altered during transient therefore enabling Security Protocol Data model (SPDM) to protect data during transient is necessary. In this paper, we propose a scalable attestation framework that verify the trustworthiness of platform components in HPC and data center systems using Merkle tree data structure and enabling SPDM across the platform to protect data during compute against cyber-attacks. Using this method, we are providing flexibility to the relying party to verify attestation measurements in high scale system against cyber-attacks and deliver platform and module level attestation services.

title: A Review of the Cyber Security Risks in AI Powered Smart Agriculture 4.0
authors: Bhasker Sharma, Rahul Kumar Mishra
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Advanced Computing and Emerging Technologies (ACET)
abstract: Smart agriculture incorporates cutting-edge technology like edge computing, big data, artificial intelligence (AI), and the Internet of Things (IoT) to update conventional farming methods. While these innovations enhance productivity, automation, and decision-making, they also introduce significant cybersecurity and privacy challenges. This paper presents a overarching survey of current technologies and their security issues in smart agriculture. Key technologies including wireless sensor networks, data acquisition systems, cloud and edge platforms, and AI-based analytics are examined. A comparative study of intrusion detection systems (IDS) applied to smart agriculture is provided, highlighting performance, limitations, and dataset usage. Despite high accuracy on benchmark datasets, gaps such as lack of real-world validation, limited adaptability, and energy inefficiencies persist. The paper further explores emerging threats and future technologies such as 5G, fog computing, and software-defined networking and their implications for agricultural security. Open research challenges are identified, emphasizing the need for lightweight, explainable, and scalable security solutions.

title: Domain-Specific Fine-Tuning of LLMs for Multilingual Student Advisory in Higher Education
authors: Mohammad Abrar, Ahmed Sameer Mohammed Mohammed, Samaa Abdullah Nasser Al hasani, Yousuf Nasser Al Husaini, Rawad Abdulkhaliq, Wasin Alkishri
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Advanced Computing and Emerging Technologies (ACET)
abstract: Colleges are experiencing an increased need to have smart, multi-lingual support systems that can respond to various questions posed by the students. Although large language models (LLMs) have strong general capabilities, they are often inaccurate, culturally insensitive, and unsafe in low-resource and domain-specific settings. The paper introduces a two-stage fine-tuning model for developing a multilingual student services assistant specializing in English, Modern Standard Arabic (MSA), Franco Arabic, and Omani Arabic. Its purpose is to improve the factual accuracy, dialogue coherence, and refusal accuracy in the sensitive institutional environment. In Phase I, factual memorization was performed by fine-tuning the TyDi QA and MASSIVE datasets with LoRA adapters to enhance multilingual understanding. Phase II used behavioral alignment, including institutional data and the presence of refusal-inducing prompts, to improve the safe management of out-of-scope queries. The evaluation metrics were factual accuracy, BLEU, COMET, refusal precision, and multi-turn coherence, with performance evaluated against GPT-4, Aya-Base, and Vicuna. The suggested model was more successful in refusal accuracy (0.86 vs. GPT-4 at 0.82) and competitive accuracy (0.78 vs. GPT-4 at 0.81). It is noteworthy that the dialectal performance was enhanced, especially in the Omani Arabic. Domain-specific finetuning can be used to create safer and more inclusive multilingual LLM assistants in higher education and find the balance between accuracy and reliability between underrepresented languages.

title: Autonomous Transmission Line Inspection Using Transfer Learning Enhanced Deep Learning Models
authors: Oscar Tsai, Mei Yang, Shengjie Zhai
publish: 2025 4th Asia Power and Electrical Technology Conference (APET)
abstract: Autonomous UAVs using RGB cameras and deep learning are becoming essential tools for inspecting transmission lines. This work underscores how transfer learning can boost the You Only Look Once (YOLO) convolutional neural network (CNN) in detecting transmission line defects, especially when training data are limited. We introduce the Aerial Transmission Line Inspection (ATLI) dataset as the target dataset and draw on the Microsoft Common Objects in Context (COCO) dataset as the source dataset. Transfer learning is applied in two distinct stages—feature extraction and fine-tuning—which significantly enhance the detection accuracy by achieving a mean Average Precision, mAP@0.5, of 78.9 %, and reduce the training time on the ATLI dataset. The findings highlight the critical role of transfer learning in refining CNN models for complex transmission line inspection tasks, demonstrating notable gains in both accuracy and efficiency.

title: Robust SfM for Space Target Reconstruction Under Multi-Exposure Conditions Using Superpoint and Superglue
authors: Yijin Jiang, Xiaoyuan Ren, Huanyu Yin, Libing Jiang, Canyu Wang, Zhuang Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Science, and Information Processing (AICSIP)
abstract: Optical observation of space targets frequently generates multi-exposure image sequences due to uneven illumination conditions, posing significant challenges to traditional Structure from Motion (SfM) methods. To address this issue, this paper proposes a novel SfM approach specifically designed for multi-exposure space target optical images. The method is built upon the COLMAP framework and innovatively integrates a self-supervised learning-based SuperPoint feature extractor with a SuperGlue matcher. By leveraging the self-supervised learning paradigm, the proposed method effectively overcomes the difficulties in feature point extraction and matching caused by exposure variations, thereby significantly improving the quality of sparse 3D reconstruction under multi-exposure conditions. To validate the method's effectiveness, experiments are conducted using multi-exposure space target image sequences. The results demonstrate that compared to the baseline method employing traditional SIFT features and sequential matching strategies, the proposed approach exhibits substantial advantages in reconstruction success rate, camera pose estimation accuracy, and sparse point cloud quality, confirming its robustness and effectiveness under non-uniform illumination conditions.

title: A Dynamic Line Rating Method Under Extreme Weather with Trade-Off Between Capacity Enhancement and System Security
authors: Chao Zhu, Yunpeng Guo, Xiaodi Zhang, Xilin Chen, Deng Ma
publish: 2025 4th Asia Power and Electrical Technology Conference (APET)
abstract: Extreme weather events bring sever challenges to the reliability and security of power system. To address the tradeoff between transmission capacity enhancement and system security under such conditions, this paper proposes a dynamic line rating method based on a bi-level optimization framework. The inner layer employs a DC power flow model to evaluate the system performance. And the outer layer uses a genetic algorithm combined with a conductor thermal balance model to determine the optimal DLR strategy. Case studies demonstrate that the proposed method effectively enhances transmission capacity on the improved IEEE 39-bus system. This method can reduce the operational cost and expected demand not supplied penalty, which ensures the security and reliability of power system under the extreme weather conditions.

title: Analysis of Electromagnetic Environmental Impact of ±200kV Overhead DC Lines
authors: Xianmeng Zhang, Wei Liu, Gang Wu, Xiaodong Liu, Bing Chen, Ruiyong Zhang, Xu Yuan, Fei Yuan
publish: 2025 4th Asia Power and Electrical Technology Conference (APET)
abstract: This paper presents a method of analyzing and predicting the electromagnetic environmental limit for <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm \mathbf{2 0 0 k V}$</tex> overhead direct current (DC) lines. By employing analytical and empirical formulas, key parameters are analyzed such as the synthetic electric field intensity, audible noise, and radio interference under various conductor arrangements for the Yangzhou-Zhenjiang <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm \mathbf{2 0 0 k V}$</tex> DC project. The results demonstrate that for all conductor configurations considered, the synthetic electric field intensities remained well below the preset limit standards, while the levels of audible noise and radio interference were extremely low. This research underscores that under rational design, effective management, and strict control, the electromagnetic environmental impact of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm 200 \text{kV}$</tex> overhead DC lines can be confined safely, offering robust support for the construction of an intelligent, efficient, and eco-friendly modern power system.

title: Green Synthesis of Amorphous Iron Oxide-Based Nanoparticles
authors: Vincenzina Strano, Vanna Torrisi, Simona Boninelli, Maria Miritello
publish: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Metrology for Green Technologies, Renewable Energy and Ecological Sustainability (MetroGREENST)
abstract: This work reports the green synthesis of iron oxide-based nanoparticles using Aloe vera leaves extract. The nanoparticles appear nearly spherical with diameters up to 50 nm; structural and optical analyses showed their amorphous nature. A comparative study was conducted using commercial <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{Fe}_{2}\mathrm{O}_{3}$</tex> nanopowder, proving the effectiveness of the proposed green approach as simple and sustainable alternative for producing nanoscale iron-oxide-based particles.

title: Attracting Global Professionals to the IEEE IAS PPIC Conference: A Collaborative Approach Leveraging Linkedin
authors: Dante E. Barragan, Marc Elliott, Robin Stroud, Michael Feldkirchner
publish: 2025 IEEE IAS Pulp and Paper Industry Conference (PPIC)
abstract: The annual IEEE IAS Pulp & Paper Industry Committee (PPIC) conference stands as a beacon for professionals in the pulp & paper industry, offering a platform for innovation, collaboration, and growth. This year, one PPIC Subcommittee's main objective is to harness the power of social media, particularly LinkedIn, to broaden the reach and attract a diverse group of attendees. Drawing from the insights of a previous article, the authors continue the work on LinkedIn as the primary channel for engagement. In a pioneering collaborative effort, the PPIC joins forces with LinkedIn to craft a strategy that enhances our professional presence on LinkedIn. The goal is not only to attract new members to the PPIC but also to empower the current ones. Linkedln provides invaluable advice on optimizing profiles for both organizations and professionals, ensuring a polished and engaging online presence. Furthermore, Linkedln offers guidance on crafting compelling posts designed to captivate readers and inspire them to participate in the PPIC conference while promoting their personal brand and company brand. The involved parties consisting of a PPIC committee member, a paper manufacturer representative, an industry supplier representative, and a LinkedIn representative, highlight the significance of LinkedIn for recruiting talent and enhancing company branding, illustrate the value of social media followership, address the challenges of standing out amidst the clutter of social media, emphasizing the need for a distinctive and visible presence. This article outlines a comprehensive strategy that leverages LinkedIn's best practices and analytics to amplify the conference's visibility and appeal. By doing so, the authors aim to create a vibrant community of professionals eager to share knowledge, experiences, and opportunities for advancement within the electrical field in the pulp, paper, and wood products industry.

title: A Data-Driven Approach to Predict Bike Rentals Using ML Models
authors: Meeta Chaudhary, Purnima Garg, Rohit Vashisht, Sonia Deshmukh
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Advanced Computing and Emerging Technologies (ACET)
abstract: Bike-sharing is an efficient mode of transportation that is becoming popular in urban areas. It is essential to predict the demand in bike rentals correctly in order to ensure that the operators of the system can utilize the resources effectively. This paper will use machine learning models to forecast the number of bikes being rented on a daily basis based on different environmental and seasonal factors. We compare Linear Regression, Decision Tree, as well as Random Forest models on a publicly available dataset. Among them, the highest predictive performance was highest in the Random Forest model. The use of the visualization and feature engineering methods is implemented to increase the accuracy of models. This study offers a strong methodology to predict bike utilization patterns and helps the interest groups in the operations planning.

title: Area-Based Algorithm for Mitochondria Dynamics Tracking
authors: Jirapong Saelor, Tzu-Yen Liao, Yu-Te Lin, An-Chi Wei
publish: 2025 IEEE 18th International Conference on Nano/Molecular Medicine &amp; Engineering (NANOMED)
abstract: Recent advances have enabled highresolution tracking of mitochondrial dynamics, yet current Nellie-based fission-fusion estimation approaches identify only a single event type per frame. To address this, we developed area-based and component-based algorithms that integrate both spatial and temporal information exported from Nellie. By analyzing changes in mitochondrial area and the spatial relationships between neighboring mitochondria across frames, our methods can detect both fission and fusion events with improved accuracy. When applied to INS-1 cells under mitochondrial stress, the proposed algorithms outperformed Nellie's default method, capturing subtle variations in fission activity. In silico validation with simulated mitochondria further confirmed high detection accuracy, reaching up to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 6 {\%}}$</tex> for fusion and 95% for fission events. Together, these results demonstrate that our algorithms provide a robust and versatile tool for quantitative analysis of mitochondrial dynamics, enabling more precise investigations of cellular stress responses and mitochondrial behavior.

title: Multi-Objective Polynomial Regression for Model Selection Using Brute Force Strategy
authors: Ali Ridha Ali
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)

title: Detection Method of Different Fault Phase Secondary Ground Fault After Active Arc Suppression in Distribution Network
authors: Yuan Ma, Wei Zhang, Shaoming Pan, Liangyuan Chen, Wei Huang
publish: 2025 4th Asia Power and Electrical Technology Conference (APET)
abstract: In order to solve the problem of different fault phase secondary ground fault by non-fault phase voltage rising for a long time after active arc suppression in distribution network, the equivalent circuit of two-point grounding fault is constructed, and the variation of zero-sequence voltage and line zero-sequence current is analyzed, the calculation formula of fault current is derived, and a new method of detecting the different fault phase secondary ground fault is proposed. In practical application, it is only necessary to obtain zero sequence voltage, current variation and measure three-phase ground voltage to realize fault starting, fault phase selection, fault resistance identification and fault line selection, etc., the method has the advantages of strong resistance tolerance, high sensitivity, simple operation and easy realization. The simulation results show the effectiveness of the proposed method, which provides a new idea for the detection of different fault phase secondary ground fault after active arc suppression in distribution network.

title: Integrating Experimental Microbiology and Machine Learning to Assess Antibacterial Potential of Laminaria Hyperborea and Sargassum Wightii
authors: Divya Vijayakumar, Palaniappan Seedevi
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Advanced Computing and Emerging Technologies (ACET)
abstract: The marine algae are good reservoirs of bioactive compounds that have potential to be used as antibacterial. The antibacterial effect of Laminaria hyperborea and Sargassum wightii was assessed in this work with the help of both experimental and machine learning-driven predictive models. The therapeutic potential of algal extracts was indicated through the use of the experimental analysis that proved the inhibitory effects of algal extracts against clinically relevant bacteria. These results were supplemented with three machine learning algorithms, including the Random Forest (RF), Support Vector Regression (SVR), and Elastic Net Regression (ENR) to predict the antibacterial activity. As the comparative performance analysis showed, RF had the best predictive accuracy (92.1 %), precision (91.3 %), recall (90.7 %), and F1 score (91.0 %), compared to the SVR and ENR that produced moderate prediction efficiencies. These results indicate the high quality of antibacterial consistency of S. wightii over L. hyperborea and the importance of combining ML with experimental microbiology. The paper highlights the promise of integrating bioactivity of seaweed components with computational modeling to speed-up the process of natural antibacterial discovery and help solve the rising issue of antibiotic resistance.

title: Design and Manufacture of Water Quality Monitoring Boat Using Iot Technology
authors: Laith Al-Juboori, Shama Ahmed Dad, Amna Abdulla Ahmed, Amna Hassan Abdulla, Maryam Mohammed Mousa, Moza Saeed Ali
publish: 2025 Advances in Science and Engineering Technology International Conferences (ASET)
abstract: This work designs and manufactures a prototype of boat to monitor some seawater elements with minimal human intervention. This design uses Internet of Things (IoT) technology, radio frequency monitoring applications and Remote-control technology. The power required to operate this boat is 12 V DC from battery. This developed system builds a real-time dashboard to control and monitor the movement and all sensing elements on that boat. The developed IoT based system is establish a network of devices that can sense and communicate / transmit data with other devices. Arduino IDE used in this work to collect all sensor data from all sensors that used (pH, water temperature, and turbidity level). The designed boat indicates the element level of all sensing devices through the Mobile App, this helps the operator to monitor the temperature/pH element levels and turbidity of the water. The boat is built using sustainable material by 3D printing. The machine is environmentally friendly and can be operated in any condition.

title: Adaptive Enhanced Dual-Step Domain Adaption Network for Cross-User Gesture Recognition with SEMG Signals
authors: Tong Liu, Kuiying Yin, Shengli Zhou
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Micro/Nano Sensors for AI, Healthcare, and Robotics (NSENS)
abstract: Gesture recognition based on surface electromyography (sEMG) signals is a common pattern recognition task. However, due to the individual variability of sEMG signals, it is still challenging to improve the performance of cross-user recognition systems. In this paper, an adaptive enhanced dual-step domain adaption network (AE-DSDAN) model combining a ResNet and Manifold learning layers was proposed for more advanced feature extraction. The model significantly reduces the inter-domain distribution differences by performing source and target domain feature alignment through the local maximum mean difference (LMMD) method. In addition, AE-DSDAN employs a collaborative training and cross-domain consistency regularization strategy, which enhances the robustness and generalization ability of the model through the collaborative learning of the teacher network and the student network. The experimental results show that AE-DSDAN achieves 90.23 ± 2.67% accuracy in the NinaPro DB5 dataset, which proves the effectiveness and robustness of the model and can be applied to cross-user MPR scenarios.

title: A Comparative Study of JAYA-SMC Method and Fuzzy Logic Control for Solar Systems Maximum Power Point Tracking
authors: Mokhtar Jlidi, Rabeh Abbassi, Houssem Jerbi, Faiçal Hamidi, Mohamed Aoun, Oscar Barambones
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Sustainable Energy and Net-Zero Emissions (SENZE)
abstract: With the aim of accelerating renewable energy integration and meeting Net-zero targets, maximizing the efficiency of photovoltaic (PV) systems is paramount. This necessity has driven researchers' attention to investigate advances in crucial techniques like maximum power point tracking (MPPT). This paper investigates and compares two approaches for optimizing the PV power generation. The first is the hybrid JAYA-SMC algorithm. JAYA is a simple and effective population-based technique like genetic algorithms, particle swarm optimization, and differential evolution. Sliding Mode Control (SMC) features a simple algorithm and a high degree of robustness. JAYA and SMC are combined for MPPT. The second approach is fuzzy logic control (FLC), which optimizes the power consumption of a PV system by adjusting the converter duty cycle, allowing the system to operate at its maximum efficiency under varying conditions. This study provides a critical evaluation of these two methods for MPPT applications, offering a clear framework for researchers to select the most appropriate control strategy based on the distinct demands of their target application.

title: A Robotic Grasping System Based on Data Glove and VLA Model
authors: Lizhi Mao, Fei Fei, Qianru Zhang, Yuxiao Yang
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Micro/Nano Sensors for AI, Healthcare, and Robotics (NSENS)

title: A Two-Stage Robust Optimization-Based Coordinated Dispatch Method for Mobile Emergency Resources Under Ice Disasters
authors: Shuai Shao, Yujie Li, Nan Xiao, Haohe Yang, Zhongyi Yang, Shengwen Shu
publish: 2025 4th Asia Power and Electrical Technology Conference (APET)
abstract: Ice disasters frequently occur in southern and plateau regions of China, posing severe threats to the safe and stable operation of power systems. To address the limitations of existing post-disaster recovery studies—which often focus on single systems while neglecting the coordination between power and transportation networks and fail to handle multiple uncertainties—this paper proposes a two-stage robust optimization-based method for coordinated dispatch of mobile emergency resources. First, a two-stage robust optimization model for the joint dispatch of emergency resources in a coupled power-transportation network is established. The model aims to minimize the load restoration cost under the worst-case scenario, dynamically optimizing resource allocation and repair routes while strictly considering multiple coupling constraints such as mobile power source integration, repair crew scheduling, power flow, and line status. Subsequently, an improved column-and-constraint generation (C&CG) algorithm is adopted, incorporating mechanisms such as scenario screening and traffic accessibility constraints to enhance solution efficiency. Finally, case studies based on a 21-node distribution system demonstrate that the proposed method can effectively coordinate repair operations and emergency power supply under road blockages and limited resources. Compared with non-coordinated dispatch schemes, it improves system restoration efficiency by approximately <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 5 \%}$</tex>, significantly enhancing power supply recovery capability and overall system resilience. This study provides a scientific decision-support tool for power grid disaster prevention and mitigation.

title: Overview of LDPC Decoding Based on Deep Learning
authors: Xiangwei Ren, Tao Tang, Hong Wen, Wenjing Hou, Hongzhi Liu, Ruonan Jing
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Science, and Information Processing (AICSIP)
abstract: LDPC codes, as an error correcting code close to the Shannon limit, are widely used in modern communication systems such as 5 G, satellite communication, deep space communication, and other fields. Traditional LDPC decoding algorithms, such as belief propagation (BP) algorithm and its variants, have achieved good performance, but face challenges such as high complexity and slow convergence, especially when dealing with long code lengths and high noise scenarios. The rise of deep learning has brought new opportunities for LDPC decoding, and its powerful features learning and pattern recognition capabilities are expected to break through the bottleneck of traditional decoding. Based on the overview of deep neural network decoding methods for LDPC codes by data-driven and model driven approaches, this paper analysis and comparison of error rate performance of short code <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{H} (8,16)$</tex> matrix by convolutional neural networks (CNN), residual network (ResNet), and Kolmogorov-Arnold Network (KAN).

title: Detection of Paddy Crop Diseases and Early Diagnosis Using Faster R-CNN
authors: Raghuraj Singh, Dharmendra Kumar, Abhishek Singh, Ayush Adhikari, Jitendra Pratap Singh
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Advanced Computing and Emerging Technologies (ACET)
abstract: Diseases in rice leaves are a major concern of rice crops in the global arena. They influence the food production and the earnings of the farmers. It is quite crucial to identify the disease that a rice leaf has at an early stage and properly so that the farmers can handle it effectively. However, conventional methods of detecting illnesses are timeconsuming, costly and not necessarily accurate. In this work, a novel system is presented, which is capable of detecting rice leaf diseases in real-time on a Faster Region-Based Convolutional neural network (Faster R-CNN). The system is concerned with issues such as busyness of background, various manifestations of diseases and lack of information. It was trained on both public data and those images of actual rice fields. The system is able to recognize three key diseases, including rice blast, brown spot and hispa, along with the healthy leaves.

title: Efficient Routing Scheme Using Quadrant Turn Model for 2D Mesh NoC Architecture
authors: Umar Adeel, Muhammad Irtiza, Kalkidan Kerala, Ali Alnoman, Adeel Akram
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: The scalability of many core Systems on Chip relies on efficient, deadlock-free communication, making 2D mesh-based Networks on Chip (NoCs) increasingly critical. Routing efficiency in such topologies requires balanced minimal path selection, adaptivity, and deadlock prevention. This work presents the Quadrant Turn Model (QTM), a deadlock-free framework that partitions the mesh into four spatial quadrants, and enforces quadrant-specific turn restrictions, eliminating cyclic dependencies without additional virtual channels. Building on QTM, two complementary routing algorithms, namely, Quadrant based Deterministic Even (QDE) and Quadrant based Deterministic Odd (QDO) are proposed to introduce controlled adaptivity within selected quadrants to enhance load balancing while maintaining deterministic routing elsewhere.Both algorithms are implemented in the SystemC-based Noxim simulator on a 4×4 mesh network and evaluated across diverse traffic patterns, including Butterfly, Bit Reversal, Shuffle, and Transpose. Results indicate that QDE and QDO reduce latency, increase throughput, and lower energy consumption compared with conventional deterministic and adaptive schemes. The quadrant-aware design mitigates hotspot formation, optimizes minimal path utilization, and demonstrates that selective adaptivity combined with turn restrictions can improve (NoC) performance without extra hardware overhead.

title: Weather Forecast Prediction: An Integrated Approach for Analysing and Measuring Weather Data
authors: Syed Farhan Ashraf, K. Jyothi, Musharaf Aejaz, Ambati LeelaLohithReddy
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Advanced Computing and Emerging Technologies (ACET)
abstract: Despite being essential for daily decision-making, travel, agriculture, and emergency response, weather prediction remains a serious scientific problem due to the dynamic and nonlinear nature of atmospheric systems. Overfitting, poor interpretability, and uneven performance over time are some of the disadvantages of machine learning (ML) and deep learning (DL) models, despite their potential. Using heterogeneous meteorological data, this study suggests a unified pipeline for short- to medium-range forecasting that combines interpretable outputs, a multi-model prediction framework, and resilient feature engineering. The feature space incorporates temporal-cyclical encodings, atmospheric indicators (dew point, pressure tendency, wind shear indices), and historical weather records (temperature, humidity, pressure, wind, and precipitation). The process benchmarks various models, including deep sequence models (LSTM, 1D CNN, CNN-LSTM), ML regressors (Random Forest, Gradient Boosting, XGBoost), and statistical baselines (persistence, ARIMA), culminating in a stacked ensemble optimised via Bayesian hyperparameter tuning. Performance is evaluated using RMSE, MAE, MAPE, and skill scores relative to persistence, while calibration is assessed through reliability diagrams and residual diagnostics. The system enables real-time inference with SHAP-based interpretability of key drivers. To improve usability and decision assistance, a simple user interface provides forecasts, UV index, air quality, and context-aware recommendations. When compared to individual models, experimental results show better calibration and lower error, providing a scalable, understandable, and useful framework for environmental time-series forecasting.

title: Traffic State Monitoring and Prediction System Based on LiDAR Technology: A Case Study of Taihu Suzhou Bay Bridge
authors: Hao Wu, Jiaxin Tong, Qian Chen, Minlu Zeng, Wen Guo, Fangyu Feng, Jianying Zheng, Xiang Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Micro/Nano Sensors for AI, Healthcare, and Robotics (NSENS)
abstract: With the increasing number of vehicles and the acceleration of urbanization, traditional traffic monitoring methods face challenges in terms of accuracy and real-time performance. To address these issues, this paper proposes a bridge traffic state monitoring and prediction system based on LiDAR technology. The system first collects vehicle point cloud data in real-time through LiDAR and extracts vehicle trajectories using an intelligent trajectory acquisition method. It then incorporates a multi-class event detection algorithm to automate the detection of traffic events. Additionally, the GT-LSTM model is used for short-term traffic flow prediction, and the GCN-CS-LSTM model is applied for multi-modal driving behavior trajectory prediction. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed system significantly improves monitoring accuracy and prediction reliability in complex environments. The research can benefit the perception and prediction of urban traffic condition and enhance the traffic efficiency and safety.

title: 200 Mj Thin-Disk Picosecond Laser With Beam Aberration Compensation
authors: Zihan Zhou, Jiajia Mao, Hang Yang, Xiang Gao, Junjie Jiang, Dayong Zhang, Liujing Xu, Xia Yu, Kun Liu, Hong Zhao
abstract: We have demonstrated a picosecond laser system based on water cooled Yb:YAG thin disks with output energy exceeding 200 mJ at the repetition rate of 1 kHz. The laser beam is shaped by a dual-telescope beam shaper and the low-order aberrations are well compensated.

title: Integrated Spectral and Geospatial Analysis of Mechanical Vibrations in a Light Urban Vehicle for Piezoelectric Energy Harvesting Optimization
authors: Antonino Quattrocchi, Andrea Venuto, Fabrizio Freni, Antonio Cannuli, Roberto Montanini
publish: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Metrology for Green Technologies, Renewable Energy and Ecological Sustainability (MetroGREENST)
abstract: This paper presents an integrated methodology for the spectral and geospatial analysis of mechanical vibrations in a light urban vehicle (Vespa Piaggio PX150) with the aim of optimizing piezoelectric energy harvesting (EH) systems. A triaxial accelerometer and GPS module were installed on the vehicle to acquire vibration and geolocation data along a real urban and suburban route. The signals were processed using Fast Fourier Transform (FFT) and Power Spectral Density (PSD) analysis, followed by a novel frequency approach based on Sturges's rule, to identify dominant power bands. The results were georeferenced to produce a chromatically coded geographic map that highlighted optimal EH zones. The study confirms that lateral and vertical vibrations, particularly in the 45–65 Hz range, are most promising for EH applications. The proposed methodology is a first step in establishing a comprehensive framework for the design and development of vibration-based EH systems in urban mobility contexts.

title: Toward Unified Multimodal Frameworks: A Systematic Review of AI-Driven and Immersive Technologies for Inclusive STEM Education
authors: Thenuka Chandrabanu Liyanage, Santhusha Mallawatantri
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: Special Educational Needs and Disabilities (SEND) learners remain underrepresented in Science, Technology, Engineering, and Mathematics (STEM) due to barriers such as abstract concepts, limited resources, and rigid teaching models. Advances in Artificial intelligence (AI) and immersive technology show promise for improving accessibility, engagement, and personalization. This study synthesized 19 peer-reviewed articles (2015–2025) following PRISMA 2020 guidelines. Findings highlight benefits of immersive environments for motivation and cognition, and of LLMs for adaptive feedback, while noting challenges of cost, scalability, and ethics. Few studies integrated both domains, underscoring a research gap and the need for unified multimodal frameworks to support inclusive STEM learning.

title: A Deep Learning Approach for Early Detection of Depression from Structured Questionnaire
authors: Yash Upadhyay, Nitesh Jha, Vikas Upadhyaya
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: Depression, a leading cause of disability worldwide, often remains undiagnosed due to the subjective nature of self-reporting and limited access to clinical evaluations. This research proposes a deep learning-based framework for the early detection of depression using structured questionnaire data. The study employs a hybrid data preprocessing pipeline involving robust scaling and statistical transformation. Dimensionality reduction was achieved via Principal Component Analysis (PCA), and SMOTE. The core classification engine is a custom Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) designed to handle transformed tabular data and enhanced with dropout regularization, batch normalization, and Swish/ReLU activations. Additionally, the model leverages focal loss to effectively manage class imbalance, a common issue in mental health datasets. Our approach was tested on a real-world dataset of behavioral and emotional indicators collected via survey, achieving a test accuracy of 97.64%, precision of 0.97, recall of 0.98, and F1-score of 0.98. These results demonstrate the feasibility of deep learning as a supportive tool for scalable and automated mental health screening. This paper lays the foundation for integrating such models into digital health ecosystems, potentially assisting clinicians and users in recognizing depressive tendencies early and efficiently.

title: Solar-Powered Green Hydrogen for Saudi Vision 2030: Techno-Economic Optimization with Storage Constraints in Ha'il Province
authors: Abdullah Albaker, Mashari Alangari
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Sustainable Energy and Net-Zero Emissions (SENZE)
abstract: This study presents a techno-economic assessment of solar-powered green-hydrogen production in Ha'il province, Saudi Arabia, in line with the strategic imperatives of Saudi Vision 2030. Over the past decade Ha'il has emerged as a renewable-energy hub: three utility-scale photovoltaic (PV) plants, collectively rated at several hundred megawatts, are already operational or under construction, expanding the region's low-carbon generation base and enabling downstream hydrogen conversion that could displace fossil fuels in local industry and national supply chains. To identify the least-cost facility layout, a linear-programming model is formulated that minimizes the annualized system cost, expressed as the levelized cost of hydrogen (LCOH), while satisfying hourly energy-balance and investment constraints; decision variables include PV capacity, electrolyzer size, and hydrogen-storage volume, and a dedicated storage submodel tracks inventory dynamics so that part of the output can be buffered for deferred transport or export. Model behavior is examined through three numerical case studies: (i) a no-storage baseline, (ii) a hybrid configuration that channels 50 % of daily hydrogen production into storage, and (iii) a sensitivity analysis in which storage capacity is systematically varied to assess its impact on economic and operational indicators. The obtained optimization results demonstrate the impact of integrating the hydrogen storage into the renewable generation projects on the system viability, which leads to providing practical guidance on optimal design and governance of exploiting green hydrogen production in areas rich in solar energy, such as Ha'il.

title: Measurement and Validation Strategy in Adsorbent Coatings for thermochemical Energy Storage
authors: Davide Palamara, Emanuela Mastronardo, Candida Milone, Mengistu Gelaw, Andrea Frazzica, Luigi Calabrese
publish: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Metrology for Green Technologies, Renewable Energy and Ecological Sustainability (MetroGREENST)
abstract: This research establishes a measurement and validation strategy for novel adsorbent coatings in thermochemical energy storage (TCES). We developed composite coatings utilizing SAPO-34 and 13X zeolites within a sulfonated polymer matrix, chosen for enhanced water vapor diffusion and cost-effectiveness. A silica gel-filled sulfonated polymer served as a benchmark. Our strategy involved coating on aluminum substrates with varying filler concentrations. We then employed a systematic multidisciplinary measurement approach to characterize their mechanical properties, thermophysical and water vapor adsorption capacity, providing crucial data for evaluating coating performance and suitability for TCES applications.

title: Protection and Faulted Line Identification Scheme for Same-Tower Multi-Circuit AC-to-DC Systems Based on Polarity Recognition
authors: Zhenxing Li, Zhe An, Yi Zhu, Hanli Weng, Qiujie Wang, Hong Tan
publish: 2025 4th Asia Power and Electrical Technology Conference (APET)
abstract: Embedded high Voltage Direct Current (HVDC) technology contributes to enhancing power transmission capacity and renewable energy consumption capability in regional grids. Its combination with AC-to-DC conversion technology presents an engineering solution with both feasibility and economy. However, the distinct control logic, unique topology, and complex coupling characteristics lead to significant degradation in the adaptability of conventional protection and fault identification schemes. To address this, an incomplete decoupling method adapted to the coupling features of same-tower multi-circuit AC-to-DC systems is proposed in this paper. Considering the protection requirements under symmetrical monopolar topology, a protection and fault identification scheme based on polarity recognition is developed using voltage mutation characteristics. Simulation results validate the effectiveness of the proposed scheme: fault removal is achieved within one cycle, accurate faulted line identification is realized, and strong tolerance to fault resistance is demonstrated.

title: A Sparse Reconstruction Approach for Electrical Impedance Tomography Based on First-Norm Projected Nonlinear Landweber
authors: Abdulla Desmal
publish: 2025 Advances in Science and Engineering Technology International Conferences (ASET)
abstract: This work introduces a sparse representation approach for electrical impedance tomography (EIT) based on the projected nonlinear Landweber (PNLW) method. The algorithm performs nonlinear steepest descent iterations, followed by a firstnorm projection operator. The step length for each iteration is determined using the maximum singular value of the Jacobian matrix. To reduce computational cost, the Jacobian is computed via the reciprocity principle. The numerical results section demonstrates promising conductivity reconstructions.

title: A Convolutional Neural Network-Based Open-Circuit Fault Diagnosis Method for Three-Level Back-to-Back Converters
authors: Xiaoyue Sun, Zhenbin Zhang, Zhen Li, Haoyu Chen, Huimin Huang
publish: 2025 4th Asia Power and Electrical Technology Conference (APET)
abstract: Three-level neutral-point-clamped back-to-back (3L-NPC-BtB) converters are widely used in wind energy systems, large power motor drives, transportation electrification systems, and more. Fault diagnosis is crucial for ensuring the reliability of these converters. Among various methods, data-driven fault diagnosis does not rely on precise analytical models. Instead, it extracts features from historical data to classify faults, making it more adaptable to complex topologies. Existing signal processing-based methods (e.g., wavelet packet transform) often require expert-designed and manually optimized feature extraction, which is not only cumbersome but also may not yield optimal features. Furthermore, these methods often suffer from high computational cost and long diagnostic time. To address these limitations, we propose a fault diagnosis method for 3L-NPC-BtB converters based on convolutional neural networks (CNN). In this method, three-phase current data is transformed using Clarke and Park transformations, and the resulting waveforms are input as dual-channel data into the CNN for fault diagnosis. The CNN automatically extracts fault features, offering a faster and more efficient diagnosis with reduced computational load. Finally, simulation results confirm the accuracy and speed of the proposed diagnosis method.

title: Design of a Glitch-Free PLL with Asynchronous Feedback Divider Supporting Multiple Reference Clock Switching
authors: Manish Rathi, Anand Kumar Sinha, Ateet Omer
publish: 2026 39th International Conference on VLSI Design &amp; 25th International Conference on Embedded Systems (VLSID)
abstract: This paper presents novel implementation of Reference Switching Control Logic (RSCL) which ensures stable clock at PLL output whenever the source of reference clock changes. This logic maintains same frequency at PLL output even if the reference clock of significantly different frequency or phase is applied. The circuit ensures glitch free PLL output by ensuring that phase of reference clock and feedback clock always align despite change in the source of reference clock. This results in an uninterrupted functioning of PLL. With the reference frequency changing from 20 MHZ to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 5 ~ M H z}$</tex> and back to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 0 ~ M H Z}$</tex>, it is seen that output frequency of PLL with RSCL remains constant at 5.5 GHz with max drift of 622 ppm. It is to be noted that with the use of RSCL, this drift has reduced by approx. 12 times. This work is implemented in 28nm technology.

title: AI-Based Microstructural Analysis of Carbon Fiber Epoxy Composites for Hydrogen Storage Applications: A U-Net Segmentation Approach
authors: Jean Facchini, Fakhreddine Ababsa, Mohammadali Shirinbayan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: In this paper, we discuss the automated microstructural characterization of carbon fiber reinforced epoxy composites used in 700 bar hydrogen storage systems, where structural reliability is highly sensitive to manufacturing defects. Porosity, even at levels of 5 to 7%, can reduce Young's modulus by up to 65%, making systematic detection essential. Traditional evaluation, which relies on manual inspection of images obtained by scanning electron microscopy (SEM), is laborious, subjective, and insufficiently standardized for large- scale quality assessment. This work proposes a deep learning-based segmentation pipeline for SEM images, comparing three architectures (U-Net, DeepLabV3, and Mask R-CNN). Among the models evaluated, U-Net achieved the best performance, with a Dice score of 0.866 and an IoU of 0.792, while offering the lowest computational footprint. The proposed framework enables fast, reproducible, and operator-independent microstructural quantification, supporting both manufacturing optimization and numerical simulation validation for hydrogen storage composite components.

title: Enhancing Brain Tumor Detection in Multi-Class MRI Scans Using Attention-Guided U-Net Architecture and Data Augmentation Techniques
authors: Aqeel Majeed Breesam, Sarah Riyadh Adnan, Roaa Safi Abed Alah
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: Clinical diagnosis and therapy planning require accurate segmentation of brain tumors with the use of magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) images. Nevertheless, the complexity of brain tissues, the diversity of tumour phenotypes and the little amount of annotated data are significant challenges to automatic segmentation methods. This paper will present an Attention-Guided U-Net architecture, which has been enhanced by data augmentation methods to obtain robust and accurate tumor classification. The attention mechanism enables the model to pay specific attention to important areas of the tumor whereas the augmentation strategies (random rotations, flips, translations, and zoom transformations) enhance the generalization at the expense of underfitting. The model was trained and evaluated using LGG MRI dataset with an accuracy of 99.5%, Dice coefficient of 0.7787 and IoU of 0.6410. The experimental evidence proves that there is a significant enhancement of the segmentation accuracy and model robustness when attention modules are combined with systematic augmentation.

title: A Conceptual Framework for Authentication in Agentic AI Ecosystems: Protocol Analysis and Taxonomy
authors: Badal Bhushan, Karthik Pappu, Akshay Mittal
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: Agentic AI platforms launch short-lived software agents that act with delegated authority across services and organizations. Existing authentication protocols were designed for traditional web applications and long-lived microservices, not ephemeral autonomous agents, creating uncertainty about their applicability to agentic scenarios. This paper systematically analyzes authentication mechanisms for agentic ecosystems, including SPIFFE/SPIRE for workload identity, OAuth 2.0 and OpenID Connect for user delegation, Token Exchange for delegation chains, DPoP for sender-constrained tokens, CIBA for human step-up, and decentralized identity approaches for cross-domain portability. We propose a five-pattern taxonomy organizing these mechanisms by interaction type: user-to-agent, orchestrator-to-agent, agent-to-internal service, agent-to-external API, and cross-domain federation. For each pattern, we evaluate capabilities and limitations based on specifications and implementations, identify integration prerequisites, and highlight operational challenges. To validate the feasibility of protocol composition, we implement a hybrid prototype that demonstrates agent-to-internal-service authentication. Measurements show that sender-constrained tokens add 52ms of cold-path and 11ms of warm-path overhead, with token caching providing a 4.6× speedup. Our analysis indicates that protocol compositions can address core agentic requirements (ephemerality, delegation, replay prevention, auditability) within a single trust domain, while identifying critical research gaps, including multi-hop delegation semantics, federation automation, and high-churn performance.
