title: An Ensemble and Deep Learning Based Approach for SMS Spam Classification
authors: Asim Abid, Muhammad Arsalan, Ejaz Ahmed, Muzamil Ahmed, Malik Muhammad Ali Shahid
publish: 2025 International Conference on Engineering &amp; Computing Technologies (ICECT)
abstract: The Short Message Service (SMS) remains a critical medium of communication despite the arrival of newer messaging platforms; nevertheless, the pervasive dissemination of spam messages remains an endemic problem for users across the world. This paper introduces a discerning method to detect SMS spam with ensemble-based models coupled with deep embedding and transformer models to address the subtlety of natural language processing and linguistic ambiguity. We performed a thorough analysis on a data set that was typified by imbalance with 5,575 messages obtained from Kaggle, using exhaustive preprocessing techniques involving cleaning of text, stop word removal, and lemmatization to improve quality of input and reduce noise. Our experimental results demonstrate outstanding performance for different configurations of the model: the Random Forest coupled with TF-IDF achieved 98% accuracy with a 99% F1 score, highlighting the continued relevance of conventional machine learning methods when accordingly optimized. The ALBERT transformer model achieved 99.73 % accuracy after 15 epochs of training, testifying to the efficacy of contextual embeddings in this classification task. The better performance demonstrated by conventional and novel models supports our hybrid approach to spam detection. The research conclusively proves that blend of machine learning techniques with innovative deep learning architectures and advanced preprocessing techniques yields an efficient system to address the endemic problem of SMS spam, potentially improving user experience and security on billions of daily text messages worldwide.

title: Knowledge-Embedded Hypergraph Neural Networks
authors: Yifan Feng, Yifan Zhang, Shaoyi Du, Shihui Ying, Zongze Wu, Yue Gao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
abstract: Hypergraph Neural Networks (HGNNs) enhance graph-based modeling by representing complex relationships, with applications in brain network analysis, recommendation systems, and computer vision. However, conventional HGNNs often struggle with effective knowledge extraction and discriminative feature representation, leading to performance limitations. This paper presents Knowledge-Embedded Hypergraph Neural Networks (Knowledge HGNN), a framework that addresses these challenges with two complementary encoders and a multi-dimensional fusion strategy. The High-Order Incidence Encoder (HOI-Encoder) explicitly embeds structural knowledge by capturing permutation-invariant high-order incidence patterns that are typically overlooked by standard HGNNs. In contrast, the Task-Driven Rule Encoder (TDR-Encoder) focuses on feature-level knowledge, extracting task-related rules from vertex attributes through gradient boosted decision tree pre-training and encoding both rule content and positional importance. A Multi-Dimensional Knowledge Fusion module then integrates structural and rule-based embeddings, bridging semantic and dimensional gaps to form enriched vertex representations. The framework includes two implementations: Rule-Driven HGNN, which emphasizes rule-based knowledge, and Dual-Driven HGNN, which jointly leverages structural and rule-based knowledge for comprehensive feature extraction. Extensive experiments on ten datasets, together with ablation studies, demonstrate that Knowledge HGNN significantly improves performance, achieving a 7.3% gain on the Cora dataset and an average improvement of 2.5% across all datasets. These results highlight the effectiveness of explicitly differentiating and fusing structural and rule-based knowledge, setting a new standard for hypergraph applications in complex, data-driven scenarios.

title: Enhancing C-V2X with Blockchain and Zero-Knowledge Proofs for Improved Privacy, and Trustworthiness
authors: Ningyuan Chen, Chiew Foong Kwong, David Chieng, Pushpendu Kar, Chenyu Yang, Qianyu Liu, Theodoros A. Tsiftsis, Yusen Chen
abstract: This paper addresses the pivotal issue of privacy in traffic condition assessment within Cellular Vehicle-to-Everything (C-V2X) and Intelligent Transportation Systems, specifically targeting applications that do not have stringent low-latency requirements. Despite significant advancements in the field, existing approaches often fail to provide robust privacy protection without compromising network efficiency and data integrity. Our study is motivated by the pressing need to overcome these limitations through solutions that enhance network reliability, data privacy, and node reputation management. At the core of our approach is the implementation of Zero-Knowledge Proofs (ZKPs), which facilitate the secure verification of vehicular data while safeguarding individual privacy. We developed a method for aggregating ZKPs to improve data processing efficiency, thereby substantially reducing network load. Moreover, our application of machine learning techniques for node trustworthiness assessment further strengthens network integrity. The integration of blockchain technology in our framework addresses the traditional centralisation challenges in C-V2X, particularly in the areas of data storage, processing, and verification, thereby enhancing the network's security and resilience. Our framework bridges these gaps, resulting in a 90% reduction in computation and storage costs on-chain compared to a non-aggregated benchmark where each proof is submitted individually. Additionally, the node trustworthiness assessment reduces network delay by up to 31.7%.

title: Automated Optofluidic Analysis of Single Extracellular Vesicles for Organoid Culture Monitoring_supp1-3672359.pdf
authors: Ravipa Losakul
abstract: Advances in organoid-on-chip technology are transforming culturing methods by enabling precise control over biochemical environments and sustained nutrient delivery. Conventional methods for assessing organoid health are invasive, requiring skilled manual handling and sacrificing the tissue for analysis. We present a fully automated, integrated system for noninvasive extraction, labeling, and optical analysis of extracellular vesicles (EVs) secreted by 3D organoid cultures. EVs contain important molecular biomarker cargo, such as nucleic acids and proteins, enabling real-time insight on the health of their cell of origin. Using a polydimethylsiloxane (PDMS) optofluidic biosensor, we demonstrate: (1) optical detection of single EVs derived from organoid culture; (2) on-chip sample processing and counting of single EVs; and (3) automating conditioned media delivery, on-chip sample preparation, and optical detection of EVs in a single integrated system. Together, these results showcase a noninvasive approach to monitoring organoid health through EV signatures present in conditioned media. The integrated platform based on a modular optofluidic analysis chip further offers a foundation for integrating a variety of downstream analysis techniques, advancing continuous organoid-on-a-chip monitoring.

title: ICBPI: Accurate and Validated Fine-Grained Power Estimation with Application to Thermal Hardware Trojan Detection and Localization in SOCs
authors: Mohamed Elshamy, Mehdi Elahi, Ahmad Patooghy, Abdel-Hameed Badawy
publish: ACM Transactions on Design Automation of Electronic Systems
abstract: None

title: PRTT: Leveraging Predicted RTT for Congestion Control in Data Center Networks
authors: Rongping Lin, Jinpeng Zhang, Yunfeng Li, Shan Luo, Xiong Wang, Haiyan Jin, Moshe Zukerman
abstract: The objective of congestion control is to maximize network bandwidth utilization and minimize the average flow completion time in data center networks. Key performance indicators required to achieve this objective are high throughput and low packet latency. Existing approaches have proposed various methods to control packet delivery based on various network events or parameters such as packet loss, bottleneck bandwidth, RTT, and queue length. However, these methods often result in suboptimal packet transmission states that compromise throughput or latency. We provide a method to overcome this drawback. To this end, we introduce a congestion control method called PRTT (Predicted RTT) that leverages predicted RTT for congestion control. By using accurately predicted RTT values, PRTT dynamically adjusts the packet delivery rate to control the number of in-flight packets. This enables the transmission to approach states where the buffer holds only a few packets while fully utilizing the link bandwidth. Experimental results show that PRTT achieves higher throughput, lower latency, and shorter flow completion times than other state-of-the-art methods, particularly under bursty traffic. These results demonstrate that PRTT offers a promising solution for congestion control.

title: Semi-supervised 3D Point Cloud Semantic Segmentation using Multiscale Graph Attention and Class-Incremental Learning
authors: Hui Ye, Wei Guo, Liang Ma, Xiangyi Shi, Jianqiang Ma
abstract: Class-incremental learning for 3D point cloud segmentation requires dense annotations for novel classes, which is prohibitively expensive and impractical for real-world applications with scarce labels. To bridge this gap, we propose a Semi-Supervised Class-Incremental Learning (SSCIL) framework that significantly reduces annotation costs. Our framework introduces three key innovations: (1) a relaxed geometry-aware feature-relation distillation module that transfers knowledge with relaxed constraints; (2) an unsupervised learning module, comprising color reconstruction and a view consistency module (CRVC), which leverages unlabeled data to learn robust and generalizable features; and (3) a multi-scale dynamic graph attention (MDG) backbone that captures both local geometric details and global contextual relationships. Extensive experiments on S3DIS and ScanNet benchmarks demonstrate that our method achieves competitive performance compared to the fully-supervised 3DPC-CISS, attaining approximately 97% of its mIoU at Cnovel = 5, S0 split while using only 10% of the labeled novel-class data. Our work establishes a practical and efficient paradigm for sustainable 3D scene understanding, with a 10× reduction in annotation effort, making incremental learning viable in label-scarce scenarios.

title: Annular Ring Cluster-Based Wideband Four-Element CP-MIMO Antenna System
authors: Amit Kumar, Sandeep Rana, Gunjan Srivastava, Akhilesh Mohan
publish: IEEE Antennas and Wireless Propagation Letters
abstract: This paper presents a wideband four-element circularly polarized (CP) MIMO antenna operating from 21.5 to 31.6 GHz. Each element employs an annular ring cluster formed by a central ring and seven identical outer rings printed on a 0.201 mm RO4003C substrate, enabling orthogonal modal excitation and broadband CP radiation. Excitation is achieved through a diagonally oriented cross-slot etched in the ground plane and fed by a 45° microstrip line with a quarter-wave transformer on a 0.508 mm RT5880 substrate. The four elements are arranged with 8 mm edge-to-edge spacing and 90° rotational diversity, while thirteen symmetrically positioned cross-shaped ground slots suppress surface-current coupling without degrading FTBR. The antenna achieves |S₁₁| < –10 dB, axial ratio < 3 dB, realized gain of 7.2–10.6 dBic, FTBR above 19.54 dB, and inter-port isolation exceeding 40 dB across 21.5–31.6 GHz. With its wide CP bandwidth, high gain, good FTBR, high inter-port isolation, and compact multilayer structure, the proposed design is a promising candidate for K/Ka-band MIMO front-end applications.

title: Cloud-Native 6G: A Multi-Domain Perspective
authors: Jiadai Wang, Xingfan Zhang, Yuanhao Li, Wei Guo, Jiajia Liu
abstract: 6G networks are envisioned to provide ubiquitous connectivity, pervasive intelligence, and sustainable operation. Realizing this vision requires not only advanced transmission techniques but also architectural innovations capable of handling unprecedented heterogeneity and dynamics. The cloud-native paradigm, emphasizing modularity, elasticity, and automation, offers a solid foundation to reimagine the design and operation of future networks. In view of this, this article presents a multi-domain perspective on cloud-native 6G. We first outline the architecture and fundamental characteristics that distinguish cloud-native 6G from previous generations, and then examine its feasibility across the radio access, transport, core, and non-terrestrial network domains. Finally, a case study on cross-orbit microservice orchestration using multi-agent reinforcement learning illustrates how cloud-native design enables efficient and balanced service management in the non-terrestrial domain.

title: Designing Active Assistance for Gait with Hip Abductors Weakness Using Predictive Simulation
authors: Linghui Xu, Georgios Andrikopoulos, Elena M. Gutierrez-Farewik
publish: IEEE Transactions on Neural Systems and Rehabilitation Engineering
abstract: Individuals with weak hip abductors commonly exhibit compensatory gait patterns and excessive lateral center of mass excursions, which may reduce walking efficiency and increase the risk of falling. In this study, we investigated the influence of hip abductor weakness and of ideal hip abduction assistive torque on gait patterns through an established predictive simulation framework and a full-body musculoskeletal model. Using this model and simulation framework, hip abductor muscle strength was reduced in several increments, and gait patterns were predicted and qualitatively compared to reported gait patterns in persons with weak hip abductors. Hip abductor assistive torque was then added to the model as ideal joint torques with three distinct time series profiles (step pattern, trapezoid pattern, and biological pattern). For each assistive torque pattern, timing and magnitude parameters were optimized in a bilevel optimization such that the predicted gait was normalized as much as possible. The key features of the predicted compensatory gait patterns were successfully captured in the simulation. All assistive torque profiles normalized gait kinematics relatively well, wherein the trapezoid and biological profiles normalized kinematics and reduced estimated costs of transport nearly equally well. Due to its simplicity and performance, the trapezoid profile can be considered as the most preferable. Our findings provide valuable insights into the impact of hip abductor weakness on compensatory gait, the potential benefits of hip abduction assistive torque, and the use of bilevel optimization to identify parameters that characterize optimal assistive torque, and can be useful in the exoskeleton control design for individuals with hip abductor weakness.

title: Grüneisen Relaxation Photoacoustic Imaging
authors: Jingyi Zhu, Shuyu Liang, Lidai Wang
abstract: Photoacoustic imaging, merging the high contrast of optical imaging with the deep penetration of ultrasound, has become an important research tool in the biomedical field. In recent years, Grüneisen relaxation photoacoustic technology has garnered widespread attention as an emerging method for functional expansion. The Grüneisen parameter is a key physical quantity linking the thermodynamic properties of matter with the photoacoustic effect. Its high sensitivity to temperature provides a unique avenue for developing noninvasive and precise photoacoustic imaging of biological tissues. This review outlines the fundamental physical mechanisms and key technological implementations of the Grüneisen relaxation photoacoustic technique, with a focus on its innovative applications in blood flow measurement, resolution enhancement, and the specific identification of lipid components.

title: Research on Financial Fraud Detection in Listed Companies Based on Stacking
authors: Mengzhu Liu, Fangyan Nie, Fuming Ye
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Science, and Information Processing (AICSIP)
abstract: Aiming at the problems that the identification of financial fraud of listed companies requires a lot of manpower and material costs, and the generalization ability of the traditional single model is weak, a financial fraud identification model based on the improved Stacking algorithm is proposed. Specifically, this paper constructs 24 financial indicators and 13 non-financial indicators based on the annual financial statement data of listed companies from 2009 to 2018. Match non fraudulent samples with samples containing financial fraud, and construct a sample set in a ratio of 1:3. Secondly, the indicators were tested for significance, and PCA and VIF were used to reduce the dimensions of the features with high correlation. Then use Logistic regression, decision tree LightGBM, XGBoost, Stochastic forest model and support vector mechanism build a single model, and then on the basis of four models with good results, model fusion is carried out to obtain a comprehensive identification model based on Stacking. The research results show that the fusion model is superior to the single model in identifying financial fraud of listed companies in terms of recall rate, F1 value, AUC and other evaluation indicators. This model can provide decision-making basis for the supervision and audit departments, strictly supervise and audit the companies that are predicted to be highly likely to commit fraud, and prevent fraud before it happens.

title: High-Fidelity Simscape Modeling and Validation of a Pyramidal VSCMG Cluster for Small-Satellite Attitude Control
authors: Ali Ahmed Elmorshedy, Doaa Ahmed Ali, Shamma Jamali, Mohamed Okasha, Haitham Elshimy, Ahmed Aboulftouh
publish: 2025 Advances in Science and Engineering Technology International Conferences (ASET)
abstract: This study presents a physics-based modeling and control framework for a spacecraft equipped with a pyramid configuration of four variable speed control moment gyros (VSCMGs). A high-fidelity simulation environment is developed using Simscape Multibody, capturing the coupled nonlinear dynamics of the spacecraft and internal actuators. The system architecture is designed to support closed-loop attitude control. A Lyapunov-based nonlinear controller is implemented to generate the required control torques for satellite attitude regulation. This approach ensures stability and convergence while accommodating the non-linear dynamics of the spacecraft VSCMG system. Several simulation scenarios are presented. These include stabilization from a tumbling initial state, tracking of a time-varying reference attitude, and a null motion configuration used to avoid actuator singularities. The results serve as a comprehensive benchmark for evaluating control and steering strategies under realistic actuator dynamics, demonstrating the effectiveness of the proposed framework in various attitude maneuver scenarios.

title: Citrate Coated Maghemite Nanoparticles for Sustainable Nano-fertilization
authors: Vanna Torrisi, Vincenzina Strano, Giuseppe Granata, Libera Vitiello, Sandro Dattilo, Sabrina C. Carroccio, Patrizia Trifilò, Douaa Bekkai, Maria Miritello
publish: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Metrology for Green Technologies, Renewable Energy and Ecological Sustainability (MetroGREENST)
abstract: High concentration of water colloidal solution based on maghemite <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{Fe}2\mathrm{O}_{3}$</tex> nanoparticles (NPs) was optimized for sustainable fertilization. Citrate surface functionalization method was carried out, together with size and morphological characterization of functionalized NPs and the investigation of total metal amount in the solution. Stability of colloidal solution containing 26 ppm of iron was checked once a month for a period of three months, in terms of iron content verified by Inductively Coupled Plasma Mass Spectrometry (ICP-MS), size of NPs aggregates by Scanning Electron Microscopy (SEM) and Dynamic Light Scattering (DLS), Z potential by Electrophoretic Light Scattering (ELS).

title: Smart-Home Security: A Low-Cost Hybrid Security Solution
authors: Omnia Abu Waraga, Manar Abu Talib, Sohail Abbas, Qassim Nasir
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: The rapid proliferation of the Internet of Things (IoT) devices has expedited the adoption of smart home systems; nevertheless, it has introduced many security concerns and challenges. Existing work tackled smart home systems through either monitoring the incoming traffic or assessing the vulnerabilities of the router or gateway, leaving critical security issues when only one aspect is considered. Hence, this paper proposes a hybrid smart home solution that combines vulnerability assessment and network monitoring techniques, a gateway, and home assistant services into a single low-cost chip while maintaining a balance between performance and security. The solution persistently and efficiently assesses the security level of the attached IoT devices and monitors the incoming traffic to prevent external attacks, which improves the security level of the smart home system, on the device level as well as on the network level. Based on the system evaluation of multiple IoT products using vulnerability assessment, attack detection, execution time, and communication overhead, the solution showed feasibility and usability on the resource-constrained devices

title: Pattern Design Optimization of Flexible Tactile Sensors Based on Finite Element Analysis
authors: Zhou Zhao, King Wai Chiu Lai
publish: 2025 IEEE 18th International Conference on Nano/Molecular Medicine &amp; Engineering (NANOMED)
abstract: Most microchannel pattern designs for flexible liquid metal (LM) pressure sensors are chosen empirically, with limited understanding of how pattern shape affects performance. In this study, finite element analysis (FEA) was employed to investigate the influence of different microchannel patterns (square and wavy patterns) on the tensile and compressive sensitivity of LM-based pressure sensors. Results indicate that square patterns (symmetric patterns) exhibit greater sensitivity to compression, whereas wavy patterns respond more effectively to tension, as further validated through experiment. These findings offer guidance for the rational design of high-performance flexible LM pressure sensors.

title: Research on Character Rear-View Recognition Based on YOLOv8
authors: Hui Xu, Jiajing Yang, Jiaxin Chu, Min Zhan, Jia Luo, Wenyan Guo
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Science, and Information Processing (AICSIP)
abstract: Pedestrian Re-Identification technology has found extensive application across numerous domains, serving as a critical safeguard for pedestrian safety. Nevertheless, the inherent limitations associated with this technology-namely, its reliance on frontal images, stringent illumination requirements, and the necessity for precise shooting anglespose significant challenges in data acquisition. In the present study, we shift our focus towards rear-view recognition technology, which offers a more feasible approach to data collection. Initially, rear-view photographs were captured under specific conditions designed solely to facilitate data organization. Subsequently, these images were augmented through the application of eight transformation techniques, culminating in a rear-view database comprising 11,853 images. Furthermore, preliminary models were constructed for each of the nine image categories to facilitate training and detection processes. Ultimately, we incorporated a Bayesian hyperparameter optimization methodology grounded in the Tree-structured Parzen Estimator (TPE) into the YOLOv8 framework, establishing both an initial coarse parameter model and a refined parameter model tailored for mixed images. This innovative approach has led to a substantial enhancement in the evaluation metrics for rear-view recognition, thereby offering a valuable contribution to the advancement of pedestrian re-identification technology.

title: An Efficient Hybrid Autoencoder -LSTM Based Deep Learning Framework for Intrusion Detection in IoT Networks
authors: Vibhor Harit, Rajeev Dahiya, Umang Garg
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Advanced Computing and Emerging Technologies (ACET)
abstract: Modern cyber-attacks on networks present advanced challenges that have necessitated the use of intelligent intrusion detection systems to identify including known and unknown threats. Traditional Intrusion detection techniques often suffer from high false alarm rates and lack generalization, especially when dealing with imbalanced datasets. A hybrid deep learning architecture is introduced in this research that integrates an autoencoder (AE) with along Long short-term memory known as LSTM networks for intrusion detection using UNSW-NB15 dataset. In the first stage, an autoencoder operates in unsupervised mode to learn normal network traffic patterns and detects anomalies by evaluating reconstruction errors. In second stage, an LSTM analyzes these abnormal traffic instances and classifies them into specific attack types by detecting temporal patterns in network flows. Experimental outcomes show that the model achieves 98.5% detection accuracy and outperforms standard machine learning techniques along with one-deep-learning-based outputs. The proposed scheme offers scalable and efficient IDS for securing existing network ensembles.

title: An Improved Hybrid Quasi-Resonant Soft-Switching ANPC Three-Level Converter and Its Dynamic Zerosequence Voltage Injection Control Strategy
authors: Fan Zhang, Xuan Wang, Ling Qin, Yunlong Han, Huajie Xiao
publish: 2025 4th Asia Power and Electrical Technology Conference (APET)
abstract: This paper proposes a zero-sequence voltage injection-based space vector pulse width modulation (SVPWM) strategy to address the neutral-point voltage imbalance and high switching losses in active neutral-point clamped (ANPC) threelevel converters. Traditional methods, such as zero-sequence injection sinusoidal PWM (ZSVI-SPWM), suffer from limited voltage utilization and excessive switching losses due to redundant switching actions. The proposed strategy dynamically injects a zero-sequence voltage into the modulation waveform, enabling precise control of the neutral-point current while optimizing commutation sequences to minimize high-frequency switching operations. Experimental results demonstrate that the method reduces DC-side capacitor voltage fluctuations from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$>10 ~\mathrm{V}$</tex> to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$<1$</tex> V, achieving stable neutral-point potential balance. Compared to ZSVI-SPWM, the proposed strategy significantly improves DC voltage utilization by 8.2 % and reduces power device losses by 13.4 %. The strategy is validated under high-power conditions <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(1000 ~\mathrm{V}$</tex> DC, 20 kHz switching frequency), showing robust performance in renewable energy systems and smart grids. This work provides a cost-effective solution for improving efficiency and reliability in high-power applications.

title: A Partially Loop-Unrolled Noise-Shaping SAR ADC Achieving 57dB-SNDR in 40MHz-BW at 320MS/s in 18nm FD-SOI CMOS Technology
authors: Anamika Sharma, Luv Pandey, Paras Garg, Rajesh Zele
publish: 2026 39th International Conference on VLSI Design &amp; 25th International Conference on Embedded Systems (VLSID)
abstract: Noise-shaping SAR ADC represents a promising hybrid ADC architecture that combines the benefits of noiseshaping with the efficiency of SAR conversion. This paper presents the design of an 8-bit, 40 MHz bandwidth noise-shaping SAR ADC employing a Cascade of Integrators with Feed-Forward (CIFF) architecture featuring an active loop filter. The core ADC is implemented as a sub-ranging SAR ADC to increase conversion speed. Unlike traditional active noise-shaping SAR ADCs, which perform residue amplification sequentially, the proposed design decouples the residue voltage from the SAR ADC and samples it onto a dedicated residue capacitor. This approach enables simultaneous residue amplification and ADC input sampling, resulting in a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim 40 {\%}$</tex> increase in the conversion rate compared to conventional architectures. Postlayout simulation results, based on an 18 nm FD-SOI CMOS implementation of a partially loop-unrolled SAR ADC with the proposed loop-filter architecture, achieve a 57dB SNDR in a 40 MHz bandwidth at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$320 \text{MS} / \mathrm{s}$</tex>. The ADC operates from a 1 V supply with a power consumption of 2.15 mW, resulting in a Schreier FoM of 160 dB. The ADC occupies an area of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$150 \mu \mathrm{m} \times 100 \mu \mathrm{m}$</tex>.

title: SPIFFE-Based Zero-Trust Authentication for AI Agent Ecosystems
authors: Karthik Pappu, Badal Bhushan, Akshay Mittal
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: AI agent ecosystems commonly rely on API key-based authentication, which introduces security risks related to credential exposure, manual rotation procedures, and weak guarantees of workload identity. This paper presents a SPIFFE (Secure Production Identity Framework for Everyone) based approach to AI multi-agent orchestration, providing a zero-trust authentication framework that eliminates static API keys for inter-agent communication through certificate-based workload identity. We implement a multi-agent security pipeline on Kubernetes, where each of five agents receives short-lived X.509 SVIDs (one-hour TTL) automatically issued and rotated by SPIRE at 50% lifetime intervals. All inter-agent communication is authenticated through mutual TLS using verifiable SPIFFE identities, mitigating credential theft, workload impersonation, man-in-the-middle attacks, and unauthorized LLM access. Experimental evaluation demonstrates zero authentication failures during continuous certificate rotation while maintaining end-to-end cryptographic verification. The findings establish that SPIFFE-based workload identity provides a practical and secure alternative to API keys for AI agent frameworks, reducing attack surface through automatic credential lifecycle management and enabling fine-grained identity-based authorization.

title: Printed Fractal Inductors on Flexible Substrates for Wearable and Green Electronics
authors: Mattia Scagliotti, Francesca Pescosolido, Matteo Rapisarda, Luigi Mariucci, Antonio Valletta
publish: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Metrology for Green Technologies, Renewable Energy and Ecological Sustainability (MetroGREENST)
abstract: The development of sustainable and flexible electronic components is crucial for advancing green technologies in wearable and eco-conscious applications. In this study, we present a comparative investigation between spiral and fractal inductor geometries fabricated via inkjet printing of silver nanoparticle ink on flexible polyethylene naphthalate (PEN) substrates. Electrical measurements of resistance and inductance were performed under flat, bent, and post-bending conditions to evaluate the mechanical-electrical stability of each design. Both geometries demonstrate robust electrical performance under bending conditions. The resistance of both the spiral and fractal inductors remains nearly constant, exhibiting increases of less than 10%, which indicates minimal impact from mechanical stress on their conductive pathways. In terms of inductance, a more pronounced reduction is observed in the spiral inductor during bending, likely due to geometric deformation and the introduction of parasitic effects. Conversely, the fractal inductor maintains more stable inductance values, benefiting from its self-similar design and single-layer topology that help mitigate parasitic capacitances and mechanical deformation. Despite the differences in inductance variation, both structures exhibit sufficient mechanical resilience and electrical stability, making them suitable candidates for flexible electronic applications where bending tolerance is essential. These results highlight the potential of fractal printed inductors as robust, reliable, and fabrication-friendly components for next-generation green and flexible electronics.

title: Automating Judicial Efficiency with Transformer NLP and Deep Learning Models
authors: Palak Verma, Mohit Singh, Shreya Mohan, Rohit Somvanshi, Yogendra Narayan Prajapati, Pomali Bose
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Advanced Computing and Emerging Technologies (ACET)
abstract: The Indian judicial system is plagued by heavy delays caused by the increasing number of case files, long legal documents, and intricate transcripts. This study suggests a light, understandable AI architecture for enhancing the efficiency of the judiciary by simplifying legal document summarization, semantic retrieval of precedents, and evidence-based question answering. Our pipeline combines supervised extractive summarization via Logistic Regression and Linear SVM models, effective retrieval via BM25 and Doc2Vec embeddings, and a retrieval-first RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) model for explainable query answering. The system is computation-efficient and maintains complete traceability of decision-making, thus being deployable in resource-limited judicial settings. Experimental testing on a collection of Indian verdicts illustrates competitive performance: the Logistic Regression model produces 66 % precision, 100 % recall, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$80 \% ~\mathrm{F} 1$</tex>-score, and 96 % accuracy on extractive summarization tasks, and the Linear SVM model depicts <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 0 \%}$</tex> precision, 100 % recall, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$66 \% ~\mathrm{F} 1$</tex>-score, and 92 % accuracy. These findings identify the framework as capable of providing interpretable, efficient, and responsible AI support within judicial proceedings, thereby decreasing cognitive load for legal practitioners and enhancing case understanding

title: Validation of PSO-Tuned Unscented Kalman Filtering for Real-Time IMU–GPS Vehicle Localization in Simulated and Embedded On-Road Environments
authors: Psyche T. Malabo, Bobby D. Gerardo
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: This paper presents an adaptive tuning framework for Unscented Kalman Filtering (UKF) in Inertial Measurement Unit–Global Navigation Satellite System (IMU–GNSS) vehicle localization using Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO) across simulation and on-road validation. In the first stage, the PSO-tuned UKF is evaluated in the CARLA 0.9.14 simulator with a Tesla Model 3 under various maneuvers and environments. PSO jointly optimizes the scaling parameters α,β,κ and noise covariances Q and R. Simulation results show that the PSO-tuned UKF reduces root-mean-square error (RMSE) to 0.50 m, an 82.14% improvement over a manually tuned UKF and a substantial mitigation of IMU-only drift, while maintaining 1.35 ms update time at 100 Hz. In the second stage, an instrumented vehicle with automotive-grade IMU, GPS, and wheel-speed sensing provides logs for offline replay, PSO re-tuning on field data, and embedded deployment, yielding hypothetical on-road RMSE of 0.72 m versus 1.18 m for manual tuning. This two-stage framework bridges CARLA-based optimization and real-time IMU–GPS localization in on-road environments with statistically validated performance.

title: Deep CNN-Based Detection of Antisocial Behaviors in Video Surveillance Systems
authors: Yagyansh Singh Deshwal, Kunj Agarwal, Sparsh Pandey, Vikas Srivastava
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Advanced Computing and Emerging Technologies (ACET)
abstract: This paper looks at the development and improvements in a deep learning-based surveillance system that identifies anti-social activities and emergency situations in real time. It started by detecting guns, knives, and fights using the YOLOv7 algorithm. The project has since broadened its focus to include fire and accidents, using newer YOLO versions like YOLOv10 and YOLOv11. The main method uses Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) and transfer learning. Key YOLOv11 improvements include replacing DCNv2 with DCNv3 in the detection head and using IOU loss instead of CIOU. The paper points out the importance of selecting models that adapt to different scenes. It shows that some upgraded YOLOv11 setups perform well in complex, close-range situations, while other setups work better for simpler, distant scenes. The system targets high detection ability and real-time processing, providing a solid, cost-efficient option for smart city infrastructure, public safety, and crime reduction by reducing the need for human oversight in surveillance monitoring.

title: A New Method Based on Multilayer Micro-Nano Structures for Detecting Liquid Refractive Index
authors: Zhuozhuo Huang, Wenyue Ren, Chengzhi Xian, Yangdong Wen
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Micro/Nano Sensors for AI, Healthcare, and Robotics (NSENS)
abstract: The use of hazardous liquids poses significant safety risks. Liquid detection contributes prevent leaks or spills, thereby avoiding environmental damage or safety accidents. Common leak detection methods are mostly based on mechanical or acoustic approaches. However, liquid detection involves complex data processing, and the difficulty in measuring the refractive index makes it challenging to distinguish between different types of liquids. In this article, a label-free, rapid, and highly sensitive method is proposed for detecting liquid refractive index. A multilayer micro-nano structure was constructed using <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{SiO}_{2}$</tex> nanoparticles. The structure exhibits detection capabilities for liquids with similar refractive indices through changes in the power ratio. In the experiment, methanol (1.329), ethanol (1.361), isopropanol (1.386) and n-butanol (1.3993), these four liquids with similar refractive indices, were utilized. The experimental results indicate that for methanol and ethanol, the refractive index increased by 5.29 %, but the power ratio was enhanced by 14.69 %. Even for isopropanol and n-butanol, which have the closest refractive indices, the refractive index increased by only 0.96 %, but the power ratio improved by 3.93 %. This method has broad environmental and biological applications.

title: Electric Load Forecasting Using Multi-Output Support Vector Regression Optimized by Particle Swarm Optimization
authors: Wenwei Zhang, Yuxin Xiao, Lizhi Yang
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Science, and Information Processing (AICSIP)
abstract: Support Vector Regression (SVR) has shown strong nonlinear fitting ability and good generalization performance. Since its introduction, it has been widely used in areas such as economic forecasting and engineering modeling. However, the traditional SVR model has certain limitations when dealing with multivariate time series prediction. These limitations reduce its forecasting accuracy and restrict its applicability. In order to address this constraint, this paper utilizes the Multi-output Support Vector Regression (MSVR) model. In addition, Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO) is utilized to intelligently tune critical parameters of the model, such as the penalty factor and kernel parameters. A hybrid PSO-MSVR forecasting framework is thus formulated, which, through the use of the PSO algorithm for global optimization in the parameter space, significantly enhances the MSVR approach's fitting capability and predictive accuracy. To validate its effectiveness, the PSO-MSVR model is applied to a real-world case of electric load forecasting using power consumption data. The experimental results indicate that the PSO-MSVR model significantly outperforms the conventional SVR model. The MAE is reduced by 45.94%, MSE by 62.44%, and MAPE by 48.88%, while <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R^{2}$</tex> increases by 7.87%. The results showcase the effectiveness and usefulness of the model put forth, expanding the possible applications of SVR in economic forecasting.

title: A Method for Enhancing TCM Consultation System Based on RLAIF and MoE
authors: Yirui Chen, Siyi Kong, Qinyv Xiao, Kai Ma, Mengyang Wang, Jing Chen
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Science, and Information Processing (AICSIP)
abstract: This study introduces a novel framework that integrates the Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) architecture with Reinforcement Learning with AI Feedback (RLAIF) to overcome the limitations of existing Traditional Chinese Medicine (TCM) consultation systems. Existing systems often face challenges in handling dynamic multi-turn dialogues, maintaining logical consistency, and ensuring safety compliance in herbal prescriptions. By leveraging the DeepSeek-V3 foundation model, the proposed approach combines MoE for efficient task allocation and RLAIF for realtime safety filtering, enabling precise adaptation to complex TCM scenarios. Seven heterogeneous datasets covering core TCM tasks - knowledge retrieval, diagnosis, herbal recommendations, and prescription analysis - were used to validate the model effects. The experimental results show a significant improvement using our approach over state-of-theart models such as GPT-4o and Gemini 2.0 Pro, achieving weighted accuracy of 0.895 in knowledge tasks, transitivity of 0.953 in logical consistency, and superior performance in entity extraction (F1: 0.902) and herbal recommendations (nDCG: 0.981). The framework effectively bridges TCM's historical expertise with modern AI technologies, enhancing diagnostic accuracy, safety, and scalability.

title: Mangrove Above-Ground Biomass Prediction Using Multisource Remote Sensing-Based Ensemble Learning Approach
authors: Yeftanus Antonio, Livia Janice Widiapradja, Nuning Anugrah Putri Namari, Alvian Shanardi Wijaya, Michael Suthirta, Tohru Djunaedi Sato
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: Mangrove ecosystems are critical carbon sinks, yet accurate estimation of above-ground biomass (AGB) remains challenging due to the limited coverage of field-based measurements in dynamic coastal environments. This study develops a multisource ensemble-learning framework for mangrove AGB estimation in Pesawaran District, Indonesia, by integrating optical imagery from Sentinel-2, SAR backscatter from Sentinel-1, GEDI LiDAR–derived structural metrics, and environmental variables within a monthly, time-aligned dataset from 2020 to 2022. Multiple ensemble strategies, including bagging, boosting, and stacking, were evaluated to assess their ability to handle heterogeneous and multiscale predictors. The results show that Random Forest achieved the highest predictive performance (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> = 0.96), demonstrating strong robustness to noisy inputs, sparse GEDI footprints, and temporal variability. Boosting-based and stacking models exhibited lower accuracy and instability, reflecting their sensitivity to data sparsity and predictor misalignment. Structural LiDAR metrics emerged as dominant drivers of biomass prediction, while optical and climatic variables provided complementary non-linear information. Overall, the findings highlight the effectiveness of stable, variance-reducing ensemble methods for multisource mangrove AGB estimation and support their application in blue-carbon accounting, restoration monitoring, and sustainable coastal management.

title: A Lightweight Graph-Based Importance Weighting Scheme for the $\text{LR}\pm$ Model
authors: Dhruv Sharma, Shivika Kaushal, Mohit Singh Adhikari, Bhavnita Oinam, Vikas Kamra, Juhi Singh
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Advanced Computing and Emerging Technologies (ACET)
abstract: Machine learning models are prone to difficulties in real data that has missing values. The <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{LR} \pm$</tex> model is an augmentation of Logistic Regression which addresses this by treating the non-occurrence of one of the features as a state to which missingness is learned. LR posit LR posit closes the gap between discriminative Logistic Regression and generative Naive Bayes models, offering an alternative interpretation of missing data. The typical LR+ model, however, utilizes this information but gives the same consideration to the lack of any feature. In this paper, the author proposes a new enhancement, the Graph-Weighted LR+ (WLR+), which enhances this, applying the missingness indicator of each feature by the structural importance. We obtain this score of importance based on a feature correlation graph, which is a computationally cheap procedure, an attention mechanism which works on data. Using an intense testing of a set of five dynamic, real-world datasets, we show that WLR plus consistently offers an observable performance considerate as compared to the basic LR plus base. Our results indicate that the graph-based method not only leads to better performance of the final linear model but also the lightweight philosophy, which offers the best combination of the efficiency and predictive strength.

title: Test Procedure to Assess the Resilience of Rotating Machine Insulation Systems Against Shear Stress
authors: Lena Elspass, Stephan Schlegel, Hans Bärnklau, Andreas Gündel, Jens Proske, Maria Kosse
abstract: This article introduces a test procedure that allows the evaluation of different GVPI-manufactured insulation systems of rotating machines regarding their ability to withstand thermomechanical stress.

title: Bipolar Silicon Synapse for Bidirectional Spiking Neural Networks
authors: Tamal Chowdhury, Pradip Mandal
publish: 2026 39th International Conference on VLSI Design &amp; 25th International Conference on Embedded Systems (VLSID)
abstract: A recently evolved Bidirectional Spiking Neuron (BSN) gives extra leverage to the spiking neural networks to process bipolar input signals without additional offset. In this research, we propose a Bipolar Silicon Synapse (BSS), compatible with BSN, able to generate either positive or negative post-synaptic current depending on the nature of the spike received from the pre-synaptic BSN. The BSS consists of a novel bipolar voltage-to-current (BVTC) converter that converts the synaptic potential to bipolar synaptic current. Like conventional synapses, the proposed BSS has the synaptic weight update option to enable the learning mechanisms. The bipolar synapse is designed and simulated in TSMC 65 nm technology node with a 1.2 V power supply. The design can generate an Excitatory Post Synaptic Current (EPSC) from 100 pA to 100 nA, depending on the pre-synaptic spike rate and synaptic weight. It also has a tunable accelerated time constant ranging from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$50 \mu ~\mathrm{s}$</tex> to 0.7 ms. The proposed synapse can support a wide range of pre-synaptic spike frequencies from sub-kHz to MHz. The design consumes a silicon area of only <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.0022 ~\text{mm}^{2}$</tex>.

title: Responsible AI from Farm to Finance: A Unified Credit and Carbon Scoring Model for Data-Sparse Rural Economies
authors: Deepa Shukla, Sunil Gupta
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: In rural economies, particularly in developing regions like India, two urgent and interlinked challenges persist: limited access to formal credit due to "thin-file" consumer profiles and insufficient monitoring of soil organic carbon (SOC), critical for climate-resilient agriculture. This paper proposes a novel dual-task machine learning framework that unifies credit scoring and SOC classification using a synthetic dataset comprising 5,000 records simulating rural households. The dataset integrates demographic, behavioral, psychometric, agronomic, and remote sensing features such as NDVI, LST, and rainfall anomalies, inspired by real-world data sources including SoilGrids250m and Sentinel-2 [10], [23].We develop a multi-task learning (MTL) architecture using XGBoost and Random Forests to simultaneously predict creditworthiness and SOC classes. Model explainability is ensured via SHAP values, revealing key cross-domain predictors like mobile transaction frequency, rainfall intensity, and cognitive resilience scores [19], [30]. Experimental results demonstrate high performance in both tasks, achieving an F1-score of 0.82 for credit risk prediction and a macro-average accuracy of 0.78 for SOC classification. Fairness and model bias are evaluated using group fairness indices aligned with ESG and Responsible AI frameworks [18], [29], [33].This study also introduces the Unified Rural Credit & Carbon Dataset [8], an openly accessible synthetic dataset designed for reproducibility and policy experimentation, linked to the author’s Ph.D. thesis on thin-file credit scoring [6], [7]. The proposed framework addresses Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) 1, 13, and 9 by offering scalable, ethical AI-based decision tools for inclusive finance and climate adaptation in data-scarce regions. The convergence of financial inclusion and carbon intelligence provides a new paradigm for ESG-aligned development in rural economies [9], [13], [22].

title: The Effect of the Semiotic Strategy on Reading Comprehension and Reflective Thinking Skills
authors: Suha Hamdi Moawad Salah, Abdoulaye Kaba
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Communication, Networking and Services (ICCNS)
abstract: The main purpose of this research was to investigate the effect of the semiotic strategy on reading comprehension and reflective thinking skills among students. The study was conducted using a quasi-experimental approach. The researchers selected two groups of first-year students in one of the private schools in the United Arab Emirates (UAE). The sample comprised 60 male and female students categorized into experimental and control groups. The strategy was applied to the experimental group, and the control group was taught with the normal method. The findings of the study indicated statistically significant differences between the mean scores of the experimental group and control group on the post-test in developing reading comprehension due to the use of semiotic strategy. Similarly, the results of the study indicated statistically significant differences between the mean scores of the experimental group and the control group developing reflective thinking skills due to the use of semiotic strategy.

title: The Role of Wind Turbines as a Source of Sustainable Energy in the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia
authors: Taghreed F. Altamimi
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Sustainable Energy and Net-Zero Emissions (SENZE)
abstract: This paper examines wind power as an alternative source of sustainable strategic capabilities for Saudi Arabia in the context of its Vision 2030 energy transformation goals. The review juxtaposes global advances in wind technology with environmental- and social-impact evaluations and presents a scenario of favourable conditions for the deployment of largescale wind power in Saudi Arabia. Two major national initiatives are supporting the Kingdom's move towards low-carbon energy: the Dumat Al Jandal wind farm and the creation of a Saudi Wind Atlas. The studies include the development of wind power systems for hot and dusty environments; smart grid with artificial intelligence based forecasting systems, and Arabian environment-matching wind-solar hybridization. There is a double benefit for Saudi Arabia from wind energy of reducing its greenhouse gas emissions and domestic fuel consumption as well as promoting technology advancement, job creation and economic diversification. The review bridges from evidence of technological advances to policy frameworks in order to provide a contemporary examination of wind power's ability to speed up sustainability targets at both national and international levels.

title: ImaginSynth: Bridging the Gap Between 3D Workflow and Machine Learning with Open Source Synthetic Data
authors: Tejas Pravinbhai Patel, Sandeep Shivam, Amit Kumar Padhy, Bharadwaj Vulugunda, Chaitanya Kulkarni, Chandrashekhar Medicherla
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: This paper explores the realm of synthetic data, emphasizing its crucial role in addressing the extensive data requirements of computer vision in deep learning. Synthetic data, created rather than collected, offers a unique solution to democratize access to diverse datasets, which is essential for robust model training. The paper introduces ImaginSynth, an open-source framework developed in Python, designed for creating synthetic data by leveraging the capabilities of Blender, a popular 3D toolset. Emphasizing accessibility and readability, ImaginSynth acts as a bridge between the mature tools of the 3D workflow and machine learning frameworks. The authors make a compelling case for the significance of open-source synthetic data in mitigating issues such as fairness and bias. Additionally, the paper delves into the impact of domain randomization on synthetic training data, conducting experiments to fine-tune a convolutional neural network (CNN) on small synthetic datasets and assessing its performance on real images. The study sheds light on the potential of synthetic data tools, like ImaginSynth, to revolutionize the intersection of 3D workflows and machine learning, contributing to the advancement of computer vision technologies.

title: Integration of LLM and RAG to Enhance Process Mining for Open Library Book Loans
authors: Yassmin Syaharah Juliaman, Angelina Prima Kurniati
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: Academic libraries record every loan, renewal, and return transaction, yet these circulation logs are rarely analyzed systematically for compliance and performance. This study proposes a methodological framework integrating multi-dimensional conformance analysis, semantic query routing, and comprehensive RAG evaluation to enhance process mining interpretability for library practitioners. Loan data from Telkom University’s Open Library (60,176 rows, 2020–2025) are analyzed using PM4Py and the Inductive Miner. Token-based replay yields excellent control-flow quality (fitness of 1.0 and precision of 0.999995) yet reveals critical operational failures: 33.49% late returns, 42.14% duration violations, and extreme outliers where items remain unreturned for more than 1,600 days. These conformance summaries, member and book profiles, and global statistics are converted into textual snippets, embedded with the all-MiniLM-L6-v2 model, and stored in ChromaDB. A Streamlit-based assistant built on Gemini-2.5-Flash answers natural-language questions about process variants, compliance, and member behavior. Evaluation on 25 questions compares a baseline LLM against the proposed RAG pipeline through complementary manual and automatic assessments. Manual scoring showed mean answer quality improving from 0.760 to 1.200 (57.9% increase, Cohen's d= 0.515, p= 0.052), while BERTScore confirmed high semantic similarity (F<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">BERT</inf>= 0.807 to 0.822). RAG metrics demonstrate effective grounding (RAGAS faithfulness= 0.752) with relevancy (0.656). System performance averaged 17.4s per query with zero cost under free-tier API usage. The results demonstrate that integrating process-mining artifacts into an LLM–RAG workflow makes conformance checking more interpretable and operationally useful for library practitioners.

title: AI-Automated Proctoring and Evaluation Model for Digital Education Ecosystems
authors: Vanshika Rastogi, Tushar Singh, Satyam Srivastav, Sreesh Gaur
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Advanced Computing and Emerging Technologies (ACET)
abstract: With the growing adoption of digital learning platforms, there is an increasing need for online examination systems that are both secure and widely accessible. This study outlines and proposes a system that uses natural language processing (NLP) to automatically evaluate both objective questions and descriptive answers. Furthermore, it leverages computer vision models to facilitate real-time monitoring. Experiments conducted on 200 exam sessions across 100 participants achieved a detection accuracy of 93.2%, demonstrating around a 20% decrease in false positives compared to prior systems. The platform is designed to scale effectively for large deployments, providing cloud-based support with reliable latency even during peak usage times. The proposed framework uniquely combines multi-modal AI monitoring with automated assessment and embedded privacy safeguards into one unified platform. The results demonstrate significant improvements in both efficiency and reliability, positioning this system as a viable option for trustworthy and ethical online assessments.

title: KD-MoLoRA: An Efficient Fine-Tuning Approach with MoLoRA and Knowledge Distillation
authors: Shi Cheng, Kaile Su, Xiangrong Tong
abstract: MoLoRA has demonstrated robust performance in parameter-constrained environments. However, it remains inefficient in terms of inference time, memory consumption, and parameter utilization. In this paper, we enhance MoLoRA both internally and externally, proposing a novel fine-tuning framework KD-MoLoRA that combines MoLoRA with knowledge distillation. First, we utilize FFT to obtain a high-performance teacher model and employ MoLoRA to fine-tune the pre-trained student model. To improve MoLoRA internally, we propose a structurally decomposed low-rank perturbation method. Second, we apply knowledge distillation to MoLoRA externally for model compression. Subsequently, to mitigate performance degradation from knowledge distillation, we introduce a cross-task attention alignment mechanism that aligns intermediate layer attention distributions across multi-task, multi-layer semantic paths. Finally, extensive experiments on the GLUE multi-task dataset demonstrate that KD-MoLoRA retains 97.0% of MoLoRA’s performance while reducing memory consumption by 15.8% and inference time by 7.3%.

title: Non-Vacuum Collection Chamber Leakage-Adsorption Mathematical Model and Method for Data Correction of Leakage Detection
authors: Fanghao Chen, Chao Fan, Songsong Fu, Yangyang Zheng, Huibiao Geng
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Science, and Information Processing (AICSIP)
abstract: With the increasing on orbit time of spacecraft, the requirements for leakage rate of spacecraft are becoming more and more strict. When non-vacuum collection chamber method is adopted for leakage detection of large cabin and satellite, the penetration and adsorption of sealing materials on helium should be considered. In this study, when helium accumulation leakage detection of non-vacuum collection chamber of aerospace sealed structure products is carried out, the parameter measurement of sealing performance of nonvacuum collection chamber is realized by constructing helium leakage adsorption equation of collection chamber, the overall leakage rate data of the product is corrected to make the final leakage rate of spacecraft leak detection by non-vacuum collection chamber method more accurate.

title: Spatial Opinion-Driven Community Detection in Social Networks
authors: Leonidas Boutsikaris, Spyros Polykalas
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Science, and Information Processing (AICSIP)
abstract: This paper introduces a novel hybrid algorithm that enhances the Louvain community detection algorithm by integrating spatial mapping based on opinion distributions on social networks. Nodes are represented by individuals' opinions embedded in a geometric space. Minimum Bounding Rectangles (MBRs) encapsulate the spatial distribution of opinions within communities and by incorporating individual opinions the algorithm identifies communities that are both structurally and opinion-connected. Experimental results show that the modified algorithm preserves the structural integrity of networks while improving opinion cohesion within communities and enhancing opinion separation across them. These findings highlight its effectiveness in balancing structural and opinion-based clustering.

title: A Software-Defined Radio Based Prototype for Aircraft Blackbox Detection in Simulated Environments
authors: Aryam Albasata, Ghalya Almesafri, Yasmeen Alameeri, Qassim Nasir, Manar Abu Talib, Homaiza Saud Ahmad, Fatima Dakalbab
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Communication, Networking and Services (ICCNS)
abstract: One of the biggest challenges facing the aviation industry today is the rapid and accurate location of airplane black boxes after accidents. It is difficult to instantly find and identify the signals generated by the Black box's beacons due to the limited capabilities of current technology, especially in different types of environments like land and maritime regions. Retrieving crucial flight data and audio recordings from the cockpit may become more challenging if the black boxes are harder to locate than anticipated. To improve aviation safety, this issue needs to be resolved. The primary objective of our study is to address this issue by developing a versatile handheld gadget utilizing Software-Defined Radio technology. Through the effective integration of GNU Radio Companion, Raspberry Pi, and other components, this method tends to read, process, and analyze signals supplied by airplane black boxes. Our method speeds up search and rescue efforts and increases the likelihood of recovering vital flight data by assisting in the real-time detection and localization of black boxes in simulated environments, with planned extensions for underwater conditions. The versatility and adaptability of Software-Defined Radio technology, which supports the optimization of signal processing capabilities and device performance, are what drive its adoption and integration into a handheld device. Performance criteria including signal detection accuracy, response speed, and device portability are used to evaluate our solution's dependability and effectiveness in real-world circumstances through thorough testing and evaluation.

title: New Perception Approach: Dynamic Manifold-Quantum Entanglement Coupling Model
authors: Haili Xu, Hong Liu
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Science, and Information Processing (AICSIP)
abstract: The intelligent driving perception system faces challenges such as high-resolution image missed detection, multi-sensor Spatio-Temporal asynchrony, degradation in harsh environments, and inconsistent multimodal fusion. This article proposes the “Dynamic Manifold Quantum Entanglement Coupled Control Model”, which constructs an integrated framework including a dynamic manifold embedding layer, a quantum entanglement fusion layer, a neural differential decision layer, and a topology optimization layer through the adaptive curvature of the manifold and the non local correlation mechanism of quantum entanglement. Experiments have shown that this model improves environmental perception accuracy by 46%, dynamic scene trajectory smoothness by 63%, multi vehicle collaborative response speed by 47%, and significantly enhances robustness in extreme environments such as rain and fog compared to traditional BEV+Transformer technology. The research provides a new paradigm of quantum geometric fusion for intelligent driving perception technology, which combines theoretical innovation and engineering application potential.

title: Satellite Positioning Error Compensation Method Based on GBAS and Machine Learning
authors: Ji-Guang Chen, Ji-Ming Du, Meng Zhang, Wen-Hao Chen, Lei Chen
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Science, and Information Processing (AICSIP)
abstract: Errors in satellite positioning systems typically arise from the superposition of multiple interference factors. Single correction models struggle to effectively address positioning deviations in complex noise environments. This paper proposes a robust regression-based error correction method that utilizes real-time position data from Ground-Based Augmentation Systems (GBAS) as reference signals. A linear projection model is constructed to map noisy positioning results to ground-truth coordinates. The optimization problem is solved using the Alternating Direction Method of Multipliers (ADMM), with algorithmic convergence rigorously validated through both theoretical analysis and experimental testing. During simulations, training data were generated by injecting stochastic noise into satellite orbit and clock parameters, while model performance was evaluated under varying noise intensities and regularization parameters. Results demonstrate that the proposed method significantly reduces positioning errors (from an initial 213.2 meters to 4.8 meters) while maintaining strong robustness against noise interference.

title: Modeling and Collaborative Optimization of Wind-Storage-Charging Systems Based on VAE-WGANGP Model
authors: Xiao Qiang, Zhang Shiling, Wu Jungang, Ye Fan
publish: 2025 4th Asia Power and Electrical Technology Conference (APET)
abstract: Driven by the “dual carbon” goals, wind-storagecharging systems face the problem of characteristic mismatch between the randomness of wind power output and the rigid demand of charging loads. Existing complex multi-objective optimization models are time-consuming to solve, while single generative models (such as GAN and VAE) have issues of insufficient scenario diversity or low detail accuracy, and weak connection with storage-charging control. To address the above problems, this paper proposes a VAE-WGAN-GP wind scenario generation model that integrates Variational Autoencoder (VAE) and Wasserstein Generative Adversarial Network with Gradient Penalty (WGAN-GP). Meanwhile, the rule-based collaborative optimization strategy of “charging when there is surplus and discharging when there is shortage” is designed, combined with the energy storage SOC threshold protection mechanism. This strategy prioritizes the absorption of surplus wind power output, secondarily suppresses load fluctuations, and simplifies the traditional multi-variable optimization problem. The results show that the scenarios generated by the VAE-WGAN-GP model have consistency with distribution of real data; the collaborative optimization strategy achieves wind power consumption rate of 54.88%, an average daily energy storage switching frequency of 0 times, and a charging load fluctuation rate of 0.74%, with the time consumption of single scenario generation reduced to the second level; after introducing Gaussian noise, the fluctuations in performance indicators are all less than 0.5%, which verifies the strong anti-interference ability of the model. It is proved that this scheme can simplify the control complexity and provide support for the engineering application of wind-storage-charging systems.

title: Enhancing Cognitive Security through Explainable Fake News Detection
authors: Sarkis Sarkissian, Khouloud Samrout, Modhuparna Manna, Mohamad Nassar
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: Social media platforms have emerged as a powerful vehicle for the rapid dissemination of fake news, which can profoundly affect individuals’ daily lives and incite violence. The widespread nature of this phenomenon underscores the urgent need for effective fake news detection mechanisms. This study explores explainable AI for identifying false information online, with the goal of mitigating its harmful societal impacts. More particularly, many state-of-the-art solutions treat fake news detection systems as black boxes, which can lead to diminished user trust due to the lack of transparency. In this paper, we compare four existing approaches to explainable fake news detection on the Twitter/X platform. Unlike traditional evaluations that focus on metrics such as classification accuracy, we assess these methods through the lens of explainability.

title: A Two-Dimensional First-Principle Model for Bacterial Cellulose Sensors: A Single-Ion Foundation for Mechanoelectric Modeling
authors: Francesca Sapuppo, Luca Patanè, Maria Gabriella Xibilia
publish: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Metrology for Green Technologies, Renewable Energy and Ecological Sustainability (MetroGREENST)
abstract: Bacterial cellulose (BC) functionalized with ionic liquids (ILs) is gaining recognition as a sustainable and biocompatible platform for flexible electroactive sensors. While singleion transport models are grounded in well-established theoretical frameworks, their application to BC-IL systems requires refined spatial characterization to fully capture the underlying transduction mechanisms. This work introduces a two-dimensional finite element model that simulates the mechanoelectric response of BC-based sensors in a cantilever configuration. The model integrates mechanical deformation and single-carrier ionic flux, enabling a physics-informed analysis of charge generation and spatial current profiles. By offering detailed insight into the coupling between local strain and ionic transport, this modeling framework provides a robust foundation for future extensions to dual-ion systems and supports first-principle design strategies in green soft electronics.

title: An Optimization Model for Search Engine Optimization (SEO) Services Across United States of America, Europe, and Australia Using Linear Programming
authors: Kent Paul Delima Tempiatura, Rex Aurelius Robielos, Maela Madel Cahigas
publish: Proceedings of the 2025 9th International Conference on Software and e-Business

publish: Proceedings of the 2025 2nd International Conference on Economic Data Analytics and Artificial Intelligence
