title: Dual Attention-Driven Synergetic Generative Adversarial Networks for Face De-Identification
authors: Bo Shen, Jun Yang, Fei Yang
publish: Proceedings of the 2025 3rd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Systems and Network Security
abstract: None

title: Synthetic Logic: Coherence Without Truth, Inference Without Reasoning, Meaning Without Grounding
authors: Constantine Andoniou
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: An enigmatic situation exists as Generative AI technology produces coherent speech and convincing arguments through surface-level logical behavior yet lacks truth assessment capabilities. The paper develops Synthetic Logic as a conceptual system which explains why large language models create logical-like effects without performing actual logical operations. Synthetic Logic develops from statistical equilibrium, gradient smoothing and reinforcement of recursive patterns, without requiring any need for explicit axioms or truth conditions or inference rules. The system produces output that looks organized, as it applies probabilistic methods to duplicate patterns appearing in structured reasoning processes. The research establishes Synthetic Logic through five core elements: Gradient Coherence, which uses probabilistic connections instead of deductive methods for achieving coherence; Probabilistic Anchoring, which produces text that follows existing distribution patterns to create brief moments of correctness; Recursive Resonance, to repeat patterns instead of performing evaluation tasks; Synthetic Contradictions whereby the system generates text that appears smooth while hiding multiple opposing statements which form; and Surface Validity, a type of apparent correctness which emerges from stylistic plausibility rather than rule-based inference. The research creates a classification system for Synthetic Contradictions before it examines human learning from unconfirmed coherent information and investigates how synthetic environments influence our perception of validity. The increasing use of probabilistic generativity threatens to destroy truth-based logic which will create a post-logical world where coherence separates from truth and reasoning develops into a stylistic practice instead of mental processing. The research shows that this transition includes dangerous elements and promising aspects which support systems that protect human choices during synthetic reasoning operations.

title: Performance of Low Cost Hair Pin Bandpass Filters on Phenolic Paper Substrate for the 2.4 GHz WiFi Band
authors: Akbar Qamar
publish: 2025 Advances in Science and Engineering Technology International Conferences (ASET)
abstract: Minimizing emissions in wireless communication has become increasingly vital as the proliferation of devices strains network efficiency. In this context, cost-effective and low-loss methods are imperative. This paper delves into the exploration of diverse filter response targets, synthesizing and simulating filters to ensure operational efficacy before hardware implementation. The research investigates losses in substrates such as fiberglass and phenolic, probing potential cost-performance trade-offs in detail. Specifically, the focus extends to evaluating phenolic substrate performance at higher frequencies and port symmetry analysis. The objective is to contribute to the optimization of wireless communication systems through meticulous filter design, addressing the pressing need for efficient networks amidst burgeoning device connectivity.

title: CyberGuard: A Deployable Deep Learning Model for Cyber Attacks Threat Detection, and Prevention
authors: Mohamed Wafiq, Ahmed Fahmi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: Cyber threats have been exponentially increased by recent advancements in technology, and the vulnerabilities are being exploited by malicious actors to manipulate, steal, destroy or disclose private information. Web applications, especially the ones with sensitive data, are the main targets, and they are continuously attacked through SQL Injection (SQLi), Cross-Site Scripting (XSS), and Phishing. It is not easy to detect these attacks owing to their constantly changing and unclear nature. The present study makes use of deep learning with Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) and Bidirectional LSTM (BiLSTM) networks, which are trained on three labeled datasets for SQLi, XSS, and phishing attacks, helping the model to grasp complex temporal dependencies and contextual cues. The end objective is to turn this model into a complete security framework that can protect electronic systems holding confidential information.

title: The Collapse of Data: Comparing the Resiliency of Different AI Models to Model Collapse Using AI-Generated Image Data
authors: Kevin Audreylius, Jonathan Lucas Fontana, Tjeng Wawan Cenggoro
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: As the demand for data increases for generative artificial intelligence, many organizations have begun to use generative data to train their models. This increase in demand raises concerns regarding model collapse, a phenomenon where model performance declines when trained on AI-generated data. This study aims to analyze the effect of contamination on three image generation models and observe each model’s resiliency toward model collapse: Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs), Variational Autoencoders (VAEs), and Denoising Diffusion Implicit Models (DDIMs). Each model was trained on a CIFAR-10 dataset with contamination ratios of 0%, 0.2%, 2%, and 20%, and the datasets were contaminated with generated images from a pre-trained Stable Diffusion model. Each model was evaluated under identical conditions and evaluated using Fréchet Inception Distance (FID), Inception Score (IS), Kernel Inception Distance (KID), precision, and recall. Results indicate that synthetic data contamination leads to performance degradation across all models in different ways. GAN exhibited the fastest and most severe degradation. VAE demonstrated high resilience in maintaining color semantics and metric stability, at the cost of reduced diversity. DDIM-based diffusion models maintain object structural coherence but suffer significant color corruption. These findings emphasize the value of controlling synthetic data usage in generative model training to ensure the long-term reliability of models.

title: Frequency-Aware Feature Optimization with MPDIoU for UAV Infrared Detection
authors: Lingling Zheng, Shuiping Zhang, Wenting Zhang, Xueyang Zhong
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Science, and Information Processing (AICSIP)
abstract: The task of UAV object detection of infrared images often struggles to detect and distinguish objects that are small and at different scales, particularly in occluded environments. To address the challenges, we propose an enhanced YOLO11 framework. First, a Wavelet-Transform Convolutional layer is introduced at the end of the backbone network. By cascading wavelet decomposition with smallkernel convolutions, Wavelet Convolutions achieves an expanded receptive field with minimal parameter overhead, significantly enhancing multi-scale and small-target feature extraction capabilities. Second, a Context-Aware Feature Interaction module is designed to guide bidirectional weighted fusion of shallow detail features and deep semantic features through channel attention mechanisms, explicitly modeling target-scene contextual dependencies to improve robustness for occluded objects and clustered small targets. Finally, a loss function incorporating Minimum Point Distance Optimization via MPDIOU is fused to enhance detection performance in complex scenarios. Experiments on the HIT_UAV infrared dataset demonstrate that the proposed method achieves <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{8 1. 9 \%} \mathbf{m A P} @ 0.5$</tex>, surpassing the baseline YOLO11n by 3.2%. These results fully validate the effectiveness and superiority of our approach for infrared UAV target detection in aerial imagery.

title: Experimental Study on Temperature Field of Steel Components Under Electric Fire
authors: Xilong Chen, Xian Gao, Tao Zhu, Weihua Wang
publish: 2025 4th Asia Power and Electrical Technology Conference (APET)
abstract: 36 square steel plate specimens were subjected to temperature field testing under both the ISO-834 standard temperature curve and the electric power heating curve (EHC), respectively, under varying fire protection layer thicknesses. The non-intumescent steel structure fire protection coating used as the fire protection layer with different thicknesses of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0 ~ m m}, \mathbf{2 0 ~ m m}$</tex>, and 30 mm. Steel members have respective section shape coefficients of 131 and 173. The measured data were validated using Abaqus' finite element model. The temperature field of steel beams was calculated and compared with the equivalent thermal conductivity of square steel plates, and the non-intumescent fire protection calculation method in the GB51249-2017 Technical Code for Fire Protection of Steel Structures was validated. The findings demonstrated that: The average temperature of the steel plate reaching 540° C is in good agreement with the standard calculation results, indicating that the method used to calculate the temperature of steel members under the action of equivalent exposure time and non-expansive protective materials is also appropriate for calculating the temperature of steel members under the electric heating curve. There is good agreement between the temperature field of the steel member and the square steel plate with the same section shape coefficient.

title: Model Context Protocol Business Analyst (MCP-BA): A Governed and Explainable Framework for Enterprise Analytics : Operationalizing Identity-Aware Reasoning and Context-Driven Decision Intelligence in Enterprise AI
authors: Tejas Pravinbhai Patel, Sandeep Shivam, Amit Kumar Padhy, Bharadwaj Vulugunda, Chaitanya Kulkarni, Chandrashekhar Medicherla
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have demonstrated remarkable capacity for natural-language analytics, yet their adoption in enterprise decision pipelines remains constrained by ungoverned data access, inconsistent context alignment, and the absence of audit traceability. This paper introduces the Model Context Protocol Business Analyst (MCP-BA)—a governed reasoning framework that operationalizes the Model Context Protocol to connect user intent, organizational data, and analytical tools under identity-aware and policy-controlled execution. The architecture combines a crawler-vector layer for continuous context refresh with an audit-enforced reasoning loop that transforms LLMs into compliant analytical micro-services. A synthetic enterprise evaluation demonstrated substantial gains over RAG and Atlas baselines, improving context precision by 17 points, governance accuracy by 42%, and audit completeness to 100%, while reducing reasoning latency by 29%. The proposed framework establishes a repeatable design pattern for explainable, secure, and high-trust enterprise analytics, bridging the gap between intelligent automation and corporate accountability.

title: Adaptive $t$-Design Dummy-Gate Obfuscation for Cryogenic-Scale Enforcement
authors: Samuel Thomas Punch, Krishnendu Guha
publish: 2026 39th International Conference on VLSI Design &amp; 25th International Conference on Embedded Systems (VLSID)
abstract: Cloud quantum services can reveal circuit structure and timing via scheduler metadata, latency patterns, and co-tenant interference. We introduce Noise-Adaptive Dummy-Gate Obfuscation (NADGO), a scheduling and obfuscation stack that enforces operational privacy for gate-model workloads by applying per-interval limits on observable information leakage. To support confidentiality and fair multi-tenancy, operators need a way to audit compliance at acceptable overheads. NADGO combines: (i) hardware-aware <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$t$</tex>-design padding for structured cover traffic; (ii) particle-filter timing randomisation to mask queue patterns; (iii) CASQUE subcircuit routing across heterogeneous backends; and (iv) a per-interval leakage estimator <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\widehat{\Delta}_{t}$</tex> with locked calibration artefacts and a dual-threshold kill-switch. We prototype on a 4-qubit superconducting tile with cryo-CMOS control and evaluate depth-varied local-random circuits and small QAOA instances. Monitoring runs at a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$6.3 \mu$</tex> s control interval, and we record per-interval decisions in an append-only, hash-chained audit log. Across Monte Carlo (Tier I) and cloud-hardware emulation (Tier II) evaluations, NADGO keeps <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\widehat{\Delta}_{t} \leq \Delta_{\text {budget }}$</tex> in nominal operation (interval-abort <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{SLO} \leq 1 \%$</tex>) and, under attack, yields high AUC separation with concentrated aborts. At matched leakage targets, microbenchmarks indicate lower latency and cryogenic power than static padding, while end-to-end workloads maintain competitive cost envelopes. <sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</sup><sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</sup>This publication has emanated from research conducted with the financial support of Taighde Éireann - Research Ireland under Grant number 13/RC/2077_P2 at CONNECT: the Research Ireland Centre for Future Networks.

title: Cascaded Deadbeat Predictive Control with Harmonic Suppression for PMSM
authors: Xinyu Bai, Yulong Liu
publish: 2025 4th Asia Power and Electrical Technology Conference (APET)
abstract: Under low-switching-frequency conditions, the cascaded dead-beat predictive speed-control (DPSC) structure can preserve the stability of the permanent-magnet synchronous-machine (PMSM) drive; the extended control period and the reduced voltage-modulation resolution help to attenuate undesirable harmonics, resulting in a relative increase in specific stator-current components (e.g. the 5th and 7th harmonics). This article proposes a novel method based on DPSC with specific harmonic suppression. This method predicts and suppresses specific harmonic current. The effectiveness of the proposed method is validated by simulation. It not only reduces the total harmonic distortion (THD) of the machine current, but also alleviates the torque ripple and accelerates the transient response of the system.

title: Research on Optimization Strategy of Transformer Inrush Current Criteria for the Rapid Identification of Evolving Faults
authors: Xiwen Shan, Yarong Guo, Tiezhu Wang, Shicong Ma
publish: 2025 4th Asia Power and Electrical Technology Conference (APET)
abstract: Transformer inter-turn evolving faults exhibit a gradual worsening from minor to severe states. Current protection systems, utilizing second-harmonic inrush current blocking, face delays due to cross-window second-harmonic calculations during fault progression, hindering timely blocking release. This study proposes criteria for releasing inrush current blocking specific to transformer evolving faults. Through a detailed analysis of evolving inter-turn short-circuit fault characteristics, two independent schemes for blocking release are introduced. Multi-scenario simulations confirm their reliability in identifying evolving faults, promptly releasing blocking, and preventing maloperation under diverse inrush conditions. The research aims to expedite blocking release for evolving faults, enhance transformer protection performance, offer technical support for rapid fault clearance, and secure power system stability.

title: PolyEMAC: Polynomial Error Metrics Analysis in Approximate Computing
authors: Mohamed Nadeem, Chandan Kumar Jha, Rolf Drechsler
publish: 2026 39th International Conference on VLSI Design &amp; 25th International Conference on Embedded Systems (VLSID)
abstract: Approximate Computing (AC) is a design paradigm widely used in error-resilient applications. The goal of AC is to gain benefits over exact designs by trading some accuracy for a reduction in power, delay, and area. Varying error metrics are used to assess the degree of approximation. Several formal methods exist for computing these error metrics. However, these methods often fail to provide theoretical bounds on computational resources and are mostly limited to single error metrics. In this work, we address the limitations of the prior methods by introducing PolyEMAC, a novel approach that combines Cutwidth Partitioning and Answer Set Programming (ASP) to compute several error metrics for approximate circuits with constant cutwidth in polynomial time w.r.t. circuit size and the number of approximations. We evaluate several approximate adders in terms of cutwidth, memory consumption, and computation time to highlight the efficacy of the proposed methodology.

title: Auto Computer – Code generation with VLMs to control a Windows GUI
authors: Rashnan Mohamed Shihab, Ibrahim Thorig
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Innovations in Engineering, Science and Technology for Sustainable Development (ICEST)
abstract: This paper introduces the Auto Computer, an agent architecture that uses a planner-reflector-executor framework with the Qwen2.5VL vision language model to autonomously control Windows 11 GUIs through natural language commands via a custom Agent Computer Interface. Manual evaluation on a custom dataset revealed that while the system successfully process natural language instructions and executes relevant actions, it failed to achieve task completion across all tests due to difficulties in recognizing completed tasks and maintaining continuity. Key insights gained were that the most frequently occurring errors were Screenshot Analysis and Grounded Action generation issues. Despite these issues, the model demonstrated strong capabilities in understanding desktop screenshots and identifying flaws in its own actions, leading the authors to conclude that current VLMs show promise for GUI automation, however they require specialized architecture and dedicated modules for improved visual coherency and precise grounding. One such module is the reflection module which has shown to aid in early trajectory correction and is expected to be a key focus in future architectures aiming to complete GUI based automation tasks.

title: NeuroSym-FI: A Neuro-Symbolic LLM-Assisted Framework for Explainable Fault Detection in Power Systems
authors: Hassan Mahmoud, Haitham Mahmoud, Jihad M. Al Ja’Am, Moad Idrissi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: Reliable fault detection remains a cornerstone of safe and efficient power system operation, yet existing artificial intelligence (AI) solutions often face a trade-off between accuracy and interpretability. While deep learning models such as LSTM networks excel in identifying complex fault patterns, their black-box nature limits transparency and trust in real-world deployment. To address this challenge, this paper introduces NeuroSym-FI—a Neuro-Symbolic, Large Language Model (LLM)-assisted Fault Intelligence framework that unites data-driven learning with symbolic reasoning and contextual explanation. The proposed system integrates an LSTM-based temporal model for waveform analysis, a symbolic inference layer for logical validation of detected faults, and a GPT-4o-driven LLM component that generates human-readable, standards-aligned recommendations according to IEC and IEEE protection guidelines. Using the publicly available Electrical Fault Detection and Classification dataset, NeuroSym-FI achieved 98% classification accuracy, a ROC–AUC of 0.995, and a Neuro-Symbolic Consistency Index (NSCI) of 0.93, outperforming traditional Random Forest and KNN-based models. Beyond numerical gains, the framework establishes an interpretable reasoning chain that enhances operator confidence and situational awareness. These findings demonstrate the potential of neuro-symbolic and language-assisted AI to deliver both high performance and explainability in intelligent power grid monitoring and protection systems.

title: Secure Transmission Optimization in RIS-Aided UAV Networks Using Diffusion and Entropy Regularization
authors: Runhui Zhao, Hong Wen, Wenjing Hou, Tang Tao, Yu Pang
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Science, and Information Processing (AICSIP)
abstract: With the evolution of 5G and upcoming 6G communication systems, Physical Layer Security (PLS) has received increasing attention as a complementary mechanism to traditional cryptographic techniques. Reconfigurable Intelligent Surface (RIS), as an emerging wireless physicallayer technology, introduces new degrees of freedom for enhancing PLS by reshaping spatial wireless channel characteristics through controllable reflection elements, thereby improving the secure transmission rate for end users. This paper focuses on the joint optimization of Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) beamforming and RIS phase shift matrices under practical constraints to maximize the secure rate of legitimate users. Although Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) has achieved notable success in UAV-RIS cooperative optimization tasks, Mainstream algorithms are still limited by issues such as unstable training and insufficient policy expressiveness. To address these challenges, this work incorporates diffusion models into the secure communication policy optimization framework. By leveraging the reverse diffusion process to construct a more expressive policy network and estimating policy entropy via Gaussian Mixture Models (GMM), the proposed method effectively balances exploration and exploitation. Experimental results in a UAV-RIS-assisted secure communication scenario demonstrate that the diffusionbased approach outperforms conventional methods in both decision quality and training stability, highlighting its strong potential for application in complex physical-layer security systems.

title: Investigating the Role of Length in Bio-Polymer-Derived Active Deformation Sensors
authors: Giovanna Di Pasquale, Salvatore Graziani, Sara Sadat Hosseini, Antonino Pollicino, Francesca Sapuppo, Carlo Trigona
publish: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Metrology for Green Technologies, Renewable Energy and Ecological Sustainability (MetroGREENST)
abstract: In recent years, growing concerns about the environmental footprint of electronic devices, measurement systems, and sensors have driven the search for sustainable technologies. Polymeric composites have emerged as promising materials for the development of advanced sensing systems. However, many of the proposed solutions remain environmentally unfriendly or fail to provide satisfactory transduction performance. There is therefore an urgent need for new materials and technologies that enable the production of greener, high-performance devices that can be easily recycled or safely disposed of. In previous work, the authors extensively investigated Bacterial cellulose-based composites as viable candidates for the fabrication of sustainable active deformation sensors. In this paper, the role of length in the transduction capabilities of such sensors is investigated.

title: An Optimization Strategy Considering the Multi-Energy Complementarity for Agricultural and Pastoral Parks
authors: Hailong Zhang, Zhen Niu, Linxiang Zhao, Shijun Wang, Xin He, Ying Qi
publish: 2025 4th Asia Power and Electrical Technology Conference (APET)
abstract: China's agricultural and pastoral complexes are rich in biomass resources-such as crop residues and livestock waste-but the relatively low penetration of distributed generation systems constrains the efficiency of energy utilization. In response, this study proposes a coordinated optimization framework for agricultural and pastoral parks based on multi-energy complementarity. The approach begins by quantitatively describing the dynamic conversion of biomass energy across the entire supply chain, including collection, processing, and power generation. Based on these characteristics, a supply load model is constructed that captures the coupling of electrical and thermal energy flows and incorporates flexible loads participating in demand response. On this foundation, a multi-energy collaborative optimization model is developed with the aim of minimizing total operating costs, thereby enabling the coordinated scheduling of biomass, electrical, and thermal energy. Finally, comparative case studies under typical operating conditions are conducted. Simulation results indicate that, relative to conventional operation, the proposed strategy enhances operating economy by 12.6% through more effective use of flexible resources and strengthened multi-energy coordination.

title: Contextual Stress Detection in Social Media Using Transformer-Based Language Models
authors: Gaurav Kr. Rajput, Dampy Singh, Preeti Rani, Vineet Saxena, Jyoti Ranjan, Ajay Chakravarty
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Advanced Computing and Emerging Technologies (ACET)
abstract: Stress has increasingly become a global mental health issue, though frequently casual or otherwise mental health-related stress could remain concealed to health professional due towards reliance on self-reported mental health (e.g., self-report or clinical interviewing) that may mitigate providing an accurate assessment or evaluation of users' self-reported mental health. As users continue to use social media to share everything that is on their minds as it relates to thoughts, feelings, or experiences, we have the opportunity to detect stress signals contained within user-generated content. This paper proposes a semiautomated approach to detecting stress in social media posts derived from their text using machine learning. More specifically, we used two publicly available datasets, the Dreaddit dataset which is a Reddit-based stress-labeled dataset, and the CLPsych 2015 Twitter dataset which contains tweets from users who disclosed mental health concerns in the content of their tweets. In our proposed system we perform a complete preprocessing of the text, we then encode the input text using a Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers (BERT) to extract the contextual semantics. A fine-tuned BERT model was used to classify the posts into either stressed or non-stressed. Prediction performance was evaluated against traditional machine learning classifiers (Support Vector Machine (SVM), Logistic Regression, Random Forest, and XGBoost). BERT performed better than all classifiers providing a maximum F1-score of 0.89, which is a substantial improvement over the baseline models.The viability and efficiency of transformer-based models for stress detection in social media settings are demonstrated by this study

title: Integrated Planning and Optimal Dispatch of Source-Grid-Load-Storage Systems with Renewable Energy Considering Policy Constraints
authors: Yong Sun, Haotian Zhao, Zhenyuan Li, Baoju Li, Dexin Li, Guanqun Zhuang
publish: 2025 4th Asia Power and Electrical Technology Conference (APET)
abstract: With the rapid penetration of renewable energy sources, the source-grid-load-storage (SGLS) system has become a key solution to address the intermittency and volatility of RESs while promoting localized consumption. Existing planning research overlooks critical policy constraints. In this paper, a SGLS integrated planning and optimal dispatch under policy constraint is proposed. A mixed-integer linear programming (MILP) model is proposed to minimize total costs, incorporating operational and policy constraints. A case study for a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">${4 8 0 \sim M W}$</tex> load validates the model, the scheme meets all policies and minimizes the total planning and operation costs. Sensitivity analysis shows longer storage scheduling cycles and higher load adjustability reduce storage capacity.

title: Multi-Class Text Sentiment Analysis Using Gated Recurrent Unit (GRU)
authors: Deepika Yadav, Ramveer Singh, Jyoti Rani, Atul Kumar Singh, Shishu Pal Singh, Mandeep Singh
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Advanced Computing and Emerging Technologies (ACET)
abstract: With the rapid expansion of web-based applications, the growth rate of the number of reviews or comments is also exponentially increasing. Analyzing the attitudes, behavior, and emotions behind these reviews and comments provides valuable insights for running an effective business. However, it is a challenging task for researchers to analyze or extract users' attitudes manually from the massive amounts of social media data. The digitalized text sentiment analysis provides an electronic-based, fast, efficient, and automatic system that effectively identifies or classifies the user's reviews, opinions, and sentiments based on user feedback. The existing literature reviews have classified multi-class text sentiments by using machine learning classifiers and covered a limited number of sentiment datasets. This research paper presents a digitized, automatic system for multi-class text sentiment analysis, classifying sentiments using the Gated Recurrent Unit (GRU), an advanced deep learning classifier, and experiments on a large dataset. The research was conducted on a well-known Flipkart product dataset, and their textual sentiments are classified into three distinct categories: ‘positive, negative and neutral. Efficiency of available Flipkart dataset was evaluated using up to five epoch values, and the training loss of model decreased with size value of epoch numbers, ultimately reaching a value of 0.7827.

title: A Secure Quantum Group Blind Signature Based on Entanglement Swapping
authors: Kai Xing, Rongbo Lu, Juxiu Zhong, Sihai Liu
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Science, and Information Processing (AICSIP)
abstract: This paper introduces a secure quantum group blind signature (QGBS) based on entanglement swapping. The scheme designed in this article ensures group blind signature and unconditional security by using quantum key distribution (QKD), quantum encryption algorithm, XOR operation and entanglement swapping. The results show that the protocol is not only completely blind, unforgeability, and non-repudiation, but also capable of resisting eavesdropper attacks. In addition, the analysis of the scheme's resistance to various attacks reveals that it demonstrates both efficiency and security.

title: NIR-DST: Noise-Immune Radiation Hardened Dual-Modal Domino-Schmitt Architecture
authors: Het Patel, Lakshya Singhal, Sai Ankit Sahoo, Anmol Aggarwal, Govind Prasad
publish: 2026 39th International Conference on VLSI Design &amp; 25th International Conference on Embedded Systems (VLSID)
abstract: This paper presents a Noise-Immune Radiation Hardened Dual-Mode Domino-Schmitt Trigger (NIR-DST), combining the high speed of domino logic with the robust hysteresis of a Schmitt trigger through a novel two-transistor NIR cell topology to minimize power, area, and delay while enhancing noise immunity. Simulated in Cadence Virtuoso using UMC 45 nm technology (1 V, 25°C), the NIR-DST reduces dynamic power by over 57% and propagation delay by nearly 40% compared to a baseline Domino Schmitt Trigger (DST). When benchmarked against Ming-Bo Lin's work, dynamic power reduces by 73% and propagation delay by 42%. The design achieves a propagation delay of less than 70 ps, a 294 mV hysteresis window, and a 150% increase in critical charge over Lin's design. The NIR-DST prioritizes speed, resulting in a moderately lower noise margin than the baseline. Adjusting parameters such as Vth or <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$Q_{\text{crit}}$</tex> can improve noise tolerance, but such changes usually trade off speed and power. Despite this trade-off, the NIR-DST achieves an 84% lower power-delay product and a 91% lower energy-delay product than Lin's design, establishing it as a superior solution for ultra-low-power, high-reliability mixed-signal applications in radiation-prone environments.

title: Hierarchical Reinforcement Learning for Detecting Safety and Reliability Vulnerabilities in Large Language Model-Assisted Healthcare Systems
authors: Abigail Akosua Addobea, Theophilus Siameh, Isaac Obiri Amankona, Jamal. N. A. Hassan, Zhang Miao
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Science, and Information Processing (AICSIP)
abstract: The integration of large language models into the telemedicine industry has revolutionized digital health by promoting effective clinical decision-making and patient interactions. However, the deployment of LLMS in healthcare imposes critical risks to health data. Moreover, the existing standards of vulnerability detection lack the practical approach to dynamic and enhanced interactions in context-dependent healthcare systems. To overcome these potential hurdles, this paper presents a Hierarchical Reinforcement Learning (HRL) framework explicitly designed to detect and mitigate vulnerabilities in LLM-assisted healthcare applications. The proposed HRL model decomposes clinical interactions into hierarchical tasks, enabling efficient modeling of complex, multi-step clinical reasoning and nuanced conversational dynamics. Our approach integrates safety-aware reward engineering and policy trajectory analysis to systematically identify risky LLM behaviors. Experimental validation using realistic clinical scenarios demonstrates that our HRLbased approach significantly outperforms conventional methods, providing robust detection of safety and reliability vulnerabilities in healthcare LLM systems.

title: LES-Quality Urban Wind and NFZ Mapping via Multi-Fidelity Learning
authors: Jinan Charafeddine, Aminallah Rabia, Hani Hamdan, Samir Yahiaoui
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: Reliable UAV operation in dense urban environments requires fast yet accurate estimation of building-induced turbulence. Conventional RANS/URANS simulations underpredict critical shear and TI/TKE peaks, whereas LES delivers high fidelity but remains computationally impractical for real-time use. This work introduces a data-driven, deep-learning multi-fidelity surrogate that upgrades low-cost CFD predictions into LES-quality flow fields and turbulence indicators relevant for NFZ assessment. The model learns cross-fidelity corrections from heterogeneous datasets spanning numerical simulations, wind-tunnel experiments, field measurements, and laboratory setups. Across all conditions, the proposed surrogate substantially improves turbulence estimation and hazard classification while operating in real time. These results demonstrate the potential of learning-based multi-fidelity approaches for reliable, turbulence-aware UAV guidance in complex urban environments.

title: ADMM-Based Collaborative Optimization Framework for Path Planning of Autonomous Trucks in Unstructured Road Environments
authors: Zheng Li, Peng Chen, Zhifa Chen, Bin Zhou, Guizhen Yu, Xinkai Wu
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Science, and Information Processing (AICSIP)
abstract: Mining transportation roads represent a typical unstructured environment characterized by irregular boundaries and the absence of standardized lane markings. These conditions pose significant challenges for bidirectional unmanned mining truck path planning, where conventional planning methods often fail to account for interactions between opposing paths, leading to insufficient safety margins and operational inefficiencies. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a bidirectional cooperative path planning framework based on the Alternating Direction Method of Multipliers (ADMM). The framework establishes an integrated collaborative optimization model by incorporating bidirectional safety distance constraints and kinematics constraints derived from actual mining truck steering characteristics. This model is then decomposed into alternating optimization subproblems for individual unidirectional paths. Simulation experiments validate the effectiveness of the proposed method in complex real-world mining road scenarios, providing a novel solution for bidirectional traffic management in open-pit mining unmanned transportation systems.

title: A RopeRCFusion: A Roadside Perception Radarcamera Feature Fusion Method in Bird's-Eye-View
authors: Ming Liu, Zhaoyang Zhang, Zhiji Deng, Yongjun Fang, Han Wu, Qiankun Li, Jun Yin
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Science, and Information Processing (AICSIP)
abstract: This paper introduces a multi-sensor sensing method in roadside scene. Radar and image sensors are mainly used to detect targets in traffic scenes. The image sensor can identify the appearance information such as target type, license plate, car color, etc., But the image sensor is not sensitive to the position and speed. Therefore, using a single camera sensor cannot accurately perceive or determine behavioural information related to spatial position and velocity. For the radar sensor, detecting position and speed is his advantage, and based on the spatial position and speed information, further judge behaviours, such as over speeding, under speeding, and S-curve driving. However, the radar sensor cannot recognize the target. Based on the analysis, combining these two complementary sensors is valid and necessary for solving the problem. In the research and application of radar and image sensor fusion, the commonly used fusion methods are feature-level fusion and target-level fusion. Most of the feature-level fusion methods are used in autonomous driving. Due to the difference between the vehicle-side and the roadside scene, most of the roadside is still using the target-level fusion method. This paper focuses on the feature-level fusion method of the roadside. A feature-level radar image fusion method is proposed for data in traffic scenes. On the basis of image perception, this method uses radar to improve the accuracy of target detection and detection accuracy. Finally, the method is compared with the image method on our traffic scene dataset to further illustrate the effectiveness of the method.

title: Design and Development of an Autonomous Self-Driving Robot Using Deep Learning Technique
authors: Abdel Ilah N. Alshbatat, Bassam Mahasneh, Moath Awawdeh
publish: 2025 Advances in Science and Engineering Technology International Conferences (ASET)
abstract: Self-driving autonomous robots using deep learning techniques have expanded widely over the last few years. Nowadays, robots are everywhere, from factories to hospitals, and from agriculture to education. Despite the numerous advantages of the autonomous self-driving robots, the main challenge facing researchers today is to create an advanced robotic system that endows the robot with the ability to perceive its environment and work alongside humans. Consequently, in this research, we leverage the power of Convolutional Neural Network (CNN), along with advanced hardware to predict the steering angle of the robot based on the provided images. For this purpose, a Logitech <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0 8 0}$</tex> p pro stream webcam is used in conjunction with the NVIDIA Jetson Xavier NX Developer Kit to capture images from the robot's environment and map them into steering commands. In addition, we augmented the system with the necessary modules to drive a robot without any human intervention. To validate the whole system, we present an autonomous mobile robot platform that consists of three systems: the steering system, the driving system, and the control system. The experimental results demonstrate that the proposed framework can drive the robot autonomously in a highly reliable and robust manner with a navigation success rate of 95%.

title: Automated Multi-Site Federated Learning Infrastructure with Terraform-Based IaC on AWS
authors: Abhirup Mazumder, Vinoth Punniyamoorthy, Sumit Saha, Ram Sekhar Bodala, Suhas Malempati, Lokesh Butra, Shiva Kumar Reddy Carimireddy, Aswathnarayan Muthukrishnan Kirubakaran
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: Federated learning enables hospitals to collaboratively train machine learning models without transferring sensitive patient data, but deploying multi-site FL infrastructure remains slow, complex, and error-prone due to heterogeneous systems, strict privacy regulations, and extensive security requirements. This work presents FedMed-IaC, an automated Infrastructure-as-Code framework that uses Terraform and AWS to provision secure, reproducible, and HIPAA-aligned FL environments across diverse hospital networks. The framework automates end-to-end tasks including compliance validation, secure connectivity, FL server orchestration, client onboarding, and resource scaling. In a four-hospital evaluation on chest X-ray pneumonia classification, FedMed-IaC reduces deployment time by up to 94%, eliminates 89% of configuration errors, and achieves full verification of targeted HIPAA controls. Federated models trained using the framework improve accuracy by 23% over single-hospital training and reach more than 98% of centralized performance while maintaining data locality. FedMed-IaC further demonstrates clean scalability to more than ten institutions. By encoding security and operational workflows as code, the framework significantly lowers the engineering burden of healthcare FL and enables broader adoption of privacy-preserving clinical collaboration.

title: An Innovative Electromagnetic System for Simulating Engineering Failures Utilizing 3D Printing Techniques
authors: Muthanna Aziz, Waleed Ahmed, Rihab Hamza
publish: 2025 Advances in Science and Engineering Technology International Conferences (ASET)
abstract: Mechanical failure is a critical subject in engineering education; however, traditional destructive tests are costly, time consuming, and difficult to repeat with different failure loads. They often require significant resources and expose students to risks, limiting their opportunity to fully understand diverse failure mechanisms. To overcome these limitations, this work presents an innovative, smart, and practical experimental system that students can easily use as a self-learning pedagogy to demonstrate and practice the mechanical failure of structures and components. The system utilizes smart modules manufactured using additive manufacturing technology and designed with predefined failure points. Controlled through a microcontroller unit (MCU) and electromagnetic actuation, the modules can be triggered to fail at specific forces, imitating the behavior of actual materials under test. A graphical user interface enables students to configure parameters and observe different failure scenarios. The modules can be reassembled and reused to demonstrate various mechanical tests. This approach provides an economical and environmentally friendly alternative to destructive testing, allowing students to practice both theoretical calculations and practical demonstrations of failure mechanisms. By enabling safe, repeatable, and engaging experimentation, the system enhances engineering education and promotes deeper understanding of mechanical failure behavior.

title: Sustainable Ethyl Cellulose-Based Raman Sensors Incorporating Ag-Coated MgFe Hydrotalcites Nanoplatelets for Cypermethrin Detection
authors: Elena Palmieri, Luca Maiolo, Luca Montaina, Ivano Lucarini, Tamara Posati, Valentina Mussi, Fabio Leccese, Francesco Maita
publish: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Metrology for Green Technologies, Renewable Energy and Ecological Sustainability (MetroGREENST)
abstract: In precision agriculture and environmental monitoring, rapid and label-free sensing technologies are essential. Moreover, the widespread deployment of sensor networks in these fields requires sustainable solutions for both device fabrication and end-of-life disposal. Cellulose-based Raman sensors offer a promising approach to address these challenges. In this work, we present a biodegradable Raman sensing platform based on ethyl cellulose substrates functionalized with silver-coated MgFe hydrotalcite nanoplatelets. Using this device, we demonstrate the detection of cypermethrin, a widely used synthetic pyrethroid insecticide in large-scale agricultural applications.

title: Uncertainty Quantification Method for UAV Intelligent Decision-Making Algorithms Based on KL Divergence
authors: Yiyi Yin, Yanhuan Jiang, Xin Wang, Xuetao Zhang, Lu Xin, Pengfei Du
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Science, and Information Processing (AICSIP)
abstract: To address the issues of high model complexity and unclear effective boundaries of algorithms in the context of reinforcement learning algorithms (used as decision-making methods), a method based on Kullback-Leibler (KL) divergence and Monte Carlo theory is proposed to quantify the output uncertainty of the actor network in the Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) algorithm. Taking the AI pilot system as the research object, an uncertainty quantification model based on the characteristic of KL divergence in measuring distribution distances is established. Considering the source, distribution and magnitude of the uncertainty of the input state and the sensing equipment, the Monte Carlo simulation method is used to generate the random state quantity under the constraint of the probability density function of each input quantity. By comparing the distribution difference of output action before and after disturbance injection, the best estimate and standard uncertainty of quantization result are obtained, and the uncertainty of reinforcement learning model is quantified. Simulation results show that this method can effectively measure the random uncertainty of PPO algorithm.

title: Cascaded ESO-Based Sliding Mode Control Under Noise and Matched Disturbances for Mobile Robot
authors: Mahmoud Elhaj, Raouf Fareh, Sofiane Khadraoui, Baraa Masoud, Omar Gad
publish: 2025 Advances in Science and Engineering Technology International Conferences (ASET)
abstract: In this paper, a robust control approach for a differential drive mobile robot using Cascaded Extended State Observer Sliding Mode Control (CESO-SMC) is presented. The main idea is to enhance robustness through sliding mode control and combine it with a multistage extended state observer for disturbance rejection and noise attenuation. Each Cascaded Extended State Observer (CESO) stage refines disturbance estimation with progressively decreasing bandwidth. Moreover, an adaptive bandwidth mechanism using a gradient-based adaptive law is introduced into the inner stages of the observer to dynamically tune its response. A proportional-integral sliding surface with an exponential reaching law is used to improve tracking accuracy. Stability analysis of the closed-loop system is conducted under bounded disturbance estimation error. Simulation results under varying disturbances show reduced tracking error compared to the Extended State Observer Sliding Mode Control (ESO-SMC) and the standard Active Disturbance Rejection Control (ADRC). Furthermore, the results under measurement noise show reduced signal variation.

title: An Underwater Target Localization Method Utilizing Hydroacoustic Communication Signals with a Towed Cylindrical Array
authors: Yongshou Yang, Wenjing Cao, Jiawang Jing, Qing Hu, Weixin Kong, Guangbiao Chen, Zhongkang Wang
abstract: Underwater target localization using a towed cylindrical array and underwater acoustic communication signals is well-suited for large-area underwater searches, offering high positioning accuracy, operational flexibility, and efficiency. To address the amplitude step artifacts in long target echo signals caused by short-segment time gain control, a signal amplitude compensation method is developed to achieve distortion-free correction. To overcome the limited angular resolution of conventional beamforming, a sliding cylindrical-array beamforming approach based on minimum variance distortionless response (MVDR) is proposed, with the number of array elements involved in each beamforming operation optimized for performance. In addition, to reduce the large variance in target echo arrival-time estimation, a range estimation method is introduced that exploits the fixed gaps inherent in underwater acoustic communication signals, combining energy-gradient detection with segmented matched filtering. Comparative lake and sea trials verify that the proposed target position estimation method delivers high accuracy, strong robustness, and real-time capability.

title: Stability Analysis of Power-Electronics-Dominated Grids Using Scaled Relative Graphs
authors: Eder Baron-Prada, Adolfo Anta, Florian Dörfler
abstract: This paper presents a novel approach to stability analysis for grid-connected converters utilizing Scaled Relative Graphs (SRG). Our method effectively decouples grid and converter dynamics, thereby establishing a comprehensive and efficient framework for evaluating closed-loop stability. Our analysis accommodates both linear and non-linear loads, enhancing its practical applicability. Furthermore, we demonstrate that our stability assessment remains unaffected by angular variations resulting from dq-frame transformations, significantly increasing the method's robustness and versatility. The effectiveness of our approach is validated in several simulation case studies, which illustrate its broad applicability in modern power systems.

title: News from Japan: Development of Low-Dielectric-Constant Insulating Resins for 5G/6G Communication
authors: Yoshimichi Ohki
abstract: Dielectric materials play an important role not only in power and electrical equipment, but also in electronic and communication equipment. One type of communication equipment, now rarely used at least for long-distance applications but important until recently, is coaxial cable. Foamed and expanded polyethylene was used as the insulating material for this coaxial cable. Because the relative dielectric constant or permittivity of air is approximately 1.0, the lowest of all substances, the dielectric constant can be kept low by foaming the polymeric insulating material. As electromagnetism teaches us, electric energy or information, whether electricity or electromagnetic waves, is transmitted not through a conductor but through the conductor's insulation. The square root of the above relative dielectric constant is the refractive index, or the inverse ratio of the velocity of light or electromagnetic waves in a material to the velocity in a vacuum. Therefore, the application of insulating resins with a low dielectric constant to corresponding equipment is important to increase the speed of information transmission and communication. Toward the realization of a society with high-speed 5G/6G communications, insulating materials used in laminates and other materials are required to have a lower dielectric constant and dissipation factor to reduce transmission loss and increase transmission speed in the high-frequency band. For the these reasons, many resin manufacturers are actively working on developing insulating resins with a low dielectric constant for 5G/6G communications.

title: Thermal Analysis of EHV XLPE Cable Systems Using FEM During Pre-Qualification Assessment
authors: Arunjothi R, Priyatosh Mahish, Himanshu Misra, Bhavani Shanker T, Meena K P
abstract: The pre-qualification (PQ) assessment of Extra High Voltage (EHV) Cross-linked polyethylene (XLPE) cable systems requires a year-long voltage application with 180 heating cycles as per IEC 62067. A key challenge in this assessment is maintaining the conductor temperature at 90-95<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$^{\circ }$</tex-math></inline-formula>C for the final two hours of every heating cycle, irrespective of installation and ambient conditions. As the test cable is energised, the conductor temperature is monitored in a parallel reference cable. Matching the sheath temperature in both reference and assessment cables ensures equivalent conductor temperature. The use of the physical reference cable increases the cost, test complexity, and instrumentation requirements due to prolong evaluation period. This paper proposes a MMA (Method of Moving Asymptotes) optimisation-based Finite Element Method (FEM) simulation approach to design a virtual reference EHV cable that replaces the physical reference cable. The virtual cable is used to find the ampacity range of EHV cables for different installations. In addition, a common current range that satisfies the IEC 62067 thermal limit across the installations is determined. The proposed method is validated against EHV PQ experimental data with three installations (under soil, cable-duct, and under the soil with High Density Polyethylene (HDPE) pipe). The study also identifies under soil HDPE as a critical installation requiring crucial temperature monitoring.

title: CIRCUS: A Causal Intervention-Based Framework for Enhancing Counterfactual Fairness in Trained Classifiers_supp1-3670269.pdf
authors: Yuhui Deng
abstract: Ensuring model fairness for preventing potential biases based on any sensitive attribute is crucial for the societal acceptance of artificial intelligence in critical applications. Among various fairness concepts, counterfactual fairness has gained prominence as it is grounded in causal inference. This concept requires that an individual’s prediction in the original world remains consistent with that in the counterfactual world where the sensitive feature value is modified. In this article, we aim to mitigate counterfactual biases of the model through causal intervention. Specifically, we first achieve effective causal intervention and counterfactual generation by proposing the causal inference tabular generative adversarial network (CITGAN) architecture. Unlike prior approaches based on variational autoencoders (VAEs) that inherently lack structural causal model (SCM) fidelity due to simultaneous generation, CITGAN strictly enforces causal consistency via an end-to-end topological generation process. By integrating exogenous variable inference with sequential generation, CITGAN ensures that functional dependencies are structurally preserved. Building on CITGAN, we propose the CIRCUS framework, a causal intervention-based framework designed to intuitively enhance the counterfactual fairness in trained classifiers. CIRCUS generates counterfactually discriminatory samples (CDSs) via causal intervention, guided by gradients and feature contributions, and subsequently applies bias correction preprocessing to their labels for classifier retraining. Experimental results demonstrate that CIRCUS effectively enhances counterfactual fairness while maintaining robust classification performance. Specifically, for the deep neural network (DNN) model, the <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\text {MMD}_{\text {L}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> and <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\text {MMD}_{\text {K}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> values are reduced by averages of 39.7% and 40.4%, respectively, compared with the second-best result. For the residual network (ResNet) model, these reductions amount to 56.7% and 54.5%, respectively.

title: MMFashion+: Multimodal Federated Learning for Personalized Clothing Recommendation
authors: Xinhui Yu, Peizhi Yan, Sophie Liu, Chunhua Wu
abstract: Federated clothing recommendation aims to suggest clothing items to customers (users) based on their past purchase behaviors in a privacy-preserving manner, enhancing user experience while safeguarding privacy. Current federated recommendation systems face two main challenges. The first is limited information exploration. Existing methods mainly rely on item ID-based embeddings and ignore the assistance of multimodal information about items. The second challenge is data heterogeneity, caused by users perceiving the same clothing item differently, which leads to performance degradation. We therefore propose a multimodal federated learning framework for personalized clothing recommendation (MMFashion+). To address the first challenge, we design two branches to capture user preferences for clothing design and price, respectively. The design preference branch leverages image and text modalities, while the price preference branch incorporates item price information. To better model user design preferences, we introduce a threshold-based regularization loss to push the learned embeddings of user preferences and dislikes apart. To address the second challenge, we introduce personalized image and text adapters to guide the local model in capturing item attributes that align with users’ interests. This approach enables user-specific item representations that effectively capture individual design preferences. To evaluate the effectiveness of MMFashion+, we construct four datasets by extracting four clothing types (i.e., long sleeves, pants, dresses, and outerwear) from a real-world clothing dataset released by a fashion retail company. Experimental results demonstrate that MMFashion+ consistently outperforms existing methods, confirming its effectiveness in addressing the two key challenges in federated clothing recommendation.

title: HIMO: Cross-Arbitrary-Modality Image Invariant Feature Transform with Hierarchical Intrinsic Major Orientation
authors: Chenzhong Gao, Wei Li, Desheng Weng, Ran Tao, Xiang-Gen Xia, Qian Du
publish: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
abstract: Invariant feature extraction is a critical challenge in intelligent image processing, particularly with the rapid advancement of multi-source/modal imaging. Cross-modal matching has attracted considerable attention, yet current studies primarily focus on targeted modalities rather than realizing a general approach. In this paper, cross-arbitrary-modal image invariant feature extraction and matching is studied. Inspired by human vision, a purely handcrafted invariant feature transform is proposed for universal cross-modal image matching, named Hierarchical Intrinsic Major Orientation (HIMO). Based on orientation information, a full-chain non-data-driven algorithm is designed that hinges on an Intrinsic Major Orientation (IMO) extraction. The HIMO incorporates a novel keypoint detector utilizing Difference-of-Feature Suppression (DoFS), a Polar-Pyramid descriptor (PolarP), and a Cascaded Dynamic Multi-scale Strategy (CDMS) to effectively address common challenges such as intensity distortion, rotation, scale differences, geometric deformation, and image noise. To validate the proposed method, two massive cross-modal datasets-General Cross-modal Zone (GCZ) and Wide-area Diverse Sources (WDS)-are introduced, alongside two practical evaluation metrics. Comprehensive experiments compared with 10 traditional and 15 deep-learning state-of-the-art algorithms on 5 datasets fully demonstrate that the proposed HIMO achieves superior performance in terms of robustness, stability, and generalization across diverse imaging conditions.

title: Seamless Fault-Tolerant Operation of Two-Transformer Full-Bridge Resonant PWM Converter With Built-In Circuit Redundancy
authors: Anyeol Jung, Ji-Won Lee, Minsung Kim
abstract: Conventional full-bridge resonant pulse-width-modulated (PWM) dc/dc converter employing two transformers can be used for 48-V level low-voltage dc/dc converter (LDC) applications. However, when one of the secondary-side legs suffers from open/short circuit problems, the semiconductor devices can be destroyed by short-circuit current or the transformer can be saturated. To overcome this problem, this article proposes a reconfigurable resonant PWM full-bridge converter that has two split capacitors and one bidirectional switch. The main feature of this converter is that it can transfer the half of the rated power seamlessly under open/short circuit problems without using the extra feedforward control duty while not affecting the output voltage. Also, this power transfer does not exceed the predetermined rated power of either of transformers. This trait enables the use of compact-size transformers without having transformer saturation problem. A 1-kW rated prototype that converts 400-V input to 52-V output is designed and tested to validate the concept of reliable operation.

title: Consensus Control for PDE-ODE MASs with Multi Delays: A Dual-Mode Adaptive Event-Triggered Strategy and Novel Stability Analysis Criterion
authors: Xiaofei Xing, Zhi Liu, C. L. Philip Chen, Yun Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: Consensus in fractional-order multi-agent systems (MASs) with time delays has attracted considerable research interest in recent years. Numerous existing findings rely heavily on the stability theory of fractional-order delay systems. In these findings, however, certain stability rates remain inestimable, while others lack rigorous estimation. This paper develops enhanced Mittag-Leffler stability criterion for fractional-order systems incorporating infinite distributed and discrete delays, which provides precise characterizations of convergence rates and establish a pioneering framework for fractional-order stability analysis. Moreover, this work proposes a novel dual-mode adaptive event-triggered strategy (DMAETS) to optimize limited communication resources and enhance network utilization efficiency. The DMAETS framework features time-varying event-triggering thresholds and adaptive control gains that dynamically adjust based on real-time system states. Through innovative DMAETS-based controller designs (internal/boundary configurations) and the proposed stability criterion, the fractional-order coupled PDE-ODE MASs (FCPOMASs) achieve consensus, with corresponding consensus conditions derived as linear matrix inequalities (LMIs). Finally, two simulation examples are presented to illustrate the validity of the theoretical analysis and the feasibility of the control schemes.

title: Integrated Trajectory Planning and Cooperative Adaptive Cruise Control for Platoon Merging Con-sidering Road Friction
authors: Ye Yao, Zhiquan Qi, Ruixin Sun, Mingwei Zhang
abstract: Connected autonomous vehicle technology significantly enhances the operation and efficiency of merging behaviors. This paper proposes a hierarchical scheme for vehicle platoon to achieve trajectory planning and cooperative adaptive cruise control in adjacent parallel lane merging scenarios. The scheme be gins with a tactical layer, where yield trajectories for platoon vehicles are planned using Pontryagin's Minimum Principle, incorporating a trajectory adjustment method that considers road friction (RF) and riding comfort (RC). A two-phase approach is developed for merging vehicles, planning both longitudinal and lateral motion while taking into account RF and RC. At the planning layer, a multi-objective model is formulated to achieve integrated optimization of merging sequence and trajectory planning, with the aim of reducing merging duration and mitigating the disruption to the platoon and traffic flow. An iterative solution algorithm based on gradient descend method is presented to obtain optimal decision variables, which guide the determination of the desired trajectory according to the tactical layer. At control layer, an adaptive backstepping-based cooperative adaptive cruise control (CACC) strategy is proposed to maintain platoon stability while considering time delay, input saturation and uncertainties in dynamic parameters of vehicles related to road friction. Internal stability and string stability are analyzed theoretically. A trajectory tracking method is also employed to guide vehicles along desired trajectory, with a state machine facilitating coordination between CACC strategy and the trajectory tracking method. Numerical simulations and Hard-in-the-Loop (HIL) experiments verify the effectiveness of the proposed hierarchical scheme, demonstrating reduced merging duration, mitigated disruption to both the traffic flow and the platoon, and robust performance under varying road friction conditions.

title: Advances in Bifacial Photovoltaic Technology: A Short Review from 2000 to 2025
authors: Michael Tesfaye, Mihreteab Kassa, Yohannes Abate, Koket Firissa, Biruh Chalie, Robenus Dame, Yacouba Moumouni, Ahmad Abdelhadi
publish: 2025 Advances in Science and Engineering Technology International Conferences (ASET)
abstract: The global energy market has been making significant efforts to transition to renewable energy. As a result, one of the main technologies that researchers have focused on is photovoltaic cells. Bifacial photovoltaic (BPV) modules have evolved in solar energy adoption by allowing solar radiation to be collected on both their front and rear surfaces simultaneously, offering higher energy yield and a lower levelized cost of energy (LCOE) than monofacial modules. This short review presents and highlights the advancements in BPV technology from 2000-2025 by synthesizing the evidence derived from 60 peer-reviewed articles, conference papers, and technical reports. The literature was categorized into three main topics: 1) Performance, 2) Techno-economics, and 3) Optimization. It illustrates important developments in cell technologies such as PERC, n-PERT, and HJT, and improvements in module design, encapsulation materials, rear-side energy harvesting, and mount configurations. Furthermore, it addresses how improved simulation tools, standardization techniques, and electrical and thermal modeling have enabled a broader commercial adoption of bifacial PV technology. Numerous optimization options, ranging from cell-level doping and coating to farm-level layout and albedo management, have also enhanced bifacial performance and cost-effectiveness. The review underscores the important role of BPV technology in achieving global sustainable energy plans and illustrates useful insights for researchers, developers, and policymakers to encourage rapid deployment and integration into future energy systems.

title: Iterative Latent-Query Attention for Pancreatic Region Identification in Contrast-Enhanced CT Slices
authors: Maher Alrahhal, Abdul-Kadir Hamid, Talal Bonny, Samar Mouti
publish: 2025 Advances in Science and Engineering Technology International Conferences (ASET)
abstract: Accurate pancreas identification from abdominal CT scans is a critical step in computer-assisted diagnosis and treatment planning for pancreatic diseases. This paper introduces LatentQueryFormer, a novel transformer-inspired deep learning framework designed to classify pancreas regions from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2 D$</tex> contrast-enhanced CT slices. The proposed model employs an efficient latent query attention mechanism to iteratively aggregate spatial features, followed by transformer-based self-attention refinement for enhanced representation learning. Evaluated on the Pancreas-CT-SEG dataset using a 70:30 train-test split, LatentQueryFormer achieved robust performance, including accuracy of 97.21%, AUC of 0.9721, macro F1-score of 0.9733, and a low error rate of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2. 7 9 \%}$</tex>. These results demonstrate the model's capability to effectively handle class imbalance while maintaining high sensitivity and specificity. The proposed framework offers strong potential for integration into AI-assisted clinical pipelines, and its architecture is adaptable for downstream segmentation and volumetric classification tasks.

title: Applications of AI in oral diseases with special reference to microbial infections
authors: Neveen M. El-Shafaey, Dina Darwish
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: Computer power is growing in tandem with the availability of massive amounts of data prepared for processing through AI applications in the medical industry and the relative ease with which information can be accessed from anywhere in the globe. One area where artificial intelligence could soon make a big impact in dentistry diagnostics. More doctors are seeing the link between a patient's oral health and their overall wellness. An important part of this association is the existence of moderate chronic inflammation in the mouth. Using standard procedures to detect this inflammation can be difficult. The main function of the immune system in the mouth is to prevent infections in the oral cavity, which can harm the teeth, jaws, gingivae, and mucosa. The gingival crevice, saliva, and oral mucosa all constitute distinct oral microbiomes or domains, and each of these hosts' defenses is unique. This paper aims to provide a comprehensive review of AI applications and advances for the identification of oral diseases. This study delves at the possibilities of AI to revolutionize the early detection and treatment of oral infections and inflammations through the use of radiography.

title: A Few-Shot Learning Method for Unmanned Aerial Vehicle Sensor Fault Diagnosis
authors: Jie Fang, Yizong Zhang, Deyong Xiao, Yingzhou Li, Qinsong Xu, Liya Yu
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Science, and Information Processing (AICSIP)
abstract: UAV sensor systems face elevated fault risks during complex environment operations where their sensor systems are vulnerable to operational faults. Deep learning has advanced diagnostic capabilities, yet its strengths are often constrained in small-sample scenarios. To address the challenges of deep learning in few-shot UAV sensor fault diagnosis, this paper proposes CLS-Net (Convolutional LSTM Siamese Network), a novel few-shot learning method for UAV sensor fault diagnosis. Each sub-network integrates a CNN and an LSTM as a joint feature encoder. CLS-Net quantifies sample-pair similarity through L<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</inf>-distance computation in the encoded metric space Comprehensive evaluations on realworld multi-rotor UAV flight data confirm that CLS-Net achieves a statistically significant improvement over baseline methods in fault detection accuracy under few-shot conditions.
